strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, use Skill 1's linear damage to clear minions while poking your lane opponent. Avoid forced fights, as your early damage is low and cooldowns are long. When your allied Jungler comes for a gank, ensure you land Skill 2's guaranteed stun to secure a kill setup, gaining assists and steadily farming gold.
strategy.midGame: As your skill levels rise and you acquire equipment, your burst damage spikes drastically. Hide in bushes and unleash your full combo ('Skill 2 (Stun) -> Skill 3 (Continuous Damage) -> Skill 1 (Finisher)') on isolated enemies. Because your passive reduces the enemy's magic defense, you can instantly delete squishy targets.
strategy.lateGame: In the late game, you boast overwhelming burst damage capable of one-shotting enemy mages and marksmen with a single combo. Play like an assassin, sharing map vision with your team and looking for ambush opportunities from blind spots and bushes. Taking out one enemy before a teamfight starts is a massive contribution that can decide the outcome of the game.
strategy.teamfight: You are extremely squishy and completely defenseless while your skills are on cooldown after a full combo, so never charge in from the front. In teamfights, position yourself in the backline. Use Skill 2's stun to defend yourself against diving enemy assassins, or capitalize on the chaos to land a full combo on a squishy backline hero, making sure to disengage immediately after.
strategy.commonMistakes: Unleashing your full combo on enemies with high HP or magic defense, such as tanks or fighters, will fail to kill them and leave you vulnerable to a fatal counterattack. Also, Skill 3's foxfire targets random enemies within range; casting it inside a minion wave will scatter the damage, leading to the critical mistake of failing to kill the enemy hero.
strategy.strengths: Your biggest strength is the extreme ease of use and reliable CC provided by Skill 2's targeted, guaranteed stun. Additionally, the synergy between your magic defense-reducing passive and Skill 3's multi-hit damage gives you some of the highest single-target burst damage in the game once you have items. Bush ambush tactics are incredibly potent.
strategy.weaknesses: Your skills are heavily single-target focused, and since Skill 3 selects targets randomly, you are very weak at wave clearing and teamfights against multiple grouped enemies. Lacking any mobility skills, you have very low maneuverability and no escape tools other than Skill 2's stun once an enemy closes the gap. The window of vulnerability after using all your skills is also extremely large.

synergy.reason: Along with Daki's reliable single-target stun, Son Goku's high firepower can instantly burst kill the enemy.
This is because it is easy to connect the enemy's physical strength that Daki has reduced to consecutive kills by taking advantage of Assassin's skill reset.

counters.reason: This is because the damage reduction in the heavy sword state allows him to withstand combos, and the high firepower of his counterattack can cause him to be defeated.
This is because before you can fire the skill, you will be attacked by stealth and Daki, who has no durability, will die instantly.

passive.name: Suppressive Fire
passive.description: The 5th normal attack or enhanced normal attack after activating a skill becomes a strafing attack that deals 3 damage to enemy heroes in a straight line in front of you. Inflicts physical damage of 70 + 4.5% of maximum HP (1% increase for every 100 physical attack power) to enemy hero units, and 207 (120 + 50% of physical attack power) to non-hero enemy units.

skill1.name: Blowfish Grenade
skill1.description: Throws a blowfish bomb at the specified location, dealing 520 (520 + 120% of additional physical attack power) physical damage to enemies within the area and reducing enemy movement speed by 25% for 2 seconds. Additionally, you gain visibility of the target the bomb hits.

skill2.name: Shark Cannon
skill2.description: Fires a single rocket in the specified direction, knocking back enemies within range. When it hits the first enemy hero unit, it explodes, dealing 450 (450 + 100% of additional physical attack power) physical damage to enemies within the area and magic damage equal to 5% of the target's missing HP.

skill3.name: Air Support
skill3.description:
Summons a pufferfish airship in the specified direction and bombs it for 14 seconds. The airship drops a bomb at a random enemy within the bombing area every second, and after a certain period of time, it deals 520 (520 + 120% of additional physical attack power) physical damage to enemies near the drop location and reduces the enemy's movement speed by 25% for 1 second.
Mechanical weapons can gain visibility within the bombing area of ​​the Puffer-type airship.

hero_009.status_text: 魯班7号 ろばんななごう 機械兵器 調整 数値強化 見習いマークスマン 0/100 マークスマン 連射型マークスマン Rangedポーク 終盤 難易度：ノーマル

strategy.earlyGame: Focus your laning phase around your passive's strafe, which deals max HP percentage damage. A powerful technique is to basic attack the air four times and use the fifth enhanced attack (strafe) on the enemy hero. Win damage trades by applying a slow with Skill 1's bomb and guaranteeing the follow-up enhanced basic attack (strafe). Always be on high alert for ganks.
strategy.midGame: As you complete your items and gain attack damage, the max HP percentage damage of your strafe becomes even more lethal. Skill 2's rocket can cross the entire map, allowing you to snipe and kill low-HP enemies fleeing in distant lanes. When enemies approach, it is crucial to push them away with Skill 2's close-range knockback and immediately counterattack with a strafe.
strategy.lateGame: Acting as a stationary turret with overwhelming firepower, you bear the sole responsibility of being your team's main damage source in teamfights. Deploy Skill 3's airship to secure vision and continuous AoE damage, and repeatedly unleash strafes from behind it while weaving in your skills. You can melt even the tankiest enemies in seconds with your max HP percentage damage, so your top priority is simply staying alive and continuously attacking.
strategy.teamfight: Rely entirely on your allied tanks and supports for protection, dealing damage from the absolute safest backline position. Maximize your damage output by cycling through Skills 1, 2, and 3, ensuring you weave in your passive's strafe between each skill cast. If an enemy assassin flanks you, calmly knock them back with Skill 2 and defend yourself by shredding them with your devastating strafe damage.
strategy.commonMistakes: The most common mistakes are stepping too far forward to get caught by enemy CC, or getting isolated and targeted by assassins. Because you cannot move during your strafe animation, it is highly dangerous to strafe when you need to dodge enemy skills, resulting in unnecessary damage taken. Firing Skill 2 recklessly into the distance leaves you without your only self-defense knockback, which should also be avoided.
strategy.strengths: Your greatest strength is the max HP percentage physical damage barrage from your passive 'Strafe,' which can be instantly triggered after casting a skill. This gives you unparalleled carry potential, capable of easily shredding even the hardest tanks. You also possess map-wide sniping capabilities with Skill 2 and extensive vision control with Skill 3's airship.
strategy.weaknesses: Lacking any dashes or movement speed buffs, your mobility is incredibly low, giving you zero escape potential. Your only self-defense tool is Skill 2's close-range knockback, leaving you helpless and easily killed if multiple enemies dive you. Furthermore, you are rooted in place during your powerful strafe, making you an easy target for enemy skillshots and long-range attacks.

synergy.reason: Because his strong control creates an environment where he can safely deal damage.
This is because his ability to maintain his front line is high, and he creates space that makes it easy for him in the back to attack.

counters.reason: Because of their high mobility and burst damage, they can ignore the vanguard and be defeated directly.
Because he has a skill that nullifies physical damage, he is at a disadvantage when trading damage in lane battles.

passive.name: Flame Incantation
passive.description:
Enemies hit by her active skills gain 1 stack of burn effect, and she gains 1 stack of movement speed boost. Each can stack up to 10 times and lasts for 3 seconds.
For each burn stack, enemies take an additional 10 (+2% magical attack) magic damage when damaged by her active skills.
For each movement speed stack, her movement speed is increased by 2%.

skill1.name: Scorching Barrage
skill1.description:
Fires 5 fireballs in the target direction. Each fireball deals 280 (+30% magical attack) magic damage to enemy heroes upon hit and disappears. It also deals equal damage to non-hero units along the path.
If multiple fireballs hit the same enemy hero, the damage of subsequent hits is reduced to 30%.

skill2.name: Chaos Cinder
skill2.description:
Fires Chaos Cinder in the target direction. When the flame hits an enemy or reaches its maximum range, it transforms into a flame vortex, stunning the first enemy hit for 1 second.
While the flame vortex persists, it deals 125 (+20% magical attack) magic damage to nearby enemies every 0.5 seconds and slows their movement speed by 30% for 1.5 seconds.

skill3.name: Blazing Brilliance
skill3.description:
Fires a continuous beam of fire in the target direction. The direction can be adjusted during the cast, dealing 150 (+21% magical attack) magic damage to enemies in its path up to 12 times.
Upon casting, she gains a shield that absorbs 850 (+88% magical attack) damage and grants Control Immunity while the shield persists.
After 0.5 seconds of casting, the skill can be canceled by casting it again.

hero_010.status_text: Angela Scorchette Adjustment Trainee Mage 0/100 Mage Artillery Mage Burst/Shield Balance Difficulty: Normal

strategy.earlyGame: Use Skill 1's fireballs to quickly clear minions and seize lane priority. Due to your passive's burn effect, hitting skills consecutively increases your damage, so try to hit the enemy hero through the minion wave. Skill 2's fire vortex stun has a narrow hitbox, so predict enemy movements to land it reliably.
strategy.midGame: Ambushing enemies from the bushes becomes a highly effective tactic. Landing the combo of 'Skill 2 (Stun) -> Skill 1 -> Skill 3 (Beam)' rapidly builds your burn stacks, allowing you to incinerate almost any enemy. Since your passive grants you a movement speed boost when your skills hit, you can smoothly roam the map and reposition after using skills.
strategy.lateGame: With your magic attack fully scaled, Skill 3's beam possesses devastating destructive power, capable of melting even enemy tanks in an instant. Before teamfights, poke from a distance using Skills 1 and 2 to disrupt the enemy formation. Since activating Skill 3 grants you a massive shield and Super Armor, you can ignore minor damage and CC, forcibly maintaining your massive beam.
strategy.teamfight: From behind your tanks or from the bushes, throw Skill 2's stun into narrow corridors or grouped enemies. Immediately fire Skill 3's beam at the stunned targets, sweeping across multiple enemies to wipe them out. Since the beam's direction can be adjusted slowly, sweep it to track fleeing enemies. Survive approaching enemies by relying on your shield and Super Armor.
strategy.commonMistakes: You cannot move while casting Skill 3's beam, so activating it without checking your surroundings will result in you being focus-fired by other enemies. Be careful, as losing the shield also removes your Super Armor. Furthermore, missing Skill 2, the primary setup for your combo, makes it much easier for enemies to dodge your Skills 1 and 3, drastically dropping your damage output.
strategy.strengths: Your greatest strength is the staggering burst damage when all your skills connect. Combined with your passive's burn effect, you have the overwhelming firepower to melt even the tankiest enemies in a single combo. Additionally, the shield and Super Armor during Skill 3 allow you to uniquely ignore enemy CC and counterattacks to ensure your damage goes through, despite being a mage.
strategy.weaknesses: Your main skills rely on difficult skillshots, and missing Skill 2 cuts your threat level in half. You lack mobility, and because you are rooted during Skill 3, you are completely helpless if a highly mobile assassin gets behind you or if the enemy has enough damage to break your shield and burst you down. Your heavily telegraphed, linear attacks are also a clear weakness, making them easy to predict.

synergy.reason: Can cleanse CC, making it safer for her to channel her ultimate without being interrupted.
Provides high durability and shields at the frontline, holding enemies in place so she can easily land her skills.

counters.reason: Can quickly close the gap with high mobility and easily assassinate her while she is immobilized during her ultimate.
Can stop her with a targeted hard CC and burst her down instantly.

passive.name: Encouraging Thoughts
passive.description: Whenever her skills hit an enemy, she gains a 25% movement speed boost for 2 seconds.

skill1.name: Blossoming Fan
skill1.description: Throws a fan in the target direction that returns like a boomerang, dealing 550 (+80% magical attack) magic damage to enemies in its path. The damage is reduced by 20% for each enemy hit, down to a minimum of 40% of the initial damage.

skill2.name: Honeysweet Breeze
skill2.description: Summons a whirlwind at the target location, dealing 300 (+50% magical attack) magic damage to enemies in the area and knocking them up for 1.5 seconds.

skill3.name: Meteor Storm
skill3.description: Summons a meteor shower that continuously damages nearby enemies. Meteors deal 400 (+100% magical attack) magic damage upon hitting an enemy. Each skill cast can hit the same target up to 4 times, but damage from the second hit onwards is reduced to 50%. While active, her movement speed continuously increases (lasts 5 seconds, up to half of her maximum speed).

hero_011.status_text: Xiao Qiao Breezeheart Trainee Mage 0/100 Mage Poke Mage Burst/Ranged Late Game Difficulty: Easy

strategy.earlyGame: Use the long range of Skill 1's fan to clear minions and poke the enemy hero from a safe distance. Hitting an enemy with a skill triggers your passive movement speed boost, so the fundamental tactic is to throw the fan and immediately accelerate to dodge enemy attacks. Skill 2's knockup is a powerful self-defense tool, so save it for critical moments instead of using it recklessly.
strategy.midGame: Leverage your high mobility and range to actively roam and gank other lanes with your allies. Ambush from the bushes with the 'Skill 2 knockup -> Skill 1 double hit' combo to chunk the enemy's HP in an instant. Simply activating Skill 3 automatically deals damage to enemies in a wide area and boosts your movement speed, making it extremely useful for both chasing and escaping.
strategy.lateGame: Once fully geared, the double-hit damage from Skill 1's fan alone is enough to leave squishy backline enemies near death. During teamfight standoffs, continuously poke by throwing Skill 1 to break the enemy formation. By combining your passive's acceleration with Skill 3's movement speed boost, you can sprint across the battlefield, fighting while dodging enemy skills.
strategy.teamfight: Continuously deal damage with Skill 1 from behind your allies, and look for an opening to catch enemy carries or assassins with Skill 2's knockup. When the fight turns into a chaotic brawl, activate Skill 3 and step slightly into the enemy formation to rain down meteor damage over a wide area. Utilizing Skill 3's auto-targeting and your passive's acceleration to maintain distance while dodging enemy attacks is a powerful strategy.
strategy.commonMistakes: Missing Skill 2's whirlwind, your only CC and self-defense tool, or wasting it on minion waves leaves you defenseless against approaching assassins, making you an easy kill. Additionally, overextending to catch enemies in Skill 3's radius can get you instantly burst down by focused fire due to your low durability, so extreme caution is required.
strategy.strengths: Your strengths are your overwhelming long-range damage from Skill 1's fan and your high mobility from the movement speed boosts triggered by skill hits and Skill 3. As long as you can land your Skill 1 skillshot, you can constantly maintain your acceleration to outmaneuver enemies. Skill 2's knockup has a fast cast time, making it an incredibly excellent and powerful CC tool for both offense and defense.
strategy.weaknesses: Your durability is extremely low, and while your movement speed is high, you have no dash abilities. This makes you incredibly fragile if you are locked down by targeted, guaranteed CC like stuns. It is also important to note that Skill 1's fan damage decreases with each enemy it hits, meaning you cannot deal maximum damage to an enemy hero hiding behind a minion wave.

synergy.reason: Can pull enemies into one spot, making it easier for her to land her knock-up and ultimate on multiple targets.
Can lock down powerful enemy assassins, creating a safe environment for her to deal damage from the backline.

counters.reason: Can close the gap from afar and quickly burst down her fragile health bar before she can combo.
Possesses long-range stuns, allowing him to easily interrupt and poke her from a safe distance.

passive.name: Dragon's Roar
passive.description: For every 3% of max HP lost, he gains 1% damage reduction.

The enhanced basic attack has increased range, deals 65 (+15% physical attack) physical damage to nearby enemies, and slows their movement speed by 25% for 2.5 seconds.

hero_012.status_text: Zilong Fearless Spear Trainee Assassin 0/100 Assassin/Fighter Diver Mobility Early Game Difficulty: Normal

strategy.earlyGame: Farm gold and experience with a solid playstyle by closing the gap using Skill 1's dash and enhanced basic attack, and dealing damage while healing with Skill 2's continuous thrusts. Your passive reduces damage taken as your HP drops, allowing you to fight tenaciously even when slightly wounded. Avoid forced engagements until you unlock Skill 3, as you lack hard CC.
strategy.midGame: Unlocking Skill 3's jumping knockup gives you a powerful initiation tool. Ambush enemies with the 'Skill 3 dive -> Skill 2 poke -> Skill 1 chase' combo. The empowered state applied after hitting Skill 3 adds bonus magic damage based on the target's max HP, allowing you to deal significant damage even against tanks.
strategy.lateGame: Fight persistently on the frontline, utilizing your passive's damage reduction that scales with missing HP and Skill 2's healing. When your HP gets critically low, your empowered state's percentage damage doubles, allowing you to unleash terrifying burst damage and turn the tables on your enemies. Accurately land Skill 3 on multiple enemies to steer late-game teamfights in your favor.
strategy.teamfight: Look for an opening to dive directly onto the enemy backline mages or marksmen with Skill 3 for a knockup. Thanks to your passive's damage reduction, you can barely survive even when focus-fired in the middle of the enemy team. Heal with Skill 2 while at low HP, and use Skill 1's mobility to either disengage from the fight or chase down low-HP stragglers.
strategy.commonMistakes: Overestimating your passive's damage reduction and diving into a position where you take so much focused fire that your healing cannot keep up will lead to a quick death. Furthermore, missing Skill 3, the setup for your combo, prevents you from closing the gap and applying the empowered percentage damage, severely gutting your damage output. Avoid using Skill 1 purely for damage and leaving yourself without an escape.
strategy.strengths: Your greatest strength is your 'astonishing tenacity at near-death', granted by your passive that reduces damage taken as your HP drops. Combined with Skill 2's healing, you excel at winning clutch, razor-thin fights. Skill 3 allows you to dive and knock up from a distance, and the added percentage magic damage ensures stable damage output regardless of the target.
strategy.weaknesses: Since almost all of your damage relies on close-range physical attacks, you are highly vulnerable to CC like slows and stuns that prevent you from closing the gap. Missing Skill 3 makes recovery extremely difficult. Due to the nature of your passive, you must lose some HP to achieve true tankiness, making you weak against overwhelming instant burst damage that can one-shot you from full HP.

synergy.reason: Her massive magic burst damage pairs perfectly with his ultimate knock-up, quickly wiping out enemies.
Provides shields and healing, greatly enhancing his survivability after diving into the enemy formation.

counters.reason: Can completely lock down his mobility with powerful suppression skills, leaving him vulnerable to focused fire.
Uses chain stuns and shields to block his engage and interrupt his attempts to disengage with skills.

His 4th basic attack is empowered, dealing 450 (+70% magical attack) magic damage to enemies in the area and knocking them back for 0.75 seconds.
Whenever he casts a skill, he gains 1 stack of a shield (up to 3 stacks). Each stack absorbs 300 (+35% magical attack) damage for 3 seconds.

The field lasts for 4 seconds, dealing 50 (+5% magical attack) magic damage every 0.5 seconds to enemies within.

hero_013.status_text: Mozi Peacekeeper Adjustment Trainee Mage 0/100 Mage Mage/Fighter Poke/Control Balanced Difficulty: Hard

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, utilize the Passive shield and the long-range artillery of Skill 2 to clear minions and harass the enemy from a safe distance. Since you gain a shield every time you cast a skill, you can stably progress through the laning phase while minimizing damage taken. The knockback from the enhanced basic attack is also useful for poking.
strategy.midGame: In the mid game, use the stun effect of Skill 2 and the continuous damage from its field to assist teammates in getting kills or poke during objective fights. The combo of repositioning with Skill 1's dash, landing an enhanced basic attack, and following up with Skill 2 is powerful. You need to maintain your shield to increase your sustained combat capability.
strategy.lateGame: In the late game, with full equipment, the magic damage and stun from Skill 2 become a massive threat to enemies. Before teamfights, poke from a long distance to whittle down the enemy's HP. If you can land Skill 2 on the enemy carry and stun them, you can create an advantageous situation instantly. Do not step forward carelessly; strictly maintain positioning that leverages your range.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, disrupt the enemy formation with the long-range stun from Skill 2. When enemies approach your team, deploy the energy barrier of your Ultimate to stun enemies in a wide area. If you dash into the middle of the enemy team with Skill 1 and use your Ultimate, you can lock down multiple enemies for a long time, creating an environment where your team can easily deal damage.
strategy.commonMistakes: A common mistake is overestimating your durability and diving alone into the enemy team, missing your Ultimate, and taking focus fire. Also, if the accuracy of your Skill 2 is low, your contribution to the team drops significantly. Even with the Passive shield, you are still a mage, so you must be careful when your skills are on cooldown and you are defenseless.
strategy.strengths: The greatest strength is that all skills have hard CC (knockback, stun). Skill 2 in particular can safely stun from a long distance, and thanks to the Passive shield, you have high durability despite being a mage. It has an excellent balance of offense, defense, and CC, making it highly capable of supporting allies.
strategy.weaknesses: The weakness is that Skill 2 travels in a straight line and is easy to dodge once the projectile speed is learned. Also, while deploying the Ultimate, you cannot move, making you vulnerable to being interrupted by long-range attacks or CC. Furthermore, due to high reliance on magic power, damage tends to be somewhat lacking until fully equipped, and missing skills leaves a large opening.

synergy.reason: Her high burst damage easily eliminates enemies that he locks down with his continuous crowd control.
Provides strong frontline protection, allowing him to safely poke and land his skills from a distance.

counters.reason: His long-range poke and ultimate can easily interrupt his abilities and zone him out.
Her mobility and range make it difficult for him to land his skillshots, allowing her to easily kite him.

passive.name: burst of vitality

Rolls in the specified direction, strengthens the next normal attack, and deals 454 (285 + 100% of physical attack power) physical damage to enemies along the route. After activating an enhanced normal attack, you can immediately use a normal attack with increased range.
If there is an enemy hero near where you rolled, your movement speed increases by 80% (decreases continuously over 2 seconds).

hero_014.status_text: Sun Shangxiang Sonshouko Heavy Artillery Princess Adjustment Apprentice Marksman 0/100 Marksman Heavy Artillery Marksman Charge/Kill Endgame Difficulty: Hard

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, use the extended range from Skill 1's roll and the enhanced basic attack to pressure enemies from a safe distance. Hitting basic attacks reduces the cooldown of Skill 1, so keep attacking minions to increase Skill 1's uptime. The movement speed increases after rolling, so strictly practice hit-and-run tactics.
strategy.midGame: In the mid game, initiate your burst damage with the Skill 2 bomb. When Skill 2 hits, the enemy's physical defense is reduced, and basic attacks against marked enemies deal additional damage. The combo of "Skill 1 -> Skill 2 -> enhanced basic attack" can shred HP instantly. In small skirmishes in the jungle or river, utilize your mobility to reposition.
strategy.lateGame: In the late game, she can output extremely high physical damage. In teamfights, position yourself in the backline, adjusting your position with Skill 1 while reliably whittling down the enemy front line with enhanced basic attacks. The long-range ballista of her Ultimate is perfect for finishing off fleeing enemies or dealing AoE damage to multiple enemies as poke before a fight starts.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, it is crucial to keep an appropriate distance with Skill 1's roll to avoid ever being caught by enemy assassins or fighters. Lower enemy defense from behind your frontline with Skill 2, and spam enhanced basic attacks to deal damage. Utilize the Passive effect that increases movement speed when enemies are nearby, and keep attacking while constantly moving.
strategy.commonMistakes: A common mistake is rolling forward into the enemy team with Skill 1 and getting bursted down instantly. Use the roll for escaping or repositioning, and only roll aggressively when safety is guaranteed. Also, missing Skill 2 significantly drops your damage output, so if you lack the awareness to land it reliably instead of just throwing it randomly, you won't be effective.
strategy.strengths: Her greatest strengths are overwhelming burst damage, and the high mobility and long range provided by Skill 1. The defense reduction effect from Skill 2 allows her to deal heavy damage even to tanky enemies, and the long-range AoE attack from her Ultimate is also powerful. Weaving in basic attacks increases skill uptime, making her sustained damage excellent as well.
strategy.weaknesses: Her weakness is the lack of CC skills and extremely low durability; once caught, she will be killed without being able to do anything. Also, since her main damage source relies on basic attacks and Skill 1, she is vulnerable to attack speed reduction and blind effects, and losing mobility puts her at a massive disadvantage instantly. A positioning mistake leads to instant death.

synergy.reason: This is because she can restrain the enemy with strong CC and create an opening for her to inflict burst damage from a safe position.
This is because she can force group battles with a wide range of launches and create an environment where she can move freely and defeat enemies individually.

counters.reason: This is because her mobility will be completely nullified with a guaranteed hard CC, and she will be defeated without giving her a chance to escape.
This is because you can use a long-range stun to stop her movement and prevent her from hitting and away, which is her specialty.

hero_015.status_text: Zhuangzi Sou Dream Sage Apprentice Support 0/100 Support/Tank Guardian Support Team Buff Late Stage Difficulty: Easy

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, focus on stacking Skill 2 while using the periodically triggering Passive "Dream Realm" to safely lane. Since it provides CC immunity, damage reduction, and increased movement speed, it is easy to evade enemy ganks. Use the illusionary butterflies of Skill 1 from a distance to slow enemies and support your allies.
strategy.midGame: From the mid game onwards, stick with your teammates and use the movement speed buff and damage stacks from Skill 2 to gain an advantage in small skirmishes. Skill 2 grants stacks to you and your allies, providing a powerful movement speed buff at max 5 stacks, making it extremely strong for chasing or retreating. Keep the automatic trigger of the Passive in mind when positioning.
strategy.lateGame: In the late game, protecting allies with your Ultimate against the enemy's powerful CC skills becomes the most important task. As you are the cornerstone of teamfights, always position yourself near your allied carries. Restrict enemy movements with the wide AoE slow of Skill 1, boost ally mobility with Skill 2, and control the board with the CC immunity from your Passive and Ultimate.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, your primary role is to deploy the "Dream Realm" Ultimate the moment the enemy uses crucial CC skills, cleansing CC for all allies and granting them a shield and CC immunity. At the same time, stand on the frontline and spam Skill 2 to deal damage to enemies while boosting your team's mobility. Utilize your own CC immunity to disrupt the enemy formation.
strategy.commonMistakes: Common mistakes include using the Ultimate solely to protect yourself, or wasting it before the enemy's CC arrives. It is the strongest defensive skill for protecting the entire team, so you must accurately judge the timing. Also, despite having CC cleanse from the Passive, your base durability isn't exceptionally high, so be careful not to get isolated.
strategy.strengths: The greatest strength is the periodic self-CC cleanse and damage reduction, along with the massive AoE CC cleanse and shield provided to the entire team by the Ultimate. He can completely neutralize enemy control heroes. Furthermore, the stacking damage and movement speed increase from Skill 2 allow him to powerfully support allies and deal damage in extended teamfights.
strategy.weaknesses: Lacking hard CC, his ability to proactively catch enemies or set up kills is low. He relies heavily on the environment his allies create to deal damage, and if his team isn't fed, it's difficult for him to carry the game alone. Also, his counter-measures against burst damage are limited aside from the shield, making him vulnerable to being burst down instantly.

synergy.reason: By releasing CC, we can continue to take advantage of her mobility and safely deliver continuous high firepower.
By removing the enemy's CC while his ULT is active, he can deal fatal area damage without interruption.

counters.reason: Because it doesn't rely on CC and attacks with overwhelming burst damage, the strength of CC removal cannot be utilized.
This is because continuous pokes and stuns from a distance will keep them from getting close, creating a situation where you have no choice but to use ULT.

passive.name: Passive Skill: Natural Will
passive.description:
[Tag] Purify, damage reduction, movement speed increase
Every 6 seconds, enters the "dream realm" for 2 seconds and recovers 40 HP (40 + 0.6% of additional HP). "Dream Realm": Removes CC from self and strengthens self for 2 seconds. While enhanced, all CC will be nullified, damage taken will be reduced by 15%, and movement speed will be increased by 15%.
[HP recovery] Lv.1: 40 / Lv.15: 80

skill1.name: Skill 1: Butterfly
skill1.description:
[Tag] Enhancement, movement speed reduction, damage
CD: 8 seconds / MP consumption: 60
Releases a phantom butterfly in a fan-shaped area and strengthens passive skills for 2 seconds. The butterfly deals 400 (400 + 72% of magic attack) magic damage to enemies within range and reduces enemy movement speed by 30% for 2 seconds.
[Basic damage] Lv.1: 400 / Lv.2: 480 / Lv.3: 560 / Lv.4: 640 / Lv.5: 720 / Lv.6: 800
[Movement speed reduction] Lv.1: 30% / Lv.2: 36% / Lv.3: 42% / Lv.4: 48% / Lv.5: 54% / Lv.6: 60%

Deals 250 (250 + 40% of magic attack) magic damage to nearby enemies and applies 1 stack of engraving, and each stack deals 125 (125 + 20% of magic attack) magic damage to the target for 5 seconds. Maximum 5 stacks. At the same time, give 1 stack of Imprint to yourself and nearby friendly heroes, and each stack increases the target's movement speed by 3%. Maximum of 5 stacks, duration is 5 seconds. Passive effect: Activates skill 2 once every 7 seconds.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Tenjin Unity
skill3.description:
[Tag] CC release, super armor, shield
CD: 60 seconds / MP consumption: 150
By deploying a dream realm, it removes CC from self and nearby allied heroes, and also grants a 700 (700 + 13% of additional HP) shield and CC immunity to self and allied heroes for 2 seconds.
[Cooldown] Lv.1: 60 / Lv.2: 55 / Lv.3: 50
[Granted Shield] Lv.1: 700 / Lv.2: 1050 / Lv.3: 1400

passive.name: magnetic barrier
passive.description: Skills can also deal the same amount of damage to towers and crystals, and CC skills can jam towers and crystals for 1.2 seconds. When you damage a tower or crystal with a skill, you will steal parts and recover 280 HP.

skill1.name: Small Overlord's Shield
skill1.description: Deploy your shield and attack. Gain a shield of 650 (650 + 16% of additional maximum HP) and increase your movement speed by 50% for 3 seconds (effect gradually decreases). At the same time, strengthens the next normal attack, jumps and slams the ground forcefully, dealing 569 (350 + 122% of physical attack power) physical damage to enemies within range and knocking them up for 1 second.

skill2.name: mechanical magic claw
skill2.description: Operate a mechanical panda and attack with magic claws within a fan-shaped area, dealing 580 (400 + 100% of physical attack power) physical damage to the enemy and stunning them for 1 second.

skill3.name: runaway panda
skill3.description:
Operate a mechanical panda and attack by spinning while spreading your arms for 3 seconds, dealing 380 (380 + 110% of additional physical attack power) physical damage to nearby enemies every 0.5 seconds. While spinning, reduce enemy movement speed by 15% for 0.5 seconds every second.
Other skills can be activated while spinning.

hero_016.status_text: Liu Zen Ryuzen Runaway Engine Adjustment Apprentice Support 0/100 Support/Tank Tactical Support Progress/CC Early Difficulty: Normal

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, utilize the shield and movement speed boost from Skill 1 to aggressively approach the enemy and apply pressure with the knock-up from your enhanced basic attack. Also, take advantage of your skills damaging towers and healing you at the same time; push the minion wave and hit the enemy tower to chip its health while maintaining your own HP.
strategy.midGame: In the mid game, securely secure kills in coordination with allies using the powerful CC chain combo of Skill 1 and Skill 2. The basic flow is "approach and knock-up with Skill 1 -> stun with Skill 2". Furthermore, fully utilize the trait of being able to jam towers; forcing a tower dive to get a kill even without minions is a potent strategy.
strategy.lateGame: In late-game teamfights and sieges, Liu Shan's tower neutralization ability shows tremendous power. Jam the enemy defense towers with Skill 1 and 2 while swiftly destroying the towers with your allies. It is crucial to land the CC of Skill 1 and 2 on the enemy carry, and use the spinning attack of the Ultimate to deal continuous damage and slow them.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, stand at the very frontline, rushing into the enemy formation with Skill 1 to disrupt it. Your role is to neutralize the enemy carry by consecutively landing the knock-up from Skill 1 and the stun from Skill 2. After that, activate the spinning attack of your Ultimate to stick to enemies and continuously slow them. You can cast other skills while spinning, allowing for flexible responses.
strategy.commonMistakes: A common mistake is forcefully chasing highly mobile enemies with Skill 1 and getting isolated from your team. Also, overestimating your tower diving capability and rushing into a tower when allies cannot follow up, resulting in taking enemy counterattacks and dying meaninglessly, is very common. Sense of distance and timing with allies are key.
strategy.strengths: His greatest strength is his unique siege capability—he can deal direct damage to structures (towers and crystals), jam them to disable their attacks, and even heal his own HP in the process. Additionally, he possesses two types of powerful hard CC (knock-up on Skill 1 and stun on Skill 2), making him exceptionally adept at locking down enemies.
strategy.weaknesses: His attack range is extremely short, and he cannot deal damage or apply CC unless he gets in point-blank range. If kited from a distance with slows or CC, he will be whittled down without being able to do anything. His only gap-closing tool is the movement speed boost from Skill 1, making it a weakness that enemies with mobility skills can easily escape him.

synergy.reason: By continuing to hold down the enemy on the front lines, you can create an environment where you can continue to safely unleash ultra-high firepower from a distance.
Combined with his tower dive and CC, he can finish off enemies at once with continuous attacks.

counters.reason: Because it has high mobility, it can be destroyed by true damage from a distance before it can get close.
The acceleration from his skills makes it easier for the entire team to avoid his engagement, completely blocking his strengths.

passive.name: Lamentation
passive.description: Activate normal attacks and skills by playing. Every 3 times played, the next normal attack becomes an energy code and deals 290 (290 + 80% of magic attack) magic damage to enemies on the route.

skill1.name: Kyoka
skill1.description:
Plays a musical note, dealing 340 (340 + 47% of magic attack) magic damage to up to 2 enemies within range. Hit notes bounce back towards nearby enemies (up to 2 times).
If multiple notes hit the same hero target, the damage dealt by subsequent notes is reduced to 30%.

skill2.name: Rika
skill2.description:
Moves in the specified direction and plays the guitar, dealing 450 (450 + 60% of magic attack) magic damage to nearby enemies and reducing movement speed by 15% for 2 seconds. If you hit a hero, you will recover 100 (100 + 15% of magic attack) HP. When hitting a non-hero unit, the HP recovery effect is halved. While moving, damage reduction effect increases by 10% and movement speed reduction is nullified.
If you activate "Kyouka" while moving, you can move again.

skill3.name: Magical sound attack
skill3.description:
Plays violently for 5 seconds, dealing 200 (200 + 20% of magic attack) magic damage every 0.5 seconds.
Movement speed increases by 50% at the beginning of the performance, and decreases continuously over 3 seconds. If there are enemy heroes nearby during the performance, you will receive a 10% damage reduction effect, and for each additional enemy you will receive an additional 5% damage reduction effect.

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, use the bouncing notes of Skill 1 to safely clear minions and harass the enemy simultaneously. After casting 3 times, his basic attack is enhanced into an energy chord that deals piercing magic damage, so smartly weave skills and basic attacks to seize control of the laning phase. Be careful about taking poke damage.
strategy.midGame: From the mid game, participate in small skirmishes utilizing the mobility and healing effects of Skill 2. Hitting an enemy hero with Skill 2 restores HP, and taking reduced damage while moving increases your survivability. Poke with Skill 1, approach with Skill 2 at opportune moments, and focus on applying slow to assist your team in securing kills.
strategy.lateGame: In late-game teamfights, the explosive power of the Ultimate is the key. Utilize the massive movement speed boost upon starting the performance to dive straight into the enemy formation. Since the damage reduction effect scales with the number of nearby enemies during the performance, positioning to catch as many enemies as possible is vital. Target the enemy carry and decimate them with massive AoE continuous magic damage.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, following up after initiation is the rule. Wait for the enemy to use their major CC skills, then activate the Ultimate to gain acceleration and damage reduction while plunging into the center of the enemy team. Further use Skill 2 to slow enemies and heal yourself, while spamming the Passive's energy chords to unleash overwhelming AoE burst damage.
strategy.commonMistakes: A frequent mistake is overestimating the powerful damage reduction of the Ultimate and diving first into the enemy formation without team support. No matter how tanky you become, focused fire will kill you. Also, failing to fully utilize his survivability by only hitting minions with Skill 2's heal is another common failure.
strategy.strengths: The greatest strength is the overwhelming AoE continuous damage during the Ultimate, combined with a damage reduction effect that gets stronger the more enemies are around. He shines when diving right into the middle of multiple enemies in a teamfight, possessing the potential to single-handedly shatter the enemy formation. His sustained combat ability from the Passive's piercing attacks and Skill 2's self-heal is also excellent.
strategy.weaknesses: His combat power and survivability without his Ultimate are extremely low, making positioning during its cooldown very difficult. Also, because he needs to get very close to enemies to deal damage, he is in danger of being killed without doing anything against long-range poke or enemies with strong hard CC (stun or knockback) that can stop his dive.

synergy.reason: Jump into a group of enemies and force them together, making it easy to hit with your ultimate.
Increased movement speed and recovery can support approaching enemies and survival.

counters.reason: A powerful suppression skill can completely stop your actions during ultimate.
Since he also has the suppression skill, his movements are blocked when he approaches.

passive.name: kiss of death
passive.description: Attacking an enemy from the front will not result in a critical hit, but attacking from behind will always result in a critical hit. Initial critical damage is 125%, and critical damage increases by 0.5% for every 1% critical rate. Attacking non-hero units head-on increases damage by 20%, up to a maximum of 2000 damage when attacking non-hero units.

skill1.name: arc light
skill1.description: Attacks twice with double blade, dealing 150 (150 + 65% of additional physical attack) physical damage with each attack.

skill2.name: quick feet
skill2.description:
Teleports forward and attacks one enemy near the destination, dealing 270 (270 + 85% of additional physical attack) physical damage and imprinting a mark on the enemy for 10 seconds. Additionally, own attack speed increases by 40% for 3 seconds.
Each time you attack a stamped enemy, reduce the target's movement speed by 25% for 1 second and reduce the cooldown of "Swift" by 1 second. Damage taken from enemies with engravings is reduced by 30%.
You can only mark one enemy at a time.

skill3.name: illusion dance
skill3.description:
After a short preparation time, enter stealth for 4 seconds. While in stealth, recovers 150 HP (150 + 25% of additional physical attack + 5% of additional HP) every 0.5 seconds and increases movement speed by 40%.
Attacking breaks stealth, increases your physical attack by 120 for 3 seconds, and increases your movement speed by 30% for 1 second each time you damage an enemy.
Passive: Defeating or assisting a hero resets all skill cooldowns.

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, utilize your Passive, which guarantees critical strikes on attacks from behind, to quickly farm the jungle by attacking monsters from behind. Attacks from the front do not crit, so positioning is everything. When ganking a lane, use the multi-hits of Skill 1 and the dash of Skill 2 to ensure you always engage from the enemy's back.
strategy.midGame: In the mid game, proactively target isolated or low HP enemies. Enter stealth with the Ultimate, gaining movement speed and HP regeneration as you sneak behind the enemy. Strictly execute the assassin combo: "Approach with Ultimate -> mark and gain attack speed from behind with Skill 2 -> deal damage with Skill 1" to instantly execute the enemy carry.
strategy.lateGame: In late-game teamfights, never initiate the fight yourself. Wait for your allies to engage and the enemy's CC skills to be depleted, or when enemy HP is low, before diving in from stealth with your Ultimate. Since kills or assists reset the cooldown of all skills, run through the battlefield aiming for consecutive kills.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, aim for the enemy backline (mages and marksmen) from behind. Approach unnoticed with the Ultimate, then unleash burst damage with Skill 2 and 1 for a quick kill. Once you get a kill or assist, your Passive resets your skills, allowing you to re-enter stealth with the Ultimate to either disengage or hunt the next target.
strategy.commonMistakes: The most common mistake is fighting an enemy face-to-face. Frontal attacks do not crit, so your damage output will be zero. Another typical failure is charging in from stealth during a teamfight without waiting for your team, absorbing enemy CC, and dying instantly. Engaging when you cannot secure a kill is also a fatal error as it misses the skill reset.
strategy.strengths: Her greatest strength is the overwhelming burst damage from guaranteed critical hits attacking from behind, combined with the full skill cooldown reset on kills and assists. If she can participate in a kill, she can repeatedly use her Ultimate's stealth and heal, making her ability to systematically wipe out the enemy team (pentakill potential) extremely high.
strategy.weaknesses: Since she deals no damage in frontal combat, she is weak in situations where enemies don't let her get behind them, or when their backs are against a wall. Also, having very low durability and no hard CC, she will instantly melt if her stealth is broken or if she gets stunned upon diving. Her heavy reliance on teammates to whittle down enemy HP is another weakness.

synergy.reason: Powerful controls stop the enemy's movement, making it easier to target attacks from behind.
It collects enemies and lowers their defense power, so it's easy to aim for skill reset by consecutive kills.

counters.reason: Silence skill and high durability can withstand burst damage and hinder combos.
It can withstand attacks due to its high durability and shield, and there is a high risk of being defeated by a counterattack.

passive.name: stone strike
passive.description:
Every time you deal damage with a normal attack or skill, you have a 50% chance to petrify the enemy for 0.75 seconds. Petrifying the same target has an 8 second cooldown.
Summons the power of the earth and gains a 300 (300 + 8% of additional HP) shield (resets every 3 seconds).

skill1.name: hurricane shock
skill1.description:
Charges in the specified direction and strengthens the next normal attack for 4 seconds. Enhanced normal attack deals 250 (250 + 50% of additional physical attack) physical damage to enemies within range and reduces enemy movement speed by 25% for 2 seconds.
If skill 1 is activated while skill 3 is activated, the charging speed will be slightly slower.

skill2.name: shock impact
skill2.description: Slams the hammer hard at the specified location, dealing 700 (700 + 200% of additional physical attack) physical damage to enemies within the area. Deals 50% additional damage to enemies in the center of the area.

skill3.name: storm hammer
skill3.description:
Swings the hammer for 3.2 seconds, increasing movement speed by 10%. Deals 300 (300 + 95% of additional physical attack) physical damage to nearby enemies every 0.4 seconds, and deals 50% additional damage to enemies in the outer area.
While swinging the hammer, your movement speed increases by 5% every 0.4 seconds, and you also gain a shield of 70 (70 + 3% of your additional HP), up to a maximum of 4 stacks.

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, trade aggressively utilizing the petrify effect from the Passive and the periodically acquired shield. Stick to the enemy with the dash from Skill 1 and the slow from the enhanced basic attack, aiming to trigger petrify while dealing damage. The ideal approach is to fight when the shield is up, creating an HP advantage and seizing lane priority.
strategy.midGame: In the mid game, pressure enemies with the high burst damage of Skill 2. The combo of approaching with Skill 1 and landing Skill 2 is strong, especially because hitting the center of Skill 2 deals extra damage. If the Passive petrify triggers, you can reliably land the center of Skill 2, chunking down the enemy's health instantly.
strategy.lateGame: In the late game, act as the frontline for your team's formation. Dive into the middle of the group with the spinning attack of the Ultimate, spreading damage and petrify to a wide area of enemies. While the Ultimate is active, movement speed increases and shields continuously stack, giving you high durability. Play the role of pressuring the enemy carry while acting as a shield for your allies.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, dive into the enemy formation with Skill 1, instantly deploying the Ultimate to deal continuous damage and petrify the entire enemy team. Because the shield is constantly replenished while spinning, you can disrupt their formation while absorbing enemy attacks. For enemies whose CC has worn off, aim for a center hit with Skill 2 to deal massive damage and secure the kill.
strategy.commonMistakes: Since the cast animation of Skill 2 is somewhat slow, blindly casting it on enemies who aren't petrified or slowed often results in it being easily dodged, meaning you deal no damage. Also, since petrify relies on chance (50%) and has an 8-second cooldown per target, beware of getting isolated and killed from forced dives assuming petrify will land.
strategy.strengths: The greatest strength is the extremely powerful control capability where all damage instances can trigger hard CC (petrify), even if probability-based. The continuous AoE petrify from the Ultimate has the power to shatter teamfights. Furthermore, she is a versatile fighter who combines massive burst damage from Skill 2's center hit with high survivability from her shields.
strategy.weaknesses: Weaknesses include the long animation of Skill 2 making it hard to land, and being unable to cast other skills while channeling the Ultimate, which is her main damage source. Also, the reliance of CC on probability introduces instability, where petrify might fail to proc at crucial moments, letting enemies escape or counterattack. Due to not having top-tier mobility, she is susceptible to being kited.

synergy.reason: Combined with Kanematsu's powerful control, you can deliver reliable burst damage.
By blocking and controlling enemy attacks on the front lines, you can safely deal continuous damage.

counters.reason: Because it has high mobility and can poke from a long distance, it is difficult for Kane Mutsuya, who mainly attacks from short range, to fight.
It has powerful poke and control from a distance, allowing you to deal damage before you can get close.

passive.name: hourglass of time
passive.description: Each time you activate a skill, your movement speed increases by 15% to 30% for 2 seconds.

skill1.name: space-time bomb
skill1.description:
Throws a bomb in the specified direction, dealing 200 (200 + 20% of magic attack) magic damage to the first enemy hit and attaching the bomb to the target.
After 3 seconds or when the target with the bomb dies, it deals 575 (575 + 60% of magic attack) magic damage to enemies within the area and reduces their movement speed by 15% for 2 seconds.
Defusing the bomb will cause an immediate explosion.

skill2.name: wave of time
skill2.description:
Channel the power of time and space to increase the movement speed of yourself and nearby allied heroes by 30% for 2 seconds. After the duration ends, recovers HP equal to 25% of the damage received while increasing movement speed.
Passive effect: Reduces skill cooldown by 7.5%.

skill3.name: flow of time
skill3.description:
Fires a reinforced bomb in the specified direction. When you hit an enemy or reach the maximum distance, you will develop a field that deals 500 (500 + 45% of magic attack) magic damage to enemies within the area and greatly reduces their movement speed for 1 second. When activated by interrupting an enemy's skill, it also applies a silence effect for 1 second.
After 5 seconds, the field will explode, dealing 1500 (1500 + 135% of magic attack) magic damage to enemies within the area.

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, attach the Skill 1 bomb to enemy heroes to harass from a safe position. Whittle down enemy health with the explosion's magic damage and slow effect, while avoiding enemy attacks using the movement speed boost from the Passive. As a support, prioritize helping your allied carry farm and avoid unnecessary skirmishes to focus on leveling up.
strategy.midGame: In the mid game, the time manipulation of Skill 2 becomes extremely important. By increasing the movement speed of the entire team, you can boost gank success rates or help allies escape enemy pursuit. Since a portion of the damage taken is restored at the end of the effect, timing Skill 2 just as the enemy uses their skills will drastically increase your allies' survivability.
strategy.lateGame: In late-game teamfights, the deployment of the wide-area field from the Ultimate decides the outcome. Use it to split the enemy formation or block escape routes. The silence and massive slow upon hit disrupts enemy skill casting, creating an opening for your team to attack. You need to constantly boost and heal your allies utilizing the cooldown reduction from Skill 2.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, position yourself in the backline. Activate Skill 2 in response to the enemy's initiation (engage) to absorb and restore damage taken by your allies. It is most effective to fire the Ultimate into grouped enemies to silence them and interrupt their combos. Keep throwing Skill 1 bombs relentlessly, and continuously adjust your positioning with the Passive movement speed boost.
strategy.commonMistakes: A common mistake is wasting Skill 2 just for a simple movement speed boost, leaving it on cooldown and unable to heal allies when the enemy's powerful burst damage arrives. Also, throwing the Ultimate into a random spot where enemies easily dodge it means losing your greatest disruption tool, putting your team in crisis. Skill timing is everything.
strategy.strengths: The movement speed buff for the entire team and the pseudo-nullification of damage (rewind healing) from Skill 2 are his greatest strengths, boasting extremely high resilience against enemy burst damage. Furthermore, the massive AoE silence and slow from the Ultimate provide powerful counter-engage capabilities to ruin enemy skill combos, excelling in teamfight control.
strategy.weaknesses: Sun Bin himself lacks hard CC (such as stuns or knock-ups) and cannot output burst damage, making him completely dependent on the firepower and coordination of his team. When allies are not fed, his ability to turn the game around is weak. Also, because Skill 2's heal is based on "damage taken," it cannot prevent instant death damage and is weak against continuous damage.

synergy.reason: With increased mobility and silence skills, you can move around safely and deal damage.
Speed ​​up and recovery can increase the survivability of less mobile Marksmen.

counters.reason: Possessing high mobility and burst damage, he can instantly defeat Sun Ping, who has low durability.
He has excellent poke and mobility, and can continuously apply pressure while avoiding Sun Ying's skills.

hero_021.sub_role: Area attack type Mage / continuous attack / recovery

passive.name: Passive Skill: Dark Medicine
passive.description: Normal attacks and skill hits will inflict a poison mark on the enemy (up to 5 stacks), dealing 20 (20 + 5% of magic attack) magic damage per second for 7 seconds. The healing skill gives you and allied heroes a mark of healing for 7 seconds (maximum of 5 stacks), and recovers the HP of allied heroes by 3 (3 + 2% of magic attack) and yourself by 3 (3 + 2% of magic attack + 0.15% of additional HP) every second.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Elixir of Death
skill1.description: Throws a vial containing deadly poison. When the vial falls to the ground and explodes, the poison will flow out for 4 seconds. Poison deals 65 (65 + 14% magic attack) magic damage per second to enemies within the area and reduces movement speed by 30% for 2 seconds. Poison can be produced once every 12.5 seconds (up to 3 stacks).

skill2.name: Skill 2: Diagnosis of right and wrong
skill2.description: Throws a medicine bottle, dealing 130 (130 + 30% of magic attack) magic damage to the enemy it hits, while also recovering 65 (65 + 15% of magic attack) HP of allied heroes around him.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Ruler of Life
skill3.description: Explodes all nearby engravings, dealing 150 (150 + 40% of magic attack) magic damage to enemies and restoring 70 (70 + 20% of magic attack) HP to allied heroes. Each stack increases the additional damage and recovery effect by 50%. Doctor of Light and Darkness recovers an additional 400 HP (400 + 30% of magic attack + 5% of additional HP).

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, use basic attacks and skills to apply poison marks on the enemy, harassing them with continuous damage. Control the area with the poison vial of Skill 1, while applying healing marks to yourself and your allies with Skill 2 to keep HP high. Focus on stacking up to 5 marks, and gradually build an advantage through favorable damage trades.
strategy.midGame: From the mid game, demonstrate your strength in continuous damage and AoE healing during small skirmishes. Keep applying poison stacks to enemies while constantly spreading healing stacks to allies with Skill 2. If you cast your Ultimate when both sides have accumulated stacks, you can deal massive burst damage to enemies while significantly healing your entire team, steering the battle in your favor.
strategy.lateGame: In the late game, with full equipment, the continuous poison damage and healing amount become massive. In teamfights, position in the backline, whittling down the enemy frontline while continuously healing allies. Stack management is everything; detonating the Ultimate the moment 5 stacks are applied to both allies and enemies allows you to achieve both overwhelming teamfight sustain and AoE burst damage.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, do not push too far to the frontline. Relentlessly spam Skill 1 and 2 from behind your allies. Maintain your allies' HP and stack poison on enemies simultaneously. Once enemy marks are full or ally HP drops dangerously, detonate instantly with the Ultimate. Because chaotic fights make it easier to apply stacks to multiple targets, the longer a fight drags on, the more it becomes Bian Que's domain.
strategy.commonMistakes: Pushing too far forward trying to get a kill, drawing focus fire, and easily dying due to low durability is a common mistake. Additionally, panicking and casting the Ultimate when only 1-2 stacks are accumulated results in mediocre damage and healing, completely failing to utilize Bian Que's strengths. Poor stack management is fatal.
strategy.strengths: His unique ability to simultaneously deal continuous poison damage to enemies and provide AoE healing to allies is his greatest strength. Detonating the Ultimate at 5 stacks delivers massive AoE damage and healing, possessing the potential to completely turn the tide of a teamfight. The longer the battle lasts, the wider the gap between poison and healing becomes, overwhelming the enemy.
strategy.weaknesses: With extremely low mobility and lacking any movement skills for escape or hard CC for self-defense, he has no way to protect himself if an assassin gets close. Furthermore, because he requires "time" to accumulate stacks before dealing burst damage or healing, he cannot react to ultra-high burst damage ambushes that end fights instantly, often dying before he can act.

synergy.reason: Tanks with high durability fight on the front lines, making it easier to accumulate poison stacks and heal allies.
Since you can block enemy attacks, you can create an environment that provides sustained recovery and damage.

counters.reason: It is difficult to carry into sustained battles because it inhibits recovery and deals burst damage.
It has high mobility and burst damage, and will defeat you without giving you a chance to recover.

passive.name: Passive: Reaper's Revenge
passive.description: Gains Shadow Power upon dealing or taking damage, increasing Movement Speed and healing effects (up to 70 Movement Speed and 80% increased healing).

skill1.name: Skill 1: Blood Echo
skill1.description: (Can be charged) Spins his scythe, splashing blood in all directions, dealing 252 (180 + 40% Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies and recovering 90 (90 + 2% extra HP) Health. Passive: When taking damage, has a 20% chance to cast Blood Echo. Damage and healing increase with charge time (up to 3 times. Effect halved if hitting non-hero units).

skill2.name: Skill 2: Deathscythe
skill2.description: Summons a scythe that retracts after 1.2s, pulling enemies in its path, dealing 325 (325 + 120% extra Physical Attack) magical damage, and slowing them by 25% for 2.5s. Passive: Deals 30% extra damage to targets below 30% HP.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Lay Waste
skill3.description: Charges Shadow Power to max and leaps to the target location, dealing 481 (300 + 100% Physical Attack) magical damage and taunting enemies. Base taunt duration is 1–1.5s, increasing by 0.2s for every 1000 extra HP (up to 2.5s).

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, utilize the AoE damage and self-healing from the scythe of Skill 1 to lane tenaciously. Taking damage triggers a passive increase to movement speed and healing effects, and grants a chance to auto-cast Skill 1, making him very strong in melee brawls. Use the charge attack to simultaneously clear minions and harass the enemy.
strategy.midGame: In the mid game, use the pull of Skill 2 to coordinate with ally ganks or block enemy escape routes. Because Skill 2 has a slight delay before pulling back, the trick is to predict the enemy's movement and place it ahead of them. It deals extra damage to low HP targets, so you take on the role of catching and executing fleeing enemies. Proactively secure vision.
strategy.lateGame: In the late game, act as the absolute main tank and initiator of the allied formation. Since stacking bonus HP extends the taunt duration of the Ultimate up to a maximum of 2.5 seconds, it is crucial to properly build defensive items. Dive the enemy carry with the Ultimate and lock them down with a long taunt, creating the time and environment for your allies to deal maximum damage.
strategy.teamfight: The initiation of a teamfight rests entirely on Bai Qi's Ultimate. With shadow power charged, jumping into a clustered group of enemies with the Ultimate to taunt multiple targets is his most important job. Immediately after taunting, charge Skill 1 to heal while dealing damage, place Skill 2 to pull back fleeing enemies, and annihilate the opposing team alongside your allies.
strategy.commonMistakes: A common mistake is diving alone with the Ultimate from a distance where allies cannot follow up. Even if you land the taunt, lacking allied fire support means you will just get surrounded and killed. Also, since the Ultimate's taunt duration relies on bonus HP, building offensive items instead of defense leaves you failing your role and becoming just a squishy target.
strategy.strengths: His greatest strength is possessing one of the best lockdown skills in the game—a wide AoE and long-duration "taunt" from his Ultimate. By forcing enemies to attack him, he can completely disable enemy carries from casting skills. Additionally, his durability in the dead center of the enemy team is incredible, thanks to the passive procs from taking damage and the healing from Skill 1.
strategy.weaknesses: The weaknesses are the difficulty of landing Skill 2 due to its delay, and the lack of any immediate gap-closer other than the Ultimate. If you miss the Ultimate, you easily become a sitting duck, and long-range heroes adept at kiting will whittle down your health before you can even get close. Also, in the early stages when bonus HP is low, the taunt duration is short.

synergy.reason: She can unleash AoE and true damage while enemies are grouped by Bai Qi's taunt.
Her shields and support further enhance Bai Qi's survivability when taking damage in the middle of team fights.

counters.reason: His true damage ignores Bai Qi's high physical defense, dealing massive damage.
His long-range poke and crowd control prevent Bai Qi from getting close to land his taunt.

hero_023.sub_role: AoE Mage / Sustained Damage/Recovery / Early Game

passive.name: Passive: Blood of Eternity
passive.description: Casting a skill summons a shadow crow. Crows automatically attack nearby enemies, dealing 50 (50 + 7% Magical Attack) magical damage per hit, with an extra 80 (80 + 14% Magical Attack) magical damage to minions (halved against monsters). Also restores 20 (20 + 0.5% extra HP) Health. Summoning a crow grants 30 Shadow Power, increasing Movement Speed and healing. Movement Speed can increase by up to 30, and healing by up to 10%. Up to 6 crows can be summoned simultaneously. Crows disappear when Shadow Power drops to 0.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Abyssal Shift
skill1.description: Fires a shadow orb that deals 200 (200 + 24% Magical Attack) magical damage to enemies in its path. Casting this skill again makes Mi Yue descend at the orb's location, dealing 200 (200 + 24% Magical Attack) magical damage to nearby enemies. If the descent hits an enemy, she summons 1 shadow crow.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Ravenous Tether
skill2.description: Fires a shadow chain that tethers to the first enemy hit for up to 4s. Stacks a 1.25%–2.5% (scales with skill level) slow effect every 0.5s, absorbing 10 Physical Attack and 30 Magical Attack. Also deals 30 (30 + 7% Magical Attack) magical damage and heals 10 (10 + 0.25% extra HP). The chain breaks if they are over 1000 units apart. Tethering for 4s summons 1 shadow crow. Passive: Basic attacks are enhanced, dealing 200 (40 + 100% Physical Attack + 20% Magical Attack) magical damage and slowing the target by 10%, while granting 10 Shadow Power.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Umbral Moon
skill3.description: Fades into the shadows, cleansing all debuffs. Increases Movement Speed by 30% and becomes immune to all effects, then descends again after 2s. Deals 380 (380 + 70% Magical Attack) magical damage to nearby enemies when fading into shadows and when descending. If the descent hits an enemy, summons 1 shadow crow.

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, command Skill 1 and Skill 2 to summon shadow crows and maintain shadow power during the laning phase. Linking an enemy hero with the Skill 2 chain drains their attack power while dealing continuous damage and healing you, making it exceptionally advantageous for 1v1 damage trades. Always keep crows deployed to speed up minion clearing.
strategy.midGame: In the mid game, leverage your overwhelming 1v1 prowess to actively split push. If a single enemy comes to defend, link Skill 2 to win the duel; if surrounded by multiple enemies, use the teleportation of Skill 1 or the invincibility of the Ultimate to disengage safely. Invade the enemy jungle and draw the enemy's attention.
strategy.lateGame: In late-game teamfights, rather than engaging frontally, it's effective to flank or attack from the rear aiming for the enemy carry. Approach with Skill 1, land Skill 2 on the enemy mage or marksman to absorb their damage output. If in danger, disengage using the invincibility and movement speed boost of the Ultimate, then re-enter the fight persistently leveraging the passive healing.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, the top priority is linking the Skill 2 chain to the enemy hero with the highest damage output. Since it absorbs physical and magic attack, it greatly diminishes the enemy's firepower. Maintain the maximum number of shadow crows (6) to scatter continuous damage and healing, and if focused, use the Ultimate to negate damage and make the enemy waste their skills.
strategy.commonMistakes: Missing the Skill 2 chain prevents you from absorbing attack power and healing yourself, drastically reducing combat capability. Forcing a duel while the chain is missed is the biggest mistake. Also, using the Ultimate's invincibility offensively can leave you isolated in the enemy formation with no escape, leading to death, so be very careful about when to use the invulnerability.
strategy.strengths: Thanks to the attack power absorption of Skill 2 and the astonishing self-healing of the passive shadow crows, she boasts top-tier performance in 1v1 brawls. Furthermore, she possesses extremely high survivability with the full invincibility and debuff cleanse of the Ultimate, paired with the long-range mobility of Skill 1. Her elusive playstyle of drawing enemy aggro and then escaping is her strength.
strategy.weaknesses: Having absolutely no hard CC (stuns or knock-ups), she struggles to lock down enemies to protect allies or securely catch fleeing targets. Also, lacking burst damage and relying on sustained damage, she cannot out-heal incoming instantaneous ultra-burst damage and will die. Being overly specialized in small-scale skirmishes rather than large teamfights is her weakness.

synergy.reason: Provides control to lock down enemies, making it much easier for Mi Yue to land her tether (Skill 2).
Draws enemy aggro on the front line, allowing Mi Yue to safely approach to absorb stats and deal damage.

counters.reason: Possesses high durability and burst damage, farming fast enough to counter Mi Yue's pressure.
Highly mobile and can poke from a distance, making it very difficult for Mi Yue to close the gap and land her tether.

passive.name: Passive: Bloodline of the Beast
passive.description: Hitting an enemy hero with a halberd skill enchants the weapon for 8s. While enchanted, his basic attacks and skills deal a certain amount of true damage. If not enchanted, basic attacks and skills grant 30% Physical Pierce. Every basic attack on an enemy while enchanted restores 80 (80 + 1.3% extra Physical Attack) Health.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Impaling Thrust
skill1.description: Swings his halberd in the target direction, dealing 570 (570 + 220% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies in range. If it does not hit an enemy hero, the halberd loses its enchanted state. While enchanted, each hit on an enemy hero heals him for 130 (130 + 2.5% extra Physical Attack) HP.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Trembling Grasp
skill2.description: Absorbs the souls of enemies in the target area, dealing 100 (100 + 35% extra Physical Attack) physical damage and slowing them by 15% for 3s. Gains a shield stack for each soul absorbed, up to 3 stacks. Each stack grants a 350 (350 + 7% extra HP) shield. 25% of the shield converts into HP every second at a ratio of 2 Shield = 1 HP.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Tremble, Mortals
skill3.description: Descends to the target location, deploying a killing zone and knocking up enemies in range for 1s. Immune to CC while descending. Upon landing, swings his halberd, dealing 650 (650 + 240% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies in range. If enchanted, heals for 130 (130 + 2.5% extra Physical Attack) HP for each enemy hero hit. While inside the zone, gains 150 Physical Defense and 150 Magical Defense. Enemies touching the edge take 150 (150 + 75% extra Physical Attack) physical damage and are slowed by 30% for 2s. If the target is a hero, Lu Bu heals additional HP.

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, the top priority is hitting an enemy hero with Skill 1 to put your weapon into an enchanted state. Basic attacks after enchanting deal true damage and restore HP, making him extremely strong in lane brawls. Absorb souls from minions or enemies with Skill 2 to gain a shield, minimizing damage taken while aggressively taking damage trades.
strategy.midGame: In the mid game, dominate small skirmishes utilizing the true damage of the enchanted state and the durability from the Skill 2 shield. Land Skill 1 on clustered enemies to deal massive damage to multiple targets while healing. Since the shield can be converted to HP, you can fight tenaciously. Bolster your combat power with items that provide attack speed and physical defense.
strategy.lateGame: Late-game teamfights are Lu Bu's domain. Dive right into the center of the enemy formation with the massive AoE descent of the Ultimate, knocking up multiple enemies. Upon landing, immediately use Skill 1 to enter the enchanted state, gaining the defensive buff of the killing zone, and spam true damage basic attacks. He wields overwhelming destructive power that can instantly slice through even enemy tanks.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, the timing of the Ultimate is everything. When enemies are grouped or in sync with ally CC, dive in with the Ultimate to shatter the enemy formation. After landing, you MUST hit an enemy hero with Skill 1 to become enchanted. Staying inside the zone increases defense and slows enemies touching the edge, so rampaging within the zone is the basic tactic.
strategy.commonMistakes: The most fatal mistake is missing Skill 1, losing the enchanted state, and continuing to brawl with normal basic attacks. Your damage output plummets, and since you cannot heal, you will die easily. Also, because the Ultimate's animation is long, aiming it where highly mobile enemies can easily dodge it, leaving you isolated under focused fire, is another common failure.
strategy.strengths: The greatest strengths are the "defense-ignoring true damage" and "HP restoration on every basic attack" granted by the enchanted state, allowing him to easily cut down even the tankiest of enemies. Furthermore, the ultra-wide engage and formation-splitting capability of his Ultimate are among the best in the game, giving him the terrifying carry potential to single-handedly flip a teamfight from a disadvantage.
strategy.weaknesses: His movements are generally sluggish, and the long cast animations of Skill 1 and the Ultimate make it easy for highly mobile enemies to dodge his attacks. Additionally, if he cannot maintain the enchanted state (by missing Skill 1), he absolutely cannot unleash his true potential. Missing the Ultimate also leaves a huge opening, making him vulnerable to kiting from a distance.

synergy.reason: She can reset his Ultimate's cooldown, allowing him to jump twice and dominate team fights.
Pulls enemies together, ensuring Lu Bu's devastating AoE Ultimate hits multiple targets.

counters.reason: Uses his high mobility to dodge Lu Bu's attacks while safely poking him with true damage from afar.
Can use his skills to knock Lu Bu away the moment he lands from his Ultimate, preventing him from disrupting the formation.

passive.name: Passive: Blaze
passive.description: Basic attacks and skills apply ignite marks to enemies and enemy towers/crystals for 4s. At 4 stacks, the target ignites, taking 125 (125 + 17% Magical Attack) magical damage per second and being slowed by 15% for 1s. Ignited targets cannot be marked again.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Wildfire

Upon summoning, knocks back nearby enemies in the target direction, dealing 200 (200 + 30% Magical Attack) magical damage. If a fire domain is covered by the wind domain for more than 1s, the fire will spread in the direction of the wind to form new fire domains. The damage of the new and old fire domains can overlap. Fire domains deal 100 (100 + 25% Magical Attack) magical damage to enemies every 0.5s.
