strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, use Skill 2 to shoot a fire arrow, creating a combustion area lasting 6.5 seconds to harass enemies with continuous magical damage. The Passive allows normal attacks and skills to apply Ignite marks to enemies; at 4 stacks, they are ignited, dealing additional magical damage and slowing their movement speed, giving you the upper hand.
strategy.midGame: Utilize the strong wind from Skill 1 to expand the combustion area created by Skill 2 in a designated direction, dealing continuous magical damage to enemies in a wide range. It increases allies' movement speed while reducing enemies' movement speed, and the Passive's healing effect (restoring HP when you are inside a combustion area) enhances survivability.
strategy.lateGame: Create up to 6 combustion areas and cast Skill 3's flame to pass through them, generating a powerful hot wind. This deals massive damage to enemies and applies a 1-second stun. Combined with the Passive's ignite damage, it allows you to control teamfights.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, combine the wind zone and combustion areas to deal continuous magical damage and slow enemies over a wide area. Aim for stuns with Skill 3's hot wind, and use Skill 1's knockback to keep your distance when enemies approach. Fighting within the combustion areas to gain HP recovery and sustain yourself is the key.
strategy.commonMistakes: A common mistake is not utilizing the wind zone of Skill 1 to expand the combustion areas and relying solely on single-hit damage. Also, be aware that stepping outside the combustion area will cause you to lose the Passive's HP recovery effect, leaving you vulnerable to being killed by enemy attacks.
strategy.strengths: The ability to generate wide-ranging combustion areas by combining Skill 2 and Skill 1, dealing powerful continuous magical damage, is a major strength. Additionally, it boasts self-protection and disruption capabilities, such as HP recovery within your combustion areas, knockback from Skill 1, and stun from Skill 3.
strategy.weaknesses: Expanding combustion areas requires coordination with Skill 1; relying on them individually has limited effectiveness. Furthermore, generating the powerful hot wind requires hitting combustion areas with Skill 3, which is a weakness as enemies can easily predict the position and dodge it.

synergy.reason: Zhong Kui can hook enemies directly into Zhou Yu's fire domains, ensuring they take massive sustained damage.
Can protect Zhou Yu with shields and a teleport when targeted by assassins, ensuring he survives to continue outputting damage.

counters.reason: High mobility allows him to easily bypass the fire domains and quickly burst down Zhou Yu.
His long-range poke and stuns make it very difficult for Zhou Yu to set up his fire formations.

strategy.earlyGame: Survive the early game by hitting enemy heroes with the main body and puppet's attack animations to collect fragments while recovering HP. Since fragments are also automatically gained every 10-5 seconds, it is crucial to collect 50 fragments to quickly assemble the puppet and prepare to overwhelm enemies with a puppet that lasts for 10 seconds.
strategy.midGame: Release the puppet with Skill 1 to deal physical damage and knock up enemies along the path, fighting while controlling the puppet. Use Skill 2's four hidden weapons to deal massive damage to enemies on the path, and utilize the purify effect, shield, and movement speed increase gained upon recalling the puppet to balance survival and offense.
strategy.lateGame: Use the collected fragments to disguise yourself as an enemy hero with the same abilities and launch a powerful ambush. Deal physical damage to enemies with Skill 3's threads; when 2 threads hit, it deals additional damage based on lost HP and slows movement speed to ensure the kill. Utilize the main body's damage reduction and control the puppet carefully.
strategy.teamfight: Disruption using the puppet is powerful. Use Skill 1's knock up to break the enemy formation, and deal area damage with Skill 2's hidden weapons. Ensure enemies don't escape using the damage and slow from Skill 3's threads. If the puppet is destroyed, the main body gets stunned and cannot summon it for a period, so recalling the puppet before it is destroyed is essential.
strategy.commonMistakes: A fatal mistake is letting the puppet be automatically destroyed after staying more than 2500 distance away for 5 seconds, or having the enemy destroy it, which strips the main body of vision and stuns it for 2 seconds. Also, reckless charging should be avoided, as the puppet cannot be summoned for 20-16 seconds.
strategy.strengths: Disruption via the puppet and the Passive ability to transform into enemy heroes are the biggest strengths. It has high attack power with Skill 1's knock up and additional damage and slow from Skill 3's threads. Since recalling the puppet grants a purify effect and shield, it excels in both survival and disruption.
strategy.weaknesses: The puppet's damage is reduced to 50% of the original, and it takes 100% increased damage, making it fragile. If the puppet is destroyed, the main body is stunned and cannot summon the puppet for a long time, meaning you must always pay close attention to the positioning of the main body and puppet, as well as enemy attacks.

synergy.reason: Provides CC immunity and damage reduction, allowing Yango to safely execute his combos.
Provides a solid frontline with strong CC, creating the space Yango needs to safely set up his Puppet combos from a distance.

counters.reason: Can use stealth to sneak up on Yango's defenseless Main Body and assassinate him in one hit.
Can use her invincibility frames and high mobility to dodge the Puppet's combo, then quickly close the gap and kill Yango's Main Body.

passive.name: Passive: Unyielding Might
passive.description: When taking damage while HP is below 50%, he empowers himself. For 8s, every skill or basic attack that hits an enemy restores 220 (220 + 6% extra Max HP). This effect can only be triggered once every 25 seconds (scales with hero level).

skill1.name: Skill 1: Wind Slash
skill1.description: Swings his greatsword, unleashing a shockwave in the target direction. Deals 420 (420 + 130% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies in range and slows their movement speed by 25% for 2s. If the shockwave hits an enemy, he can cast Wind Slash a second time within 5s in a target direction, dealing 420 (420 + 130% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies in range and knocking them up for 1s.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Gale's Benediction
skill2.description: Deals 300 (300 + 100% extra Physical Attack) magical damage to nearby enemies and grants a 500 (500 + 12% extra Max HP) shield for 5s. His next 3 basic attacks are enhanced to deal 150 (150 + 45% extra Physical Attack) true damage. Lasts 8s. Each enhanced basic attack hit reduces the cooldown of Skill 3 by 1s.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Unruly Blade
skill3.description: Throws his chain blade in the target direction. The chain blade stops when it reaches its maximum range or hits an enemy. The chain blade stuns the first enemy hit for 1s, dealing 420 (420 + 10% extra Max HP) physical damage to nearby enemies, and pulls Dun to the blade's location. Upon arrival, deals an additional 420 (420 + 10% extra Max HP) physical damage to nearby enemies. Dun is immune to CC while casting this skill.

hero_027.status_text:
Apprentice Tank 0/100
Tank/Fighter
Defense Tank
Dash/CC
Early Game
Difficulty: Normal

strategy.earlyGame: Harass enemies by dealing physical damage and slowing them with the shockwave from Skill 1's greatsword, followed by a knock up from the second cast if the first hits. When HP is below 50%, utilize the Passive to recover HP by hitting enemies with skills or normal attacks after taking damage, making it important to fight persistently.
strategy.midGame: Deal magical damage with Skill 2 while gaining a powerful shield based on max HP to increase durability. Utilize the true damage from the 3 enhanced normal attacks after Skill 2; hitting enemies reduces Skill 3's cooldown, allowing you to create advantageous situations with a balanced offensive and defensive playstyle.
strategy.lateGame: Initiate into the enemy formation with Skill 3's chain blade by stunning an enemy for 1 second and pulling yourself towards them. Since the pull also deals additional physical damage, combine it with the shield and true damage from enhanced normal attacks to target and eliminate the core enemy hero, gaining the upper hand in combat.
strategy.teamfight: Initiate fights with the CC-immune dash and stun of Skill 3, and pin down multiple enemies with the knock up from the second stage of Skill 1. Endure enemy attacks with Skill 2's shield, trigger the Passive's HP recovery effect, and steadily chip away at both the enemy frontline and backline with the true damage of enhanced normal attacks.
strategy.commonMistakes: A mistake is missing the first stage of Skill 1, which prevents the second stage (knock up) from being cast, greatly reducing your disruption capability. Also, Skill 3's chain blade stops at the first enemy hit or at max distance, so hitting an unintended enemy can put you in a perilous situation.
strategy.strengths: The powerful CC capabilities from Skill 3's stun and pull, and Skill 1's slow and knock up, are its strengths. Combined with high durability from Skill 2's shield and the Passive's HP recovery, as well as the true damage from enhanced normal attacks after Skill 2, it boasts both firepower and durability.
strategy.weaknesses: Skill 3 stops at the first enemy hit, so being blocked by minions prevents you from approaching the intended target. Also, the Passive's HP recovery effect has a 25-second cooldown, making your durability drop right after it triggers; if enemies outlast the healing, you become much easier to kill.

synergy.reason: Dun establishes the frontline and absorbs enemy skills, allowing Gongsun Li to deal free damage.
Dun's heavy CC locks down enemies, giving Li Bai the time to safely build his stacks and unleash burst damage.

counters.reason: His true damage ignores shields, completely nullifying Dun's durability advantage.
Uses his high mobility and true damage to dodge Dun's CC while unilaterally draining his health.

passive.name: Passive: Frozen Touch
passive.description: Dealing skill damage to an enemy applies a freeze mark for 7s. When 3 stacks are applied, it deals 250 (250 + 30% Magical Attack) magical damage and freezes them for 0.75s. Subsequent freeze effects applied quickly will only last 0.5s.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Ocean of Tears
skill1.description: Summons an impassable water pillar at a target location, dealing 500 (500 + 70% Magical Attack) magical damage to enemies in range and knocking them up for 1s. The water pillar lasts for 8s, slowing nearby enemies by 10%. If Lady Zhen is within range when the pillar is summoned, she freezes herself, becoming invincible for 3s. If the freeze is manually canceled or ends, the pillar shatters, dealing 150 (150 + 35% Magical Attack) magical damage to enemies in range and slowing them by 10% for 3s. Enemies at the center of the explosion take double damage.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Flowing Sigh
skill2.description: Fires a water droplet in the target direction. It locks onto enemies and bounces between them up to 5-7 times (increases at Skill Lv.3/5). Each bounce deals 230 (230 + 40% Magical Attack) magical damage. It can also bounce off her water pillar. Once it reaches the max bounce limit, it disappears after 3s.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Destructive Deluge
skill3.description: Releases a wave of water that travels forward, knocking back non-hero enemy units. Upon hitting an enemy hero or reaching its max distance, it forms a water pool, dealing 300 (300 + 65% Magical Attack) magical damage to the first hero hit and slowing their movement speed by 90% for 1s. The pool lasts for 5s, dealing 150 (150 + 32% Magical Attack) magical damage per second to enemies inside and slowing them by 25% for 1.5s.

hero_028.status_text:
Apprentice Mage 0/100
Mage
CC Mage
CC/Freeze
Balanced
Difficulty: Hard

strategy.earlyGame: Efficiently deal magical damage and harass enemies by casting Skill 2's water stream to bounce between enemies 5-7 times. Dealing skill damage applies Ice marks; 3 stacks deal additional damage and a 0.75-second freeze effect, so aim at enemy heroes through minions.
strategy.midGame: Summon an ice pillar with Skill 1 to deal magical damage and a 1-second knock up to enemies in the area, restricting their movement. The ice pillar slows surrounding movement speed, so bouncing Skill 2's water stream off the pillar ensures you stack Ice marks and aim for the freeze effect.
strategy.lateGame: Form a water zone with Skill 3's water stream, knocking back non-hero units and dealing massive damage and a powerful 90% slow to heroes hit. The water zone lasts 5 seconds, dealing damage and slow every second, completely blocking approaching enemies and creating attack opportunities for your allies.
strategy.teamfight: Divide the enemy formation with the wide water zone and massive slow from Skill 3. Aim for knock ups with Skill 1's ice pillar while protecting yourself with the invincibility effect when inside the area. Bounce Skill 2's water stream among grouped enemies to continuously freeze multiple targets and dominate the teamfight.
strategy.commonMistakes: A common failure is not hitting enemy heroes with Skill 3's water stream, preventing the formation of an effective water zone. Also, failing to utilize the invincibility effect from summoning Skill 1's ice pillar and allowing enemies to close in and kill you before you unfreeze yourself prevents you from fully using your disruption abilities.
strategy.strengths: The ability to apply Ice marks with all skills and the powerful CC from the 3-stack freeze effect are the biggest strengths. The wide-area damage and slow effects from Skill 2's bounces and Skill 3's persistent water zone give her immense control and firepower in chaotic fights where multiple enemies gather.
strategy.weaknesses: While Skill 3 knocks back non-hero units, the water zone doesn't form until it reaches max distance or hits an enemy hero, creating a massive vulnerability if it misses. Additionally, during Skill 1's invincibility effect, you cannot move, leaving you fragile if you cannot counterattack immediately after it ends.

synergy.reason: His knockbacks and stuns hold enemies together, making it much easier for Lady Zhen to hit multiple targets with her skills and ultimate.
His acceleration covers Lady Zhen's lack of mobility, and his lockdown helps her trigger her passive freeze more consistently.

counters.reason: He can use untargetability and high mobility to slip through her CC and instantly burst her down while she's defenseless.
His long-range ambushes and silence can completely delete her before she even has a chance to cast a skill.

passive.name: Passive: Conquest
passive.description: Casting a skill grants 1 stack of enhanced basic attack (up to 5 stacks) and increases attack speed by 35%. The enhanced basic attack unleashes a long-range flurry of slashes, healing for 90 (90 + 1.4% extra Physical Attack) HP (healing is halved against non-hero units).

skill1.name: Skill 1: The King's Blade
skill1.description: Slashes forward, dealing 300 (300 + 90% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies in his path (can be cast up to 3 times). The 3rd slash knocks enemies up for 0.5s. If cast less than 3 times, the cooldown is reduced by 35% for each remaining cast.

skill2.name: Skill 2: The King's Return
skill2.description: Unleashes a powerful wave of sword energy, dealing 320 (320 + 100% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies in its path and slowing them by 25% for 2s. Hitting an enemy hero recovers 200 (200 + 3% extra Max HP) Health (effect halved against non-hero units). Basic attacks reduce the cooldown of The King's Return by 5%. While Sacred Tree Blessing is active, unleashes an additional wave of sword energy.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Sacred Tree Blessing
skill3.description: Calls upon the blessing's power to cleanse himself of CC, dealing 230 (230 + 75% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to nearby enemies. For 8s, increases his Max HP by 450 (450 + 7% extra Max HP) (increases by 20% for every nearby enemy hero) and increases Resistance by 10%. While active, casting The King's Return or enhanced basic attacks unleashes an extra wave of sword energy (dealing 50% damage), and he continuously gains enhanced basic attack stacks. Tap the skill again to cancel it early.

hero_029.status_text:
Apprentice Fighter 0/100
Fighter
Berserker Fighter
Lifesteal
Balanced
Difficulty: Hard

strategy.earlyGame: Deal physical damage to enemies on the path with Skill 1's forward slashes, and aim for a knock up on the third slash to gain the upper hand. Casting skills grants enhanced normal attacks; the continuous long-range slashes restore HP, allowing you to sustain in the lane while steadily building up damage.
strategy.midGame: Slow enemy movement speed by 25% with Skill 2's powerful sword aura, and recover HP by hitting enemy heroes. Hitting with normal attacks reduces Skill 2's cooldown, so combine it with HP recovery from enhanced normal attacks to continuously fire sword auras and gain an advantage in mid-game skirmishes.
strategy.lateGame: Use Skill 3's blessing power to gain CC immunity, deal damage to surrounding enemies, and massively increase max HP and resistance. During Skill 3, Skill 2 and enhanced normal attacks can fire additional sword auras; use this increased durability and overwhelming attack volume to carve through the enemy formation and dominate late-game fights.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, cast Skill 3 to gain CC immunity and increase your max HP based on the number of nearby enemy heroes, making you a powerful frontline presence. Knock up multiple enemies with the 3rd stage of Skill 1, and maintain the frontline with continuous damage and healing from the additional sword auras and enhanced normal attacks during Skill 3.
strategy.commonMistakes: Failing to complete all 3 strikes of Skill 1 to miss the knock up, or missing Skill 2 on enemy heroes to lose out on HP recovery are fatal. Also, be careful not to double-tap Skill 3 during its duration, which accidentally cancels the powerful buffs and CC immunity.
strategy.strengths: Overwhelming durability from Skill 3's CC immunity, max HP increase, and resistance boost is a major strength. It combines HP recovery from enhanced normal attacks after every skill cast with disruption from Skill 1's knock up and Skill 2's slow, boasting incredibly high sustained combat ability in melee range.
strategy.weaknesses: The healing effect is halved against non-hero units, so you won't get enough sustain fighting minions. Also, Skill 1 must be cast 3 times to trigger the CC, making it hard to utilize if enemies keep their distance, and the drop in durability while Skill 3 is on cooldown is a clear weakness.

synergy.reason: His powerful suppression completely locks down a target, guaranteeing Fatih can close the gap, heal, and finish them off.
Uses stealth and his pull to group enemies together, setting up a perfect situation for Fatih to hit multiple targets and gain massive healing.

counters.reason: His true damage and wide attack range allow him to easily overpower Fatih in a brawl before Fatih can heal back up.
Her heavy CC and heavy sword burst damage can silence and completely obliterate Fatih before he can cast his healing skills.

passive.name: Passive: Mounting Rage
passive.description: All skills can cleanse CC. Successfully cleansing CC grants a stack of Rage, increasing his Physical Attack and Attack Range for 6s. Stacks up to 6 times. Rage: Each stack grants 15-29 Physical Attack and 35 Attack Range. Cleansing Hard CC grants 2 stacks, while cleansing Soft CC grants 1 stack.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Holding On To Sanity
skill1.description: Increases Movement Speed by 60% for 3s (decays over time) and enhances his next basic attack. The enhanced basic attack gains the same range as a max-stacked passive and causes him to dash toward the target (even if outside normal attack range), dealing 220 (220 + 50% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to nearby enemies.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Outburst
skill2.description: Stomps the ground, dealing 350 (350 + 110% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies in range and slowing them by 25% for 2s. If Skill 2 hits an enemy, empowers himself for 3s. While empowered, Attack Speed is increased by 200%, basic attack Lifesteal is increased by 20%, and every basic attack hit restores 30 HP for 3s. If it misses, the Attack Speed, Lifesteal, and extra healing effects are halved.

rows.label: ライフスティール割合

skill3.name: Skill 3: Leap of Fury
skill3.description: Performs a leaping slash at the target. Hitting an enemy hero grants 1 stack of Rage, deals 320 (320 + 90% extra Physical Attack) true damage, and slows them by 30% for 3s. Hitting an enemy empowers him for 5s: every instance of damage he deals inflicts additional true damage equal to 2% of the target's Max HP.

hero_030.status_text:
Apprentice Fighter 0/100
Fighter
Berserker Fighter
Kill
Late Game
Difficulty: Normal

strategy.earlyGame: The Passive allows you to dispel CC with any skill, so you can fight while ignoring enemy disruption. Use Skill 1 to massively boost movement speed, and perform an enhanced normal attack dash to deal physical damage to nearby enemies for ganks or retreats, safely earning EXP and gold in the early game.
strategy.midGame: Deal area damage and slow with Skill 2's ground stomp, and utilize the buff upon hitting (200% attack speed, 20% lifesteal, HP recovery) to boost your combat power. When hit by CC, dispel it immediately with a skill to gain Fury stacks, strengthening your physical attack and attack range.
strategy.lateGame: Close in on the target with Skill 3's leaping slash, dealing true damage and a massive slow. For 5 seconds after hitting, all damage dealt inflicts additional true damage equal to 2% of the target's max HP, allowing you to easily destroy even sturdy frontliners with max Fury stacks and overwhelming firepower.
strategy.teamfight: Dash to the enemy backline using Skill 1's movement speed boost, and deal true damage and slow with Skill 3's leaping slash. Dispel enemy CC with skills to build Fury stacks, and use the extreme attack speed and lifesteal gained from hitting Skill 2 to single-handedly obliterate the enemy formation.
strategy.commonMistakes: Missing Skill 2 halves the attack speed and lifesteal buffs you gain, making it a fatal mistake to whiff. Also, wasting skills purely to dispel CC, leaving you unable to use Skill 1's dash to engage or Skill 2's buff when truly needed, is a common error.
strategy.strengths: The ability to dispel CC with all skills, and the Passive that increases physical attack and attack range upon a successful dispel, are the greatest strengths. With additional true damage from Skill 3 and overwhelming attack speed and lifesteal from hitting Skill 2, you unleash unparalleled burst damage and survivability in duels and chaotic fights.
strategy.weaknesses: A major weakness is the drastic drop in combat power if Skill 2 misses the enemy. Since your strong engage tools are limited to Skill 1's enhanced normal attack or Skill 3, it's hard to catch highly mobile enemies, and once your CC dispel options are exhausted, you are easily killed if focused.

synergy.reason: His acceleration and healing support help Dian Wei close the gap and force his massive damage onto the enemy.
His team-wide CC cleanse gives Dian Wei the perfect environment to rampage continuously without worrying about being stopped.

counters.reason: Her high mobility and stat-draining tether completely deny Dian Wei's approach, allowing her to easily kite and whittle him down.
His Ultimate is a powerful suppression that cannot be cleansed by Dian Wei's passive, instantly shutting down his charge.

passive.name: Passive: Niten Ichiryuu
passive.description:
Casting a skill grants 1 stack of Vigor (up to 2 stacks). Based on the current number of stacks, his next basic attack is enhanced differently and consumes 1 Vigor. Enhanced basic attacks are not affected by attack speed, and hitting an enemy reduces the cooldowns of Skill 1 and Skill 2 by 1s.
1 Stack of Vigor:
If the target's HP is above 50%, the next basic attack becomes a dual-sword slash, dealing an additional 120 (120 + 40% extra Physical Attack) physical damage.
If the target's HP is below 50%, the next basic attack becomes a vertical slash, dealing 45 (45 + 15% extra Physical Attack) plus 6% of the target's lost Max HP as physical damage.
2 Stacks of Vigor:
Dashes towards the target, dealing 187 (20 + 100% Physical Attack) physical damage and slowing them by 25% for 1s.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Illuminating Slash
skill1.description: Unleashes sword energy, shooting down enemy projectiles in its path. Deals 390 (390 + 130% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies in its path and slows their movement speed by 25% for 1s. Miyamoto Musashi gains Super Armor (CC immunity) while casting this skill.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Extreme Speed
skill2.description: Dashes in the target direction, dealing 210 (210 + 75% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies in his path. If he hits an enemy, he gains a 330 (330 + 6% extra Max HP) shield and reduces the cooldown of this skill by 50%.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Duel to the Death
skill3.description:
Locks onto an enemy hero and dashes to their location, dealing 400 (400 + 130% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to enemy heroes in range and knocking them up for 1s. The target is then marked for a duel for 5s. While the duel lasts, any healing effects the target receives are delayed until the duel ends.
During the dash, Musashi gains Super Armor and 50% damage reduction.

hero_031.status_text:
Apprentice Fighter 0/100
Fighter/Assassin
Assassin Fighter
Dash/Kill
Balanced
Difficulty: Hard

strategy.earlyGame: Cast skills to build Animosity stacks and gain an advantage with enhanced normal attacks. When enemy HP is above 50%, use the double-sword slash for additional physical damage; when below 50%, use the heavy slash for lost-HP percentage damage. Utilize Skill 2's dash and shield to minimize damage taken while fighting.
strategy.midGame: Shoot down enemy projectiles with Skill 1's sword aura, dealing physical damage and slow to enemies on the path. Use the super armor during cast to block enemy disruption, rapidly close the gap with a 2-stack Animosity dash, and hit enhanced normal attacks to reduce the CD of Skills 1 and 2, maintaining your offensive momentum.
strategy.lateGame: Lock onto an enemy hero with Skill 3 and dash to them, dealing area damage and a knock up. This marks the target for a 5-second duel, delaying their healing effects and stripping their survivability. With super armor and 50% damage reduction during the dash, you can perform your role even deep within the enemy formation.
strategy.teamfight: Shoot down enemy ranged attacks and projectiles with Skill 1, protecting allies while advancing. Lock onto a key enemy target with Skill 3's guaranteed dash, disrupting enemy coordination with the knock up and healing delay. Use enhanced normal attacks from Animosity stacks to reduce skill CDs, and sustain yourself with Skill 2's shield.
strategy.commonMistakes: A typical mistake is neglecting Animosity stack management and using an enhanced normal attack that doesn't match the enemy's HP situation. Furthermore, if Skill 2 doesn't hit an enemy, you don't get the shield and CD reduction; wasting it for mobility leaves you lacking durability and easily killed in crucial moments.
strategy.strengths: Unique disruption capabilities like shooting down projectiles and super armor with Skill 1, and the targeted dash and healing delay of Skill 3, are its strengths. Situation-adaptable enhanced normal attacks via Animosity stacks, and high mobility and durability from Skill 2's shield, make it overwhelmingly strong in 1v1s and assassinations.
strategy.weaknesses: A weakness is that firepower stagnates without consuming Animosity stacks, making the hero highly dependent on skills. Additionally, Skill 3's duel only delays healing effects without fully nullifying them; if enemies heal back up after the effect ends and counterattack, failing to finish them off will leave you at a disadvantage.

synergy.reason: Can group enemies up, allowing Miyamoto Musashi to hit multiple targets with his AoE skills and deal massive burst damage.
Provides shields and powerful engage tools, greatly increasing Musashi's survivability after he dives into the backline.

counters.reason: Can use her invincibility frames to easily dodge Musashi's skills and counterattack effectively.
His targeted suppression ultimate can instantly lock down Musashi the moment he dives in, completely neutralizing him.

passive.name: Passive: Way of the Sword
passive.description: Hitting non-tower/non-crystal units with basic attacks grants 1 stack of Sword Energy. Lasts 3s. Hitting enemies continuously with basic attacks resets the duration. Max 4 stacks. At 4 stacks, enters the enhanced 'Way of the Sword' state. While enhanced, increases Physical Attack by 30 and unlocks Skill 3.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Stupor Slash
skill1.description:
Dashes in the target direction twice, dealing 350 (350 + 110% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies in his path, stunning them for 0.5s, and granting 1 stack of Sword Energy.
Casting a third time returns him to his original starting position. The interval between each cast must be within 5s.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Stroke of Genius
skill2.description:
Unleashes a sword formation at his current location, dealing 200 (200 + 65% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to nearby enemies. Enemies who touch the edge of the formation take 325 (325 + 115% extra Physical Attack) physical damage and have their movement speed heavily reduced for 1s. It also reduces their Physical Defense by 150 for 3s.
While casting, Li Bai is untargetable. Cleanses remaining debuffs after casting.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Flickering Flurry
skill3.description:
Transforms into phantom sword energy, slashing the target area 5 times, dealing 240 (240 + 60% extra Physical Attack) physical damage per slash. If it hits multiple enemy heroes, the damage is reduced by 15% for each additional hero hit, up to a maximum reduction of 30%.
Cleanses all debuffs upon cast. Li Bai is untargetable during the skill.

hero_032.status_text:
Apprentice Assassin 0/100
Assassin
Roam Assassin
Dash/Kill
Late Game
Difficulty: Very Hard

strategy.earlyGame: Hit non-tower, non-crystal enemies with normal attacks to build sword aura stacks, unlocking the Passive to boost physical attack. Harass with physical damage and stuns from the first two dashes of Skill 1, and use the 3rd cast to return to your original position, allowing safe hit-and-run tactics.
strategy.midGame: Deploy Skill 2's green lotus sword formation; enemies touching the border take physical damage, massive slow, and lose 150 physical defense, boosting your firepower. Use the untargetable state during cast to dodge fatal enemy attacks, quickly stack 4 sword auras, and prepare Skill 3.
strategy.lateGame: Cast the unlocked Skill 3 with 4 sword aura stacks to turn into a sword aura, unleashing 5 slashes in a designated area. Dive into the enemy formation using the debuff dispel and untargetability upon cast to deal massive area damage, then use the 3rd cast of Skill 1 to safely return to your original position in a classic hit-and-run.
strategy.teamfight: The basic strategy is to build 4 sword aura stacks on jungle monsters before joining a teamfight. Engage rapidly with Skill 1, use Skill 2's sword formation to lower enemy physical defense while remaining untargetable, and deal wide-area damage with Skill 3's continuous slashes. Then, immediately disengage with Skill 1 to prevent counterattacks.
strategy.commonMistakes: Plunging into the enemy formation before getting 4 sword aura stacks and dying without casting the powerful Skill 3 is the biggest mistake. Another common failure is when enemies ambush your original position, instantly bursting you down the moment you return with the 3rd cast of Skill 1.
strategy.strengths: Having two types of untargetable states from Skill 2 and Skill 3, allowing you to completely nullify enemy attacks while dealing one-sided damage, is its greatest strength. The elusive mobility of Skill 1's dashes and return lets you toy with enemies without allowing them a chance to strike back.
strategy.weaknesses: Overall firepower heavily relies on Skill 3, which requires 4 sword aura stacks, rendering you helpless in areas with no targets to hit with normal attacks. Additionally, Skill 3's damage is reduced by up to 30% when hitting multiple enemy heroes, meaning your damage per target drops when enemies are grouped tightly.

synergy.reason: His massive CC and disruption draw all enemy attention and cooldowns, allowing Li Bai to safely enter the fight.
Provides a solid frontline to hold enemies in place while Li Bai stacks his passive and prepares his burst.

counters.reason: His long-range stuns and ultimate lockdown can easily catch Li Bai and ruin his hit-and-run combo.
His suppression ultimate can instantly lock Li Bai down the moment he dashes in, ignoring his untargetability frames and guaranteeing his death.

passive.name: Passive: Chain Reaction
passive.description: Dealing damage to an enemy with basic attacks or skills applies 1 stack of Destruction (applies 5 stacks per hit to enemy minions). At 10 stacks, the target becomes 'Destroyed' for 5s, and Marco Polo recovers 30 energy. When basic attacks or skills hit a 'Destroyed' enemy, they deal an additional 100 (100 + 35% extra Physical Attack) true damage.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Resplendent Revolver
skill1.description:
Fires 5 bullets in the target direction (the number of bullets fired increases based on Attack Speed). While firing, his movement speed is increased by 10%. Each bullet deals 151 (120 + 18% Physical Attack) physical damage to the first enemy hit and restores 10 energy.
Every 3 bullets are treated as 1 basic attack.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Roaming Gun
skill2.description:
Dashes in the target direction.
Passive: When enemy heroes are nearby, Marco Polo's damage is increased by 20% and his movement speed is increased by 15%.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Fevered Barrage
skill3.description:
Dashes to the target location and then unleashes a barrage of bullets around him for 3s while slowly moving (firing speed increases with Attack Speed). Each bullet hit deals 213 (170 + 25% Physical Attack) physical damage.
Every 3 bullets fired are treated as 1 basic attack.

hero_033.status_text:
Apprentice Marksman 0/100
Marksman
Agile Marksman
Long-range Poke
Balanced
Difficulty: Hard

strategy.earlyGame: Focus on hitting enemies with normal attacks or skills to build stacks of the destruction effect. Fire a burst of 5 bullets in a designated direction with Skill 1, dealing physical damage and recovering energy to harass. Use the 10% movement speed boost to dodge enemy attacks while aiming for the true damage effect at 10 stacks.
strategy.midGame: Since increasing attack speed adds more bullets to Skill 1, build items to boost your firepower. Once the defense destruction effect triggers on an enemy, normal attacks and skills will deal additional true damage, so use Skill 2's blink to adjust your distance and unleash burst damage.
strategy.lateGame: Maintain positioning to utilize Skill 2's passive, which grants a 20% damage boost and 15% movement speed increase when enemy heroes are nearby. Cast the bullet-storm of Skill 3 in the middle of the enemy formation, dealing fatal damage to all enemies with true damage from defense destruction and overwhelming fire rate scaling with attack speed.
strategy.teamfight: Poke from afar with Skill 1 to build destruction stacks, looking for the right moment when defense destruction triggers. Once the enemy's CC skills are on cooldown, close in with Skill 2 and cast Skill 3's barrage to unleash devastating physical and true damage in an area, wiping out the enemy team.
strategy.commonMistakes: A typical mistake is recklessly jumping into the enemy formation with Skill 3 without having 10 destruction stacks, failing to output true damage and getting killed in return. Also, missing Skill 1 bullets drains your energy, preventing you from using Skill 2's blink when you need it most.
strategy.strengths: The true damage applied after defense destruction via the Passive is a major strength, allowing you to dish out high damage even against durable tanks. The movement speed boost during Skill 1 and the blink from Skill 2 grant exceptionally high mobility, letting you deal continuous damage from a safe distance.
strategy.weaknesses: Since you move slowly during Skill 3, taking hard CC (like a stun or knock up) will instantly interrupt the skill and leave you defenseless. Furthermore, to maximize damage, you must build attack speed, which means you often lack damage in the early game before acquiring items.

synergy.reason: Provides shields and hard CC protection, heavily boosting Marco Polo's survivability when he dives in with his ultimate.

counters.reason: Her physical damage immunity skill completely negates Marco Polo's sustained damage output in a duel.
His massive shields absorb the poke, and his ultimate's AoE roar can easily interrupt Marco Polo's ultimate.

hero_034.sub_role: Continuous DPS Marksman / Long-Range Poke / Balanced

passive.name: Swiftness
passive.description:
Basic attacks grant 1 stack of Swiftness, each increasing Attack Speed by 3.5%-7% and Movement Speed by 5% (up to 5 stacks).
Every 3rd basic attack fires a random red or blue token.

skill1.name: Six Commands: Pursuit
skill1.description:
Fires 6 random red or blue tokens in a designated direction, dealing 150 (+18% Physical Attack) physical damage.
If multiple tokens hit the same target, subsequent tokens deal 30% damage.
Red Token: Explodes on hit, dealing an additional 90 (+18% Physical Attack) magical damage.
Blue Token: Deals an additional 45 (+9% Physical Attack) magical damage and reduces Movement Speed by 15% for 1s.
The 6 tokens count as 1 basic attack. Damage to the same target is reduced to 30%, and skill cast speed scales with Attack Speed.

skill2.name: Law and Order
skill2.description:
Throws 8 tokens nearby, gaining brief invincibility, removing all debuffs, and increasing Movement Speed by 25% for 1s.
Token damage and effects are identical to 'Six Commands: Pursuit'.

skill3.name: Dynasty's Mandate
skill3.description: Fires a golden mandate in a designated direction. The first enemy hero hit takes 300 (+60% Magical Attack) (+120% Extra Physical Attack) physical damage and equal magical damage, and is stunned for 1s. The target's Physical and Magical Defense is reduced by 50% for 5s, and their vision is revealed. Allies moving toward the target gain 10% Movement Speed.

hero_034.status_text:
Apprentice Marksman 0/100
Marksman
連射型Marksman
Rangedポーク
Balanced
Difficulty: Easy

strategy.earlyGame: Build up the agility effect through normal attacks to boost attack speed and movement speed, gaining an edge in the lane. Utilize the red (extra magical damage and explosion) and blue (magical damage and slow) tokens fired on every third consecutive attack to clear minions and harass enemy heroes simultaneously.
strategy.midGame: Fire 6 random tokens in a cone with Skill 1, dealing physical/magical damage and slow effects. Accumulate damage by hitting multiple tokens, and use Skill 2 when enemies approach or CC you; this grants brief invincibility and dispels all debuffs while firing tokens in all directions to counterattack and reposition to safety.
strategy.lateGame: Fire Skill 3's golden decree at a key enemy target, dealing massive damage and a 1-second stun on hit. It also reduces the target's physical and magical defense by 50% for 5 seconds, allowing you to instantly melt the debuffed enemy using your Passive and Skill 1's barrage, guaranteeing a kill assist for your team.
strategy.teamfight: Continuously fire tokens with normal attacks and Skill 1 from the backline, maintaining high mobility with the agility effect. Halve the enemy frontline's defense and stun them with Skill 3's decree, then burst them down with the team's combined firepower. When ambushed or hit by CC, quickly disengage using Skill 2's debuff dispel and invincibility.
strategy.commonMistakes: A major mistake is wasting Skill 2 merely for damage, leaving you unable to dispel debuffs and resulting in death when hit by fatal CC. Also, Skill 3's decree affects the first enemy hit; if it hits a minion or an unintended target, its effectiveness is halved.
strategy.strengths: Despite being a marksman, the instant debuff dispel and invincibility from Skill 2 provide incredibly high self-preservation, which is a major strength. Additionally, the permanent attack and movement speed boosts from the Passive, combined with the 50% defense reduction from Skill 3, give you the upper hand against any enemy, from tanks to assassins.
strategy.weaknesses: Missing Skill 3's decree deprives you of your greatest weapon—the defense reduction and stun—drastically lowering your contribution to the team. Also, since you lack mobility skills, even if you dispel debuffs with Skill 2, you cannot cross physical walls, making it difficult to completely escape enemies with persistent pursuit capabilities.

synergy.reason: Provides massive shields and CC, creating a safe environment for dealing damage.
Her healing and skill cooldown resets greatly improve stability in laning and team fights.

counters.reason: His stealth and high burst damage make it easy to assassinate Di Renjie before he can react.
High mobility allows him to close the gap instantly, making it hard to escape.

passive.name: Mantra: Heart
passive.description:
Casting skills grants a stack of Mantra's Blessing, increasing Physical Defense by 32 and Magical Defense by 32 for 5s (up to 3 stacks).
After using Skill 1 and 3, he gains 1 enhanced basic attack (an additional one if hitting a target). Enhanced basic attacks deal an extra 28 (+30% Extra Physical Attack) physical damage and reduce target Movement Speed by 15% for 2s.

skill1.name: Mantra: Formless
skill1.description: Dashes forward with a punch, dealing 150 (+100% Extra Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies in the path and knocking them up for 0.5s. This skill stores 1 charge every 9s (max 2 charges). If an enemy is knocked into a wall by his Ultimate, using this skill within 8s transforms it into a flying kick, dealing additional physical damage equal to 15% of the target's lost HP.

skill2.name: Mantra: Illumination
skill2.description: Unleashes 5 punches per second for 4s (20 punches total). Each punch deals 65 physical damage (+50% to non-heroes) and physical damage equal to 0.35% of the target's max HP (increases by 0.25% per 100 Extra Physical Attack). Heals 65 (+2.5% Extra HP) HP per hit. During casting, you can change direction with the joystick, automatically firing enhanced basic attacks that apply the same effect as his passive. Healing is halved against non-heroes. Canceling the skill refunds 50%-100% cooldown based on duration cast.

skill3.name: Mantra: Truth
skill3.description: Punches forward, dealing 200 (+100% Extra Physical Attack) physical damage and knocking enemies back. Enemies knocked into a wall take an additional 200 (+100% Extra Physical Attack) physical damage and are stunned for 1s.

hero_035.status_text:
Apprentice Fighter 0/100
Fighter/Tank
突撃型Fighter
Dash/CC
Balanced
Difficulty: Hard

strategy.earlyGame: Maintain the physical and magical defense boost from the Passive's Mantra Protection triggered by skill casts. Knock up enemies with Skill 1's dash, and deal additional damage and a slow with the enhanced normal attack that follows. Rack up damage and recover HP with Skill 2's rapid punches to stably navigate the early game.
strategy.midGame: Combine Skill 1's dash and Skill 2's rapid punches to deal damage and sustain. While Skill 2 is active, enhanced normal attacks are automatically thrown, allowing you to stick to enemies and continuously deal max HP percentage damage. Since knocking back an enemy into a wall turns Skill 1 into a flying kick, always be aware of the terrain.
strategy.lateGame: Knock back enemies with Skill 3's punch; colliding them with a wall deals extra physical damage and a 1-second stun. This transforms Skill 1 into a flying kick that deals extra damage equal to 15% of lost HP, unleashing high assassination potential to one-shot the enemy's main heroes with this combo.
strategy.teamfight: It's ideal to fight near walls or obstacles, using Skill 3 to smash multiple enemies into a wall simultaneously for wide-area stuns and massive damage. Follow up on stunned enemies with Skill 2's rapid punches and the Passive's enhanced normal attacks, and chase down fleeing enemies with the evolved Skill 1 flying kick to secure the kill.
strategy.commonMistakes: The biggest mistake is failing to smash enemies into a wall with Skill 3, resulting in a mere knockback and missing out on the stun, extra damage, and the transformation of Skill 1 into a flying kick. Furthermore, hitting only non-hero units with Skill 2 and not gaining enough HP recovery is also problematic.
strategy.strengths: The overwhelming combo damage originating from the powerful stun and extra damage of hitting enemies into a wall with Skill 3, followed by the flying kick, is its strength. High dueling potential from the Passive's defense boost and Skill 2's continuous HP recovery and percentage damage makes him unparalleled when utilizing terrain.
strategy.weaknesses: Combat effectiveness is highly dependent on the presence of walls; in open areas, Skill 3 cannot show its true value, cutting the threat in half. Also, while you can change directions during Skill 2, your actions are restricted. If you don't cancel it manually, you can't dodge powerful enemy skills, leaving a large opening.

synergy.reason: Both heroes possess knock-ups, allowing them to chain powerful CC effectively.
Her reliable stun stops enemies, guaranteeing an easy wall-stun with your Ultimate.

counters.reason: His Ultimate cleanses AoE CC, completely nullifying Dharma's wall-stun.
His Skill 2 removes CC, allowing him to escape and counterattack.

hero_036.sub_role: Vanguard Tank / CC/Damage Taking / Late Game

passive.name: Victor's Resolve
passive.description: When HP drops below 30%, reduces incoming damage by 40% for 6s (90s cooldown). Casting 'Fearless Charge' or 'No Escape' grants an enhanced basic attack with different effects (up to 2 stacks).

skill1.name: Fearless Charge
skill1.description: Dashes forward, knocking enemies back, and performs a slash at the end, knocking them up for 0.75s. The dash and slash deal 225 (+5% Extra HP) physical damage and enhance his basic attack. The vertical slash deals 445 (+100% Physical Attack) (+80% Extra Physical Attack) physical damage (guaranteed critical hit with 150% base critical damage).

skill2.name: No Escape
skill2.description: Roars, reducing nearby enemies' Movement Speed by 15% for 2s, dealing 275 (+90% Extra Physical Attack) magical damage, and reducing their Attack Damage by 30% for 3.5s. Enhances basic attack and grants 25% Movement Speed (decays over 2s). The enhanced sweep restores 350 (+7% Extra HP) HP (halved against non-heroes; heals more at lower HP, up to +50%) and deals 235 (+100% Physical Attack) physical damage.

skill3.name: Overlord's Halberd
skill3.description:
Fights with back to the river. Gains Super Armor and 'Indomitable' while casting. Charges up and unleashes a shockwave dealing 900 (+260% Extra Physical Attack) physical damage. Enemies hit by the blade take an additional 50% damage and are stunned for 0.75s. Damage increases as HP decreases (up to +50%). Restores HP equal to 20% of damage dealt.
Indomitable: HP cannot drop below 1 and prevents death.

hero_036.status_text:
Apprentice タンク 0/100
Tank/Fighter
Vanguard Tank
CC/Damage Taking
Late Game
Difficulty: Normal

strategy.earlyGame: Disrupt enemy movements by knocking them back with Skill 1's forward dash and knocking them up with the final slash. When HP drops below 30%, the Passive grants 40% damage reduction; use this durability to play aggressively, dealing guaranteed critical damage with the enhanced normal attack after casting a skill.
strategy.midGame: Massively reduce enemies' movement speed and attack power with Skill 2's roar, protecting allies while making favorable trades. After casting, the enhanced normal attack sweeps a wide area and recovers HP, allowing you to stay on the frontline. Leverage the trait that increases healing when HP is low to throw off enemies' calculations in edge-of-the-seat battles.
strategy.lateGame: Deal massive area damage and stun with Skill 3's charged slash. Utilizing the super armor during cast and the "Unyielding" effect that prevents HP from falling below 1, you absorb all focused enemy attacks. Hitting with the blade restores 20% of the damage dealt, making you an unsinkable ship returning to the fray.
strategy.teamfight: Charge into the enemy formation with Skill 1 to disrupt them with a knockback and knock up, and use Skill 2 to reduce their attack power, lowering damage taken by your whole team. Endure fatal enemy blows with Skill 3's "Unyielding," then stun enemies and recover your HP with a strike that grows stronger the lower your HP is, acting as a solid frontline wall.
strategy.commonMistakes: The Passive's damage reduction effect only triggers once every 90 seconds, so making reckless charges while it is on cooldown and getting killed is a major mistake. Also, be careful with the direction of Skill 1's dash to avoid pushing an enemy tank into your backline or accidentally helping a fleeing enemy escape.
strategy.strengths: Absolute invulnerability from Skill 3's "Unyielding" effect and extremely high durability from the Passive's damage reduction are its strengths. Additionally, firepower and healing increase as HP lowers, making you excel in adversity. With abundant CC skills like attack power reduction, knockback, and stun, he is the ideal tank to protect allies.
strategy.weaknesses: Mobility is limited to Skill 1's dash, which cannot cross walls, making you susceptible to being kited by agile enemies. Furthermore, Skill 3's powerful slash has a charge time, making it easy to dodge, and the extra damage and stun only occur if the tip of the blade hits, requiring very precise positioning.

synergy.reason: Can push enemies away to keep distance, protecting the immobile marksman.
Xiang Yu leads the charge and restricts enemies, creating an opening for Nakoruru to burst them down safely.

counters.reason: High mobility allows him to bypass defenses, making Xiang Yu's durability less effective.
His defense reduction debuff significantly mitigates Xiang Yu's tankiness.

hero_037.status_text:
Apprentice Assassin 0/100
Assassin/Mage
高ダメージ型Assassin
Dash
Early Game
Difficulty: Normal

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, the basic playstyle is to accumulate "Shadow Power" by casting skills and use enhanced Basic Attacks to dash behind the enemy and deal damage. While silencing enemies in an area and reducing magic damage taken with Skill 2, farm and harass safely.
strategy.midGame: Use Skill 1, which manipulates a spirit, to move at high speed and actively gank other lanes. Take advantage of the Passive, which grants vision upon casting skills, to track enemy positions and launch surprise attacks. Since you can recover HP even when taking magic damage, you have an advantage against mages.
strategy.lateGame: In the late game, take advantage of the expanded range of enhanced Basic Attacks to exploit openings in teamfights and target the enemy's carries. Use the long-range dash of Skill 3 to quickly close the distance, keeping hit-and-run tactics in mind. Since Skill 3's cooldown is significantly reduced if interrupted, it can also be used as a feint.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, absorb enemy magic damage while disrupting enemy skill combos with the AoE silence of Skill 2. A playstyle utilizing high mobility is crucial—such as diving deep into the enemy formation with Skill 3 and disengaging with Skill 1, or conversely, approaching with Skill 1 and disengaging with Skill 3.
strategy.commonMistakes: If you engage without managing your Shadow Power, you won't be able to unleash enhanced Basic Attacks, drastically reducing your burst damage. Additionally, since you are defenseless while charging Skill 3, it's dangerous to cast it in a position where you are vulnerable to enemy CC. Also note that your recovery effect is halved against physical damage.
strategy.strengths: Overwhelming mobility through high-speed movement and long-range dashes when using skills, along with enhanced Basic Attacks that dash behind enemies, make this hero powerful. Furthermore, with the silence effect from Skill 2 and percentage HP recovery when taking magic damage, the anti-mage performance is extremely high, excelling in surprise attacks.
strategy.weaknesses: Since resistance to physical damage is low and the recovery effect is halved, you will struggle against heroes that rely on physical attacks. Moreover, because most of your damage resource relies on enhanced Basic Attacks that consume Shadow Power, poor energy management is a critical weakness that can leave you completely helpless.

synergy.reason: Great at following up after enemies are poked down, and their high mobility secures multiple kills.
Groups enemies together, making it easier for Sima Yi to land his Ultimate and AoE silence.

counters.reason: Has higher mobility, allowing him to easily escape or counterattack Sima Yi.
His massive shields and CC can completely protect the targeted squishy from burst damage.

passive.name: Silent Whispers
passive.description:
Gains vision of enemy heroes casting skills within a 2000 range and restores 10 Shadow Power (max 50).
Casting skills summons a scythe with Shadow Power, enhancing basic attacks and granting 20 Movement Speed for 5s. The first enhanced attack consumes all Shadow Power, teleporting behind the target to deal 300 (+60% Magical Attack) magical damage (true damage is doubled). Each scythe hit deals 230 (+46% Magical Attack) magical damage and true damage equal to the Shadow Power consumed.
Every 20 Shadow Power consumed increases the enhanced attack's range by 3%.

skill1.name: Shadow Bite
skill1.description: Controls a fast-moving spirit for 2.5s, gaining CC immunity. The spirit deals 115 (+23% Magical Attack) magical damage to enemies it touches. Upon basic attacking or when the effect ends, Sima Yi dashes to the spirit's location, dealing 115 (+23% Magical Attack) magical damage to enemies in his path and reducing their Movement Speed by 25% for 1s.

skill2.name: Desolate Ruin
skill2.description:
Unleashes spiritual energy to suppress the surrounding area, dealing 250 (+50% Magical Attack) magical damage and silencing enemies for 0.75s. Healing is increased by 10% while inside the suppression area.
Passive: Taking magical damage restores 25% of the damage taken as HP over 3s (halved for physical damage).

skill3.name: Death's Descent
skill3.description: Steps back and charges briefly before dashing to a targeted location, dealing 500 (+100% Magical Attack) magical damage (halved for enemies in the path). If interrupted, the cooldown is reduced by 90%.

passive.name: Master's Lesson
passive.description:
Basic attacks grant 1 stack of Admonition for 5s (max 5 stacks).
At max stacks, enters an enhanced state for 5s, increasing Movement Speed by 60 and Attack Speed by 30%. Basic attacks deal 80 (+28% Extra Physical Attack) true damage and reduce the cooldown of Skill 1 and 2 by 1s.

skill1.name: Sage's Admonition
skill1.description: Swings his ruler in a target direction, pulling enemies in front of him. Deals 320 (+100% Extra Physical Attack) physical damage and reduces their Movement Speed by 25% for 1.5s - 2.5s (duration scales with skill level).

skill2.name: Righteous Anger
skill2.description:
Enhances himself for 2s, gaining 25% Movement Speed and 45% Damage Reduction.
If hit by an enemy hero's basic attack during this time, reflects 50% of the damage back to the attacker and reduces their Movement Speed by 15% for 2s. The reflected damage applies basic attack effects.
Passive: Basic attacks have a 25% chance to trigger an extra basic attack dealing 50% damage and applying basic attack effects.

skill3.name: Sage's Authority
skill3.description:
Dashes in a target direction, tying the first enemy hero hit to a lantern, dealing 600 (+200% Extra Physical Attack) physical damage, and restricting their movement to the lantern's vicinity for 5s.
The tied target's damage output is reduced by 20%.

hero_038.status_text:
Apprentice Fighter 0/100
Fighter
狂戦士型Fighter
Dash
Late Game
Difficulty: Normal

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, land Basic Attacks to accumulate stacks of "Discipline". Use the enhanced state at 5 stacks (increased Movement Speed and Attack Speed, plus True Damage) to gain the upper hand in damage trades. It's effective to pull enemies in with Skill 1, preventing their escape while stacking your attacks.
strategy.midGame: Utilize the Movement Speed increase and 45% damage reduction from Skill 2 to forcibly dominate small skirmishes and 1v1 fights. Because you can reflect damage and apply a slow when taking Basic Attacks from enemies, you can play aggressively against physical attackers. Maintaining the enhanced state is crucial.
strategy.lateGame: In the late game, aim for powerful lockdown with the "Bright Lantern" from Skill 3. By binding the main enemy damage dealer for 5 seconds so they cannot escape, and reducing their damage dealt by 20% during that time, you create an advantageous situation for your team. Maintain the frontline using your damage reduction.
strategy.teamfight: Your primary role is to reliably catch and lock down key enemy targets with Skill 3. Activate the damage reduction and damage reflection of Skill 2 at the right timing to endure the enemy's concentrated fire. If you enter the Passive's enhanced state, your skill cooldowns are also reduced, allowing you to fight tenaciously.
strategy.commonMistakes: If you engage in combat without Discipline stacks accumulated, you will lack both damage and durability. Also, if the Skill 3 dash misses, you lose your biggest strength—the lockdown—increasing the risk of being isolated in the enemy formation. Mistiming Skill 2 will result in being easily defeated.
strategy.strengths: With high damage reduction and Basic Attack reflection from Skill 2, along with True Damage from the Passive, this hero boasts unparalleled strength in 1v1 brawls. The powerful lockdown ability of Skill 3, which binds an enemy to a specific location for 5 seconds while reducing their damage, is the greatest weapon.
strategy.weaknesses: Because the true potential cannot be unleashed without continuously landing Basic Attacks at close range, this hero is weak against long-range kiting and powerful CC that prevents approaching. Additionally, the strict range and hitbox of Skill 3 make it difficult to lock down highly mobile enemies.

synergy.reason: The combination of split-pushing and recalling allows for a relentless strategy that constantly confuses the enemy.
Fuzi's long lockdown creates the perfect window for Li Bai to safely unleash max burst damage.

counters.reason: Her high mobility and damage reduction gives her the edge in basic attack trades.
Can easily push Fuzi away, making it hard for him to close the gap and stack his passive.

passive.name: One Against a Thousand
passive.description:
Passive: Continuous movement increases Movement Speed by 2% per 100 units moved. Enters Charge State upon moving 2000 units. Stopping or dropping below 375 Movement Speed cancels the Charge State.
Basic attacks in Charge State knock enemies back and deal physical damage equal to 10% of max HP. Moving towards enemies grants 20% increased Movement Speed.

skill1.name: Azure Dragon Cleave
skill1.description:
Swings the Azure Dragon Crescent Blade, dealing 215 (+127% Physical Attack) physical damage to nearby enemies.
Charge State: Dashes forward, knocking enemies back and dealing physical damage equal to 10% max HP. Cleaves at the end of the dash, dealing 250 (+150% Physical Attack) physical damage.

skill2.name: Azure Dragon Assault
skill2.description:
Cleanses all CC and increases Movement Speed by 30% (decays over 2s).
Charge State: Cleanses all CC, leaps forward to knock enemies back, and deals 200 (+112% Physical Attack) physical damage.

skill3.name: God of War Descent
skill3.description:
Ignites fighting spirit, reducing the movement distance required to enter Charge State by 50% for 10s.
Charge State: Summons a cavalry charge forward, knocking enemies back and dealing physical damage equal to 10% of max HP. Then reduces the movement distance required to enter Charge State by 50% for 10s.

hero_039.status_text:
Apprentice Fighter 0/100
Fighter
突撃型Fighter
Dash/先手
Balanced
Difficulty: Hard

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, keeping moving to maintain the Passive "Charge" state is an absolute requirement. Seize control of the lane by utilizing the powerful knockback, max HP percentage damage, and high base damage of your Charge state Basic Attacks and Skill 1. Continuous movement and precise control are constantly required.
strategy.midGame: Take advantage of your high mobility to quickly roam to other lanes. Ganking by knocking enemies back toward your allies with Charge state skills is highly effective. Even if you get hit by CC, you can immediately cleanse it with Skill 2, increase your Movement Speed, and re-enter the Charge state, so keep roaming the map without stopping.
strategy.lateGame: In late-game teamfights, cast Skill 3 to halve the movement distance required to enter the Charge state, allowing you to launch continuous knockback attacks. Disrupt the enemy formation with your cavalry charge, deal max HP percentage damage, and quickly kill the defenseless enemy carries alongside your teammates.
strategy.teamfight: Your main role is to disrupt enemy focus and destroy their formation with continuous AoE knockbacks from Skill 3. It's effective to flank behind the enemies and hit them with charge attacks to push them toward your team's territory. Conserve the CC cleanse of Skill 2, and rampage while neutralizing enemy disruption.
strategy.commonMistakes: If you stop moving or your Movement Speed drops, breaking the Charge state, your skill performance drops drastically and you become a mere target. If you are hit by heavy lockdown immediately after wasting your CC cleanse skill, you often get killed without returning to the Charge state or fulfilling your intended role.
strategy.strengths: The overwhelming mobility from the Passive and the powerful knockback along with max HP percentage damage in the Charge state are the biggest strengths. Possessing a self-CC cleanse skill, and the ability to continuously knock enemies back when Skill 3 is active, grants extremely high formation disruption and surprise attack capabilities.
strategy.weaknesses: Because you must constantly keep moving, the mechanical difficulty is very high. You are highly vulnerable to powerful slow effects that cancel the Charge state or chain CC. A clear weakness is that once stopped, there is a high risk of being burst down by concentrated fire before you can re-enter the Charge state.

synergy.reason: His movement speed buffs make it much easier for Guan Yu to maintain his Charge State.
Her shields and CC provide protection, allowing safe engages and disengages.

counters.reason: His ultimate restricts movement, completely denying Guan Yu his Charge State.
Her guaranteed stun immediately stops his charge, leaving him vulnerable to heavy damage.

hero_040.sub_role: AoE Mage / Continuous Damage/True Damage / Late Game

passive.name: Explosion of Lotus
passive.description:
Hitting enemies with skills applies 1 stack of Floral Mark (lasts 8s). At 4 stacks, the mark detonates, restoring 120 (+12% Magical Attack) HP.
Detonating the mark deals 160 (+64% Magical Attack) true damage to nearby enemies and reduces their Movement Speed by 50% for 1s.

skill1.name: Falling Petals
skill1.description: Throws a lotus flower in a target direction. The lotus deals 180 (+57% Magical Attack) magical damage to enemies in its path both as it flies out and returns.

skill2.name: Petal Dance
skill2.description: Teleports to a targeted location and scatters 3 flower orbs at nearby enemies. Each orb deals 140 (+28% Magical Attack) magical damage. Hitting an enemy with an orb reduces the cooldown of Skill 2 by 4s.

skill3.name: Flowery Array
skill3.description:
Creates a formation at her current location. When the formation appears and disappears, it deals 210 (+50% Magical Attack) magical damage to enemies within.
While Diaochan remains inside the formation, the cooldowns of Skill 1 and Skill 2 recover significantly faster.

hero_040.status_text:
Apprentice Mage 0/100
Mage
範囲攻撃型Mage
Continuous Damage/True Damage
Late Game
Difficulty: ベリーHard

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, focus on hitting enemies with the outgoing and returning lotus of Skill 1 to apply "Floral Mark" stacks. Use the True Damage, HP recovery, and slow effect from the 4-stack explosion to gain an advantage in the laning phase. Avoid risky close combat and harass from a safe distance.
strategy.midGame: A playstyle that dodges attacks while dealing damage with the blink and flower orbs of Skill 2 becomes crucial. If the flower orbs hit, Skill 2's cooldown is reduced, allowing you to continuously deal damage tenaciously in small skirmishes when combined with the mark explosions (recovery and True Damage).
strategy.lateGame: In the late game, deploy the ultimate formation with Skill 3, and fighting within it grants an overwhelming skill rotation rate. Because you can continuously cast Skill 1 and 2 without pause inside the formation, the AoE True Damage and continuous recovery from mark explosions allow you to survive and fight against multiple enemies without falling.
strategy.teamfight: Deploy the formation with Skill 3, and chain mark explosions while dodging enemy skills by fully utilizing the invulnerability frames (blink) of Skill 2 within the formation. By spreading True Damage and slows to all surrounding enemies while continuously recovering your own HP, you serve as both a damage source and an aggro magnet in teamfights.
strategy.commonMistakes: If you fail to hit enemies with the flower orbs of Skill 2, the cooldown will not be reduced, breaking your mobility and damage output and exposing a fatal opening. Also, stepping outside the Skill 3 formation prevents your skill rotation from speeding up, losing your core strength. Getting hit by heavy CC and having your combo interrupted is also strictly forbidden.
strategy.strengths: The loop of True Damage, HP recovery, and slow from the Passive mark explosions is extremely powerful. With the evasion capabilities from Skill 2's blink and the overwhelming skill rotation rate inside Skill 3's formation, you have the potential to annihilate multiple enemies while dodging all their attacks if your mechanical precision is high.
strategy.weaknesses: Since you need to continuously cycle your skill combos, getting caught by hard CC like silence or stuns will leave you helpless and easily killed. Also, despite having True Damage, your base durability is low, making you highly vulnerable to sudden high burst damage that takes you down before your recovery can keep up.

synergy.reason: His AoE CC cleanse allows her to dance uninterrupted inside her Ultimate.
His strong AoE taunt groups enemies together, maximizing her AoE damage.

counters.reason: His suppression Ultimate cannot be cleansed by Purify, completely locking her down.
Her high burst damage and silence can easily kill Diaochan before she can spam her skills.

passive.name: Moonlight Dance
passive.description: Has two basic attack modes. If the target is outside melee range, she automatically switches to ranged. The first of every 3 basic attacks is a charge if in melee mode. The 3rd basic attack deals an additional 150 (+35% Magical Attack) magical damage and marks the target for 5s (cannot mark towers/crystal). Every 1 Magical Attack increases Attack Speed by 0.03%.

skill1.name: Crescent Moon Splice
skill1.description: Unleashes a moonlight shockwave in a designated direction, dealing 400 (+65% Magical Attack) magical damage and marking enemies.

skill2.name: Searing Sword
skill2.description: Pulls nearby enemies in, dealing 120 (+31% Magical Attack) magical damage and stunning them for 0.5s. She also gains a 450 (+80% Magical Attack) shield, reduces enemy Movement Speed by 25% for 2s, and marks the pulled enemies.

skill3.name: New Moon
skill3.description: Dashes, dealing 420 (+65% Magical Attack) magical damage to enemies in her path. Hitting a marked enemy resets the cooldown of New Moon.

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, apply marks to enemies from a safe distance using Skill 1 to farm and harass. Since your 3rd Basic Attack can also apply a mark, prepping it by hitting minions is also important. Play cautiously until you reach level 4 and unlock Skill 3, as your mobility is low before then.
strategy.midGame: Utilize the mechanic where hitting a marked enemy resets the cooldown of Skill 3 to dash freely across the battlefield. Confuse the enemy with a combo where you apply marks with Skill 1, Skill 2, and enhanced Basic Attacks, and then use Skill 3 to deal damage while moving.
strategy.lateGame: Even in late-game teamfights, managing marks and resetting Skill 3 is your lifeline. Dive into the enemy formation, disrupt their positioning, and protect yourself with the pull, stun, and shield from Skill 2. High mechanical precision and situational awareness are required to avoid hitting unmarked enemies with Skill 3.
strategy.teamfight: Mark multiple enemies with Skill 1, then dive into the enemy formation with Skill 3. Use Skill 2 to pull enemies together, stun them, and gain a shield. Then apply marks again to either disengage or pursue with Skill 3. By continuously dealing magic damage while constantly moving, you inflict casualties while avoiding enemy attacks.
strategy.commonMistakes: The biggest mistake is hitting an unmarked enemy or an enemy whose mark just expired with Skill 3, putting it on cooldown. This completely strips you of your mobility and damage source, leaving you defenseless right in the middle of the enemy formation to be easily killed.
strategy.strengths: The greatest strength is the overwhelming mobility that theoretically allows infinite movement and attacks by constantly resetting Skill 3's cooldown by consuming marks. The pull, stun, and shield from Skill 2 are also excellent, giving this magic damage-based assassin extremely high carry potential.
strategy.weaknesses: Because you become completely helpless if Skill 3 goes on cooldown, the mechanical difficulty is extremely high and heavily dependent on player skill. It is also highly vulnerable to targeted CC or silence, making you fragile if your combo is interrupted. A high reliance on mana regeneration is another weakness.

synergy.reason: Groups enemies together, making it easier for Luna to hit multiple targets and reset her Ultimate.
Provides CC cleanse and immunity, allowing squishy Luna to dive the enemy team safely.

counters.reason: Hard locks her with point-and-click CC, completely shutting down her mobility.
Reliable CC and burst damage can interrupt her combos and burst her down.

passive.name: Sanctification
passive.description: If a damaged target dies within 3s, gains 1-3 EXP orbs. Upon reaching Lv.15, gains a one-time 'Divine Bestowal'. Casting it increases his max level to 25 and grants 20 Magical Attack per level up. If cast on an ally, that ally gains 1200 EXP, an increased level cap, and a chance to gain EXP orbs. If playing Roam, grants additional sanctification effects based on the ally's role.

skill1.name: Divine Seal
skill1.description: Seals the area with divine power, dealing 70 (+14% Magical Attack) magical damage. Then deals 60 (+12% Magical Attack) magical damage every 0.4s for 7 hits. Sealed enemies suffer gradually decreasing Movement Speed (up to 30%), Physical Defense (up to 10%), and Magical Defense (up to 10%). Upon hit, gains 30% Movement Speed for 1s (decays).

skill2.name: Divine Punishment
skill2.description: Punishes the insolent by creating a magical circle, dealing 60 (+12% Magical Attack) magical damage 3 times. The circle explodes shortly after, dealing 300 (+70% Magical Attack) magical damage and knocking enemies up for 0.75s.

skill3.name: Heavenly Law
skill3.description: Gathers power and unleashes 3 shockwaves forward. The first 2 deal 120 (+20% Magical Attack) magical damage each; the final wave deals 560 (+80% Magical Attack) magical damage. Upon reaching Lv.15 and unlocking Sanctification, gains a 20% chance (+20% per level) to enter 'Heavenly Mode', allowing him to move while casting. Can be charged for up to 2s, firing upon moving or recasting. Attack range increases with charge time (max 1800). Deals 10% damage to structures.

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, use the continuous magic damage and the movement speed/defense reduction debuff of Skill 1 to poke enemies from a safe position. Gain EXP orbs from your Passive to quickly level up. Utilize your own movement speed boost to maintain a favorable distance while preventing enemy approaches.
strategy.midGame: Disrupt enemies with the combo of Skill 1 and Skill 2. The delayed explosion and knockup from the magic circle of Skill 2 are effective when placed to cut off the enemy's retreat. Fire the shockwave of Skill 3 from a distance to pressure enemies by lowering their HP before teamfights or while sieging towers.
strategy.lateGame: Once you reach Lv. 15 and unlock "Divine Power Grant", use it on a key ally to uncap their level limit, overwhelming enemies with stat differences. You will also have a chance to enter "Ascension Mode", allowing you to fire the massive charging AoE attack of Skill 3 while moving, demonstrating tremendous wave clear and teamfight power.
strategy.teamfight: From the backline, lower enemy defenses with Skill 1 and aim for knockups with Skill 2 to support your allies. When the fight stalls, fully charge Skill 3 from a safe zone to sweep the enemy formation. If Ascension Mode triggers, you can cast Skill 3 while moving, allowing you to deal damage while dodging enemy focus.
strategy.commonMistakes: Due to your low durability and mobility, stepping too far forward will result in easily getting killed if an assassin gets close. Also, since you are defenseless while charging Skill 3, poor timing or positioning will not only result in missing the skill but also turn you into an easy target for the enemy.
strategy.strengths: The biggest strength is the unique Passive that uncaps the level limit to Lv. 25, significantly buffing a single ally. Furthermore, the defense and movement speed reduction debuffs of Skill 1, along with the ultra-long-range, high-damage AoE attack of Skill 3, provide extremely high poking, tower defense, and sieging capabilities.
strategy.weaknesses: Lacking hard CC or blink skills, your self-defense capabilities when enemies get close are extremely low. Also, the Passive's Divine Power grant cannot be activated until the late game, and Skill 3 takes time to charge, making it a weakness that highly mobile enemy heroes can easily dodge your attacks.

synergy.reason: Provides a strong frontline and CC, allowing Jiang Ziya to safely poke from behind.
Benefits greatly from the level cap unlock, massively boosting her late-game damage.

counters.reason: His stealth and mobility let him easily close the gap and instantly assassinate immobile Jiang Ziya.
Can safely poke and deal burst damage from an even longer range.

passive.name: Monarch's Ambition
passive.description: Gains 1 stack of mark per second. Basic attacks consume all marks, dealing an additional 80 (+10% Magical Attack) magical damage per stack. At 4 stacks, his next basic attack becomes ranged, dealing 474 (+100% Physical Attack) (+42% Magical Attack) magical damage.

skill1.name: Hegemon's Shield
skill1.description: Gains a 600 (+18% Extra Max HP) shield for 5s. When it ends, deals 600 (+80% Magical Attack) magical damage to nearby enemies. Can be recast after 1.5s to manually detonate. If the shield is broken, damage is halved.

skill2.name: Double Intimidation
skill2.description: Charges and dashes 275-550 units, dealing 300 (+40% Magical Attack) to 600 (+80% Magical Attack) magical damage to enemies in the path and stunning them for 0.75s. Effect scales with charge time (max 1s). If interrupted or canceled, cooldown is reduced by 40%.

skill3.name: Battlefield Command
skill3.description: Selects an ally, channeling for 2.2s before teleporting to them. Immediately grants the ally a shield absorbing 480 (+12% of Target's Extra Max HP) to 960 (+24% of Target's Extra Max HP). After teleporting, heals himself and nearby allies for 300 (+8% Extra Max HP), gains 30% Movement Speed, and fully charges 'Double Intimidation' for 10s. Recasting after 0.4s teleports instantly, removes the ally's shield, and deals 200 (+40% Magical Attack) to 400 (+80% Magical Attack) magical damage to nearby enemies, but negates the healing effect. Shield and damage scale with channel time.
