strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, accumulate Passive marks and use your ranged enhanced Basic Attacks for harass and wave clear. Activate Skill 1's shield to prevent incoming damage, and manually detonate it before it is destroyed to deal magic damage to surrounding enemies, keeping damage trades in your favor.
strategy.midGame: Join fights with a combo combining the charge dash of Skill 2 to stun enemies and the shield explosion of Skill 1. Always keep an eye on the minimap. When allies in other lanes are in danger, immediately use Skill 3 to teleport and grant them a shield, demanding a global playstyle capable of overturning numerical disadvantages.
strategy.lateGame: In the late game, split push alone to draw the enemy's attention. When a teamfight is about to break out, use Skill 3 to grant a shield to your allied carry and group up. Waiting until the very end of the channeling time maximizes the shield amount and post-teleport damage.
strategy.teamfight: Appear on the frontline while protecting a key allied character with Skill 3, simultaneously healing your own HP and surrounding allies upon arrival. Afterward, act as a tank, absorbing damage with Skill 1's shield while disrupting the enemy carry with Skill 2's dash stun, setting up an environment where your allies can deal damage.
strategy.commonMistakes: If you receive hard CC during Skill 3's channeling, the teleport will be interrupted, leaving you isolated without being able to save your ally. Also, if the Skill 1 shield is broken by enemy attacks, the explosion damage is halved, so being too greedy and delaying the manual detonation too much is heavily discouraged.
strategy.strengths: The greatest strength is the global support ability to rush to allies anywhere on the map with Skill 3, providing a powerful shield and healing. Also, since the shield amount and damage scale based on max HP, building as a tank allows you to balance both damage and durability, which is excellent.
strategy.weaknesses: Heavily reliant on the Skill 3 teleport; if the channeling is interrupted, you cannot utilize your strength at all. Additionally, as a predominantly melee hero, missing the Skill 2 dash or being kited by enemies makes it difficult to deal damage. Lacking sustained damage, your solo kill potential is not high.

synergy.reason: A powerful marksman who fully maximizes the benefits of Liu Bang's global protection.
Can dive deep with her and follow up instantly with CC.

counters.reason: His true damage and percentage damage easily shred through Liu Bang's shields and high durability.
His high true damage ignores shields and defenses, dominating Liu Bang in the laning phase.

passive.name: Spear of Killing Intent
passive.description: The 4th basic attack knocks enemies up for 0.5s, dealing an additional 90 (+35% Extra Physical Attack) physical damage, and grants 20% Physical Lifesteal. Hitting an enemy with a skill or enhanced basic attack increases Attack Speed by 25%-50%.

skill1.name: Merceless Assault
skill1.description: Leaps to a designated location, dealing 250 (+85% Extra Physical Attack) physical damage and knocking enemies up for 0.5s. Can be recast within 5s to leap again and deal the same physical damage.

skill2.name: Back to the Wall
skill2.description: Dashes backward and enhances the next basic attack into a sweep for 3s. The sweep deals an additional 120 (+50% Extra Physical Attack) physical damage. If the sweep triggers the 4th basic attack, it applies the 4th attack's extra effects.

skill3.name: The Unrivaled
skill3.description: Swings his spear 4 times in a fan shape, dealing 250 (+65% Extra Physical Attack) physical damage per hit, with the final hit knocking enemies up for 0.5s. Gains Super Armor and 30% Damage Reduction while casting.

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, utilize the double jump of Skill 1 and the dash of Skill 2 to quickly clear the jungle. With the Passive increasing attack speed on the 4th Basic Attack or upon skill hits, you boast high farming speed. Take advantage of your mobility to actively invade the enemy jungle and steal their resources.
strategy.midGame: Armed with overwhelming mobility, dash across the map to gank and secure objectives. Dash in the opposite direction of your target with Skill 2, then launch a surprise attack on the enemy with the enhanced sweeping Basic Attack and the knockup from Skill 1. A playstyle focused on hit-and-run tactics to exploit enemy openings is crucial.
strategy.lateGame: In late-game teamfights, your low durability becomes apparent, so engaging from the front is dangerous. Split push to scatter the enemy formation and target isolated squishy enemies, or flank the teamfight from the side/rear to assassinate the enemy carry, strictly following an assassin's movement of quickly disengaging using skills.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, use the AoE multi-hit attack and super armor (CC immunity and damage reduction) of Skill 3 to throw the enemy formation into chaos. Jump in right after key enemy skills are used, disrupt them with knockups while dealing damage, and use your mobility to leave the frontlines before being focused down.
strategy.commonMistakes: Because Skill 2 has a unique mechanic of dashing in the opposite direction of the cast, mistakes of accidentally jumping right into the middle of the enemy team or failing to escape are frequent. Also, overestimating your mobility and forgetting your low durability, jumping in while enemies still have CC available, will lead to instant death.
strategy.strengths: The game's top-tier overwhelming mobility provided by three movement skills (two charges on Skill 1, one on Skill 2) is the biggest strength. This mobility grants high-level farming speed, split-pushing ability, escape potential, and chasing power, making him a highly powerful hero in macro play that dominates the entire map.
strategy.weaknesses: Base durability stats are extremely low, making him fragile and easily killed without being able to do anything if hit by CC. Also, because the controls are busy and require getting used to the unique backward dash of Skill 2, he has the weakness of being extremely dependent on the player's mechanical skill and map awareness.

synergy.reason: Reliable lockdown creates an opening for Han Xin to safely burst targets down.
Long-range stuns stop enemies, strongly supporting Han Xin's engages and escapes.

counters.reason: His Ultimate traps him completely, neutralizing Han Xin's mobility and leading to certain death.
Her high mobility and invincibility frames allow her to dodge his attacks and counter-kill him with sustained damage.

passive.name: Freezing Soul
passive.description: Leaving combat grants a 450 (+58% Magical Attack) shield. Enemies dealing physical damage to the shield are afflicted with Chilled. When the shield breaks, deals 400 (+75% Magical Attack) magical damage to nearby enemies and applies Chilled. The 3rd basic attack is enhanced, firing 3 icicles dealing 180 (+36% Magical Attack) magical damage (triggers 100% basic attack effects). Active skills and enhanced basic attacks apply Chilled, reducing Movement Speed and Attack Speed by 25%-50% for 2s (decays over time).

skill1.name: Shattered Ice
skill1.description: Summons an ice crystal at a target location, dealing 350 (+50% Magical Attack) magical damage to enemies and applying Chilled. Grants vision of the area and hit enemies for 2s.

skill2.name: Ice Prison
skill2.description: Summons frost power at a target location, dealing 250 (+50% Magical Attack) magical damage after a short delay and freezing enemies for 2.5s. Frozen enemies take an additional 150 (+20% Magical Attack) magical damage.

skill3.name: Winter's Grasp
skill3.description: Summons a blizzard at a target location for 5s, dealing 200 (+35% Magical Attack) magical damage every 0.5s and applying Chilled. Casting the blizzard instantly refreshes her passive shield.

strategy.earlyGame: Safely manage the laning phase by utilizing the Passive shield and "Cold" effect (reduced movement and attack speed). Harass enemies and chip away their HP from a distance using the icicle from the 3rd enhanced Basic Attack and the AoE damage and vision grant from Skill 1. The AoE damage and slow triggered when your shield breaks also helps in self-defense.
strategy.midGame: While the freeze effect of Skill 2 is powerful, it has a delay before activating, so the basic combo is to slow the enemy's movement speed with the Cold effect of Skill 1 before placing Skill 2. Ambushing from bushes, dealing bonus damage to frozen enemies, and securing kills with your team is a highly powerful tactic.
strategy.lateGame: In the late game where teamfights frequently occur, the wide-area blizzard of Skill 3 dictates the course of battle. Hinder enemy advances or retreats and split their formation with continuous damage every 0.5 seconds and the Cold effect. Activating Skill 3 immediately refreshes your Passive shield, so it can also be used to enhance your survivability.
strategy.teamfight: Deploy Skill 3 in densely packed enemy areas or around objectives (towers, dragons) to zone enemies by spreading slows and damage over a wide area. The ideal scenario is landing the freeze from Skill 2 on enemies whose movement is restricted, creating a situation where your team can attack one-sidedly.
strategy.commonMistakes: Because Skill 2's freeze has a delayed activation, throwing it randomly without predicting the enemy's movement will result in misses. Furthermore, lacking mobility and escape skills, stepping too far forward or letting an assassin get close means you will be taken down quickly despite the shield. Careful positioning is strictly required.
strategy.strengths: All skills feature powerful CC such as the Cold effect (reduced movement and attack speed) or freezing, giving her extremely high enemy control capabilities. Also, possessing a wide-area continuous damage skill makes her excellent at breaking formations in teamfights and zoning during objective fights.
strategy.weaknesses: Lacking any movement skills (blinks), her mobility is low, making her fragile in close-combat situations. Additionally, her greatest weapon, the freeze from Skill 2, is easily dodged if seen, making it difficult to land independently. It requires either comboing with an ally's CC or possessing high predictive skills to read enemy movements.

synergy.reason: Draws aggro and locks down enemies at the front, making it easier for her to land Ice Prison.
Layering their massive AoE magic damage decimates enemies in team fights.

counters.reason: High mobility lets her easily dodge freezes and instantly burst Zhaojun down.
His strong AoE CC cleanse and immunity completely neutralizes Zhaojun's freezing strengths.

passive.name: Secret techniques/secrets
passive.description: Increases Movement Speed by 20% when moving towards nearby enemy heroes.

skill1.name: Secret technique/alter ego
skill1.description: Attacks surrounding enemies with a fist blade, dealing 220 (220 + 90% of Physical Attack) physical damage. At the same time, summon a clone to randomly attack surrounding enemies, dealing the same amount of damage. The clone prioritizes attacking the enemy hero with the lowest HP.

skill2.name: Secret technique/shadow eclipse
skill2.description:
Fires a dark dagger in the specified direction, dealing 165 (165 + 57% of additional Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies along the route and reducing Movement Speed by 90% (recovers over 2 seconds). Enemies hit by the dagger are marked and receive physical damage equal to 24% of their Max HP after 3 seconds.
Attacking a marked enemy again will instantly stun the enemy for 1 second and recover 140 HP (140 + 60% of additional Physical Attack).

skill3.name: Secret technique/assassination
skill3.description: Charges in the specified direction and deals 660 (660 + 188% of additional Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies along the route.

skill4.name: Secret techniques/concealment
skill4.description: Enters stealth state 1.5 seconds after activation (duration: 24 seconds). When you break out of stealth, your attack speed increases by 50% for 5 seconds. Staying near an enemy hero for more than 3 seconds will break stealth.

strategy.earlyGame: Possessing high damage from the early game, the combo of applying a slow and mark with the dagger from Skill 2, followed by stunning and healing with the clone attack of Skill 1, is powerful. With a Passive that increases Movement Speed when moving toward enemies, aggressively target isolated enemies to secure early kills and widen your lead.
strategy.midGame: Gaining high burst damage from the long-range dash of Skill 3 dramatically improves your assassination capabilities. Target squishy backline heroes like mages and marksmen for surprise attacks. Ensure the mark explosion from Skill 2 (24% missing HP damage) triggers to guarantee the kill before disengaging.
strategy.lateGame: You slightly fall off in the late game when enemies group up and acquire defensive items. Avoid head-on teamfights; instead, sneak up from outside the enemy's vision and focus exclusively on instantly killing isolated farming enemies or the carry at the back of a teamfight. You are required to take down the enemy's key player, even if it results in a trade.
strategy.teamfight: Diving straight into the middle of a teamfight will get you melted instantly, so positioning yourself to flank from the side or rear is mandatory. Wait for your team to initiate and for the enemy's CC skills to be consumed, then use the Skill 2 -> Skill 3 -> Skill 1 combo to instantly assassinate a squishy carry and escape.
strategy.commonMistakes: Missing the dagger from Skill 2 prevents both the stun and the percentage damage of the mark explosion, drastically increasing the chance of a failed assassination. Another classic mistake is forcing an engage into an enemy group in the late game, failing to secure a kill, and being immediately counter-killed. Playstyle must account for your low durability.
strategy.strengths: The greatest strength is the overwhelming single-target assassination capability from the early to mid game, thanks to the slow, stun, percentage damage, and healing of Skill 2 combined with the high physical damage of Skills 1 and 3. Simply approaching enemies increases your Movement Speed, giving you excellent chasing power to prevent fleeing targets from escaping.
strategy.weaknesses: Having low defense and HP, you are easily killed if hit by CC. Although you have target missing HP percentage damage, your ability to break through tank/support-guarded formations or impact late-game teamfights is low. A clear weakness is that the longer the game drags on, the harder it is to exert influence.

synergy.reason: This is because it can support the enemy by lowering their defense power and taking their vision, and maximizes Lanlingou's burst damage.
This is because it attracts the enemy's attention in group battles, making it easier for the stealth Prince of Lanling to assassinate marksmen from behind.

counters.reason: Because they can secure their visibility with their skills, they can detect Prince of Lanling's stealth surprise attack in advance and take countermeasures.
He has extremely high durability and CC, and is able to withstand Lanlingo's burst damage and end up being restrained.

skills.name: guardian of the great wall
skills.description:
When equipped with a Light Sword: Increases Movement Speed by an additional 40, and applies 1 stack of Equilibrium Seal to enemy heroes for 5 seconds each time you deal damage with a basic attack or skill. After applying 5 stacks of the engraving, it will detonate, dealing 225 (140% of Physical Attack) physical damage to the target, greatly reducing Movement Speed for 1 second, and inflicting silence.
When equipped with a heavy sword: The attack speed of normal attacks is reduced, but additional damage is dealt by 50% of physical attacks. While the skill is active, you will become super armored and receive a 45% damage reduction effect.

skills.description: Rush in the specified direction. While the skill is active, you can use the direction keys to perform a second lunge in the specified direction. Deals 134 (70 + 40% of Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies along the route with each charge. If your charge hits an enemy, you can activate Skill 1 again within 5 seconds.

The light sword rotates for 3 seconds after reaching its maximum distance, dealing 160 (160 + 50% of additional Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies within the range every 0.5 seconds, and reducing enemy Movement Speed by 25% for 1 second. When Legend of the Twin Swords touches the rotating light sword, it will collect the light sword and reduce the cooldown of skill 2 by 5 seconds.

forms.form_name: When equipped with a heavy sword
forms.description: 指定方向に4回連続で剣を振る。 Each attack deals 320 (320 + 85% of Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies within range, knocks them back, and reduces enemy Movement Speed by 25% for 2 seconds.

skills.name: flowering blade

synergy.reason: This is because it provides damage reduction and CC nullification, increasing safety when Hana Mulan dives deep into enemy territory.

passive.name: elf step
passive.description: When a normal attack hits, deals 166 physical damage (100% of Physical Attack + 20% of Magical Attack) and gains energy. When full of energy, Dancing Elf gains a stack of Acceleration and Laurel Mark, which strengthens his next basic attack. Increased Movement Speed: Consumes energy to fly rapidly.魔法攻撃100につき移動速度が30増加する（最大450まで）。 Enhancement: When an enhanced normal attack hits a target along the route, it deals 269 (70 + 120% of Physical Attack + 30% of Magical Attack) physical damage. Dancing Elf's penetration/lifesteal is compatible with both physical and magical attacks, and the one with the greater effect is applied between Physical Pierce/lifesteal and magic defense penetration/lifesteal.

skill1.name: Mai/Scattered leaves
skill1.description: Fires a laurel leaf that deals 525 (525 + 120% of additional Physical Attack + 110% of magic attack) magic damage and acquires 1 stack of Laurel Seal when it hits the target. When it hits an enemy hero or reaches the maximum distance, it deploys a ring, dealing 175 (175 + 40% of additional Physical Attack + 37% of magic attack) magic damage to the enemy it touches, and reduces Movement Speed by 15% for 1 second. Each 100 increase in magic strength reduces the cooldown by 0.1 seconds (up to a maximum of 1.5 seconds).

skill2.name: Whirlpool/Whisper of the Forest
skill2.description: Receive the blessing of the Golden Forest and fully recover your energy. Immediately activates passive effects (including increased Movement Speed, 1 stack of Laurel Mark, and strengthens your next normal attack), and also gains 1 stack of Laurel Mark. At the same time, nullifies Movement Speed reduction and increases attack speed by 20% for 3 seconds.

skill3.name: Laurel dance in full bloom
skill3.description: Gain 1 stack of Laurel Seal when your energy is maxed out. Increases Movement Speed by 10% when skill is activated. Consumes the engraving to fire a laurel leaf, dealing 130 (72 + 35% of Physical Attack + 30% of magic attack) magic damage to the target with the lowest HP within range, and reduces the cooldown of Whirling: Whispers of the Forest by 0.2 seconds. When you reach 6 stacks of laurel engravings, you will unlock "Lauren Dance - Full Bloom" (up to 12 stacks of laurel engravings can be obtained). Every 3 leaves counts as 1 normal attack.

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, accumulate energy with Basic Attacks and use the Passive's Rapid Flight (Movement Speed boost) and piercing enhanced Basic Attacks to harass from a safe distance. Apply marks while dealing damage and slows with the laurel leaves of Skill 1, keeping in mind advantageous positioning as a magic damage-focused marksman.
strategy.midGame: Enhance your offensive and defensive mobility by fully utilizing the full energy recovery, Attack Speed increase, and slow immunity of Skill 2. Accumulate 6 or more laurel marks to unlock Skill 3, and use its auto-tracking magic damage against low HP enemies within range to gain the upper hand in skirmishes and gank pursuits.
strategy.lateGame: Since your Passive Movement Speed increases in proportion to your Magic Attack, you gain extremely high mobility in the late game. The continuous barrage of Skill 3, unleashed with maximum marks, boasts tremendous wave clear and kill potential. Maximize the benefits of physical/magical penetration and lifesteal to burst out damage from outside the enemy's range.
strategy.teamfight: Rely on the Rapid Flight of your Passive and the slow immunity of Skill 2 to strictly kite enemy assassins and fighters, keeping your distance. Chip away at their health with Basic Attacks and enhanced Basic Attacks from a safe position, and when enemy HP is low, cast Skill 3 to clean up kills all at once with its auto-tracking attacks.
strategy.commonMistakes: If you rely too much on the Passive's Movement Speed boost and step too far forward, you will be easily killed upon receiving hard CC (stuns or knockups). Also, since Skill 3 targets the enemy with the lowest HP within range, poor timing and positioning might cause it to be absorbed by enemy tanks, failing to deal effective damage.
strategy.strengths: She has the unique trait where her Basic Attacks deal magic damage and apply the higher value between her physical and magical penetration, as well as lifesteal. With the energy-consuming dash (Rapid Flight) and Skill 2's slow immunity, her greatest strength is possessing extremely high mobility and kiting ability despite being a marksman.
strategy.weaknesses: Lacking instant burst damage, it takes time to dish out enough damage to overcome enemy healing or shields. Furthermore, as a trade-off for high mobility, her base durability is extremely low, leaving her fragile and prone to being instantly killed if caught by targeted hard CC or unavoidable AoE burst damage.

synergy.reason: To build a stable front line with strong CC and durability, and create an environment where Erin can continuously unleash firepower from a safe distance.
Because it physically pushes back and protects enemies from approaching, Erin's survival rate increases as she fights while making use of her mobility.

counters.reason: She is a powerful assassin who can quickly close the distance with her overwhelming mobility and instantly kill the soft Erin.
It combines pokes from Rangeds and rapid approaches with continuous dashes, and Erin is defeated without giving her a chance to respond.

passive.name: Words/Curses
passive.description: If the interval between normal attacks or skill damage is less than 1.5 seconds, enter a continuous attack state on the target, dealing 120 (120 + 70% of magic attack) true damage every 1.2 seconds.

skill1.name: Kotodama/Bulwark
skill1.description: Summons 4 magic barriers at the specified location. When an enemy touches the barrier, the barrier disappears, dealing 360 (360 + 75% of magic attack) magic damage and stunning the target for 0.75 seconds. When the stun ends, the enemy's Movement Speed is reduced by 25% for 0.5 seconds. Enemies who come into contact with the barrier again within a short period of time will receive half the damage they receive. Stun effects do not stack, but Movement Speed is reduced by 25% for 1.25 seconds.

skill2.name: Kotodama/Number of lives
skill2.description: Summons an eroding magic circle to the specified location for 5.5 seconds. Deals 110 (110 + 24% of magic attack) magic damage to one enemy within the range of the magic circle every 0.5 seconds and gains vision within the range. For non-hero units, magic circle damage is increased by 50%. If the Lord of the Spirit is within the range of the magic circle, Movement Speed increases by 60. A maximum of two magic circles can be maintained at the same time. Every 12 seconds (varies depending on Cooldown Reduction), you can stack Skill 2 once (up to 3 stacks).

skill3.name: Word spirit/manipulation
skill3.description: Use the magic of words to suppress one enemy hero for 2.2 seconds, dealing 100 (100 + 18% of magic attack) magic damage to the target every 0.5 seconds. When the Lord of Words completes the activation of skill 3, a magic area is created at the target's feet for 0.5 seconds, dealing 240 (240 + 30% of magic attack) magic damage to enemies who step within the area, and stunning them for 0.75 seconds.

strategy.earlyGame: Utilize Skill 2's magic circle to clear minion waves quickly while securing vision within the area for safe positioning. It deals 50% extra damage to non-hero units, allowing for efficient farming. Your movement speed increases while inside the circle, so use it to your advantage for positioning. To trigger the true damage from your Passive, ensure you land basic attacks and skills within 1.5-second intervals to gain an advantage in early small-scale skirmishes.
strategy.midGame: You can place up to two magic circles simultaneously, enhancing your lane control. Place the 4 magic barriers from Skill 1 in pathways to prevent enemies from approaching, or to stun them for 0.75 seconds and assist teammates in securing kills. Enemies will not be stunned again if they touch the barriers consecutively, but their movement speed will be reduced, so it's effective to place them to cut off their escape routes. Combine this with the true damage from your Passive for high burst damage.
strategy.lateGame: Skill 3's 2.2-second suppression becomes extremely powerful. You play the role of reliably locking down troublesome enemy assassins and carries, allowing your team to focus and eliminate them. If you fully channel Skill 3, a magic domain is generated at your feet, dealing additional magic damage and a 0.75-second stun, so try to complete the channel whenever possible. The true damage from your Passive also triggers every 1.2 seconds, providing high sustained damage.
strategy.teamfight: Place Skill 1's barriers on the enemies' path to restrict their movement and control the teamfight with stuns and movement speed reduction. Lay down Skill 2's circle on the frontline to deal continuous damage and increase your movement speed, then use Skill 3 to suppress and completely neutralize the core enemy hero for 2.2 seconds. Maintain the consecutive attack state of your Passive using Skills 1 and 2, and contribute as your team's damage source by dealing continuous true damage.
strategy.commonMistakes: If you move and cancel the channel during Skill 3's suppression, the magic domain that deals additional stun and damage won't be generated, and the lockdown time will be wasted. Additionally, failing to understand that Skill 1's stun doesn't stack on the same enemy and overlapping the barriers is inefficient. Another common mistake is wasting Skill 2 on empty space instead of enemy minions, failing to utilize the 50% extra damage to non-heroes.
strategy.strengths: The greatest strength is the extremely powerful single-target suppression skill lasting up to 2.2 seconds, allowing you to reliably neutralize specific enemies. Moreover, the high minion clearing ability and vision from Skill 2, the area zoning and stun from Skill 1, and the continuous application of true damage from the Passive allow you to deal effective damage even to highly defensive tanks. The mobility gained from the movement speed increase within Skill 2's area is also excellent.
strategy.weaknesses: During the powerful suppression of Skill 3, you cannot move and become defenseless, making you vulnerable to being focused by other enemies while channeling. Furthermore, Skill 1's stun cannot stack on the same enemy, so you cannot stun multiple enemies for a long duration. To deal the true damage from the Passive, you must continuously land attacks within 1.5-second intervals, which becomes difficult against highly mobile enemies who frequently distance themselves.

synergy.reason: This is because it can provide follow-up damage to restrained enemies and support to increase Liang's survivability.
This is because you can possess Liang, give him a shield, and safely activate his control skills.

counters.reason: He has a strong ability to protect his allies by blocking Liang's ultimate with powerful shields and interference skills.
With its high mobility, it can evade Liang's skills, close the distance in an instant, and deal burst damage.

passive.name: Deadly Ninja Bee
passive.description: Strengthens the next normal attack every 5 seconds. Deals 100 (100 + 100% of magic attack) magic damage to hit enemies, knocks them back, and recovers 10 energy. Sliding the left stick during an enhanced basic attack or skill will increase your Movement Speed by 50% for 0.5 seconds and roll in that direction again. This effect decreases continuously.

skill1.name: Flying Dragon Flame Formation
skill1.description: Charges in the specified direction and performs a jumping roundhouse kick. Deals 600 (600 + 80% of magic attack) magic damage to enemies within range and knocks them up. Duration is 0.75 seconds. You can recover 25 energy when you hit an enemy.

skill2.name: flower butterfly fan
skill2.description: Throws a fan in the specified direction, dealing 450 (450 + 90% of magic attack) magic damage to the first enemy hit and greatly reducing Movement Speed for 0.5 seconds. At the same time, reduce the target's magic defense by 50 for 3 seconds. When hitting an enemy, restore 10 energy and also deal the same amount of magic damage to the nearest unit behind the target (damage to heroes is halved).

skill3.name: Super deadly ninja bee
skill3.description: Charges in the specified direction, dealing 800 (800 + 110% of magic attack) magic damage to enemies along the route and knocking them back. Enemies hit will have their physical attacks reduced by 20% for 2.5 seconds. You can recover 25 energy when you hit an enemy.

strategy.earlyGame: Utilize the enhanced basic attack prepared every 5 seconds to knock back enemies and recover energy while progressing through the laning phase. Throw Skill 2's fan to deal magic damage and reduce movement speed from a distance, while lowering the enemy's magic defense to harass them. Utilize the rolling movement from the Passive, triggered by inputting a direction immediately after a skill or enhanced basic attack, to dodge enemy attacks, secure a safe position, and manage your energy.
strategy.midGame: After reducing the enemy's magic defense with Skill 2, aim for kills with a combo using Skill 1's dash to knock up enemies in the area for 0.75 seconds. Since energy is recovered upon skill hits, it's crucial to land skills accurately to prevent energy depletion. Utilize the high mobility from the Passive's rolling to quickly roam to other lanes, and support your team's kills with Skill 1's knock-up or the enhanced basic attack's knockback.
strategy.lateGame: Take on the role of disrupting the enemy formation with the powerful dash and knockback of Skill 3. It reduces the enemy's physical attack by 20% for 2.5 seconds, making it highly effective against physical carries. Lower the frontline's magic defense with Skill 2, and utilize its effect of dealing damage to enemies behind them to chip away at the backline. Continuously chain skills and Passive movement to employ hit-and-run tactics, exploiting enemy openings and fighting while recovering energy.
strategy.teamfight: Spam Skill 2 from a distance to lower the enemy's magic defense and spread damage to enemies in the back. When you spot an opening, dash in with Skill 1 to knock up multiple enemies for 0.75 seconds, then use Skill 3 to knock them back, reducing their physical attack by 20% and dividing their formation. By combining the energy recovery from each skill hit and the disengage from the Passive's rolling, continuously deal damage while toying with the enemies.
strategy.commonMistakes: A typical mistake is missing skills and failing to recover energy, leading to energy depletion where you cannot cast subsequent skills. Also, misjudging the direction of the Passive's rolling movement and diving straight into the enemy or under their tower is common. Skill 2 has the characteristic of dealing damage to enemies behind the target, but if you position yourself to only deal single-target damage without utilizing this, your poke efficiency drops significantly.
strategy.strengths: Possesses abundant CC, including Skill 1's knock-up, Skill 3's knockback, and the enhanced basic attack's knockback. In addition to the magic defense reduction and powerful long-range poke capability of Skill 2, the ability to roll after every skill or enhanced attack grants extremely high mobility and evasion. Furthermore, Skill 3 applies a debuff that reduces the enemy's physical attack, providing outstanding burst damage and disruption against single targets.
strategy.weaknesses: Casting skills consumes energy, and if you don't hit enemies to gain sufficient recovery, the vulnerability from missing a skill is immense. Additionally, failing to control the Passive's rolling movement risks moving into careless positions. While possessing powerful CC and mobility, the lack of durability-enhancing skills makes them very fragile; getting caught by enemy CC will result in being burst down instantly.

synergy.reason: This is because it can securely restrain the enemy and create a situation where Mai can safely hit a full combo.
It provides mobility and recovery, making it easier for Mai to perform hit-and-away attacks.

counters.reason: This is because you can disrupt Mai's combo with an unavoidable hard CC and completely neutralize her mobility.
To block Mai's approach with long-distance stun and shield, and withstand burst damage.

passive.name: Passive Skill: Mermaid Gift
passive.description:
When entering a body of water (rivers are also considered bodies of water), she enters mermaid mode, reduces the Movement Speed reduction effect applied by 50%, and recovers 60 HP (60 + 1% of additional HP) and 5 MP every second. Mermaid mode's normal attack releases a spray of water that deals area damage.
“Recovery” becomes “Mermaid Gift”. Make a contract with the selected ally and give priority to the contract partner when "Sky Melody" is activated.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Sonic Song
skill1.description:
Activates 4 waves of sonic booms that spread around you, dealing 130 (130 + 20% of magic attack) magic damage and reducing Movement Speed by 7.5% until the third wave.
The fourth wave deals 250 (250 + 40% of magic attack) magic damage and reduces Movement Speed by 30% for 1 second.
When the last wave hits an enemy, it reverberates and deals 150 (150 + 24% of magic attack) magic damage to the enemy it touches. If activated in water, it will push away nearby enemies (knocking back and stunning nearby enemies).
When hitting a non-hero unit, the echo area will be narrowed and the damage dealt will be reduced by 50%.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Wave Breaker
skill2.description:
Jump forward and enter mermaid mode. If you land on the ground, create a water area and recover 320 (320 + 40% of magic attack) HP of allied heroes upon landing. Allies in water can recover HP and MP just like the mermaid songstress.
When activated in water, the CD will be reduced by 25%.
The water area disappears after 4 seconds have passed after leaving the generated water area.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Sky Melody
skill3.description:
After a short chant, resets the skill with the longest cooldown for one ally within range, and your own skill CD becomes 10 (+120% of your ally's CD).
If an ally does not activate the reset skill, the cooldown of "Sky Melody" will be reduced by 20%. Allies can activate the reset skill within 5 seconds without any MP cost, and share the defeat reward obtained from that skill with the mermaid diva.
The order of priority for resetting skills is the contracted party, the non-contracted ally, and the mermaid diva. If the skill cannot be reset, the cooldown of "Sky Melody" will be greatly reduced.

strategy.earlyGame: Enter water areas (like the river) or generate water yourself with Skill 2 to enter Mermaid Mode, utilizing the HP/Mana regen per second and 50% reduction of movement speed slow effects to sustain in the lane. Mermaid Mode's basic attacks deal area damage, helping with minion clearing. Release Skill 1's sound waves to deal damage up to 4 times and reduce movement speed to harass, and cast it from within the water to knock back and stun enemies for favorable trades.
strategy.midGame: Use Skill 2 to create water areas while healing allied heroes, supporting your whole team to receive the same HP and Mana regen effects as the mermaid diva. The healing amount and the water area's effects can significantly increase your team's sustained combat ability. Form a contract with a specific ally using Mermaid Gift, and use Skill 3 to reset their most powerful skill with the longest cooldown, creating an overwhelming advantage in skirmishes.
strategy.lateGame: Your main job is to use Skill 3 to reset the powerful skills of allies that are key to teamfights. Prioritize your contracted partner to reset their skill, as allies can recast the skill within 5 seconds without consuming Mana, generating game-ending destructive power. Using Skill 2 in water areas reduces its CD by 25%, so utilize the river or your own water areas to frequently provide healing and water deployment to support the team.
strategy.teamfight: Generate a water area in the center of your allies with Skill 2 to provide AoE healing and continuous HP/Mana regeneration. While inside the water, Skill 1's final wave transforms into a powerful CC that knocks back and stuns enemies, preventing enemy approaches and disrupting their formation. Then, use Skill 3 at the most crucial timing to instantly reset an ally's key skill. You also share the kill rewards from the reset skill, contributing to the team's overall growth.
strategy.commonMistakes: A frequent mistake is casting Skill 1 outside of water areas, failing to obtain the crucial knockback and stun CC effects. Also, misunderstanding that Skill 3's cooldown depends on and lengthens based on the CD of the ally's reset skill, and using it carelessly to unnecessarily prolong your own CD is an issue. Since the benefit diminishes if the ally doesn't use the reset skill within 5 seconds, casting it when uncoordinated should also be avoided.
strategy.strengths: Skill 3, which can completely reset the cooldown of an ally's powerful skill, is the greatest and most unique strength. Additionally, the generation of water areas with Skill 2 provides continuous HP and Mana regen not just for yourself but for the entire team, boasting exceptionally high support and sustain capabilities. Skill 1 transforming into an AoE knockback and stun within water is also excellent as a powerful self-defense and disruption tool, showing unparalleled strength in fights within water areas like the river.
strategy.weaknesses: Since Skill 3's cooldown heavily depends on the ally's skill CD, its true value can only be shown immediately after an ally uses a powerful skill, making dependency on allies' actions very high. Furthermore, the powerful CC of Skill 1's knockback and stun requires being inside a water area to activate, making location dependency a weakness. Lacking skills to deal high damage yourself, the combat capability when alone is extremely low.

synergy.reason: Because Dolia can dominate teamfights by resetting the cooldown of her powerful AoE ult.

counters.reason: This is because you can break her position with a powerful lockdown ability and defeat her without giving her a chance to reset her ally's ultimate.
Continuous CC can hinder Dolia's support actions and reduce her influence in team battles.

passive.name: Passive skill: Shikanna Kamui
passive.description: When a skill hits an enemy, the next normal attack will be strengthened (up to 3 stacks). The next normal attack will summon Mamahaha and deal 120 (120 + 37% of additional Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies within range for each stack.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Ambe Yatro

Summons Mamahaha to attack the enemy, dealing 380 (380 + 120% of additional Physical Attack) physical damage and inflicting the Eagle Mark.
Activate the engraving with other damage from the guardian of nature, dealing physical damage to the enemy equal to 6% of Max HP (maximum engraving damage to monsters is 300).
For every 40 additional physical attacks, damage from Max HP increases by 1%. Activating the engraving will reduce the cooldown of "Ambe Yatro" by 2.5 seconds and restore 35 MP.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Lela Mutube
skill2.description:
Rush forward and deal 400 (400 + 130% of additional Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies along your route. Recovers 400 HP (400 + 5% of additional Physical Attack) for each enemy hero hit.
If you hit a monster, the damage will be increased by 50%. The amount healed when hitting a non-hero unit is 50% of the amount healed when hitting a hero.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Kamui Mutsube
skill3.description:
Summon Mamahaha. Movement Speed increases by 50% and decreases continuously over 6 seconds. While flying, you can jump over terrain once.
When you activate "Kamuy Mutsube" again, you will charge into the specified area, dealing 600 (600 + 190% of additional Physical Attack) physical damage to the enemy and reducing their Movement Speed by 25% for 3 seconds. At the same time, the damage dealt to targets hit by the attack is reduced by 30% for 3 seconds.
Passive: If you are out of combat for 4 seconds, summon Mamahaha and fly, increasing your Movement Speed by 10%. Once in combat mode, the Movement Speed increase effect is lost.

strategy.earlyGame: Utilize Skill 2's dash, which deals 50% extra damage to monsters, and its HP recovery effect upon hitting enemy units to quickly clear the jungle while maintaining high health. Apply the hawk mark with Skill 1, and trigger it with other damage to deal additional physical damage equal to 6% of max HP, while also reducing CD and recovering Mana, to farm efficiently. The enhanced basic attack from the Passive after landing a skill also contributes to clearing speed.
strategy.midGame: If out of combat for 4 seconds, your Passive increases movement speed by 10%, and casting Skill 3 increases movement speed by 50%, allowing you to rapidly traverse the map and launch ambushes. While flying, you can leap over terrain once, allowing approaches from unexpected routes. Skill 3's charge reduces the enemy's damage dealt by 30% for 3 seconds and lowers their movement speed, enabling you to finish them off with the Skill 1 and enhanced basic attack combo without giving them a chance to counterattack.
strategy.lateGame: As bonus physical attack increases, the max HP percentage damage of Skill 1's mark rises, granting you extremely high burst damage. Use the overwhelming mobility of Skill 3 to dive directly into the enemy backline, applying the mark with Skill 1, dashing with Skill 2, and unleashing the enhanced basic attack for hit-and-run tactics. Since the HP recovery from hitting heroes with Skill 2 also scales with attack power, you can maintain survivability while dealing damage to multiple enemies.
strategy.teamfight: Instead of charging straight into the teamfight, utilize Skill 3's flying state and terrain crossing to ambush fragile enemy attackers from the flank or rear. By landing Skill 3's charge on the enemy's main damage dealers, you also play a crucial role in reducing their damage dealt by 30% for 3 seconds, minimizing damage to your team. After securing a kill by applying the mark with Skill 1, use the recovery and movement of Skill 2's dash to quickly disengage to a safe position and wait for another opportunity to re-engage.
strategy.commonMistakes: Failing to trigger the hawk mark applied by Skill 1 with another attack causes you to miss out on significant percentage damage, Mana recovery, and CD reduction, drastically lowering your burst damage. Also, failing to properly weave the Passive's enhanced basic attack into your skill combo often results in insufficient total damage. Reckless charges into the middle of the enemy team using Skill 3's flying state, only to get CC'd and killed without doing anything, should also be avoided.
strategy.strengths: The greatest strength is the mobility to quickly traverse the entire map and ambush enemies, utilizing the overwhelming movement speed and terrain-crossing ability of Skill 3 and the Passive. The instant burst damage is extremely high due to Skill 1's mark dealing max HP percentage damage with high physical scaling, and Skill 3's charge makes you strong in duels by reducing the enemy's damage dealt by 30%. Furthermore, the HP recovery and extra monster damage from Skill 2 provide excellent jungle clear speed and sustain.
strategy.weaknesses: If caught by enemies with powerful CC (crowd control), you will be killed quickly as your durability is not high. Also, the skill kit is specialized for approaching and dealing damage, lacking reliable disengage skills once you dive in, posing the risk of being stranded deep within the enemy formation. Mana consumption is intense, and failing to properly trigger Skill 1's mark to recover Mana will quickly lead to Mana depletion.

synergy.reason: Because it provides a wide range of CC and allows Nakoruru to safely deal burst damage.
Because it gathers enemies while giving stealth and defense down, and can perfectly support Nakoruru's assault.

counters.reason: Because it can neutralize Nakoruru's bursts and dives with its powerful shield and knockback.
With his invincibility skills and overwhelming burst damage, he can defeat Nakoruru in close combat.

passive.name: Passive skill: Oboro sword
passive.description:
The next normal attack will change to a batto slash. Battou Slash has increased range for 2 seconds, deals 40 (40 + 35% of additional Physical Attack) physical damage to the target, and reduces Movement Speed by 15%-30% (increases based on hero level). Deals an additional 80 (80 + 70% of additional Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies at the edge of the Attack Range, and restores own HP by 125 (125 + 2% of additional HP) (half effect against non-hero units).
Battou Slash can be activated once every 5 to 4 seconds (shortened depending on hero level). Every time you deal damage with a skill, the CD of Battou Slash is reduced by 0.5 seconds.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Swallow return
skill1.description:
Jump backwards and slash at enemies along the route, dealing 330 (330 + 150% of additional Physical Attack) physical damage. The second stage of the skill can be activated after 2 seconds, but if it hits an enemy unit, it can be activated immediately.
If the landing point of the second stage is near the activation point of the first stage, the second stage will be strengthened. Jump backwards with Swallow Gaeshi and slash enemies within a wider range. Each time you hit an enemy unit, the CD of Skill 3 is reduced by 1 second (halved for non-hero units). For every 2% HP the target loses, the damage of Enhanced Swallow Gaeshi is increased by 1%.

skill2.name: Skill 2: God sending
skill2.description:
Fire a quick slash in the specified direction, dealing 580 (580 + 190% of additional Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies within range.
If your slash hits an enemy at the far end of the range, it will stun the enemy for 0.75 seconds.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Yuki Sasame
skill3.description:
Fires 4 quick slashes in the specified direction, dealing 210 (210 + 65% of additional Physical Attack) physical damage to all enemies within range each time. If an enemy hero is among the enemies hit, each slash will restore 300 HP (300 + 4.5% of additional HP).
When hitting a non-hero unit, the recovery effect is halved.

strategy.earlyGame: Make full use of the Passive's drawing slash, utilizing its long reach to damage and slow enemies from a safe distance. Landing hits with the tip of the attack range triggers additional damage and heals your HP, making it crucial to maintain the distance for tip hits. Skill 1 jumps backward while attacking, allowing you to deal damage while dodging enemy skills, and if it hits an enemy, you can immediately cast the second phase, demonstrating mobility and burst damage from the early game.
strategy.midGame: Continuously land Skill 2's swift slashes on enemies. Since hitting enemies with the tip of this skill also stuns them for 0.75 seconds, chain it from the slow effect of the Passive's drawing slash for reliable crowd control. By landing the second phase of Skill 1 near the starting point of the first phase, it becomes an Enhanced Swallow Return, dealing damage in a wider area, reducing Skill 3's CD, and finishing enemies with additional damage based on their missing HP.
strategy.lateGame: Skill 3's 4-hit combo slash is your main damage source and lifeline. Since it heals a percentage of your HP each time it hits an enemy hero, it boasts high survivability even in teamfights. Capitalize on the mechanic where dealing damage with skills reduces the Passive's CD, continuously weaving skills and drawing slashes in combat. Adjust your positioning with Skill 1 and accurately snipe key enemy heroes with the tip stun of Skill 2.
strategy.teamfight: Instead of fighting front-to-back, use your high mobility to flank from the sides or rear. Stun the enemy key targets with the tip of Skill 2, and immediately unleash the combo slash of Skill 3 to deal massive damage and heal yourself. Even if faced with counterattacks or being surrounded, use Skill 1's backward jump to deal damage while quickly disengaging, adopting a hit-and-run tactic by continuously harassing from the tip range of the Passive's drawing slash again.
strategy.commonMistakes: The most significant mistake is getting too close to enemies, wasting the crucial effects like additional damage and stun, as both the Passive's drawing slash and Skill 2's stun effect only activate when hit at the tip of the attack range. Also, since Skill 1 jumps backward, you must input the stick in the opposite direction of where you want to move; operational errors often cause players to dive into the center of the enemy team or fail to escape.
strategy.strengths: The Passive and Skill 2 have powerful bonuses for hitting with the tip, providing high mid-range harassment and control capabilities. Combining the double movement/evasion capability of Skill 1 with the high HP recovery upon hitting enemy heroes with Skill 3 grants immense potential to win one-sidedly while dodging enemy attacks. Since every skill hit reduces the Passive's cooldown, it also excels at outputting sustained mid-range damage.
strategy.weaknesses: The base durability is set low, and Skill 3's healing effect doesn't activate unless it hits enemies, so getting stopped by powerful CC will result in instant death. Maximizing the skills' power constantly requires maintaining the precise distance of 'tip hits,' making the operation difficulty and reliance on player skill extremely high. Moreover, since Skill 1's movement direction is unique, operational errors in split-second decisions can be fatal.

synergy.reason: To create an environment where Ukyo can safely activate his ultimate by blocking the enemy's CC on the front lines.
This is because you can protect Ukyo with a shield and strong interference while creating an opening for a kill.

counters.reason: It has high durability and can contain Ukyo's skill combos with CC such as silence.
This is because he can use his overwhelming mobility to avoid Ukyo's attacks while dealing accurate damage.

passive.name: Passive Skill: Protection of Holy Light
passive.description: Gain Holy Light Protection and recover 120 HP (120 + 2.5% of additional HP) every 2 seconds.移動速度が減少させられている間は、2秒ごとに追加でHPを120（120+追加HPの2.5%）回復する。

skill1.name: Skill 1: Pledge Shield
skill1.description:
Movement Speed is increased by 30% for 3 seconds and your next normal attack is strengthened. Enhanced normal attacks have a rushing effect, dealing 350 (350 + 120% of additional Physical Attack) physical damage and inflicting silence for 1.25 seconds.
Enhanced normal attack imprints an imprint on the enemy hero for 5 seconds, and deals additional magic damage equal to 1% of the imprinted enemy's HP for each damage dealt with a normal attack or skill.
Allies near the engraved enemy have their Movement Speed increased by 10%.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Spin attack
skill2.description: Summons a holy shield that circles around you and deals 290 (150 + 80% of Physical Attack) physical damage to the enemy every second. Duration is 5 seconds.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Holy Sword Judgment
skill3.description:
Raise your holy sword and leap toward the enemy hero, dealing magic damage equal to 16% of the enemy's HP and knocking up enemy heroes within range for 0.5 seconds.
Simultaneously summons a holy symbol, dealing 85 magic damage per second to enemy heroes within range for 5 seconds.

strategy.earlyGame: Capitalize on the continuous HP regeneration effect every 2 seconds from the Passive to stay in the lane longer and farm advantageously. When subjected to movement speed reductions, the healing amount increases further, allowing you to position aggressively without fearing enemy harassment. Activate Skill 2 to deal continuous physical damage to the surroundings, clearing minions quickly while approaching enemy heroes for favorable damage trades.
strategy.midGame: Cast Skill 1 to increase movement speed by 30%, utilizing your mobility to actively rotate and help other lanes. The enhanced basic attack from Skill 1 features a dash toward the enemy and a 1.25-second silence effect, allowing you to seal the enemy's skill casts and attack without allowing a counterattack. It also applies a 5-second mark to the target, enabling follow-up damage as magic damage equal to 1% of max HP, so coordinate focus with allies to secure kills.
strategy.lateGame: Utilize the powerful percentage magic damage of Skill 3 (16% of the enemy's max HP) to deal massive damage even to enemy frontline tanks. Skill 3 jumps to a targeted enemy, knocking them up for 0.5 seconds, and summons a holy seal that deals continuous damage to the surroundings for 5 seconds, excelling as the initiator of teamfights. Disrupt the enemy formation using the silence of Skill 1, the continuous damage of Skill 2, and the percentage damage and CC of Skill 3.
strategy.teamfight: Use Skill 1 to increase movement speed and charge at backline heroes heavily reliant on skills, like mages and marksmen, neutralizing them with a 1.25-second silence. Immediately activate Skill 2 to scatter damage to all surrounding enemies, and slam Skill 3 onto the most troublesome enemy to inflict a knock-up and percentage damage. Near enemies marked by Skill 1, allies' movement speed also increases by 10%, strongly supporting the entire team's pursuit.
strategy.commonMistakes: Skill 1's silence effect is most effective against heroes highly dependent on skills; wasting it on basic-attack-focused tanks diminishes its value. Also, a common mistake is hoarding Skill 3 too much, failing to realize that since its percentage magic damage relies on the enemy's max HP, using it early or mid-fight on enemies with high HP yields greater total damage than using it as a finishing blow on enemies with little HP remaining.
strategy.strengths: The greatest strength is the overwhelming lane sustain capability from the unconditional continuous HP regeneration of the Passive (doubled when slowed). The movement speed increase and targeted dash + silence effect from Skill 1 are very easy to use, reliably hindering enemy actions. Additionally, possessing percentage magic damage of 16% max HP from Skill 3 allows for effective damage against tough tanks stacking physical defense, making the hero highly adaptable despite simple controls.
strategy.weaknesses: Being a melee hero who must get close to deal damage, there is a weakness of being whittled down without being able to do anything against enemies who excel at kiting from afar or team comps with strong CC that restrict movement. Moreover, lacking defensive skills that instantly negate enemy attacks makes them prone to being focused and killed if they dive too deep into the enemy formation. The lack of a dedicated escape skill is also a drawback.

synergy.reason: This allows you to unleash high firepower from a safe position in time for Arthur's silence or launch.
This allows Arthur to silence his enemies while ensuring that he can assassinate them from behind.

counters.reason: Because it ignores Arthur's defense power with guaranteed damage and can easily chip away at the entire shield.
With his high self-healing power and mobility, he can neutralize and overwhelm Arthur's close combat and continuous combat.

passive.name: Passive Skill: Qitian Great Saint's Majesty
passive.description:
When you activate the skill, your next normal attack will be strengthened. The enhanced normal attack charges the target and deals 648 (475 + 100% of Physical Attack) physical damage. All this damage becomes a critical hit.
Saitian Great Saint's initial Critical Rate is 20%, but critical damage is 150%.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Self-defense spell
skill1.description:
I begin to think of a self-defense spell that can block the enemy's skills. Self-defense spells last up to 1.5 seconds. If you successfully block an enemy's skill, you will gain invincibility for 0.2 seconds and a shield of 859 (600 + 150% of Physical Attack + 15% of additional HP) for 4 seconds.
When you activate the skill, your Movement Speed increases by 40% for 1 second.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Combat Assault
skill2.description:
Charges in the specified direction, and if it touches a target along the route, it uses the momentum to jump and increases Movement Speed by 90% for 1 second (decreases continuously). Jumping is interrupted when a normal attack or skill is activated.
Demon Extermination: Deals 274 (240 + 20% of Physical Attack) damage to monsters touched during the attack. Monsters with less than 15% HP are instantly killed (excluding Overlords and Tyrants).

skill3.name: Skill 3: Nyoibo
skill3.description:
Stab the Nyoi stick into the ground, dealing 250 (250 + 80% of additional Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies within range and knocking them up for 1 second.
Gives 3 stacks of engravings to enemies within range. When a normal attack hits an enemy with the engraving, it consumes one stack of the engraving and deals an additional 270 physical damage.

strategy.earlyGame: Maximize the Passive where casting a skill enhances the next basic attack, making you charge at the enemy with a guaranteed critical hit. Since you have a 20% critical rate from the start, alternate between skills and enhanced basic attacks to aim for high burst damage. The charge from Skill 2 deals true damage upon touching monsters and has an instant kill effect on monsters below 15% HP, allowing for extremely fast jungle clearing.
strategy.midGame: Utilize your high mobility and burst damage to launch ambushes across lanes. When approaching enemies, use Skill 2, taking advantage of the extra jump upon hitting the target and a 90% movement speed increase to instantly close the gap. When the enemy is about to counterattack with skills, cast Skill 1 to block their skills for 1.5 seconds, gaining 0.2 seconds of invincibility and a powerful shield, allowing you to deal damage one-sidedly and secure kills.
strategy.lateGame: As you complete your equipment, the critical damage of the Passive's enhanced basic attack skyrockets, gaining the destructive power to instantly assassinate the enemy backline. Use Skill 3 to slam the Ruyi Jingu Bang into the ground, knocking up enemies in the area for 1 second and applying 3 stacks of marks simultaneously. Hitting enemies with these marks with basic attacks triggers additional physical damage, allowing massive damage against even tanky enemy heroes through consecutive attacks after Skill 3.
strategy.teamfight: Charging straight into a teamfight will result in being focused down, so flank from the sides or behind after your team initiates, targeting the most fragile enemy attackers. Quickly close the gap with Skill 2, seal the enemy's actions with the area knock-up of Skill 3, and unleash the enhanced basic attacks from your Passive. If powerful enemy skills fly towards you, immediately block them with Skill 1 to gain a shield and invincibility, surviving while disengaging.
strategy.commonMistakes: A very common mistake is inputting skills too rapidly in succession, forgetting to weave the Passive's enhanced basic attacks in between. This drastically lowers the total damage output. Additionally, while Skill 1 blocks an enemy 'skill' once, it does not trigger against basic attacks. Using it pointlessly against marksmen's basic attacks, or failing to block, not receiving the shield, and dying due to misjudgment are noticeable errors.
strategy.strengths: The enhanced basic attack after a skill guarantees a critical hit and causes a charge towards the target, boasting overwhelming instant burst damage and pursuit capabilities. Since Skill 1 can nullify enemy skills to gain a shield and invincibility, the defensive adaptability is high despite being an assassin. Furthermore, the additional true damage and instant kill effect on monsters from Skill 2 make the jungle clear speed and objective control abilities exceptionally high.
strategy.weaknesses: If you fail to block an enemy skill with Skill 1, the very low durability results in extreme fragility, leading to quick deaths. Also, since most of the damage relies on the enhanced basic attacks from the Passive, it is hard to output damage against enemies with skills that nullify or evade basic attacks. As the initial critical damage is capped at 150%, it takes time to complete items and output sufficient burst damage.

synergy.reason: This is because you can quickly follow up Goku's burst damage and lead to a sure kill.
Because by attracting and grinding down enemies, it becomes easier for Goku to safely jump in and pick up kills.

counters.reason: He has high defense and CC, and is able to withstand Goku's burst damage and counterattack.
This is because you can combine area damage and damage reduction when Goku approaches and defeat him.

passive.name: Passive skill: Sea beast protection
passive.description: Increases physical and magic defense by 100 for yourself and allied heroes within 800 range with the lowest HP. If there are no friendly heroes within 800 range, your physical and magic defense increases by an additional 50%.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Mad Wave Sword
skill1.description:
Tap: Slash with your sword, dealing 528 (350 + 110% of Physical Attack) physical damage, reducing Movement Speed by 30% and cooldown by 50% for 1 second. Slashing an enemy restores 100 HP (100 + 2% of additional HP) (halved for non-hero units).
Hold: Throws a weapon in the specified direction, dealing 528 (350 + 110% of Physical Attack) physical damage and reducing Movement Speed by 15% for 1 second. After the weapon hits the ground, it deals 99 (75 + 15% of Physical Attack) physical damage over time. When you pick up a weapon, you can slash nearby enemies and knock them back. When the skill hits an enemy, it reduces the enemy's Physical Defense by 50 for 2 seconds.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Island Fury
skill2.description: Charges in the specified direction, dealing 529 (400 + 80% of Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies along the route, knocking them up for 0.75 seconds, and then greatly reducing Movement Speed for 1 second.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Waves
skill3.description:
After charging for a short time, it cuts through the earth. Deals 700 (700 + 180% of additional Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies within range, knocks them up for 1 second, and immediately interrupts the target's skill activation.
The rift remains for 5 seconds, dealing 100 (100 + 25% of additional Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies within the area every 0.5 seconds and reducing their Movement Speed by 30%.

strategy.earlyGame: Thanks to the Passive granting a constant 100 physical and magic defense to yourself and one ally in range, you are very tanky and can fight advantageously from the early game. In isolated situations with no allies nearby, your own defense increases by an additional 50%, making you strong in solo lane duels. Land the tap cast of Skill 1 to deal damage and reduce enemy movement speed while healing your HP, and utilize the 50% CD reduction effect to cycle the skill frequently.
strategy.midGame: Utilize the hold cast of Skill 1. Throw your weapon in a designated direction to create a continuous damage area, reducing the enemies' physical defense by 50 for 2 seconds to boost your burst damage. By walking over to pick up the weapon yourself, you slash surrounding enemies, knocking them back and dealing extra damage. To secure escaping enemies, approach with the dash of Skill 2 to knock up enemies in the path for 0.75 seconds, then completely catch them with the subsequent massive movement speed reduction.
strategy.lateGame: The powerful area control of Skill 3 is the key to teamfights. After a short charge, sunder the earth, dealing massive damage and knocking up enemies in the area for 1 second, instantly interrupting enemy skill casts. Furthermore, the resulting fissure remains for 5 seconds, continuously dealing damage every 0.5 seconds and applying a 30% movement speed reduction to enemies within it, playing a role in splitting the enemy formation in two and heavily restricting movement.
strategy.teamfight: Stand on the frontline utilizing the defense increase from the Passive, protecting the ally hero with the lowest HP while fighting. Dive into the enemy team with Skill 2 to apply a knock-up, and immediately cast Skill 3 to knock up multiple enemies at once and interrupt the channeling of powerful enemy skills. Aim to divide enemies and restrict movement with the generated fissure, throw your weapon with the hold cast of Skill 1 to lower physical defense, and manipulate enemy positions with the knockback when picking it up.
strategy.commonMistakes: Picking up the weapon thrown by holding Skill 1 triggers a powerful AoE attack and knockback, but a common mistake is getting too fixated on picking it up and becoming isolated deep within the enemy formation. Also, Skill 3 has a short charge time before casting, so firing it without predicting enemy movement makes it easy to dodge. Since the Passive's defense boost is distributed to allies when they are nearby, fighting recklessly and getting killed is another risk.
strategy.strengths: The constant physical and magic defense increase from the Passive boasts high durability from the early game without relying on items. Possesses highly versatile and powerful CC and debuffs, including healing and CD reduction from tapping Skill 1, defense reduction and knockback from holding it, dash knock-up from Skill 2, and wide-area knock-up, continuous slow, and channel interruption from Skill 3. The ability to maintain the frontline while restricting enemy actions is extremely high.
strategy.weaknesses: Since skill animations and casts (especially Skill 3's charge) are generally slow, it is difficult to land skills against highly mobile enemies who dart around quickly. Additionally, as their durability relies on the Passive's defense increase, the advantage is greatly diminished if enemies build defense penetration items or have true damage. After throwing the weapon by holding Skill 1, the tap effect cannot be used until it's picked up, creating a vulnerability.

synergy.reason: Because Lapool provides strong range CC and defense up, allowing Huangzhong to fire safely.
By Lapool knocking up the enemies all at once, Diao Chan can safely set up his camp and become unrivaled.

counters.reason: Because it has high mobility and can easily avoid Lapool's skills, it can also easily destroy tanks with guaranteed damage.
This is because it has guaranteed damage that ignores defense power and shields, making Lapool's durability meaningless.

passive.name: Passive Skill: Enhanced Shotgun
passive.description:
Fires 4 bullets with a shotgun. The two middle bullets deal 87 (50% of Physical Attack) physical damage to enemy heroes, and the outer bullets deal 52 (30% of Physical Attack) physical damage to enemy heroes. Gain 1 stack of Combat Instinct for each bullet that hits an enemy hero, each stack increasing Physical Pierce by 5-10 for 6 seconds (up to a maximum of 12 stacks).
Only the middle two bullets can trigger critical hits and equipment effects. Towers are not affected by additional effects from bullets.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Double continuous attack
skill1.description: Increases Movement Speed by 40% for 1.5 seconds, and at the same time, the next normal attack will be a double attack. Each bullet deals 173 (130 + 25% of Physical Attack) physical damage.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Leading by example
skill2.description:
Charges and knocks back the enemy, dealing 300 (300 + 100% of additional Physical Attack) physical damage and stopping.
When you come into contact with an enemy hero, the cooldown of "Initiative" is reduced by 2 seconds.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Guidance of Great Virtue
skill3.description:
Removes CC applied to self and disables CC for 3 seconds. At the same time, your Physical Attack increases by 100 for 6 seconds and you gain a shield of 1000 (1000 + 18% of additional HP).
While the shield is present, convert 60% of damage dealt to enemy heroes and 20% of damage dealt to minions and monsters into shield value.
The upper limit of the shield will be 150% of the initial value.

strategy.earlyGame: Take advantage of the Passive's shotgun characteristic by getting as close to the enemy as possible to hit them with all 4 bullets for maximum damage. Every time a bullet hits an enemy hero, physical armor penetration stacks up to 12 times, making consecutive attacks at close range extremely powerful. Use Skill 1 to increase movement speed by 40% and make the next basic attack a double shot, instantly unleashing burst damage on jungle monsters or enemy heroes.
strategy.midGame: Utilize the dash and knockback of Skill 2 for ambushes. Since hitting an enemy hero with the dash reduces Skill 2's cooldown by 2 seconds, aggressively collide with enemies to disrupt their actions while creating a point-blank distance. Rapidly approach with the movement speed boost from Skill 1, block their escape by knocking them back with Skill 2, and adopt an aggressive playstyle to instantly melt enemies using the double-shot enhanced basic attack at point-blank range and the Passive's penetration stacks.
strategy.lateGame: The existence of Skill 3 is your lifeline. Upon casting, it instantly removes any CC on yourself and makes you completely immune to CC for 3 seconds. Simultaneously, you gain a massive shield and a physical attack boost, and can continuously convert 60% of the damage dealt to enemy heroes into shield value. This allows you to rampage on the frontline, demonstrating unmatched durability by continuously dealing damage while ignoring enemy control skills.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, to maximize the CC immunity and shield conversion effects of Skill 3, activate it the moment enemy CC skills fly in to cleanse and negate them, and carve your way deep into the enemy formation. Knock back troublesome enemy heroes with Skill 2 to catch them, deal damage with Skill 1's double shot and basic attacks at point-blank range, and collapse the enemy formation while recovering your own shield.
strategy.commonMistakes: The biggest mistake is trying to trade blows from a distance, as the damage from the Passive's shotgun drops drastically when farther away since the outer bullets won't hit. Also, it's common to waste the powerful Skill 3 at the start of a fight when the shield is full, only to be unable to cleanse when hit by a fatal enemy CC later, resulting in death. Failing to hit enemy heroes with Skill 2's dash and losing the CD reduction effect also leads to a drop in burst damage.
strategy.strengths: The greatest strength is the overwhelming close-quarters combat capability derived from the burst damage when at point-blank range and the physical armor penetration stacks. Moreover, with the CC cleanse and 3-second CC immunity from Skill 3, combined with the effect of converting damage dealt into a shield, you possess astonishing tenacity to forcefully continue attacking while ignoring enemy disruption. The jungle clear speed is also very fast, providing a kit that easily snowballs from the early game.
strategy.weaknesses: Since the means of attack relies on basic attacks (buckshot) and requires being completely point-blank to deal damage, if continually kited by movement speed reductions or skills that prevent approaches, you can't do anything. The shield from Skill 3 recovers by dealing damage, but if the shield is broken and CC immunity drops, or if subjected to effects like blind, you will instantly expose your fragility and be killed. Lacking mobility skills to cross walls is also a weakness.

synergy.reason: To attract enemies and make it easier for Liu Bei to hit close-range shot damage.
This is because he can immobilize the enemy with powerful restraint skills and create an opening for Liu Bei to unleash instant firepower.

counters.reason: This is because he can block Liu Bei's approach and shut him down with his abundant movement-obstructing skills.
Because of his high mobility and poke ability, he can dodge Liu Bei's attacks while dealing unilateral damage.
