passive.description: Madness increases by 3 for each normal attack before transformation. When the skill is activated after transformation, the surrounding ground will shake and the movement speed of nearby enemies will be reduced by 25% for 2 seconds.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Forbidden Area
skill1.description:
Mowing down enemies with a snake spear, dealing 600 (600 + 150% of additional physical attack) physical damage to enemies within range, and knocking back enemy heroes hit.
Hitting an enemy unit increases Madness by 3.

forms.description:
Mowing down enemies with a snake spear, dealing 600 (600 + 150% of additional physical attack) physical damage to enemies within range, and knocking back enemy heroes hit.
Hitting an enemy unit increases Madness by 3.
Slams the ground hard, dealing 741 (600 + 150% of additional physical attack + 4% of maximum HP) physical damage to enemies within range. Knocks up enemies in the center of the area for 0.5 seconds.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Demon protection
skill2.description:
Jumps to the specified location and activates the Machine Seal upon landing, granting a 650 (650 + 13% of additional HP) shield to allied heroes within range.
Activating the skill increases Madness by 3, and increases Madness by 1 for each shield given to an ally hero.

forms.description:
Jumps to the specified location and activates the Machine Seal upon landing, granting a 650 (650 + 13% of additional HP) shield to allied heroes within range.
Activating the skill increases Madness by 3, and increases Madness by 1 for each shield given to an ally hero.

Jumps to the specified location and stomps on enemies within range, dealing 621 (480 + 120% of additional physical attack + 4% of maximum HP) physical damage. Knocks up enemies in the center of the area for 0.5 seconds.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Beast Blood
skill3.description:
Roars in the specified direction and creates a storm, gaining a shield equal to 40% of maximum HP for 15 seconds. While roaring, nullifies CC for 1.25 seconds and becomes invincible. The storm blows enemies away, dealing 600 (600 + 150% of additional physical attack) physical damage and stunning enemies at the edge of the storm for 1.5 seconds. Movement speed increases by 50% while in a storm.
Demon mode: When in demon mode, deals an additional 220 (220 + 100% of additional physical attack) damage when attacking normally. When demon mode ends, the skill effect of "Forbidden Area" is automatically activated.

forms.description:
Roars in the specified direction and creates a storm, gaining a shield equal to 40% of maximum HP for 15 seconds. While roaring, nullifies CC for 1.25 seconds and becomes invincible. The storm blows enemies away, dealing 600 (600 + 150% of additional physical attack) physical damage and stunning enemies at the edge of the storm for 1.5 seconds. Movement speed increases by 50% while in a storm.
Demon mode: When in demon mode, deals an additional 220 (220 + 100% of additional physical attack) damage when attacking normally. When demon mode ends, the skill effect of "Forbidden Area" is automatically activated.

Roars in the specified direction and creates a storm, gaining a shield equal to 40% of maximum HP for 15 seconds. While roaring, nullifies CC for 1.25 seconds and becomes invincible. The storm blows enemies away, dealing 600 (600 + 150% of additional physical attack) physical damage and stunning enemies at the edge of the storm for 1.5 seconds. Movement speed increases by 50% while in a storm.
Demon Mode: When in Demon Mode, deals an additional 220 (220 + 100% of additional physical attack) damage when attacking normally. When demon mode ends, the skill effect of "Forbidden Area" is automatically activated.

strategy.earlyGame: The top priority is to quickly build up the 'Madness' gauge required to cast Transformation (Skill 3). Since every basic attack generates 3 Madness, actively attack minions and monsters. Knock back enemy heroes with the sweep of Skill 1, earning Madness based on the number of enemies hit. When an ally is about to take damage, jump to a designated location with Skill 2, providing a shield to allies in the area to efficiently build Madness.
strategy.midGame: Once Madness is fully charged, aim to initiate teamfights using the powerful Skill 3. Casting Skill 3 unleashes a roar, blowing enemies away and stunning those at the end of the trajectory for 1.5 seconds. During this roar, you are invincible and CC immune for 1.25 seconds, and gain a massive shield equal to 40% of max HP, enabling forceful dives under enemy towers or fierce counterattacks from disadvantageous situations.
strategy.lateGame: Utilize the high disruption capability and burst damage in Demon Mode (after casting Skill 3). Basic attacks during Demon Mode deal additional physical damage, and casting skills adds a Passive effect that shakes the ground, reducing enemy movement speed by 25% for 2 seconds. Position yourself on the frontline using the massive shield, and dash along the roar's trajectory with a 50% movement speed increase, disrupting the enemy formation and creating attack opportunities for allies.
strategy.teamfight: Support allies by providing shields (Skill 2) while looking for an opportunity, and unleash Skill 3's roar the moment enemies group up or allies are attacked. Ideally, split the enemy formation and neutralize multiple enemies with the stun effect at the end. After transforming, act as a frontline tank absorbing damage with massive durability, and stick to the enemy carries to severely restrict their actions by scattering enhanced basic attacks and movement speed reduction effects.
strategy.commonMistakes: The biggest mistake is failing to contribute anything right before a teamfight because Madness is lacking, as the most powerful Skill 3 cannot be cast at all without the gauge filled. Also, aiming the roar of Skill 3 in the wrong direction can save a low-HP enemy an ally was trying to kill, or fatally blast an enemy assassin right in front of your backline, assisting in their kill.
strategy.strengths: The overall team and self-durability are exceptionally high due to the massive shield equal to 40% of max HP from Skill 3, and the ability to grant shields to allies with Skill 2. The wide-area knockback and stun of Skill 3 have the power to instantly turn the tide, adaptable from engaging to disengaging in combat. While transformed, burst damage and disruption capabilities also increase, projecting a presence far beyond a simple wall.
strategy.weaknesses: Since all strengths rely on 'charging Madness to transform with Skill 3,' you are merely an entity that hands out shields until Madness is built. If enemies continuously force fights without giving you the opening to charge Madness, you will be overwhelmed without being able to use Skill 3. Furthermore, after transforming, the effect of Skill 2 granting shields to allies is lost, reducing your support capability to directly protect teammates.

synergy.reason: Because of Zhang Fei's shield and movement obstruction, he can unilaterally unleash high firepower from the safe rear.
By suppressing the enemy on the front lines, Zhang Fei can create an environment where he can safely aim for burst damage.

counters.reason: Due to its high mobility and guaranteed damage, it can nullify Zhang Fei's shield and durability.
Because it has guaranteed damage, it can ignore Zhang Fei's thick shield and deal great damage.

passive.name: Passive skill: Legend of the Five Soldiers
passive.description:
Fight by switching between the five soldiers (armor, spear, sword, sword, and bow). Each hit with a normal attack restores 20 HP (20 + 0.3% of additional HP) and switches to the next weapon with longer range. Gain 1 stack of Fighting Spirit for each normal attack/skill hit (duration: 4 seconds, maximum of 8 stacks). Each stack of fighting spirit increases attack speed by 4% (possible to break attack speed limit).
When the weapon is a spear and spear, it is in protection mode, when it is a halberd and a sword, it is in attack mode, and when it is a bow, it is in super killing mode.
The effects of Skills 1 and 2 change in each mode, and when you switch to Super Kill mode, the CD of Skills 1 and 2 will be reduced by 50%.

skill1.name: Kusengi / Split Tenbow
skill1.description: Guardian mode: Move in the specified direction while swinging a weapon, dealing 200 (200 + 65% of additional physical attack) physical damage to enemies along the route and knocking them back (no CC) to the final destination. It then stabs forward, dealing 200 (200 + 65% of additional physical attack) physical damage and knocking you up for 0.75 seconds.

forms.description: Guardian mode: Move in the specified direction while swinging a weapon, dealing 200 (200 + 65% of additional physical attack) physical damage to enemies along the route and knocking them back (no CC) to the final destination. It then stabs forward, dealing 200 (200 + 65% of additional physical attack) physical damage and knocking you up for 0.75 seconds.

Fires a weapon in the specified direction, dealing 225 (225 + 75% of physical attack) physical damage to enemies along the route and knocking them back (no CC) to the destination. Upon reaching the final destination, it will hit the ground, dealing 225 (225 + 75% of physical attack) physical damage and knocking you up for 0.75 seconds.

Jump kicks in the specified direction and fires a piercing arrow, dealing 250 (250 + 85% of physical attack) physical damage to the enemy and knocking them back for 0.75 seconds. When the target hits a wall, it binds for 2 seconds and deals 250 (250 + 85% of physical attack) physical damage.

rows.label: Damage when colliding with a wall

skill2.name: Yaarageki / Gun Seiya
skill2.description: Guardian mode: Pierce in the specified direction, dealing 450 (450 + 150% of additional physical attack) physical damage and knocking back (no CC). Restores 180 HP (180 + 3% of additional HP) for each target hit (halved for non-hero units).

forms.description: Guardian mode: Pierce in the specified direction, dealing 450 (450 + 150% of additional physical attack) physical damage and knocking back (no CC). Restores 180 HP (180 + 3% of additional HP) for each target hit (halved for non-hero units).

Mows down in the specified direction, dealing 475 (475 + 160% of physical attack) physical damage and knocking back (no CC). Restores 180 (180 + 3% of max HP) maximum HP for each target hit (halved for non-hero units).

Lock on to all enemy units within range in the specified direction (up to 5 units, priority given to heroes). He then fires an arrow at all locked-on units, dealing 500 (500 + 170% of physical attack) physical damage and 10% of lost maximum HP as true damage, knocking them back (no CC), and recovering 180 (180 + 3% of maximum HP) HP for each target hit (halved for non-hero units).
While active, you will enter a super armor state and reduce damage taken by 15%.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Soldier's Second Coming
skill3.description:
Frees all five soldiers and greatly increases movement speed and attack speed by 35% for 2 seconds. Additionally, the upper limit on fighting spirit will be removed for 6 seconds.
While active, attack the enemy using five soldiers, dealing an additional 36 (36 + 12% of additional physical attack) physical damage with each normal attack.
While the skill is active, you can attack normally while moving, and you can maintain it by entering super kill mode during that time.

strategy.earlyGame: Fight while keeping the Passive's five-weapon switching system in mind. Capitalize on the trait where every basic attack hit recovers your own HP and gradually switches to weapons with longer range, maintaining sustain during the laning phase. Landing attacks and skills builds Fighting Spirit stacks to increase attack speed, so maintain stacks by attacking minions, and fight advantageously using Skill 1's dash and knock-up in Guard Mode, and Skill 2's thrust and healing.
strategy.midGame: Understand the modes (Guard, Combat, Overkill) that change as weapons switch. Particularly, entering 'Overkill Mode' with the fifth weapon, the bow, reduces the cooldowns of Skills 1 and 2 by 50%, allowing you to frequently cycle skills from a distance to output damage. When a fight is imminent, attack minions in advance to build Fighting Spirit stacks and advance the weapon stages, creating an advantage in range and attack speed before engaging.
strategy.lateGame: The timing of Skill 3 (Ultimate) dictates everything. Upon casting, not only do you gain a massive 2-second movement speed boost and 35% attack speed increase, but for 6 seconds the Fighting Spirit stack limit is removed, and basic attacks deal additional damage. Moreover, since you can basic attack while moving, dodge enemy attacks while unleashing an overwhelming barrage of long-range attacks, acting as the ultimate carry capable of single-handedly collapsing the enemy team.
strategy.teamfight: Increase weapon range with basic attacks and initiate attacks from the safety of the backline. Cast Skill 3 the moment key enemies step forward or allies initiate the fight. While infinitely stacking Fighting Spirit, kite to deny enemy approaches, and utilize the shortened CDs of Overkill Mode to lay down a barrage of skills and basic attacks. If your HP drops, use the multi-hit healing of Skill 2 to sustain and stay on the frontline.
strategy.commonMistakes: A fatal mistake is not knowing which weapon you currently hold, diving into the enemy while in the melee Guard Mode thinking you are ranged, or conversely getting defenselessly close while in the ranged Overkill Mode. Also, failing to build any Fighting Spirit stacks before casting Skill 3, leading to the effect expiring before attack speed maxes out, or standing still to trade hits without utilizing the strength of attacking while moving, wastes their potential.
strategy.strengths: The greatest strength is the ability to 'basic attack while moving' when Skill 3 is active, and the overwhelming sustained burst damage from the uncapped attack speed increase. Kiting capabilities are exceptionally high, displaying unmatched strength against melee heroes. Also, possessing HP recovery means via basic attacks and Skill 2 grants high survivability, and the longer the fight drags on, the more Fighting Spirit stacks, growing into an unstoppable damage dealer.
strategy.weaknesses: Since you cannot freely switch weapons and can only advance stages by landing basic attacks, you are vulnerable to being counterattacked due to short range at the very start of a fight (especially when the weapon is short). Moreover, while the powerful buff from Skill 3 is down, mobility and burst damage drop significantly, leaving you helpless if an enemy assassin instantly closes the gap. Complex Passive management and high operational precision to keep moving while attacking are constantly required.

synergy.reason: Since it absorbs damage on the front lines, Chicha, who is vulnerable to damage, can safely switch weapons and fight around.
Because it gathers enemies in one place, you can deal damage all at once with skills in area attack mode.

counters.reason: Because it has high durability and interference skills, it is easy to be defeated by damage trade.
Because it approaches rapidly with high mobility, you will be defeated before you can prepare your stance.

passive.name: Passive skill: Spy ears
passive.description:
Obtain traces of enemy heroes by eavesdropping and gain 1 second of enemy visibility (activates once every 6 seconds for the same unit).
It can also see through monsters' weaknesses, and deals an additional 7.5~15% physical damage to monsters with every attack.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Infiltration operation
skill1.description:
Applies 1 stack of Dark Mark to the target when attacking normally (up to 4 stacks). Explodes after 4 seconds, dealing 230 (230 + 75% of additional physical attack) physical damage to enemies within range. Each stack of engraving deals an additional 230 (230 + 75% of additional physical attack) physical damage when it explodes, and when it reaches 4 stacks, it explodes immediately.
Activating the skill increases attack speed by 35% and attack range by 75 for 4 seconds.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Bladeton
skill2.description: Throws a chakram in the specified direction, moves like an illusion to the destination and catches it (cannot be targeted while moving). Chakram deals 260 (260 + 80% of additional physical attack) physical damage to enemies and reduces movement speed by 15% for 2 seconds. The ground the Royal Spy passes through continues to burn for 2.9 seconds, dealing 110 (110 + 30% of additional physical attack) physical damage to enemies along the route every 0.25 seconds and reducing movement speed by 15%.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Infinite Tornado
skill3.description:
Throws a chakram at the specified location, dealing 320 (320 + 100% of additional physical attack) physical damage to enemies within the range of the tornado created by the rotation of the chakram. The tornado deals 140 (140 + 40% of additional physical attack) physical damage to enemies within the area every 0.5 seconds and reduces movement speed by 50% for 5.4 seconds.
Damage to enemies inside the tornado is doubled. Even damage from tornadoes can stack Dark Marks.

strategy.earlyGame: Since the Passive allows you to deal constant additional damage to monsters, your jungle and neutral monster clear speed is exceptionally high, gaining an early farm advantage. Apply Skill 1 to enemy heroes to increase attack speed and range, and deal massive damage by detonating it instantly after landing 4 basic attacks. The Passive's eavesdrop effect grants 1 second of vision on unseen enemy heroes, allowing you to prevent ambushes and position safely.
strategy.midGame: Utilize your high objective-securing ability to bring advantageous buffs to the team. The mark damage from Skill 1 is also effective on towers, so once you force enemies out of lane, quickly destroy the tower to expand map control. Since you become untargetable during Skill 2's dash, use it as a counter to dodge powerful CC or fatal damage skills from the enemy, and use the remaining burning area to restrict enemy movement and give chase.
strategy.lateGame: Deploy Skill 3's massive tornado at a designated location to control the teamfight area. It deals powerful continuous damage and a 50% movement speed reduction to enemies within range, with damage doubled for those in the center. Since the tornado's damage also builds Skill 1's mark stacks, applying Skill 1 to an enemy inside the tornado will instantly trigger a 4-stack detonation. Preserve Skill 2's dash to ensure survival against enemy assaults.
strategy.teamfight: Place Skill 3's tornado where enemies group up or on their retreat paths to split the formation, scattering movement speed reductions and massive damage over a wide area. Apply Skill 1 to the highest priority enemy carry or tank, and aim for a reliable kill by quickly detonating the 4 stacks using the tornado's damage and your own basic attacks. Against enemy approaches or fatal attacks, use the untargetable dash of Skill 2 to dodge precisely, and continue outputting high sustained damage from a safe distance.
strategy.commonMistakes: Skill 2 is an excellent evasion skill, but the most common mistake is using it carelessly just to approach or damage enemies, only to be killed unable to dodge the fatal enemy skills that follow immediately after. Furthermore, failing to land 4 basic attacks after applying Skill 1's mark, allowing the enemy to escape without dealing the maximum detonation damage, is another common failure leading directly to insufficient burst damage.
strategy.strengths: The greatest strength is having one of the fastest objective (jungle, dragon, tower) clear speeds among all heroes, thanks to the extra monster damage from the Passive and the mark detonation of Skill 1. Also, despite being a marksman, they possess a powerful self-defense tool in the untargetable movement of Skill 2, making them highly survivable combined with the vision-granting ability of the Passive. Possesses wide-area zoning capability with Skill 3, boasting excellent performance in both offense and defense.
strategy.weaknesses: Since a large portion of damage relies on the detonation damage of Skill 1, having the mark cleansed by Purify (CC cleanse spell) or specific hero debuff cleansing skills drastically reduces burst damage. Also, compared to other pure basic-attack-focused marksmen, sustained damage solely from basic attacks in the very late game is somewhat inferior. The vulnerability after consuming Skill 2 is large, and being targeted by multiple enemies there makes them very fragile.

synergy.reason: This is because you can restrain the enemy with a wealth of movement obstructions and ensure that Fang's skill damage hits in full.
It protects you while controlling your enemies, giving you a safe amount of time to build up your bomb stacks.

counters.reason: This is because he can block Fang's mobility with powerful continuous CC and stop him from moving before he can do all the damage.
Skill 2 can remove Fang's bomb mark, which negates the deadly burst damage.

synergy.reason: This is because it can stop the enemy with a powerful movement blocker and create an opening for Gubijin's long-range pokes and ults to reliably hit.
This allows you to securely protect yourself from enemy assassin dives and buy time to safely output damage.

counters.reason: She is a marksman whose normal attacks deal magic damage, and Yuubito's physical attack nullification skills have no meaning at all.
With high mobility and continuous attacks that deal guaranteed/magic damage, you can ignore physical immunity and defeat them unilaterally.

synergy.reason: This is because Shoki can instantly destroy enemies that he has drawn in with his hook using overwhelming firepower.
This is because it is easy to inflict high-power burst damage on the enemy you have drawn and instantly kill them.

counters.reason: Due to its high sustain ability and interference resistance, it is easy to escape from Shoki's control, and on the contrary, it is counterattacked.
While the ultimate is activated, it is very hard, and if you pull it back, there is a risk that your allies will receive great damage.

passive.name: beautiful melody
passive.description:
"Breaking Line": When the skill is activated, deals 89 (50 + 13% of magic attack) magic damage in an 800 range to nearby enemies. Damage dealt to minions is increased by 100%.
"Kiyohei": When the skill is activated, recovers 56 (35 + 7% of magic attack) (+ 0.5% of maximum HP) HP of nearby ally heroes (including yourself) within 800 range.
You can switch the effect of "Beautiful Melody".

skill1.name: The song of the camouflage
skill1.description:
Shoots 3 notes in the specified direction. The note tracks nearby enemies, dealing 250 (250 + 30% of magic attack) magic damage and reducing movement speed by 50% (duration: 1 second).
After activation, the next normal attack will be strengthened and deal an additional 130 (130 + 15% of magic attack) magic damage, and the CD of all active skills will be reduced by 1 second.

skill2.name: Hu whirlwind
skill2.description: Emits sound waves around you, reducing enemy movement speed by 30% for 1.5 seconds. The user's movement speed increases by 30% (decreases continuously), and after 1.5 seconds, enemies within the range of the sound wave are dealt 250 (250 + 30% of magic attack) magic damage and are stunned for 0.75 seconds.

skill3.name: Long grudge song
skill3.description:
Soar up into the sky and start playing. During this time, you will not be able to target and your own CC effect will be removed.
After the performance, recovers 750 (750 + 65% of magic attack) (+7.5% of maximum HP) HP, recovers the HP of nearby allies, and deals 500 (500 + 75% of magic attack) magic damage to nearby enemies.

skill4.name: Beautiful melody/switching
skill4.description:
Change the effect of passive skills.
Current: When you activate a skill, it deals damage to nearby enemies (breaks). / When you activate the skill, recover the HP of nearby allies (Keihei).

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, it is important for Yuhuan to quickly clear minions by utilizing the 100% increased damage to minions from her Passive in the Dissonant stance. Use Skill 1 to deal damage and slow enemies with notes, and hit them with the enhanced basic attack to reduce skill cooldowns and take control of the lane. If allies take damage, switch to the Harmonious stance to support them with healing, developing a stable laning phase as your basic early game strategy.
strategy.midGame: In the mid game, positioning utilizing Skill 2 is required. Increase your movement speed while slowing enemies, aiming for the stun after 1.5 seconds to assist allies in securing kills. As small-scale teamfights increase, the flexibility to switch between AoE healing with the Harmonious stance and damage with the Dissonant stance according to the situation is crucial. Also, always be mindful of the CD reduction from Skill 1's enhanced basic attack, contributing to fights while maintaining a high skill rotation rate.
strategy.lateGame: In the late game, the invincibility frame, wide-area healing, and damage of Skill 3 become highly powerful weapons. Your greatest role is to avoid the enemy's strong CC and burst damage with Skill 3 while significantly healing surrounding allies to maintain the frontline. Additionally, using the stun from Skill 2 to catch the enemy carry or protect allies is also important. Always stick with your allies, and use Passive switching and CD reduction from Skill 1 to lead your team to victory in all-out teamfights.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, switching Passives according to the situation and the timing of Skill 3 will determine victory or defeat. When engaging, restrict enemy movement with Skill 2 and aim for the stun. As ally HP decreases, heal them with the Harmonious stance, and the moment strong enemy attacks or CC fly in, use Skill 3 to become untargetable, cleansing CC while healing allies. Continuously fire Skill 1 and keep reducing CDs with enhanced basic attacks to provide sustained support and disruption.
strategy.commonMistakes: A common mistake is forgetting to switch Passive stances. Remaining in the Harmonious stance when you should be attacking, or staying in the Dissonant stance when allies are in a pinch, prevents you from utilizing her strengths. Also, wasting Skill 3 simply as a damage source means you cannot avoid fatal enemy attacks or CC, leading to being easily killed in teamfights. Missing out on the CD reduction benefit by not weaving in the enhanced basic attack after Skill 1 should also be avoided.
strategy.strengths: Her greatest strength is the balance of damage and healing through Passive switching, as well as her strong self-defense and ally support capabilities with Skill 3. While Skill 3 is active, she becomes untargetable and cleanses CC, giving her very high survivability and the ability to make enemy skills whiff. Additionally, the CD reduction effect of Skill 1 ensures a high skill rotation rate, and the versatility of Skill 2 for chasing and escaping with its movement speed boost and stun is also a powerful advantage.
strategy.weaknesses: While she can balance healing and damage, her instantaneous burst damage is modest, and she lacks the ability to quickly burst down enemies alone. Also, since there is a 1.5-second delay before Skill 2's stun activates, it is easily dodged by highly mobile enemies and is somewhat unreliable as a guaranteed CC. Her survivability while Skill 3 is on cooldown relies on the movement speed boost from Skill 2, so she has the weakness of being very fragile if approached by an enemy assassin without Skill 3 available.

synergy.reason: This is because it is a tank that can endure for a long time on the front lines, and can take full advantage of Yang Yuhuan's continuous recovery and damage support.
They are strong in long-term battles where they buff each other's recovery and movement speed, raising the durability and mobility of the entire team.

counters.reason: Due to their overwhelming mobility, they were able to quickly close the distance, and Yang Yuhuan was defeated before she could recover her allies.
This is because he can safely inflict fatal injuries with ultra-long range sniping from outside of Yang Yuhuan's attack/recovery range.

passive.name: Passive Skill: Hunter
passive.description:
While hiding in the bushes, your movement speed increases by 10-20% and charges up your stealth value (maximum value is reached in 1 second). When you come out of the bushes, you spend your latency and jump once. Jump distance increases as the latency increases.
If you enter the bushes, your next normal attack will be a 2-shot attack (considered as 1 normal attack), dealing 175 (70 + 60% of physical attack) physical damage per arrow (cooldown: 4 seconds).
While in the bushes, damage to monsters is increased by 25%.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Time of Soaring
skill1.description:
Jump up into the air. While active, your movement speed increases and you can jump over terrain.
A normal attack in the air releases a powerful long-range arrow that deals 475 (230 + 140% of physical attack) physical damage, knocks the enemy back (no CC), and increases the time spent in the air.
Arrow damage increases by 2 for every 1% increase in attack speed.
If you do not shoot an arrow in the air, stack enhanced basic attacks with normal range for 3 seconds after landing.
When shooting an arrow in the air, if you move the direction controller in the opposite direction to the target, the arrow will move a certain distance in the input direction after shooting.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Wolf Soul Trap
skill2.description:
Throws a wolf soul trap at the specified location. When an enemy comes into contact with it, it gains vision and deals 380 (380 + 90% of additional physical attack) physical damage, summons a wolf soul to that location, and automatically attacks the enemy it comes into contact with.
Untriggered traps become stealthed 2 seconds after being set. Up to 4 traps can be set at the same time.
The Wolf Spirit inherits 25% of the Blue Wolf Descendant's basic stats, and has a 50% critical hit effect. Wolf Spirit reduces the target's movement speed by 5%-10% for 1 second with each attack (maximum of 5 stacks).

skill3.name: Skill 3: Leader's Will
skill3.description:
Summons wolf souls (2 + number of stacks of skill 2) in the specified direction and charges them, consuming stacks of skill 2 to activate surrounding traps.
Gains cavalry ability (can perform normal attacks while moving), increases movement speed by 10%, physical attack by 50, and attack speed by 20% for 8 seconds.
During normal attacks, wolf souls without targets attack together.

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game for Cang, utilizing bushes with his Passive is essential. Entering a bush makes his basic attack fire two consecutive shots and increases damage to monsters by 25%, allowing him to gain an advantage in jungle clearing and lane harassment. Place Skill 2 traps in bushes and enemy pathing to secure vision, guard against ganks, and apply pressure with continuous damage and slow from the wolf spirit. It is basic practice to save Skill 1, which allows terrain crossing, for escaping or chasing.
strategy.midGame: In the mid game, it is important to utilize the strength of placing up to four Skill 2 traps to control vision around dragons and inside the enemy jungle. Since a wolf spirit is summoned to slow enemies who step on the trap, you can develop small-scale fights advantageously. Once you acquire the mounted archery ability from Skill 3, use your mobility to dodge enemy attacks while dealing continuous damage. Making full use of jumping from bushes and crossing terrain with Skill 1 to take enemies by surprise is required.
strategy.lateGame: In the late game, the mounted archery capability from Skill 3 with significantly increased firepower is highly potent. Because you can perform basic attacks while moving, you can dodge enemy skills while consistently pumping out damage from a safe position. The knockback attack from the air with Skill 1 is extremely important as a self-defense measure to protect against enemy assassins. Deploy traps on the battlefield to secure vision and damage zones, and fully unleash your firepower while confusing the enemy with high mobility involving bush utilization from your Passive.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, it is important to scatter Skill 2 traps across the battlefield beforehand to secure vision and keep enemies in check. After combat begins, activate Skill 3 and reliably whittle down the enemy frontline with basic attacks while moving. If an enemy threat approaches, jump into the air and cross terrain with Skill 1, or create distance with the knockback from the mid-air basic attack. It is ideal to maintain a safe position by fully utilizing bush jumps and continuously outputting damage alongside the wolf spirit.
strategy.commonMistakes: Misjudging when to use Skill 1 leads to major mistakes. Jumping into the enemy formation in a defenseless state, or wasting it when it should be saved as a terrain-crossing escape skill, makes you a prime target for enemy assassins. Also, fighting standing still on flat ground without being mindful of the Passive's two consecutive shots or jumps utilizing bushes completely fails to draw out Cang's potential. Engaging in combat in areas without vision without placing Skill 2 traps should also be avoided.
strategy.strengths: His greatest strength is the mounted archery ability from Skill 3, which allows basic attacks while moving, and his overwhelming mobility from his Passive and Skill 1. Through bush jumps, jumping into the air and crossing terrain with Skill 1, as well as the knockback effect, he can continuously deal one-sided damage without allowing enemies to approach. Furthermore, the vision control and slow application of his Skill 2 traps are excellent advantages for both map control and self-defense.
strategy.weaknesses: While he excels in mobility and sustained firepower, he somewhat lacks instantaneous burst damage, so enemies may escape before their HP is fully depleted. Additionally, because much of his damage source relies on basic attacks and the wolf spirit, he is weak against enemies with items or skills that reduce attack speed. He is highly dependent on the player's mechanical skill; failing to properly execute mid-air movement with Skill 1, kiting with the mounted archery ability, and utilizing bushes will result in him being easily killed without demonstrating his true strengths.

synergy.reason: With his thick shield and powerful peel ability, he can create an environment where Ao, who is vulnerable to attacks, can attack safely while setting up traps.
This is because it can stop the enemy in its tracks with a variety of action hindrances, and support Ao with accurate long-range attacks and skills.

counters.reason: Because of surprise attacks from stealth and high burst damage, you will be instantly killed before you can use your mobility to escape.
In order to completely contain Ao's strength, Hit & Away, by using powerful targeted dives and movement obstruction.

passive.name: mental erosion
passive.description:
Hotenzochi: Resistance increases when you deal damage with normal attacks and skills (maximum 60% to 90%, increases according to skill level). When you reach the maximum value, you will receive super armor. When fighting a hero unit, it enters Hoten Shoji mode, knocking back surrounding enemies, enhancing its skills and range, and increasing its maximum HP by 600.
Celestial Eye: Gain 1 stack of energy (up to 3 stacks) when a skill hits. If the target is far from the Divine Successor, fire a laser that deals 80 (+25% of additional physical attack) physical damage and restores 60 (+1% of additional maximum HP) HP (halved for non-hero units).
Resistance that can be acquired other than "Hotenzochi" is up to 25%.

skill1.name: upside down
skill1.description:
Summons a Tenten dog to charge, dealing 200 (200 + 60% of additional physical attack) physical damage, imprinting a mark, and gaining vision of the enemy for 5 seconds. If you reactivate the skill while the effect is active, you will charge at the enemy and deal 300 (300 + 90% of additional physical attack) physical damage, and 15% of the target's missing HP in physical damage.
If it doesn't hit, the Renten dog stays at the destination and searches for the target. When God's successor approaches, Tentennu will return and reduce the remaining cooldown of "Heaven and Earth Reversal" by 25% to 50%.
If you die while an enemy is marked, the cooldown of "Twilight" will be reset.

skill2.name: Delusional destruction
skill2.description: Performs a mowing attack with a spear, dealing 350 (350 + 100% of additional physical attack) physical damage to enemies within range, reducing their movement speed by 15% for 2 seconds, and stunning them for 1 second (if the target's HP percentage exceeds yours, the stun time is doubled). If you hit an enemy, you will deal an additional 125 (125 + 36% of additional physical attack) true damage when using normal attacks for 5 seconds.

skill3.name: clairvoyant
skill3.description:
Fires a laser three times from the Celestial Eye, dealing 340 (340 + 110% of additional physical attack) physical damage to enemies along the route, and also recovers own HP by 300 (300 + 4.5% of additional maximum HP) (halved for non-hero units).
If multiple lasers hit at the same time, the damage will be 30% and the recovery amount will be 50%.

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, Yang Jian's damage trades utilizing Skill 2's sweep are potent. Because the stun duration is doubled when hitting Skill 2 while your HP percentage is lower than the enemy's, a strategy of intentionally taking a little damage before counterattacking is effective. Basic attacks after a Skill 2 hit apply true damage, ensuring reliable damage output. Utilize Skill 1 for long-range poke, securing vision, and as a finishing blow when the enemy's HP is low.
strategy.midGame: In the mid game, utilize the mobility and kill potential from Skill 1 to actively participate in ganks on other lanes and small-scale fights. Hitting minions or enemy heroes with Skill 1 and reactivating it to instantly close the distance is a powerful move. Since killing an enemy resets Skill 1's CD, aiming for consecutive kills is also possible. Because his Passive grants increased resistance and super armor by sustaining combat, he plays the role of disrupting the enemy formation while fighting tenaciously on the frontline.
strategy.lateGame: In the late game, he functions as a teamfight initiator or a diver targeting the enemy backline. Reliably stick to the enemy carry with Skill 1, and instantly apply pressure with Skill 2's AoE stun and true damage. Even in the late game where taken damage increases, he can exhibit high survivability through HP recovery from Skill 3's lasers, and the super armor and max HP increase from his Passive. Hitting multiple targets with Skill 3 right in the middle of the enemy formation and fighting persistently while healing is a crucial late-game strategy.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, diving into the center of the enemy is required to maximize the increased resistance and super armor from his Passive. The ideal combo is to mark the enemy backline with Skill 1 to dash in, then catch multiple targets in Skill 2 to stun them. When HP gets low, fire Skill 3's lasers in the direction where enemies are clustered to deal damage while significantly healing HP to survive. Combined with the pursuit and healing from the Heavenly Eye laser of his Passive, he functions as a formidable wall.
strategy.commonMistakes: A common mistake is missing the first phase of Skill 1, or blindly activating the second phase and ending up isolated deep in the enemy formation. Since Skill 1's CD resets when used for a finishing blow, abruptly using the second phase on an enemy with ample HP remaining prevents you from fully unleashing your firepower. Also, engaging in combat with full HP without being mindful of Skill 2's doubled stun effect means you cannot capitalize on your strength of prolonged CC, potentially resulting in unfavorable trades.
strategy.strengths: His strengths are his high 1v1 capability due to Skill 2's true damage and conditional long stun, and his excellent self-healing abilities from Skill 3 and his Passive's Heavenly Eye. The resistance and super armor granted by his Passive make him highly resistant to CC and grant very high survivability in teamfights, which is highly appealing. With Skill 1's missing HP percentage damage and CD reset upon kill, he also harbors high carry potential to chain-kill enemies one after another in chaotic brawls.
strategy.weaknesses: As for weaknesses, since his primary dash, Skill 1, is a directional skillshot, missing it drastically reduces his mobility and deprives him of a means to approach enemies. Also, because his healing ability relies on Skill 3's hit rate and number of targets, having it dodged by highly mobile enemies prevents him from tanking as expected. His survivability significantly drops if enemies buy anti-heal items, and managing the risk of intentionally lowering HP to aim for Skill 2's doubled stun is also a notable weakness.

synergy.reason: It is easy to match with burst damage and can be cut down to the kill line.
You can wear down enemies in teamfights and set up kills with dives.

counters.reason: It is kited with high mobility and CC, making it extremely difficult to hit with skills.
With its overwhelming mobility, it is able to control close combat and is left at its mercy.

passive.name: truth of heaven and earth
passive.description: Vision, normal attack, and skill range are increased by 10-20% (increases according to hero level).

skill1.name: Divine Light: Creation
skill1.description: Releases energy, dealing 225 (225 + 42% of magic attack) magic damage to enemies along the route and knocking them back. When the energy hits an enemy, it gradually stops, generates a matrix space on the spot, expands in a cross pattern, and deals 225 (225 + 42% of magic attack) magic damage to the enemy it hits. Deals 50% additional damage to enemies in the center of the cross. When the cross touches Matrix space, it causes an explosion.

skill2.name: Divine Light: Fusion
skill2.description: Creates a matrix space that lasts for 3.5 seconds. You can move at high speed, but enemy heroes cannot pass through. When another skill of the Supreme Creator comes into contact with space, it explodes and deals 210 (210 + 40% of magic attack) magic damage to nearby enemies. Matrix can be combined up to 2 times. When fused, enemies between the two matrices are dealt 50% of the explosion damage, knocked back for 0.5 seconds, and generate an additional explosion (based on the distance between the initial matrices, this explosion has up to 30% range and up to 50% additional damage). If multiple matrices explode at the same time, they will only damage the same target once. Up to three matrix spaces can be stacked.

skill3.name: Divine Light: Advent
skill3.description: Creates a rift and warps to the specified location. Generates a 600 (600 + 9% of additional maximum HP) shield and increases movement speed by 30% for 2 seconds. Simultaneously deals 300 (300 + 57% of magic attack) magic damage to surrounding enemies and reduces movement speed by 25-50% for 0.5 seconds. Damage dealt decreases depending on the enemy's distance (up to 50%). Passive: Skill activation speed is reduced according to magic attack status.

skill4.name: Divine light: destruction
skill4.description: Releases cleansing energy, dealing 600 (600 + 115% of magic attack) magic damage to enemies along the route. When cleansing energy comes into contact with matrix space, it causes an explosion.

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, Nuwa's fundamental approach is to utilize the expanded vision and range from her Passive to clear minions and poke enemies from a safe distance. Take control of the lane with the knockback and matrix deployment from Skill 1's energy blast. Placing Skill 2's matrix spaces in positions that restrict enemy movement, and hitting them with Skill 1 to trigger an explosion combo, is an effective way to rack up damage. Since her firepower is modest early on, avoid forced fights and safely level up.
strategy.midGame: From the mid game onwards, her vision and range expand further as she levels up, making it easier to grasp the entire map. By acquiring Skill 3's long-distance warp, sudden strikes on other lanes and covering allies become instantly possible. Warping to areas where teamfights are likely to erupt to deploy a shield, cutting off enemy escape routes with Skill 2's matrices, and disrupting formations with Skill 1 is a powerful sequence. Aim for matrix combination explosions to scatter AoE damage and CC, taking control of the battle situation.
strategy.lateGame: In the late game, Nuwa's range and vision reach their maximum, exhibiting overwhelming poke capability and board control. Simply placing Skill 2 from outside the enemy's vision and detonating it with Skill 1 can inflict fatal damage to the enemy backline. Skill 2's obstacles prevent enemies from approaching, boasting immense power in defensive battles and tower sieges. Skill 3's warp is useful for moving into enemy blind spots or securing escape routes. Act as a commander maneuvering the entire battlefield from the safety of the rear.
strategy.teamfight: Nuwa's role in teamfights is continuous damage output from long range and terrain control. Before combat starts, place multiple Skill 2 matrices to block enemy advance routes and divide the battlefield. Fire Skill 1 in sync with your allies' CC to trigger chain explosions of the matrices, dealing massive damage and knockbacks. If an enemy assassin approaches, knock them away with Skill 1, and if in danger, it is vital to warp to a safe location with Skill 3 to maintain distance.
strategy.commonMistakes: A common mistake for Nuwa is misjudging the warp destination of Skill 3, diving into the middle of the enemy group and becoming isolated. Although she gains a shield after warping, her inherent durability is low, making her easy to kill. Placing Skill 2 matrices in the path of allies, hindering their pursuit or escape, is also a fatal positioning error. Fighting defenselessly close to the enemy's attack range without utilizing the long range of her Passive is another typical mistake that must be avoided.
strategy.strengths: Her greatest strengths are her overwhelming vision and long range from her Passive, and her battlefield control capabilities through Skill 2's obstacle creation. She can unilaterally deal damage from a safe distance and break enemy formations. The AoE firepower from the chain explosions of Skill 2 and Skill 1 is tremendous, showing unparalleled strength in defensive battles. Furthermore, she possesses outstanding mobility and cover capability with Skill 3's long-distance warp, giving her the major advantage of being able to instantly intervene anywhere on the map.
strategy.weaknesses: While she excels in long range and control capabilities, she is extremely weak in close-quarters combat, making it difficult to cope if highly mobile enemy assassins slip into her personal space. Missing Skill 1's knockback leaves her with almost no means of self-defense. Also, due to the time lag from skill activation to impact, she is easily dodged by quick enemies, demanding high prediction accuracy to output damage consistently. Falling into a lack of firepower by mismanaging Skill 2's placement limit and cooldowns is also a weakness.

synergy.reason: Maintain a strong front line and create an environment where you can poke from a safe distance.
Use powerful CC to stop enemies and make it easier to hit them with long-range skill combos.

counters.reason: With its high mobility, it passes through walls and defenses and approaches the rear guard all at once.
With a sneak attack from stealth, you can easily take down Nuwa, who has low escape ability.

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, Nezha seizes lane control by leveraging his strength in close combat utilizing Skill 1. Skill 1 deals damage to the surroundings while applying true damage and anti-heal from Scorching Flame, granting a huge advantage in damage trades. Hitting skills grants a true damage shield and movement speed boost from his Passive, so the basic approach is to maneuver behind the enemy with Skill 2, adding more shields, and overwhelmingly pressuring the enemy through persistent fighting.
strategy.midGame: In the mid game, acquiring Skill 3's long-range dash allows him to intervene in combat from anywhere on the map. When fights break out in other lanes or encounters happen in the jungle, instantly target the enemy carry or an isolated foe and dive in. Upon landing, he gains damage reduction and increased resistance, allowing him to endure to some extent even in the center of the enemy formation. Scatter Scorching Flame on multiple enemies with Skill 1, applying anti-heal while securing a true damage shield to support the frontline.
strategy.lateGame: In the late game, his primary role is to reliably eliminate the enemy's key targets or force them out of the battlefield. Do not miss the timing when teamfights start or the moment the enemy carry becomes isolated, and dive in accurately with Skill 3. Since the Passive shield limit increases with bonus HP, continuously hitting skills on multiple targets unlocks incredible durability. Disrupt the enemy formation and gradually whittle down the entire enemy team with the sustained damage of Scorching Flame.
strategy.teamfight: Nezha's most crucial mission in teamfights is reliably diving the enemy backline with Skill 3. At the start of combat, dive toward the enemy's main damage source and disrupt their formation with the knockback. After landing, spam Skill 1 to apply Scorching Flame to as many enemies as possible, continually putting up Passive shields. Utilize Skill 2 to dodge enemy attacks while sticking closely to them, acting as a decoy to draw the enemy's attention and allow your allies to act freely.
strategy.commonMistakes: The biggest mistake is diving alone with Skill 3 at a timing or location where allies cannot follow up, resulting in isolation and a pointless death. Since the dash cannot be canceled mid-flight, misjudging the situation leaves you stranded deep inside enemy lines. Moreover, failing to maintain the Passive shield and Skill 1's Scorching Flame drastically drops his durability and firepower, making whiffing skills fatal. Forgetting the mechanic that hitting minions drastically reduces shield generation is also dangerous.
strategy.strengths: Nezha's strengths are his reliable long-range dive capability with his targeted Skill 3, and his overwhelming durability in chaotic brawls thanks to the true damage shield from his Passive. Being able to directly target the enemy backline from anywhere constantly pressures the enemy carry. Also, Skill 1's Scorching Flame applies true damage and anti-heal, making him extremely strong against heroes with high healing capabilities; his self-sufficient combat power, where continuously dealing damage recovers his shield, is highly appealing.
strategy.weaknesses: As a weakness, since Skill 3's dash has no point of return once activated, failing to kill the enemy or lacking ally backup ensures you lose your escape route. Shield generation requires hitting skills on enemy heroes as a prerequisite, so having skills dodged by highly mobile enemies makes his durability brittle. His instantaneous burst damage is low, relying heavily on sustained damage, making it hard to counter sudden burst firepower that shatters his shield and kills him instantly.

synergy.reason: With movement speed buffs and recovery, it strongly supports deep dives into the enemy's rear line.
In the melee after the dive, support the front line with a powerful shield and CC.

counters.reason: With Ult's powerful restraints, you can reliably stop a diver and strike back.
Continuous damage and sustain can negate dives in close combat.

passive.name: Passive Skill: Fiery Flame
passive.description:
Gain a true damage shield of 75 (75 + 1.5% of additional HP) for each enemy hero you damage with your skill. The upper limit of the shield is 800 (800 + 20% of additional HP). At the same time, activate Wind Fire Ring once, increasing movement speed by 5% for 3 seconds (up to 5 stacks).
True damage shields can be restored at the respawn point.
When dealing damage with a normal attack or scorching flame, or when the target is a non-hero unit, the true damage shield is 20 (20 + 0.5% of additional HP).
Wind Fire Ring can only be activated once per target.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Flame trident
skill1.description:
Deals 345 (345 + 105% of additional physical attack) physical damage to surrounding targets with a flame trident. At the same time, inflicts scorching flame, dealing 45 (45 + 20% of additional physical attack) true damage to the enemy every second for 5 seconds (up to 3 stacks. Each stack increases damage by 50%). Fiery flames reduce the target's HP recovery by 15%.
Dealing damage to a target resets the duration of the fiery flame.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Mixed Aya/Tanku
skill2.description: Roll behind the target, dealing 150 (150 + 65% of additional physical attack) physical damage and gaining a true damage shield of 24 (24 + 0.5% of additional HP). Halve for non-hero units. Cannot be used consecutively on the same target for 3 seconds.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Kenkon/Tenku
skill3.description:
Fly to the selected enemy hero's location, deal 450 (450 + 170% of additional physical attack) physical damage, and knock them back.
For 4 seconds after landing after flying, damage taken is reduced by 10% and resistance is increased by 20%.
CD will be between 50% and 100% depending on flight distance. If the selected target is in untargetable state, it will be knocked back after waiting until the untargetable state ends (maximum waiting time 0.5 seconds).

skill4.name: Skill 4: Kenkon sphere
skill4.description:
Releases the Kenkon Sphere, dealing 140 (140 + 45% of additional physical attack) physical damage to all enemy heroes on the map, gaining visibility, and allowing "Kenkon Tenku" to be activated against all enemy heroes on the map for 5 seconds.
Heroes who have gained vision will no longer be able to use Recall.

Flying sword skills share a maximum number of uses. When a flying sword hits an enemy, it can inflict sword energy on each enemy. If an enemy is inflicted with multiple sword powers at the same time, subsequent damage will be reduced to 50%. Normal attacks deal 300 (300 + 25% of magic attack) magic damage.

skill1.name: Kenzuka of protection

Enemies hit by Kenzuka have their [ICON_MAGIC_DEFENSE] reduced by 150 for 3 seconds. skill2.name: Male and female twin swords, close

skill3.name: Male and female twin swords, far

Inflicts an additional 75 (75 + 12% of magic attack) magic damage on enemies with sword energy. Flying sword skills have a shared usage limit, and will recover once every 5-6 seconds (reduced depending on skill level). If the same Kenki is activated multiple times at the same time, the damage of the latter will be reduced by 50%.

skill4.name: Kenrai
skill4.description: Instantly stack the flying sword skill twice, summoning more flying swords and strengthening the next flying sword. Passive: Increases the range of vision by 15% and increases the number of stackable "Female and Male Swords" by 1. Also, each time the flying sword hits an enemy, the cooldown of "Kenrai" is reduced by 1 second.

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, Ganjiang & Moye focus on safe lane clearing and poking using the range of their flying swords from Skill 2 and Skill 3. Since the Passive fires two flying swords simultaneously, you can aim for massive damage by accurately hitting minions or enemy heroes with the intersection of their trajectories. From the early game, utilize the bonus damage after applying sword energy to whittle down enemy HP and apply pressure. If approached, it is crucial to use Skill 1 to knock back the enemy while retreating yourself to maintain a safe distance.
strategy.midGame: In the mid game, rotating to other lanes and providing backline support in small-scale fights become your main roles. Accurately hitting the intersection of the flying swords begins to show the potential to shave off more than half the HP of squishy enemy heroes. Moreover, since Skill 1's Passive effect permanently increases magical attack with each kill or assist, it is important to actively participate in fights to build stacks. Increase your firepower with combos incorporating Skill 1's magical defense reduction effect.
strategy.lateGame: In the late game, Ganjiang & Moye's firepower reaches its zenith, possessing overwhelming poke damage capable of leaving the enemy backline near death or instantly killing them just by landing flying swords. With the bonus damage from the Passive and the stack effect from Skill 1, you can leverage your range advantage to collapse the enemy formation. Always secure a safe position behind your allies and relentlessly fire Skill 2 and Skill 3. Against enemy ambushes, executing self-defense accurately with Skill 1 is the top priority.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, never step out to the frontline; instead, function as an artillery piece continuously firing Skill 2 and Skill 3 from the furthest safe position. The ideal is to smash the intersection of the flying swords into the center of the enemy, synchronized with your allies' CC. Apply sword energy with the first flying sword, and trigger bonus damage with the subsequent ones to annihilate enemies. If an enemy assassin approaches, calmly activate Skill 1 to create distance with the knockback and retreat, then turn the tables by landing flying swords on the target with lowered magical defense.
strategy.commonMistakes: The biggest mistake is missing the intersection of the uniquely trajected flying swords. Failing to hit the intersection more than halves the damage, wasting the Passive's sword energy and causing a lack of firepower. Furthermore, wasting Skill 1, your sole means of self-defense, for movement or trivial damage leaves you with no escape when approached by an enemy assassin, leading to easy deaths. Carelessly stepping too far forward without being mindful of safe positioning is also a fatal mistake to avoid.
strategy.strengths: Ganjiang & Moye's strength is their overwhelming instantaneous burst magical damage unleashed from an ultra-long range. Their poke capability to instantly rob enemy HP from a position immune to counterattack by hitting the flying sword intersection is top-tier among all heroes. Due to the infinite stacking growth of magical attack from Skill 1's Passive, their firepower becomes increasingly uncontrollable the later the game goes. Additionally, the simultaneous knockback and retreat from Skill 1 serves as an exceptionally excellent self-defense mechanism for a Mage.
strategy.weaknesses: As a weakness, because the trajectories of the flying swords form a peculiar shape of intersecting curves, aiming difficulty is extremely high, and firepower heavily depends on the player's mechanical skill. Landing skills on fast-moving enemies is difficult. Moreover, they are utterly defenseless in close quarters while Skill 1 is on cooldown, and their low mobility means survival is hopeless once an enemy gets inside their guard. Without sustained damage sources, continually having skills dodged results in a remarkably low contribution in teamfights.

synergy.reason: Because it draws enemies to one place, it is easy to hit multiple people with skill combos.
Provides cooldown reduction and sustain, allowing for constant poke.

counters.reason: With its high mobility and burst damage, it can instantly close the distance and kill you instantly.
He is forcibly engaged with an ult from a distance and is captured before he can escape.

passive.name: True God Awakening
passive.description:
After death, he takes on the form of a true god. Gain 30 movement speed and support your allies with the Divine Protection skill.
When revived, move to the nearest friendly tower and deal 600 (600 + 180% of additional physical) physical damage to enemies within range of the starting point and knock them up for 0.75 seconds.

strategy.earlyGame: Athena's early game strength lies in her aggressive playstyle utilizing her high mobility and shields. Close the gap with the jump and enhanced basic attack charge from Skill 2, and turn damage trades in your favor with the shield deployment and knockback of Skill 1. Since Skill 2's CD resets when Skill 1 hits, you can unleash charges continuously. During jungle clears, the bonus damage of her enhanced basic attacks against non-heroes shines, enabling fast farming and proactive ganks.
strategy.midGame: In the mid game, she roams the battlefield utilizing her excellent pursuit capability and CC resistance in small-scale fights. By chaining the shields of Skill 1 or Skill 3 after approaching with Skill 2, she sticks to her targets without being hindered by enemies, reliably executing them with her missing HP percentage damage. Even if you happen to be killed, moving around in the True God state of her Passive allows you to secure vision for allies and grant them a movement speed buff; thus, you should prepare to resurrect in an optimal position while supporting your team even after death.
strategy.lateGame: In the late game, as enemy firepower increases, managing the shield of Skill 3 becomes highly important. In teamfights, dive into the frontline, bait out powerful enemy skills, and absorb them with Skill 3's shield; meanwhile, utilize the increased counterattack damage and shortened detonation time from taking damage to inflict massive AoE damage. Flawlessly cycling through the CD reset combos triggered by hitting Skill 2 and other skills is required to combine high durability and mobility while wreaking havoc on the enemy formation.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, Athena functions as a Fighter who dives into the enemy lines to disrupt their formation. Charge at the enemy carry with Skill 2, instantly deploy Skill 3 to absorb the enemy's focused fire and CC with the shield. If the counterattack triggers without the shield breaking, massive damage is dealt and Skill 2's CD is reset, allowing for further pursuit or disengagement. Even after death, fly around your allies in the True God state and re-intervene in combat with the knockup upon resurrection.
strategy.commonMistakes: Lack of precision in skill combos is the most common mistake. Failing to hit enemies with Skill 1 or Skill 3 means the CD for your primary Skill 2 won't reset, causing a drastic drop in mobility and firepower and leaving you isolated in enemy territory. Also, while Skill 3 is powerful, it renders you immobile; using it in situations where enemies can easily escape or where you take damage exceeding the shield and die before the counterattack is dangerous. Setting your resurrection position right in the middle of the enemy formation and getting spawn-killed is also a classic mistake.
strategy.strengths: Her greatest strengths are her overwhelming mobility through the continuous use of Skill 2, and her high anti-control performance utilizing the shields and CC immunity from Skills 1 and 3. She possesses the firepower to ignore enemy CC, stick to carries, and even melt Tanks with her percentage damage. Furthermore, her unique Passive, which allows her to be controlled and provide vision even after death, mitigates the penalty of dying; this tactical flexibility, letting her resurrect in any location and instantly return to combat with a knockup, is a huge advantage.
strategy.weaknesses: As for weaknesses, her mana consumption is extremely intense, and without proper mana management, she quickly runs out of gas. Since her durability relies on shields, she is highly vulnerable to firepower capable of breaking her shields or enemies with true damage. Because her design assumes skill CD resets, whiffing combos or being dodged by enemy mobility leaves her helpless. High mechanical difficulty, where her performance heavily hinges on the player's mechanics and judgment, is also a weakness.

synergy.reason: Create a kill chance with CC and peel that match the engagement.
Taking advantage of the opening Athena has opened, she can deal powerful follow-up damage.

counters.reason: Engagement can be easily hindered by mobility buffs and silence skills.
With his high mobility and forced movement skills, he can completely miss the target of skill combos.

skill2.name: Earworm

The wave can bounce up to 6 times, and up to 2 times on the same target. The second hit on the same target deals 25% damage and stun duration.

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, Cai Yan's main role is to stay close to the allied Marksman and support a stable laning phase. When taking damage from enemies, her Passive effect increases her movement speed and triggers auto-healing, so moving slightly forward to bait enemy attacks using this effect is also valid. The ricocheting sound wave from Skill 2 is a powerful poking tool that can travel through minions to stun enemy heroes, so aim to cast it precisely when minion waves meet to create favorable trades.
strategy.midGame: In small-scale teamfights from the mid game onwards, Cai Yan's healing abilities and CC truly shine. Use Skill 1 when ally HP drops, leveraging the movement speed buff to provide AoE healing for the entire team. Fire Skill 2 into areas where enemies are clustered, continuously stunning multiple targets to disrupt their coordination. If an enemy assassin dives your backline, it is crucial to immediately activate Skill 3 to vigorously heal the target's HP while raising their defense, preventing death beforehand.
strategy.lateGame: In the late game, enemy firepower becomes overwhelming, but Cai Yan's healing output also skyrockets with items. As the linchpin of teamfights, positioning to ensure you are never the first to fall is the highest priority. Stick constantly to the allied carry, and reliably protect them from enemy burst damage with the massive single-target heal and defense buff from Skill 3. Endlessly cycling through Skill 1's AoE heal and Skill 2's multi-stuns to drag enemies into a battle of attrition that breaks their spirit is the winning formula for the late game.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, position yourself in the backline and pay close attention to managing the HP of your entire team. As a signal to engage, fire Skill 2 into the enemy lines to stop their movement with chain stuns. The moment an enemy AoE attack hits your allies, use Skill 1 to heal the entire group, and do not hesitate to use Skill 3 on an ally who is focused and whose HP enters dangerous waters, raising their defense to save them. Utilize the movement speed buff from your Passive to escape enemy targeting, continuously maintaining the frontline as a walking fountain.
strategy.commonMistakes: A common mistake is hoarding healing skills until an ally's HP is critically low, resulting in the heal not landing in time and the ally dying. Also, despite her own durability not being very high, forcibly approaching an ally deep in enemy lines to heal them, only to get caught in the crossfire and killed, is a classic blunder. Using Skill 2 when an enemy is isolated, failing to fully utilize her greatest strength of chain stuns via ricochets, is also a playstyle to avoid.
strategy.strengths: Her greatest strength is her overwhelming healing capability that pulls allies back from the brink of death. Through Skill 1's AoE heal and Skill 3's massive single-target heal combined with a defense buff, she drastically enhances her team's sustained combat ability. Furthermore, Skill 2's sound wave becomes more effective the denser the enemies are, ricocheting between minions and enemy heroes to apply multiple stuns, yielding extremely high disruption potential in teamfights. She also possesses self-healing and a movement speed buff from her Passive, successfully balancing survivability and support capabilities at a high level.
strategy.weaknesses: While she is specialized in healing, she entirely lacks the ability to output damage herself, rendering her helpless if her allies fail to deal damage. Her greatest natural enemies are items and skills with anti-heal effects; once built, her healing output drops drastically, halving her effectiveness. Additionally, the heavy mana consumption when casting skills makes mana management difficult, marking another weakness. Being caught by an enemy with strong CC exposes her fragility, as she can be bursted down instantly before having a chance to use healing skills, so constant vigilance over her positioning is necessary.

synergy.reason: Provides massive healing and peel, creating a safe environment for Hou Yi to stand and deliver high sustained damage.
Significantly boosts Gao Jianli's survivability when diving into the enemy team, maximizing the impact of his Ultimate.

counters.reason: Musashi's Ultimate anti-heal effect completely negates her core strength of sustaining the team.
Her tendency to group up with allies makes the team highly vulnerable to Lady Zhen's AoE freezes and slows.

If this skill hits an enemy, its cooldown and mana cost are reduced by 50%. If he has 3 orbs, he recovers 150 (+60% Magical Attack) Health. The power of Dark Impact scales with the number of orbs.

The orbs strike progressively further away, each dealing 500 (+60% Magical Attack) magical damage. Upon casting, his Movement Speed increases by 30% for 1s, plus an extra 10% per orb consumed. The first orb slows enemies by 30% for 1s, the second slows them significantly for 1s, and the third stuns enemies for 1s.

skill3.name: Forsaken Contract

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, Donghuang boasts some of the strongest close-quarters combat prowess among all heroes. Pre-generating 3 dark orbs with Skill 1 and aggressively invading the enemy jungle to apply pressure is a highly effective move. By staying close to enemies and hitting them with the orbs, he deals max HP percentage damage while restoring his own HP, exhibiting overwhelming durability even when surrounded by multiple foes as long as the orbs hit. Focus entirely on wreaking havoc in the enemy jungle during the early game to build an advantage for your allies.
strategy.midGame: In the mid game, his greatest weapon is Skill 3, acquired at level 4. Target troublesome enemy assassins or fed carries, forcibly approaching and suppressing them. Since damage taken during Suppression is shared between both parties, tactics such as having allies beat down the enemy alongside you, or tower diving to share the tower's damage, are lethal. When approaching, release the orbs with Skill 2 to boost your movement speed, locking the enemy down with the stun on the 3rd hit or the heavy slow on the 2nd hit before jumping in as the basic combo.
strategy.lateGame: In the late game, the firepower of both teams is extremely high, so where Donghuang uses Skill 3 directly impacts victory or defeat. Carelessly suppressing the enemy Tank will result in being focus-fired by the enemy carry and dying for nothing, so you must reliably capture the main enemy carry, even if it requires utilizing Flash. By building abundant HP and defense, operate on the frontline as a figure of fear, prepared to absorb enemy attacks and drag them down with you.
strategy.teamfight: His role in teamfights is to completely shut down the enemy carry with the absolute Suppression of Skill 3. Before combat, maintain 3 orbs and take on enemy poke as the frontline. At the timing of engagement, cast Skill 2 to disrupt formations with a wide-area slow and stun, charging the enemy backline by utilizing the movement speed boost. If you activate Skill 3 while stuck to the target, you will incapacitate them for 2 seconds while reliably securing the kill through shared damage. Utilize the healing from your orbs to survive the chaotic brawl.
strategy.commonMistakes: A fatal mistake is using Skill 3 on a tough enemy Tank or Support. Due to the nature of damage sharing, suppressing an enemy with high HP will fail to finish them off, and conversely, you will receive focused fire and be killed. Furthermore, you must be cautious because immediately after firing Skill 2, your orbs are gone, temporarily depriving you of the healing and damage source from your Passive and making you very fragile. Charging into enemy lines without orbs or initiating a Suppression in an isolated position where allies cannot follow up are also typical blunders.
strategy.strengths: His greatest strength is the guaranteed single-target kill capability via Skill 3's Suppression, which cannot even be cleansed (CC removal). No matter how fed the opponent is, as long as you catch them, you can forcefully drag them down with shared damage, meaning you always hold the potential for a comeback. In addition, the percentage damage and self-healing from the dark orbs of his Passive grant him astonishing toughness in early close combat and brawls, and his overwhelming invade capability to unilaterally disrupt the enemy jungle is an exceptionally excellent trait.
strategy.weaknesses: As for weaknesses, he is completely helpless against long-range poke compositions or highly mobile enemies who refuse to enter the range of his orbs. Because he cannot heal unless he continuously lands orbs at close range, he goes down easily when whittled down from afar. Also, since he himself cannot move during Skill 3's Suppression, using it in a situation surrounded solely by enemies carries the risk of a one-sided demise. The activation of Skill 2 has a multi-second delay, making it easy to dodge if not fired predictively, which, coupled with his low mobility, is a bottleneck.

synergy.reason: While Donghuang suppresses an enemy with his Ultimate, Angela can safely land her high burst damage combo.
Guiguzi can gather enemies and boost movement speed, creating perfect opportunities for Donghuang to land his Ultimate.

counters.reason: Can continuously poke Donghuang from outside his Ultimate range, whittling down his health before he can engage.
Long-range high burst damage completely prevents Donghuang from safely approaching.

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, Guiguzi's aggressive ganks and harassment combining his Passive and skills are potent. Break line of sight with the enemy to prep the enhanced basic attack from your Passive, and use Skill 1 to enter camouflage and quickly close the gap. After touching the enemy to reduce their defense, inflict a slow and damage with the enhanced basic attack, and aim for the kill in tandem with your ally. Once you learn Skill 2 at Level 2, the pull and stun are added, allowing you to exert an overwhelming presence in small-scale fights early on and dictate the pace of the game.
strategy.midGame: From the mid game onwards, leverage Skill 3 acquired at Level 4 to become the initiator for surprise attacks involving the entire team. In jungle encounters or battles around objectives like dragons, camouflage your allies with Skill 3 and burst into the heart of the enemy team. Lower enemy defenses with Skill 1, and ideally catch multiple enemies within Skill 2's range to pull and stun them. Because you can also grant allies a powerful shield, roam as the commander who favorably develops mid-game teamfights.
strategy.lateGame: In the late game, precision in ambushes to instantaneously decimate the enemy is demanded. The team-wide camouflage and vision granted by Guiguzi's Skill 3 is the ultimate initiation tool capable of forcing a teamfight without giving the enemy a moment to react. Sync up with allied heroes capable of burst damage, and accurately catch the enemy carry with Skill 2. By applying defense reduction debuffs, it is possible to create scenarios where your allies instantly erase even the enemy's frontline Tanks.
strategy.teamfight: His role in teamfights is to execute a flawless engage that shatters the enemy formation. Approach in stealth alongside allies with Skill 3, gaining vision of the nearest enemy hero to pinpoint their location. Utilize the movement speed buff from Skill 1 to slip into the center of the enemy group, touching every enemy to lower their defense. Time Skill 2's explosion perfectly to pull multiple targets to one spot and stun them. If this chain of combos lands, the outcome of the teamfight is practically decided.
strategy.commonMistakes: A common mistake is diving the enemy lines alone without syncing the timing with allies. Guiguzi himself has low durability; even if Skill 2's pull succeeds, without allied follow-up, he will take concentrated fire and die instantly. Additionally, because there is a 2-second delay before Skill 2 triggers, mistiming it and letting enemies escape, or having the camouflage drop before pulling and exposing your defenseless self, are classic errors. Consuming the enhanced basic attack from the Passive on minions is also to be avoided.
strategy.strengths: His greatest strength is the overwhelming initiation capability through granting the whole team camouflage with Skill 3 and the pull-stun from Skill 2. He can force a teamfight without giving the enemy time to react, harboring the potential to flip the tide of battle in an instant. Furthermore, the percentage reduction debuff on enemy physical and magical defense from Skill 1 is highly potent, boosting allied firepower. He is the pinnacle of aggressive Supports, able to constantly exert pressure across the entire map with high mobility.
strategy.weaknesses: As a weakness, his defensive stats are low, relying on Skill 2's shield; thus, if an engage fails, he is built with an extremely high risk of being deleted instantly before he can run. Moreover, coordination with allies is an absolute prerequisite, so in solo play or uncommunicative teams, his strengths cannot be leveraged at all. Whiffing his skill damage and CC leaves him with nothing else to do, diminishing his presence. The fact that his strengths are easily neutralized if the enemy has heroes with wide-area CC or vision-granting skills is also a vulnerability.

synergy.reason: Guiguzi groups enemies together, allowing Gao Jianli to land devastating AoE damage perfectly.
The pull and stun perfectly sets up Angela to unleash her wide-area burst combo.

counters.reason: Can easily dodge Guiguzi's pull with his invincibility skills and counter-attack with high damage.
Can stop Guiguzi in his tracks with long-range crowd control before he can get close enough to pull.

Damage is reduced by 40% against monsters and increased by 40% against minions.

skill1.description: Fires 3 arcane bolts in a designated direction, dealing 450 (+70% Magical Attack) magical damage to enemies in their path. Multiple bolts hitting the same enemy deal 20% damage after the first. Each bolt grants 1 stack of strategy mark. Stores 1 charge every 7.5s (max 2 charges).

If an enemy is hit by this damage again within 5s, they are heavily slowed (decays over 3s). Subsequent fields hitting the same enemy deal 50% damage. Stores 1 charge every 7.5s (scales with CDR, max 2 charges).
