If the bomb kills an enemy hero, Kongming instantly gains 5 stacks of strategy marks and reduces this skill's cooldown by 80%. If the locked enemy dies before firing, the cooldown is also reduced by 80%.

The Passive effect, which increases damage to minions by 40%, allows for fast wave clearing. Poke repeatedly to chip away at the enemy's HP.
strategy.midGame: The magic orbs summoned by accumulating 5 mark stacks prioritize attacking enemy heroes, making them a very powerful damage source in small skirmishes. While dodging enemy skills with the blink of Skill 2, use the Skill 1 and Passive combo to effectively whittle down the enemy's HP and bring them into the kill range.
strategy.lateGame: Skill 3, Spirit Bomb, deals additional damage based on the enemy's lost HP, making it an extremely powerful damage source in the late game of teamfights. By waiting for the right moment when the enemy's HP is low and securing a kill, you can reduce the cooldown of Skill 3 by 80% and create a chain of kills.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, the basic strategy is to poke from the outside with Skills 1 and 2 while accumulating marks. Do not miss the moment when an enemy's HP is low; activate Skill 3 to secure a kill, immediately stack your marks to the maximum, and deal continuous magic orb damage to surrounding enemies, demonstrating overwhelming suppression power.
strategy.commonMistakes: If you use Skill 3 on an enemy with plenty of HP left, the additional damage will be low, and you won't be able to secure the kill, missing out on the cooldown reduction and the benefits of maximum mark stacks. Furthermore, neglecting Skill 2 stack management will cause you to lose your means of escape or pursuit in critical moments, resulting in easily being killed.
strategy.strengths: His strengths are high mobility from Skill 2 and stable damage output from the auto-tracking magic orbs of his Passive. Additionally, he possesses overwhelming burst damage that makes it easy to secure continuous kills, thanks to Skill 3's damage based on lost HP, significant cooldown reduction upon a kill, and the immediate activation of his Passive.
strategy.weaknesses: Skill 3 can be blocked by other enemy heroes or non-hero units, carrying the risk of not being able to reliably kill the targeted enemy. Moreover, despite having a blink, his base durability is low; if enemies approach when you have no Skill 2 stacks, the danger of being killed without a way to escape is extremely high.

It leaves a water current for 2.5s that deals 75 (+12% Magical Attack) magical damage every 0.5s. Casting this skill grants Da Qiao 30% Movement Speed for 2.5s.

skill2.name: Fate of the Sea
skill2.description: Summons a magic circle that lasts for 4s and provides vision. When it expires, it teleports one ally back to the spawn point, cleansing all debuffs and allowing them to return to their original position via the crystal's warp point. If no ally hero is warped, it leaves a mark that recalls the first ally to touch it. Activating again removes the mark. Cooldown is reduced by 50% if no ally hero is warped. Da Qiao gets priority for the warp and instantly recalls if she touches the mark.

skill3.name: Severing Bridge
skill3.description: Releases koi fish from left to right, dealing 300 (+55% Magical Attack) magical damage to enemies in the path and knocking them up for 0.5s. The koi leaves a water current for 2.5s that deals 75 (+12% Magical Attack) magical damage every 0.5s.

skill4.name: Whirlpool of the Sea
skill4.description: Summons a massive magic circle at a designated location, dealing 500 (+90% Magical Attack) magical damage to enemies within. The circle lasts 8s. Allies can tap the Whirlpool portal to teleport to the circle. Da Qiao and allies within the circle gain a 350 (+50% Magical Attack) shield, while Da Qiao gains an extra shield equal to 7% of her bonus Max HP.

strategy.earlyGame: Utilize the movement speed increase from the Passive effect and the 30% movement speed increase when activating Skill 1 to quickly roam between lanes and support allies. Skill 1 and Skill 3 both knock up enemies for 0.5 seconds and create water currents; combine these to restrict enemy movements while dealing damage.
strategy.midGame: When an ally's HP is low, place the Skill 2 magic circle to safely teleport them to the spawn point, removing debuffs and providing support to regroup. Furthermore, the portal allows them to return to their original location, minimizing the risk of leaving the battlefront and enabling continuous map control.
strategy.lateGame: When a koi from either Skill 1 or Skill 3 passes through the water current generated by the other skill, the current explodes, dealing magic damage and reducing movement speed. Increasing magic attack expands the range of both skills, allowing you to heavily support allies by dealing knock-ups and explosion damage to enemies over a wide area from a distance.
strategy.teamfight: Activate Skill 1 and Skill 3 so they intersect, triggering a water current explosion to deal damage and apply a slow to multiple enemies, tipping the battle in your favor. Additionally, placing the Skill 2 magic circle at the feet of a damaged ally to instantly recall them to the spawn point will reliably prevent ally deaths.
strategy.commonMistakes: Recklessly placing the Skill 2 magic circle in front of enemies or in the center of a fight can force allies who can still fight to recall, leading to a numerical disadvantage. You must also be very careful not to make positioning mistakes that allow enemies to get close by focusing too much on the water explosion combo of Skills 1 and 3.
strategy.strengths: Her greatest strength is the ability to instantly recall and return allies with Skill 2, making her excel at saving teammates from death. Furthermore, she is highly effective due to the combination of two types of knock-ups and AoE slows from the water current explosions of Skills 1 and 3, with the added benefit of expanding skill ranges by increasing magic attack.
strategy.weaknesses: Her individual damage output is low, and her skill kit relies heavily on coordination with allies. Particularly, since the Skill 2 magic circle takes 4 seconds from activation to teleport, it may not be able to prevent massive instantaneous damage from enemies. Also, her true potential cannot be realized if allies do not understand how the magic circle works.

synergy.reason: His strong basic attacks and CC synergize well with Da Qiao's fast engages and disengages.
Teleporting the team in to back up Mulan's ambushes can completely turn the tide of a teamfight.

counters.reason: Can easily bypass the frontline with his high mobility to assassinate Da Qiao before she can support.
His strong disengage completely nullifies Da Qiao's attempt to force fights with her Ultimate.

passive.name: Artillery Spirit
passive.description: Basic attacks grant 6-12 Physical Attack and 0.75%-1.5% Crit Rate (scales with level) for 1.5s, stacking up to 5 times. In Artillery Mode, this buff is doubled. Switching modes resets the duration and converts the buff to match the new mode.

skill1.name: Chasing Arrow
skill1.description: Huang Zhong gains 70% Movement Speed (decays over 2s). If used while in Artillery Mode, it instantly cancels the mode. Passive: Increases Physical Attack by 20 (doubled while this skill is active).

skill2.name: Mine Trap
skill2.description: Places a mine that grants vision. Enemies touching it take 360 (+80% Bonus Physical Attack) physical damage and are revealed for 3s. If the target is a hero, they are slowed by 25% for 2s and suffer 10%-20% Armor Pierce (scales with level), while Huang Zhong gains a 360 (+5.5% Bonus Physical Attack) shield for 3s. Casting speed increases if an enemy is in the area (except in Artillery Mode). Can place up to 2 mines.

skill3.name: Heavy Artillery
skill3.description: Dashes a short distance and deploys his heavy artillery, rooting himself in place. He gains 200 Physical and Magical Defense for 15s, and his basic attacks deal 350 physical damage to enemies in the center (damage is reduced by 40% for enemies outside the center). Artillery mode ends automatically after 5-9 attacks (scales with level). Deals 50% damage to towers. Passive: Entering Artillery mode resets the CD of Chasing Arrow. Cast again to cancel Artillery mode early.

strategy.earlyGame: It is important to place Skill 2 mines in bushes to grant vision and safely farm while watching out for enemy ganks. When a mine hits an enemy, you gain a shield while reducing their movement speed and armor, making it a great offensive and defensive skill that greatly helps in establishing an advantage in the laning phase.
strategy.midGame: The Passive effect increases physical attack and critical rate up to 5 stacks with each basic attack, so focus on continuously hitting targets during combat. If enemies get too close, use the 70% movement speed increase from Skill 1 to quickly retreat or move to a more advantageous position.
strategy.lateGame: Skill 3's turret mode shows its true value here. During turret mode, your physical defense greatly increases, your basic attacks turn into powerful AoE damage, and the Passive effect is doubled. Deploy the turret in a suitable position while protected by allies to dominate teamfights by dealing overwhelming physical damage to enemies over a wide area.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, it is ideal to place Skill 2 mines around you to prevent enemies from approaching before transitioning into Skill 3's turret mode. Positioning is extremely important as you cannot move while in turret mode. When danger approaches, immediately activate Skill 1 to cancel turret mode and escape with the movement speed boost.
strategy.commonMistakes: Because you cannot move during Skill 3's turret mode, you easily become an easy target for enemy long-range skills and assassins. Deploying the turret while isolated and without knowing the enemies' positions will result in you being killed easily. Also, being ambushed without using mines to secure vision is a common fatal mistake.
strategy.strengths: His biggest strengths are the overwhelming AoE damage from Skill 3's turret mode and the self-buffs (physical attack and critical rate increase) from his Passive. He is a powerful Marksman with self-defense measures, possessing vision and shield generation from Skill 2, as well as a high movement speed boost from Skill 1.
strategy.weaknesses: Since he cannot move at all during turret mode, he is very vulnerable to highly mobile assassins and mages who can attack from outside his range. Note that damage to towers is reduced by 50%, so care is needed when sieging. Moreover, maximizing his potential heavily depends on ally protection and proper positioning.

synergy.reason: Provides massive shields and peel to protect the stationary Huang Zhong from enemy divers.
Can distract enemies and chain CC them, creating a perfectly safe zone for Huang Zhong's artillery.

counters.reason: High mobility allows him to poke Huang Zhong from afar and quickly close the gap to burst him down.
Can snipe Huang Zhong from outside his artillery range, dealing massive damage with impunity.

passive.name: Demon Soul
passive.description: When his basic attacks or Skill 2 hit only a single enemy, they deal an additional 50% physical damage.

skill1.name: Spinning Blade
skill1.description: Throws a blade in the designated direction, dealing 300 (+85% Bonus Physical Attack) physical damage to the first enemy hit and significantly slowing them for 1s. The blade then bounces between nearby enemies (once per enemy), dealing the same damage and slowing them by 15% for 2s. The blade returns to Kaizer, recovering 350 (+5% Bonus Max HP) Health, reducing this skill's CD by 30%, and increasing his Attack Speed and Movement Speed by 15% for 3s.

skill2.name: Blade Storm
skill2.description: Slashes twice in the designated direction, dealing 160 (+60% Bonus Physical Attack) physical damage per hit. The second slash knocks up enemies for 1s and empowers his next basic attack. The empowered attack makes him dash to the target, dealing an extra 130 (+35% Bonus Physical Attack) physical damage. Passive: When out of combat, recovers 1% of Max HP and Max Mana per second, and gains 10 Movement Speed.

skill3.name: Invincible Specter
skill3.description: Summons his demon armor. After 1s, deals 250 (+25% Bonus Physical Attack) magical damage to nearby enemies and empowers himself for 8s. While empowered, gains 500 Max HP, 100 Physical Attack, 50 Movement Speed, and 60 Damage Block. Also deals 50 (+16% Bonus Physical Attack) magical damage to nearby enemies every 0.5s.

strategy.earlyGame: The Passive effect deals extra damage when basic attacks or Skill 2 hits a single enemy, making it powerful to strike when an enemy is isolated. Skill 1's flying blade slows the enemy while restoring his HP based on maximum HP, allowing for favorable damage trades while enhancing durability in lane.
strategy.midGame: When out of combat, the Passive effect of Skill 2 restores HP and Mana while increasing movement speed, letting you roam the map widely with fewer recalls. Slow enemies with Skill 1, and reliably corner and finish them off using Skill 2's double slash and knock-up, followed by the enhanced basic attack that has a dash effect.
strategy.lateGame: By activating Skill 3 'Demon Armor', max HP, physical attack, movement speed, and damage block are significantly increased, granting you overwhelming combat prowess. Your role is to target the isolated enemy carry and instantly eliminate them by combining your enhanced stats, Skill 2's dash attack, and the Passive's single-target bonus damage.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, activate Skill 3 to boost your durability and attack power, and dive deep into the enemy formation. While Skill 3 is active, you deal continuous magic damage to surrounding enemies, making you effective even in chaotic brawls. Hit multiple enemies with Skill 1 to increase your movement speed and attack speed to maintain an advantageous position.
strategy.commonMistakes: Fighting constantly amidst enemy minions without considering the Passive's single-target bonus damage will greatly reduce your burst damage. Additionally, durability is relatively low when Skill 3 'Demon Armor' is not active, so be careful of the mistake of forcefully initiating a teamfight while Skill 3 is on cooldown, which can lead to a quick death.
strategy.strengths: His strengths are overwhelming burst damage against a single enemy and the dramatic stat enhancements from Skill 3. He also has extremely high lane sustain due to the percentage heal of Skill 1 and the out-of-combat heal of Skill 2. As a Fighter with damage block and movement speed buffs, he boasts unmatched strength in 1v1 duels.
strategy.weaknesses: Because his main damage source is heavily reliant on single-target attacks, his weakness is that the Passive's bonus damage is hard to utilize when enemies are grouped together. Furthermore, his combat power drops significantly after the powerful Skill 3 ends, so mistiming an engagement can leave him unable to withstand enemy counterattacks and get killed.

synergy.reason: Ming's attack and movement speed buffs enhance Kaizer's single-target burst and chasing potential.
Provides shields and CC, covering Kaizer's weaknesses in durability and mobility.

counters.reason: Can continuously kite and deal damage while keeping Kaizer at bay in a 1v1 scenario.
His suppressive Ultimate can lock Kaizer down before he gets a chance to wreak havoc.

passive.name: Frenzy Prelude
passive.description: Attacks with his chain sickle. Hitting enemies at maximum range deals an extra 56 (+33% Physical Attack) physical damage and slows them by 15%. Also grants Xuance 15% Movement Speed for 0.75-1.5s.

skill1.name: Divine Hook
skill1.description: Dashes in the designated direction and empowers his next basic attack to deal an extra 4% Physical Attack as physical damage (+0.05% per 10 Bonus Physical Attack). If cast while an enemy is hooked, it pulls the target a short distance toward Xuance's dash direction. Leveling this skill increases the slow/haste effect of Frenzy Prelude.

skill2.name: Nightmare Hook
skill2.description: Swings his sickle to charge it up, then throws it. A longer charge increases the hook's range. He gains 30% Movement Speed while charging. The hook deals 320 (+140% Bonus Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies in its path and briefly roots them. It returns upon reaching max distance, applying a Hunting Mark to the first enemy hit. Xuance deals 10% more damage to the marked target and takes 15% less damage from them. Casting this again while a target is marked consumes the mark to toss the enemy behind him, dealing 320 (+140% Bonus Physical Attack) physical damage. Deals 50% extra damage to monsters. Canceling the charge reduces the cooldown by 50%. If the marked target dies or is caught by Hook Strike, the cooldown is reduced by 40%.

skill3.name: Hook Strike
skill3.description: Dashes behind the target hooked by Nightmare Hook, dealing 420 (+200% Bonus Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies in his path. If the target is far away, he uses the chain to pull himself forward. If no target is hooked, he dashes near a target and strikes with the hook, dealing 200 (+100% Bonus Physical Attack) physical damage. Takes 50% less damage while dashing. Passive: Getting a kill or assist grants 30% Movement Speed (decays) and 25% Attack Speed for 5s (stacks up to 3 times). Hook Strike stores a charge every 6s (max 3 charges).

strategy.earlyGame: Keep the Passive effect in mind, hitting enemies with the tip (far end) of the hook-sickle to deal extra damage and apply a slow while increasing your movement speed to fight from a distance. Charge Skill 2 to extend the hook's range and snare the enemy, applying the Hunting mark to reduce incoming damage while making favorable trades.
strategy.midGame: The combo of using Skill 1's dash to pull a snared enemy towards you, followed by reactivating Skill 2 to throw the enemy behind you, is extremely powerful. Take advantage of the 50% extra damage to monsters to clear the jungle quickly, and actively gank various lanes.
strategy.lateGame: The massive movement speed and attack speed boosts (up to 3 stacks) from the Passive effect upon getting a kill or assist become extremely powerful. Use Skill 2 to reliably snare the enemy's core hero, dash behind them with Skill 3 to deal heavy damage, and trigger your Passive upon killing them to aim for an unstoppable kill streak.
strategy.teamfight: It's crucial to bypass the enemy frontline and accurately snare the backline carry with Skill 2. When dashing towards the snared enemy with Skill 3, your damage taken is reduced by 50%, allowing you to force your way into the enemy formation. Taking down just one enemy activates the overwhelming self-buffs from the Passive, showing the power to wipe out the entire team.
strategy.commonMistakes: Missing the Skill 2 hook-sickle drastically reduces your combat power, as you lose the initiator of your combo and the prerequisite for Skill 3. Additionally, getting too close without using the hook's range prevents the Passive's bonus damage, and failing the throw combo after diving into the enemy formation often leads to isolation and focused fire.
strategy.strengths: His strengths include enemy snaring and forced displacement (throw) via Skill 2, high mobility and damage reduction from Skill 3's dash, and the overwhelming attack/movement speed buffs triggered by kills or assists. Once he creates an advantage, he possesses the burst potential to continuously collapse the entire enemy team.
strategy.weaknesses: He is a hero who requires extremely high player skill, as everything depends on the accuracy of Skill 2; missing the hook makes him extremely weak. He is also vulnerable to hard CC (like stuns), which can seal his mobility, expose his frailty, and bring the constant risk of being instantly killed before activating his Passive.

synergy.reason: His guaranteed suppression locks targets down, making it extremely easy for Xuance to land his hook.
Provides CC cleanse and damage reduction, ensuring Xuance isn't chain-stunned when diving the enemy team.

counters.reason: Can instantly shield and protect his allies from anywhere, perfectly disrupting Xuance's assassination attempts.
His AoE silence and explosive burst damage can burst Xuance down before he can execute his combo.

passive.name: Aim
passive.description: Staying out of combat for 1.5s grants a Focus buff (stacks up to 5 times). Each stack grants 20% Physical Pierce and 70-140 (+15% Bonus Physical Attack) physical damage. The buff loses 1 stack per basic attack hitting an enemy hero, and 5 stacks per skill hitting an enemy hero. Every 1% Crit Rate increases Wild Wind damage by 4 and Escape damage by 2.

skill1.name: Silent Vision
skill1.description: Places a device that provides vision over a 600-1000 range for 300s. Stores 1 device every 35s, up to 3. Enemy heroes can stand on the device to disable it (more heroes disable it faster). Passive: Moving near terrain or within the device's vision grants camouflage, increasing Movement Speed and Attack Speed by 15%. The Attack Speed bonus persists for 3s after leaving camouflage.

skill2.name: Wild Wind
skill2.description: Aims and fires a shot in the designated direction, dealing 750 (+250% Bonus Physical Attack) physical damage plus 8% of the target's Max HP as physical damage. Also heavily slows the target for 0.5s. Takes 2s to fully aim; firing early may cause the shot to miss. Stores up to 3 bullets. Each basic attack hit reduces the charge time by 4%. At 40%/80%/120%/160% Attack Speed, aiming time is reduced by 0.25/0.5/0.75/1s, and the weapon stow time is also reduced.

skill3.name: Escape
skill3.description: Jumps backward and fires in the designated direction, dealing 750 (+180% Bonus Physical Attack) physical damage and slowing enemies by 15% for 2s. Upon landing, gains 15% Movement Speed for 2s. Shares bullets with Wild Wind and consumes 1 upon casting. Basic attacks or damage skills cancel the movement speed boost. Passive: Leveling this skill increases basic attack range by 15.

strategy.earlyGame: Move near terrain while out of combat to gain camouflage and utilize the movement speed boost to quickly reach advantageous positions. Place Skill 1 vision devices at key choke points to prevent ganks, and harass enemies from a safe distance using Skill 2's long-range snipe that deals max HP percentage damage to dominate the laning phase.
strategy.midGame: Maintain out-of-combat status to accumulate up to 5 stacks of the Passive's focus buff, joining fights with significantly increased physical penetration and physical damage. Basic attacks gradually consume focus stacks, but activating Skill 2 consumes all stacks to maximize the power of a single shot, making sniping accuracy extremely important.
strategy.lateGame: Your build, utilizing the conversion of critical rate into skill damage and the reduction of Skill 2 aim time via attack speed, becomes complete, making the rate of fire and power of your snipes a massive threat. If enemies get close, use Skill 3 to jump back while shooting to slow them, and ensure your survival using the movement speed boost upon landing.
strategy.teamfight: Before a teamfight, repeatedly use Skill 2 to snipe from afar, heavily depleting the HP of enemy carries to create an overwhelming advantage before the fight even begins. During the fight, deal damage with basic attacks from a safe position, keeping an eye on the shared ammo count of Skill 2 and Skill 3, and switch between sniping and evading as the situation demands.
strategy.commonMistakes: Focusing solely on Skill 2 snipes and never using basic attacks is a fatal mistake that greatly reduces the team's overall damage output. It's also common to miss shots by firing before aiming is complete, or to place Skill 1 vision devices in spots where enemies can easily step on them, wasting their utility.
strategy.strengths: His greatest strengths are the ability to secure wide-area vision with up to three Skill 1 devices, and the ultra-high damage snipe from Skill 2 that hits from outside the enemy's range. He also boasts exceptional survivability through terrain camouflage and the invincible backward jump of Skill 3, standing out as a Marksman with an overwhelming presence.
strategy.weaknesses: Because attack speed and critical rate are converted through a special formula, he is not suited for continuous basic attack damage like standard Marksmen. Missing Skill 2 snipes significantly drops his damage contribution, and he is very fragile when caught by highly mobile assassins, having no countermeasures other than Skill 3.

synergy.reason: Mozi's long-range stuns lock targets in place, making it incredibly easy for Shouyue to land his sniper shots.
Lian Po's heavy crowd control holds the frontline, buying Shouyue the time and space needed to aim safely.

counters.reason: Her physical immunity skill allows her to completely nullify Shouyue's massive sniper damage.
Can instantly shield Shouyue's sniper targets across the map, preventing crucial cross-map kills.

passive.name: Vitality Stone
passive.description: Basic attacks deal magical damage. Skill hits apply a Yin stack. At 4 stacks, a Go piece spawns at the enemy's feet, dealing 100 (+10% Magical Attack) magical damage and permanently granting Yixing 1 Go piece. He gains 5 pieces per kill/assist and every 60s. Each piece grants 1 Magical Attack. Pieces generated by Yin stacks are drawn to the center of the board, dealing 170 (+11% Magical Attack) magical damage to enemies in their path. Upon taking fatal damage, he becomes ethereal, spawning a piece at his feet, ignoring all damage for 1s, and gaining 30% Movement Speed (150s CD). Consecutive hits from Yin pieces deal 50% damage.

skill1.name: Play
skill1.description: Places a Go piece at the target location, gaining vision of the area for 3s and dealing 100 (+18% Magical Attack) magical damage. Also reduces Respond's CD by 0.5s. The piece lasts 12s. Pieces within Respond's board are drawn to its center, dealing 180 (+36% Magical Attack) magical damage to enemies hit and slowing them by 25% for 1s. Hitting an enemy with a piece empowers the next basic attack to deal 223 (120 + 60% Magical Attack + 18% Physical Attack) magical damage. Reaching 50/110/181 pieces evolves the empowered attack, adding scatter, +17% damage, and +34% damage, while granting a massive Movement Speed boost for 5s. Stores 1 piece every 6s, up to 2. Subsequent hits from drawn pieces within 0.5s deal 50% damage.

skill2.name: Respond
skill2.description: Forms a Go board at the target area, dealing 160 (+25% Magical Attack) magical damage. The board lasts 10s. Enemies in the center are pulled to the center, taking an additional 160 (+25% Magical Attack) magical damage. If Yixing or enemy heroes pass through the board's edge, they gain a Yin stack, and Yixing gains 50% Movement Speed (decays) for 1s. This triggers once per target. The board disappears if he moves too far away. Deals 180% extra damage to non-hero units.

skill3.name: Tianyuan
skill3.description: Draws a huge ethereal Go board, taking 1.5s to fully form. Enemies inside are knocked up for 0.5s. The board lasts 4s, during which enemies cannot leave its boundaries. The board applies 3 Yin stacks to enemy heroes continuously and grants vision. Catching enemy heroes stores 1 charge of Play. A smaller board reduces the CD by up to 25%. While drawing, Yixing gains 30%-70% Movement Speed (decays).

strategy.earlyGame: Secure vision at designated locations while dealing magic damage with Skill 1, and draw enemies into the center of Skill 2's array to guarantee damage. Hitting skills accumulates Yin marks up to 4 stacks, which generates a go piece at the enemy's feet, so actively mix skills and enhanced basic attacks to stack marks.
strategy.midGame: Magic attack increases permanently based on the number of go pieces acquired over time, kills, or assists, and your enhanced basic attacks evolve to spread or deal increased damage. Pass through the edge of Skill 2's array to gain movement speed, and use the Passive's 1-second damage immunity upon taking a fatal blow as a shield to confidently dominate the board.
strategy.lateGame: The formation of Skill 3 'Void Go Board' heavily dictates the outcome of the match. It completely traps enemies in a wide area for 4 seconds, continuously applying Yin marks, showing immense control in teamfights. Creating a smaller board reduces the cooldown by up to 25%, making situational board placement extremely important.
strategy.teamfight: It is ideal to activate Skill 3 in sync with an ally's initiation, trapping multiple enemy heroes inside the go board. Combine Skill 1's go pieces and Skill 2's array to deal continuous damage to the trapped enemies, and fire off enhanced basic attacks to drive the enemy team to total devastation.
strategy.commonMistakes: Skill 3's go board takes 1.5 seconds to fully form, so casting it without predicting enemy movements will result in easy dodges, wasting your greatest weapon. Additionally, overextending to the frontline without the Passive's invincibility effect (150s cooldown) directly leads to a certain death due to his low mobility.
strategy.strengths: His biggest strengths are the unique, wide-area lockdown capability of Skill 3 and the vision control provided by Skill 1. Also, as the game progresses, he automatically scales his magic attack and enhanced basic attacks by collecting go pieces. Along with the insurance of his Passive's invincibility, his impact in late-game teamfights is immense.
strategy.weaknesses: Since his main damage source relies on skill combos and stacking marks, he struggles to produce instantaneous burst damage. Furthermore, Skill 3 has a delay in forming the go board, making it easy for enemies with blink skills to evade. Lacking a reliable escape skill makes him extremely vulnerable to being targeted by assassins.

synergy.reason: Heavy crowd control keeps enemies firmly locked inside Yixing's Ultimate arena.
Can easily dive into Yixing's trapped targets to unleash massive burst and clean up the fight.

counters.reason: Exceptional mobility allows him to dodge Yixing's board completely and assassinate him in the backline.
Her constant invincibility frames and dashes easily let her avoid his CC and damage while dominating him.

passive.name: Dream Eater
passive.description: Casting any skill increases his Mass. Every 10 Mass grants 6 Physical Attack, 8 Physical Defense, and 8 Magical Defense. While in combat, loses 5 Mass per second. While out of combat, loses 15 Mass per second.

skill1.name: Dream Reversal
skill1.description: Harnesses dream energy to gain a 431 (175 + 150% Physical Attack) shield for 4s. Deals 352 (250 + 60% Physical Attack) magical damage to nearby enemies and increases Mass by 30. Each basic attack hit reduces this skill's CD by 0.5s.

skill2.name: Dream Bubble
skill2.description: Throws a dream bubble in a designated direction and gains 30% Movement Speed for 2s. The bubble travels to max distance and returns to Meng Qi. Upon return, it bounces again toward nearby enemies before returning. Deals 130 (+20% Magical Attack) magical damage and applies a slow stack for 2s (max 4 stacks, slow effect scales with Mass). Catching it twice grants 30 Mass and turns it into a Dream Bomb. The Bomb can be thrown to deal 100 (+24% Magical Attack) to 550 (+132% Magical Attack) magical damage. Damage scales with the bubble's total flight time, maxing out at 5.5s.

skill3.name: Dream Travel
skill3.description: Meng Qi travels in a circular dream path. During this, he gains Mass, becomes immune to slow effects, and deals 150 (+30% Physical Attack) (+30% Magical Attack) magical damage every 0.5s to enemies in his path. Tapping the skill again or reaching the end of the path makes him dive toward the destination, dealing 150 (+30% Physical Attack) (+30% Magical Attack) magical damage to enemies in a wide area and knocking them up for 0.75s. If canceled early, the CD is reduced.

strategy.earlyGame: As the Passive increases physical attack and both defenses the more Mass you have, it's crucial to actively use skills to maintain your Mass. Gain shields while dealing damage with Skill 1, reduce Skill 1's cooldown with basic attacks, and control the lane to facilitate favorable damage trades.
strategy.midGame: Utilize Skill 2's Dream Bubble to slow enemies while accumulating Mass. Recall the bubble twice to turn it into a Dream Bomb, utilizing the mechanic where its power maximizes with longer flight times to aim for massive long-range damage. Since Mass decreases rapidly when out of combat, periodic skill usage is essential.
strategy.lateGame: Mass management becomes even more critical. Function as a frontline tank by capitalizing on the overwhelming durability and attack power at maximum Mass. Activating Skill 3 'Dream Dive' allows you to continuously gain Mass while diving deep into the enemy formation with super armor, making it powerful for directly targeting enemy carries or acting as a wall for your team.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, dive into the enemy formation with Skill 3, taking on the role of disrupting the enemy formation with a knock-up and magic damage at the end of the dive. After penetrating the enemy lines, endure attacks with Skill 1's shield, continuously use basic attacks, and chase fleeing enemies with Skill 2's Dream Bomb to wreak havoc with high durability and disruption capabilities.
strategy.commonMistakes: Neglecting Mass management and entering a teamfight with low Mass leaves you lacking in both durability and firepower, resulting in a quick death. Also, while Skill 3 provides super armor, its trajectory is easy to read, making a reckless dive into a spot where the enemy's powerful burst damage is concentrated a fatal mistake.
strategy.strengths: He boasts exceptionally high stats in both offense and defense due to the massive boost in physical attack and defense that comes with increased Mass. Equipped with frequent shields from Skill 1 and a long-range dive with super armor and knock-up from Skill 3, he excels at forcefully breaking through enemy lines and controlling the battlefield.
strategy.weaknesses: It is challenging to constantly maintain Mass because it decreases slightly during combat and drops rapidly upon leaving combat. Furthermore, his skill damage relies on magic attack while the benefits of Mass lean towards physical attack, making itemization difficult. He is also very weak against highly mobile enemies who can kite him.

synergy.reason: Sun Bin's movement speed and CDR buffs perfectly patch up Meng Qi's mobility issues and allow for more shield spam.
Groups enemies together, setting up a perfect multi-target knock-up for Meng Qi's Ultimate.

counters.reason: Lu Bu's true damage completely ignores Meng Qi's massive shields and high defensive stats.
Can easily kite Meng Qi with high mobility while shredding his health with true damage bursts.

passive.name: Late Autumn Leaves
passive.description: Basic attacks apply a mark to the target. At 4 stacks, the mark detonates, dealing 150 (+20% Physical Attack) magical damage in a small area and adding a stack to nearby enemies. Using skills or returning to an umbrella enhances her next basic attack to shoot two projectiles (the second deals 30% Physical Attack as physical damage). Her cooldowns are reduced by 1s every time she triggers a mark explosion. Passive: Increases movement speed by 50 when she leaves her umbrella behind.

skill1.name: Leave Taking
skill1.description: Dashes in the target direction, leaving her umbrella behind (if she has it). Using this skill again transports her back to the umbrella's location. The umbrella deals 150 (+20% Physical Attack) magical damage continuously to nearby enemies.

skill2.name: Frost Leaves Dance
skill2.description: Spins her umbrella, striking nearby enemies twice for 200 (+65% Physical Attack) magical damage per hit, and shoots down all enemy projectiles in the area. The umbrella continues spinning around her for a short time. Using the skill again teleports her to the umbrella's location.

skill3.name: Maple Leaves
skill3.description: Throws her umbrella in the target direction, knocking back enemies in its path and dealing 250 (+80% Physical Attack) magical damage. Using the skill again teleports her to the umbrella's location.

strategy.earlyGame: Maximize the Passive where using a skill leaves the paper umbrella behind and boosts movement speed and attack speed. Use Skill 1 to blink in a designated direction while placing the umbrella, fire two enhanced basic attacks to quickly stack Maple marks and deal explosion damage, then reactivate within 4 seconds to return safely for powerful harass.
strategy.midGame: Activating Skill 2 allows you to shoot down projectiles around you, letting you fight while neutralizing powerful enemy projectile skills or mage spells. You can blink to the umbrella even while it's circling by reactivating the skill. Dodge enemy attacks with unpredictable movements while continuously landing basic attacks to trigger mark explosions.
strategy.lateGame: Use Skill 3 to knock back enemies and throw the umbrella in a specified direction to deal damage while keeping your distance. Her true value shines through her high attack speed when without the umbrella and the continuous enhanced basic attacks during skill activation and retrieval. Accurately block key enemy skills with Skill 2, and use overwhelming mobility to toy with and eliminate enemies.
strategy.teamfight: You must constantly reposition during fights. Strictly adhere to hit-and-run tactics: use Skill 1 to dive in from a safe zone, attack, and instantly return to your original position if in danger. Show exceptional survivability by using Skill 3 to knock back targeting assassins and using Skill 2 to erase incoming projectiles.
strategy.commonMistakes: A frequent and fatal mistake is reactivating a skill without knowing where the umbrella currently is, causing you to blink right into the middle of the enemy formation or an unfavorable position. Furthermore, forcing fights while skills are on cooldown or wasting Skill 2's projectile-blocking ability at meaningless times will expose her extremely fragile durability, resulting in death.
strategy.strengths: She boasts top-tier evasion and mobility among all heroes, as Skills 1, 2, and 3 all function as blinks, complemented by Skill 2's projectile nullification and Skill 3's knock-back. She is a Marksman with immense potential, packing burst damage through Passive mark explosions and enhanced basic attacks.
strategy.weaknesses: Her mechanical difficulty is extremely high; you must always track your umbrella's position and use skills with perfect timing to realize her true value. Since she relies heavily on skill-based evasion, her base stats are very low. If hit by targeted hard CC (like a stun), she becomes helpless and will be instantly killed.

synergy.reason: Da Qiao's recall allows Gongsun Li to fully heal and instantly teleport back to her umbrella on the battlefield.
Yao's shield and buffs greatly enhance Gongsun Li's survivability while she dances around fights.

counters.reason: His targeted suppression completely shuts down her mobility, leading to an easy kill.
Her targeted stun and burst combo can easily one-shot the extremely fragile Gongsun Li.

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, use the enhanced basic attack and healing effect from your Passive every 5 seconds to take favorable damage trades. Link Skill 1 to an ally hero to boost their attack power and attack speed, increasing their jungle clear speed or lane dominance to powerfully support the team's snowballing.
strategy.midGame: Move alongside your ally carry and constantly maintain the Skill 1 buff. If enemies approach, use Skill 2 to link to them, significantly reducing their defense to assist in securing kills for your allies. Enemies touching the soul chain also take continuous damage, so positioning to hit multiple enemies is key.
strategy.lateGame: The overwhelming support capability of Skill 3 is the key. The moment an ally's health is critically low, activate Skill 3, sacrificing your own HP to instantly provide a massive heal to your ally, preventing their death. If you are linked to both an enemy and an ally, you can absorb HP from the enemy to heal the ally, dramatically increasing your advantage in chaotic fights.
strategy.teamfight: Keep Skill 1 linked to your most fed carry to maximize their firepower. Use Skill 2 on enemy tanks or fighters holding the frontline to strip their defense, helping your team take them down quickly. Carefully timing the massive heal of Skill 3 for your ally is essential to keep the frontline from collapsing.
strategy.commonMistakes: Because your own durability is low, standing in front of your allies and taking focused enemy fire to die first is a fatal mistake. Activating Skill 3's heal when your own HP is critically low risks killing yourself due to the HP cost. Also, failing to switch link targets according to the situation prevents you from realizing your true potential.
strategy.strengths: He is a specialized Support who turns a single ally into the ultimate carry by drastically improving their offensive capabilities with Skill 1 and instantly providing massive heals with Skill 3. The defense reduction from Skill 2 is also potent, constantly elevating the linked ally's combat power to overwhelming levels when combined with the Passive's healing.
strategy.weaknesses: His solo combat and self-defense capabilities are practically nonexistent; he has no impact without an excellent ally. Since the link has a range limit, being left behind by highly mobile allies breaks the connection and renders him useless. Lacking AoE crowd control means he has no way to stop aggressive enemy dives.

synergy.reason: This allows you to further enhance the range and damage of your normal attacks with links, allowing you to annihilate enemies from a safe position.
By adding a buff to the highly mobile Marksman, it will be easier to snowball from the early stages.

counters.reason: Due to high mobility and burst damage, there is a risk that all linked allies will be instantly killed.
This is because the instantaneous burst damage is high, and your allies will be defeated before Ming can recover.

passive.name: Passive: Good Omens
passive.description:
Every 5 seconds, the next normal attack deals an additional 238 (70 + 100% of physical attack + 20% of magical attack) magic damage. In addition, absorbs the enemy's HP and recovers 225 HP (225 + 40% of magic attack) for itself and allies linked to the tool, and recovers 4% of additional HP for itself.
When hitting a non-hero enemy, the amount of healing will be reduced to 50%.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Time - Carefree
skill1.description:
Throws a ritual tool in the specified direction and links it with an ally. If it does not hit, it will automatically link with an ally hero within range, increasing the target's main attack stat by 20 (20 + 10% of magic attack) (main attack stat 1 = physical attack 1 or magic attack 2) and attack speed by 10%. If hit, increase the target's main attack stats by an additional 25% (decrease over 4 seconds).
Enemies touched by the soul chain take 30 (30 + 25% of magic attack) magic damage every 0.5 seconds.
Passive: Increases own physical attack by 30, magic attack by 65, and movement speed by 7.5%.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Mastery - Glide
skill2.description:
Throws a ritual tool in the specified direction and links it with the enemy. If it does not hit, it will automatically link with enemy heroes within range and reduce the target's physical defense and magic defense by 10%. If hit, additionally reduces the target's physical defense and magic defense by 25% (reduction over 4 seconds).
Deals 30 (30 + 25% of magic attack) magic damage every 0.5 seconds to enemies touched by the soul chain.
Obtain enemy vision linked to soul chains.
Passive: Increases own physical defense and magic defense by 15%.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Peace - Longevity
skill3.description:
After chanting for 0.75 seconds, deal true damage to the enemy linked to the ritual tool and recover HP of allies.
If the tool is linked to one target, consumes 600 HP (600 + 125% of magic attack) to deal the same amount of true damage to the linked enemy or restore the same amount of HP to an ally hero.
If the tool is linked to two targets, absorbs 600 HP (600 + 125% of magic attack) from the enemy and restores HP to allies.
If the linked target is destroyed while chanting, 70% of the cooldown will be returned. The HP consumed by Soul Seer will gradually recover over 5 seconds.

strategy.earlyGame: Actively harass enemies by utilizing Human form's long range and the Passive trait that triples magic damage when continuously attacking the same target. Gain a shield and attack speed boost with Skill 2, and apply Skill 1's percentage damage and slow to exert overwhelming pressure from the early game to dominate the jungle or lane.
strategy.midGame: Use Skill 3 to switch to Tiger form, utilizing the 15% movement speed increase to swiftly roam the map and execute ganks. Right after switching to Tiger form, your next two basic attacks are enhanced. The combo of the first attack's dash and heavy slow, followed by the second attack's frontal AoE magic damage, is highly effective at finishing off fleeing enemies.
strategy.lateGame: In the late game, when enemy defenses are higher, recklessly diving into the enemy formation in Tiger form risks instant death. The fundamental strategy is to stay in Human form to poke from a safe distance with Skill 1's percentage damage and whittle down the frontline with continuous firepower from your Passive. Switch to Tiger form to deal the finishing blow once their HP is low.
strategy.teamfight: At the start of teamfights, maintain Human form, using Skill 2's shield for self-defense while dealing continuous basic attack damage from the backline. When the enemy formation breaks and you spot a low-HP enemy carry, use Skill 3 to switch to Tiger form, diving deep into the enemy ranks with the dash and powerful slow of your enhanced basic attacks to secure a guaranteed kill.
strategy.commonMistakes: A very common mistake is relying too heavily on Tiger form's dash, diving solo into the middle of the enemy formation, and dying instantly from focused fire. Also, neglecting energy recovery through basic attacks in Human form leads to fatal errors, such as being unable to cast skills when needed, failing to escape, or missing out on kills.
strategy.strengths: His greatest strength is the flexibility to switch between the continuous ranged firepower and self-shielding of Human form, and the high mobility and burst dash damage of Tiger form. He boasts incredibly high early-game combat prowess and is a versatile hero whose Skill 1 percentage damage is effective even against enemy tanks.
strategy.weaknesses: Lacking any hard CC (like stuns), he cannot fully stop enemy movements and relies partly on his allies' CC. He requires exceptional situational awareness to flawlessly juggle both forms. In late-game 5v5 teamfights, his short range and low durability are easily exposed, making positioning extremely unforgiving.

synergy.reason: This is because it provides strong CC and defense buffs from the early stages and can support Pei's aggressive jungle invasion.
Poke from a distance will reduce the enemy's health, making it easier for Pei to collect kills in tiger form.

counters.reason: With high mobility and invincibility time, you can avoid Pei's burst, and on the other hand, you are likely to be attacked.
Pei's kill potential can be significantly lowered with health rewind and movement buffs in team fights.

passive.name: Passive: Blast
passive.description: When in human mode, the range of your normal attacks increases and you recover 5 energy each time you hit an enemy with a normal attack. Normal attacks deal an additional 20 magic damage, and the damage increases if you continue attacking the same target within 3 seconds (up to 3 times the base value).

forms.name: Skill 1: Striker Stance
forms.description: Releases Qigong in the specified direction, dealing 275 (275 + 130% of additional physical attack) magic damage and 8% of current HP magic damage to enemies along the route, and reducing enemy movement speed by 25% for 1.5 seconds. Restores 20 energy when skill 1 hits.

forms.name: Skill 1: Roaring Tiger Stance
forms.description:
Tiger's fist bites the target enemy, dealing 300 (300 + 130% of additional physical attack) physical damage. Deals an additional 1% damage for every 1% HP the target loses.
If the target enemy is defeated by this skill, Tiger's Fist will restore 20 energy.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Striker Stance
skill1.description: Releases Qigong in the specified direction, dealing 275 (275 + 130% of additional physical attack) magic damage and 8% of current HP magic damage to enemies along the route, and reducing enemy movement speed by 25% for 1.5 seconds. Restores 20 energy when skill 1 hits.

forms.name: Skill 2: Guarding Stance
forms.description: Surrounds himself with spiritual energy, deals 100 (100 + 70% of additional physical attack) magic damage to nearby enemies, gives himself a shield of 450 (450 + 170% of additional physical attack) for 5 seconds, and increases attack speed by 30%. While the shield is present, Tiger's Fist deals 20 (20 + 14% of additional physical attack) magic damage to nearby enemies every 0.5 seconds.

forms.name: Skill 2: Leaping Tiger Stance
forms.description:
Charges in the specified direction and deals 160 (160 + 70% of additional physical attack) physical damage to enemies along the route. If you touch the terrain while charging, you will be stuck for 1 second, and if you charge in the specified direction again using the direction keys, you will deal 320 (320 + 140% of additional physical attack) physical damage to enemies on your route.
When the charge hits an enemy, it recovers 20 energy and reduces the CD of skill 2 by 30%.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Guarding Stance
skill2.description: Surrounds himself with spiritual energy, deals 100 (100 + 70% of additional physical attack) magic damage to nearby enemies, gives himself a shield of 450 (450 + 170% of additional physical attack) for 5 seconds, and increases attack speed by 30%. While the shield is present, Tiger's Fist deals 20 (20 + 14% of additional physical attack) magic damage to nearby enemies every 0.5 seconds.

forms.name: Skill 3: Tiger Form
forms.description:
Switches to tiger mode, increasing movement speed by 15% for 1 second and boosting next 2 normal attacks for 5 seconds.
1st enhanced normal attack: Rushes towards the target, dealing 185 (20 + 100% of physical attack) physical damage and greatly reducing movement speed. After the first enhanced normal attack is activated, the enhanced state of the next normal attack will last for 5 seconds.
2nd enhanced normal attack: Deals 185 (20 + 100% of physical attack) magic damage to enemies within range in front of you.

forms.name: Skill 3: Human Form
forms.description:
Switches to humanoid mode, increases movement speed by 15% for 1 second, and strengthens next normal attack for 5 seconds.
Enhanced normal attack fires a powerful bursting bullet at the target, dealing 265 (100 + 100% of physical attack) magic damage to the target and enemies in a fan-shaped area behind it.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Tiger Form
skill3.description:
Switches to tiger mode, increasing movement speed by 15% for 1 second and boosting next 2 normal attacks for 5 seconds.
1st enhanced normal attack: Rushes towards the target, dealing 185 (20 + 100% of physical attack) physical damage and greatly reducing movement speed. After the first enhanced normal attack is activated, the enhanced state of the next normal attack will last for 5 seconds.
2nd enhanced normal attack: Deals 185 (20 + 100% of physical attack) magic damage to enemies within range in front of you.

passive.name: Passive: Fearless Chariot
passive.description: When a weapon hits an enemy, it recovers its own energy (7 for normal attacks, 10 for skills. If it hits a non-hero, the amount recovered is halved). When energy is 30 or more, own skills are strengthened, and each time you launch a normal attack, you gain 50 energy and recover 30 maximum HP. When an enhanced skill is activated, it automatically consumes 30 energy. After leaving the battle, energy gradually decreases.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Electrifying Charge
skill1.description:
Swings your weapon twice, dealing 80 (80 + 100% of physical damage) physical damage to enemies hit each time. Recovers own HP by 30 (30 + 16% of additional physical damage) with each hit.
When enhanced: Damage increases to 120 (120 + 150% of additional physical damage) and recovery amount increases to 60 (60 + 32% of additional physical damage). For every 1% of HP lost, HP recovery effect increases by 1%.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Storm Strike
skill2.description:
Charges forward and deals 150 (150 + 75% of physical damage) physical damage to enemies along the route. At the end of the charge, the next normal attack will be strengthened, dealing an additional 200 (200 + 100% of physical damage) physical damage and reducing movement speed by 50% for 2 seconds.
When enhanced: Increases the damage of enhanced normal attacks to 300 (300 + 150% physical damage), knocks up and stuns the target for 1 second, then reduces movement speed by 50% for 1 second.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Force Field Suppression
skill3.description:
Slams the ground, dealing 250 (250 + 100% physical damage) physical damage to surrounding enemies and reducing movement speed by 50% for 1.5 seconds. At the same time, gain a shield that absorbs 1000 damage (lasts for 6 seconds).
When enhanced: Damage increases to 375 (375 + 150% of physical damage) and shield absorption increases to 1500. Additionally, it absorbs energy from surrounding enemies, recovering 50 HP and increasing movement speed by 20% for 1 second for each enemy hero hit.

strategy.earlyGame: It's crucial to attack minions or enemies to accumulate 30 or more Energy, fighting while keeping your skills in an empowered state. Skill 1 restores your HP upon hitting, and casting it while empowered yields a massive heal based on your lost HP, allowing you to exert unmatched sustain and dominate enemies during the early laning phase.
strategy.midGame: Initiate powerful ganks using the dash from the empowered Skill 2, followed by the knock-up and stun of the empowered basic attack. Since Energy depletes after leaving combat, you must manage your pace by hitting jungle monsters or minions to maintain Energy, ensuring you're always prepared to unleash empowered skills before entering small skirmishes.
strategy.lateGame: Activate Skill 3 to deploy a shield that absorbs 1000 damage and rampage through the center of the enemy formation. If you use Skill 3 while empowered with 30+ Energy, the shield increases to 1500, and you gain HP regeneration and movement speed based on the number of enemy heroes hit, making you a frontline beast with astonishing survivability in teamfights.
strategy.teamfight: Charge into the enemy formation with high Energy and use your empowered Skill 2 to knock up and stun the enemy carry. Immediately pop your empowered Skill 3 to gain a massive shield and HP regeneration to withstand enemy focus fire, while using empowered Skill 1's percentage heal to sustain your health and deal severe AoE damage to surrounding enemies, completely dominating the front line.
strategy.commonMistakes: The most frequent mistake is initiating a fight with zero Energy, which deprives you of the CC, healing, and shield benefits from empowered skills, leading to a quick death. Furthermore, since Skill 1's healing only activates upon hitting an enemy, missing it drastically drops your durability and is a direct cause of being killed.
strategy.strengths: The enhanced effects of all his skills when Energy is at 30 or above are tremendous: an ultra-heal from Skill 1, guaranteed hard CC from Skill 2, and a massive shield from Skill 3. He possesses every element a Fighter needs at a very high standard. His comeback healing potential from a low HP state using Skill 1 is particularly outstanding among all heroes.
strategy.weaknesses: Since all his strength relies on Energy, his combat power drops drastically in situations where he cannot build Energy before a fight (e.g., when pushed under the tower). Additionally, his skill range is extremely short, rendering him completely helpless if kited by long-range heroes equipped with crowd control like movement speed reduction.

synergy.reason: Because stealth and strong pull make it easier for Biron's skills and area damage to hit multiple people.
Because you can safely support Biron's forceful dive with the shield and teleport from ult.

counters.reason: Biron's strong shield offsets Biron's damage, giving him an advantageous trade in lane battles.
This is because the guaranteed damage penetrates Biron's reliable shield and causes great damage.

passive.name: Passive: Deer Spirit
passive.description: When you receive a Hard CC effect such as knockup, knockback, or traction, you will transform into a deer for 4 seconds (activated once every 60 seconds). While in deer form, you cannot attack or be attacked, and reduce the movement speed of enemies around you by 30%.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Wandering Beam
skill1.description:
Fires an energy bullet, dealing 250 (250 + 30% of magic damage) magic damage to enemies along the route. If it detects an enemy hero before reaching the end point, it will track the enemy, dealing magic damage, knocking it up for 0.5 seconds, and gaining vision for 5 seconds.
Possessed state: Tracking range increases by 20%.
Deer state: quickly returns to human form.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Accelerating Blitz
skill2.description:
Automatically attacks up to 3 enemies (prioritizing heroes) for 5 seconds, dealing 200 (200 + 30% of magic damage) magic damage each time. While active, normal attacks cannot be performed.
Possessed: Attack range increases by 20%.
Deer state: Jump forward.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Verdant Protector
skill3.description:
Possesses another allied hero and grants a shield that absorbs all damage for 750 (750 + 50% of magic damage + 10% of maximum HP). When possessed, the CD of all skills is reduced by 30%. When the shield is destroyed, the possessed state is removed and the character enters Cooldown. If you remove possession from yourself, 50% of your cooldown will be returned.
Deer status: Gives nearby allies a movement speed increase effect.

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, use Skill 1's energy sphere to track enemy heroes, gain vision, and aim for knock-ups to support your allies. Use Skill 2 to automatically attack and help with damage trades in lane. With the passive deer transformation, you can step forward without fear of hard CC.
strategy.midGame: Attach to an allied hero with Skill 3, granting them a shield that absorbs all damage to increase their survivability. While attached, all skill cooldowns are reduced by 30%, so it's important to frequently cycle Skills 1 and 2 to secure vision and deal extra damage, preparing for teamfights.
strategy.lateGame: In the late game, the shield's absorption amount increases based on max HP, making attaching to your allied carry extremely powerful. If you manually detach before the shield breaks, 50% of the cooldown is refunded. Utilize this mechanic to quickly and repeatedly attach, protecting your allies.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, use Skill 3 to attach to your team's main damage dealer, granting them a shield, and use Skill 1 to poke the enemy. When hit by a powerful enemy hard CC, intentionally take the hit to turn into a deer via your passive, reducing the movement speed of surrounding enemies by 30%, and then use Skill 3 to flexibly provide a movement speed buff to your allies.
strategy.commonMistakes: A common mistake is not detaching until the shield is completely destroyed. If it's destroyed, the cooldown starts fully. When focused and the shield is about to break, it's crucial to remember to manually detach to refund 50% of the cooldown.
strategy.strengths: The greatest strength is the ability to reliably protect allies while fighting by attaching and providing a shield with Skill 3. Additionally, taking hard CC transforms you into an invincible deer, wasting the enemy's CC skills, granting very high survivability and making you excel at continuously providing vision and disruption.
strategy.weaknesses: Individual burst damage and durability are very low, and you cannot utilize your strengths without allies around. Also, the passive deer transformation only triggers once every 60 seconds, so if you take hard CC while it's on cooldown, there's a high risk of being killed outright. You must always be careful with your positioning.

synergy.reason: She has high mobility, and Yaria's shield and CC perfectly support her ult dive.
He is a highly mobile Assassin, and when possessed by Yaria, he is able to perform powerful ganks that ignore terrain.

counters.reason: Due to the shield-effective passive and long range, Yaria's strong point, the shield, can be easily stripped away.
Due to the overwhelming burst damage, the possessed ally will be blown away along with the shield.

passive.name: Passive: Cloud God's Wings
passive.description: Enters battle in flight, increases movement speed by 50, and can move ignoring terrain. Normal attacks become melee attacks that deal physical damage equal to 4% of maximum HP (+1% additional physical damage). Damaging an enemy while flying increases movement speed by 15 and defense penetration by 5% (up to 3 stacks). When passing through terrain, the flight state persists.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Heavenly Rumble
skill1.description: Gradually increases your movement speed for 2 seconds (up to 150%). If you hold it down, the movement speed increase effect will continue. When you release your finger, you will charge in the specified direction, dealing 200 (200 + 60% of additional physical damage) physical damage to the enemy and stunning them for 0.5 seconds. After charging, move behind the target and remove your own movement speed increase effect.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Ruoying - Dance of Flowers
skill2.description: Fires feather blades around you, dealing 200 (200 + 50% of additional physical damage) physical damage to the enemy. Enemies hit have their movement speed reduced by 30% for 1 second. While active, damage taken by yourself is reduced by 30%.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Wind and Thunder Guidance
skill3.description: Jump into the air and become untargetable for 0.5 seconds. Then, it swoops down and attacks the enemy, dealing 350 (350 + 100% of additional physical damage) physical damage and knocking it up for 0.5 seconds. strategy.earlyGame: Utilize your passive, which allows you to ignore terrain while flying, to quickly clear the jungle and aim for ganks. Since your basic attacks deal percentage damage, it's easy to deal high burst damage from the early game. Use Skill 1's dash to get behind the enemy, stun them to secure a clear advantage, and take control of the match.
strategy.midGame: By fighting while activating Skill 2 to reduce incoming damage by 30%, forced engagements become possible. To maintain the flying state, make good use of the terrain and continuously deal damage to the enemy to maintain movement speed and armor penetration buffs. This is the key to improving your mid-game combat capability.
strategy.lateGame: In the late game, enemy damage increases, so defenseless charges are dangerous. Utilize the 0.5-second untargetable state from Skill 3's leap to dodge fatal enemy skills, and play the role of disrupting the enemy formation with knock-ups and rain of blades from your dive. Chipping away at the enemy frontline with percentage damage is also effective.
strategy.teamfight: Approach from outside the enemy's vision by ignoring terrain in the flying state, and use Skill 1 to dash into the enemy backline and stun them. Respond to enemy counterattacks with the untargetable state of Skill 3, and while dealing AoE knock-ups and continuous damage, utilize the passive percentage damage to disrupt the enemy ranks.
strategy.commonMistakes: If the flying state is canceled, the percentage damage of melee attacks and the ability to ignore terrain are lost, significantly reducing combat capability. It's a fatal mistake to let the flying state expire without dealing damage to enemies during combat. You must consciously maintain the flying state by constantly dealing damage or passing through terrain.
strategy.strengths: The ability to completely ignore terrain allows for ganks and escapes from unpredictable routes. With passive percentage damage based on max HP, it's easy to deal damage even against tanks. The invincibility and AoE CC from Skill 3 give this hero extremely high potential as an assassin.
strategy.weaknesses: When the flying state is canceled, mobility drops significantly, losing most of their strengths. Also, after dashing with Skill 1, you move behind the target, so if you misjudge your positioning, you risk being isolated in the middle of the enemy team and focused down. Judging the timing of entry is very strict.

synergy.reason: Because he can restrain the enemy with strong CC and ensure Yunzhongjun's dive into the back line.
To increase Yunzhongjun's survivability and kill line with shields and additional CC to help him safely escape.

counters.reason: This is because you can forcibly shoot down Yunzhongjun, who flies in while in flight, using target suppression CC.
This is because you can restrain Yunzhongjun who dives without missing with a guaranteed suppression CC and lead to a kill.

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, fight in the initial mode, utilizing the dash of Skill 1 and the movement speed reduction and damage from the sword aura of Skill 2 to sustain in the lane. Hit enemy heroes with basic attacks and skills to gain EXP, level up quickly, and aim to switch to the powerful Domination Mode or Berserk Mode.
strategy.midGame: Switch to Domination Mode with Skill 3 and gain maximum energy. In Domination Mode, physical damage reduction increases based on bonus attack speed, so manage your energy while fighting with high durability. Understand the energy acquisition mechanics when switching modes and use it appropriately.
strategy.lateGame: In the late game, utilize the bonus physical attack and attack speed from items. Depending on the situation, choose either Berserk Mode for high attack speed or Domination Mode for high physical damage reduction. Grasp the energy consumption and acquisition mechanisms of each mode, and maintain the optimal form for your role in teamfights.
strategy.teamfight: In Domination Mode, utilize high physical damage reduction to withstand damage on the frontline while poking enemies with Skill 2's AoE damage and movement speed reduction. In Berserk Mode, exert continuous damage with high attack speed, exploiting enemy openings. Switching forms according to the situation is crucial.
strategy.commonMistakes: A common failure is neglecting energy management for the modes, running out of energy at critical moments, and failing to receive passive benefits (damage reduction or attack speed increase). Since energy is consumed every second, it's essential to manually switch modes at appropriate times before combat to replenish energy.
strategy.strengths: The greatest strength is the ability to switch between two powerful forms: high damage reduction in Domination Mode and overwhelming attack speed in Berserk Mode. Although highly item-dependent, passive effects provide very high stats in both durability and damage output, allowing for flexible combat styles.
strategy.weaknesses: The powerful passive effects of each mode rely on energy, which drains every second, carrying the risk of energy shortage in prolonged fights or unexpected engagements. Also, combat capability is extremely low in the initial mode, making the early game macro before leveling up a weak point.

synergy.reason: Stealth and pull make it easier to reliably hit multiple enemies with Light Li Xin's powerful ranged ult.
With recovery and CC, it safely supports Dark Li Xin's approach and Light Li Xin's poke.

counters.reason: Because you can easily block Li Xin's burst damage and approach with a strong shield and knockback.
This is because his timer ability in the final stages is extremely high, and he can stop Yami Li Xin's strength, the split push.

When you self-disappear the magic circle, your movement speed increases by 20% and your critical rate increases by 20% for 3 seconds.

strategy.earlyGame: Deploy the longbow with Skill 1, increasing your range by 150 to safely poke enemies unilaterally. However, attack speed decreases and mana is consumed while the longbow is active, so secure an advantage by appropriately stowing the longbow to increase movement speed and recover mana with basic attacks for dynamic lane trades.
strategy.midGame: Use the Magic-Piercing Arrow of Skill 2 to deal damage and a 0.5-second silence to enemies, aiming for further magic damage with the subsequent explosion. Because your passive allows you to deal extra damage to shields, aggressively attack enemies with shields to maximize your burst damage in mid-game skirmishes.
strategy.lateGame: Once items are completed, deploy the magic circle of Skill 3 to gain critical hit rate and movement speed buffs. The passive, which applies a 30% movement speed reduction (up to 50% consecutively) upon landing critical hits, becomes extremely powerful, allowing you to unilaterally wipe out enemies from the ultra-long range of the longbow without letting them get close.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, position yourself in the backline, deploy the magic circle with Skill 3, and boost the overall critical hit rate and movement speed of your allies. Attack from ultra-long range with the longbow, using the powerful slow effect from critical hits to root enemies. Enemies with shields are particularly vulnerable to your passive, so prioritize attacking them.
strategy.commonMistakes: A typical mistake is maintaining the longbow state of Skill 1 for too long, exhausting your mana, and being unable to attack at crucial moments. Stowing the longbow recovers mana (triple the amount when attacking heroes), so it's essential to constantly monitor your remaining mana and consciously stow the bow between engagements to manage it.
strategy.strengths: The strongest point is the ability to kite without allowing enemies to approach, thanks to the long range of Skill 1 and the movement speed reduction effect triggered by critical hits. Furthermore, the passive deals equal additional damage to enemy shields, allowing you to instantly neutralize enemy heroes reliant on shields.
strategy.weaknesses: When the longbow is deployed, attack speed is reduced by 20%, and lacking any mobility skills, you are very squishy when approached by assassins. Also, mana consumption is intense, and aside from the movement speed buff from manually canceling Skill 3, you have no self-defense methods, meaning you are easily killed without allied protection.

synergy.reason: With firepower and recovery buffs, Garo's overwhelming range and firepower can be further enhanced and snowballed.
With his powerful shield and peel ability, he can protect Garo, who doesn't have escape skills, from the enemy Assassin.

counters.reason: This is because it uses its high mobility to sneak through Garo's range, quickly closing the distance and dealing burst damage.
This is because the strong burst damage from stealth can instantly kill Garo even if there is an escort.

passive.description:
The next normal attack will be strengthened every 6 seconds. Enhanced normal attacks deal an additional 209 (110 + 60% of physical attack) physical damage and restore 104 HP (55 + 30% of physical attack + 3% of missing HP).
Whenever you receive true physical damage or hit an enemy with a skill, you gain 1 stack of Fortitude (up to 10 stacks), and each stack increases your physical defense by 16-32 for 6 seconds.

skill1.description: Charges in the specified direction and deals 199 (125 + 45% of physical attack) physical damage to enemies along the route. When charging to the maximum distance, it deals 399 (250 + 90% of physical attack) physical damage to enemies within range. After a certain period of time, deals additional physical damage to enemies within the same range and knocks them up for 1 second.

This effect can stack up to 3 times, and each stack reduces enemy movement speed by 15% for 2 seconds. This skill can be activated 3 times before going on cooldown, and each activation increases physical damage by 25%.
When activated, the CD of normal attacks is reset.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Set Sail!
skill3.description:
Board a ship and set sail in the specified direction for 12 seconds. While sailing, you gain super armor effect and gradually increase your movement speed. The ship deals 549 (350 + 120% of physical attack) physical damage to enemy minions or non-large monsters and knocks them back.
If you activate the skill again in the specified direction while sailing, you will be able to get off the ship at the specified location. If the ship hits a wall, tower/crystal, large monster, or enemy hero, an explosion will occur before the sailing time ends. The explosion deals 549 (350 + 120% of physical attack) physical damage to enemies within the area and knocks them up for 1-1.5 seconds (proportional to the time traveled). Gain 5 stacks of Fortitude when your ship's explosion hits an enemy.
When the ruler of the ocean is piloting a ship, allied heroes near the ship can board the ship as passengers and sail by tapping a button. If a passenger activates a movement skill, receives a Hard CC, or if the Lord of the Seas disembarks, the passengers also disembark.

synergy.reason: By gathering enemies in one place, you can maximize Sun Ce's powerful ranged attacks.
By refreshing your skills, you can hit powerful combos again.

counters.reason: Because the debuff removal skill can negate Sun Ce's powerful CC combo.
It has high durability and CC resistance, and can withstand forcible attacks.

strategy.earlyGame: Utilize the passive enhanced basic attack (extra damage and HP recovery) that triggers every 6 seconds to gain an advantage in lane trades. Since there is also an Unyielding effect that increases physical defense every time you take damage, aggressively trade blows with enemies using the dash of Skill 1 and the sweep of Skill 2, applying pressure from the early game.
strategy.midGame: Utilize the overwhelming mobility of the boat from Skill 3 to perform powerful ganks in other lanes. While sailing, you are in a super armor state, and crashing into an enemy deals explosive damage, knock-up, and grants 5 stacks of Unyielding. Use Skill 2 to further apply a slow and secure kills.
strategy.lateGame: In the late game, utilize Skill 3 as a transport method for allied heroes. As the initiator of teamfights, charge into the enemy ranks with the boat, deliver a wide-area knock-up, then chain Skill 1's additional knock-up and Skill 2's continuous damage to lock down enemies, while enduring on the frontline with the physical defense increase from your passive.
strategy.teamfight: The most powerful tactic is to put allies on the boat and launch a surprise attack on the enemy backline. Immediately after landing a wide-area knock-up with the boat crash, follow up with Skill 1 to chain another knock-up. Decrease the movement speed of all enemies with Skill 2 while gaining high defense from Unyielding stacks via incoming damage to hold the frontline.
strategy.commonMistakes: The boat from Skill 3 explodes if it hits walls or towers, so stopping before hitting an enemy due to a steering error is fatal. Also, since Skill 2 can reset the basic attack cooldown each time it's cast, be aware that failing to weave basic attacks between Skill 2 casts will significantly drop your burst damage.
strategy.strengths: The mobility to cover a wide area of the map with Skill 3, and the powerful hard CC (knock-up) generated from it, are the greatest weapons. High durability from physical defense increases and HP recovery via the passive, along with the super armor effect of the boat, make the ability to initiate with allies one of the best in the game.
strategy.weaknesses: Steering Skill 3 requires practice, and hitting an obstacle results in a failed effect. Also, heavy reliance on skills means missing Skills 1 and 3 leaves you without mobility and CC, making you easily kited by enemies. Note that your defense is relatively low immediately after a gank if Unyielding stacks haven't built up.

passive.name: Passive: Piercing Brush
passive.description:
The third normal attack becomes an enhanced normal attack, dealing an additional 255 (255 + 50% of magic attack) magic damage to enemies along the route. When the ink from each skill touches each other, it deals 255 (255 + 50% of magic attack) magic damage to nearby enemies (50% damage if hit consecutively within 1 second).
When touched by a brush, HP is recovered by 100 (100 + 22% of magic attack) and normal attacks are strengthened.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Ink Burst
skill1.description: Splashes ink in the specified direction, dealing 200 (200 + 40% of magic attack) magic damage to enemies it touches. When it reaches its maximum distance, the ink explodes, dealing 200 (200 + 40% of magic attack) magic damage.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Seething Script
skill2.description:
Run the brush starting from one selected location and ending at your own position. The moment the brush touches the ground, it deals 150 (150 + 20% of magic attack) magic damage to the enemy. Deals 300 (300 + 40% of magic attack) magic damage to enemies touched while running the brush and reduces movement speed by 25% for 2 seconds.
1 stack of brush force every 10 seconds (maximum 2 stacks, changes depending on cooldown reduction).

skill3.name: Skill 3: Finishing Stroke
skill3.description:
Floats with ink and deals 250 (250 + 32% of magic attack) magic damage to enemies along the route. If you hit an enemy or another active skill brush while charging, you can charge again by moving again within 3 seconds. After 3-5 lunges, it jumps into the air (cannot be targeted while jumping and removes all types of CC) and attacks nearby enemies in succession, dealing 460 (460 + 70% magic attack) magic damage (3/4/5 lunges attack 4/7/10 times each; damage to the same unit is reduced to 25%).
You can charge up to 5 times, and you can activate attacks and skills even when stopped, and when interrupted, the CD will be reduced by 20% to 80% depending on the number of activations.
