synergy.reason: By drawing the enemy closer, it becomes easier to inflict area damage from Shangguan's crossing strokes.

counters.reason: This is because the assault is interrupted by a confirmed suppression CC and the transition to flight status is prevented.

strategy.earlyGame: Combine Skills 1 and 2 to cross ink and brush strokes, triggering the passive AoE explosive damage to simultaneously clear minions and harass enemies. By touching the brush strokes, you gain HP recovery and an enhanced basic attack, so aim to reach level 4 while maintaining sustain in the lane.
strategy.midGame: Once you obtain Skill 3, aggressively aim for kills. By overlapping the dash of Skill 3 towards the ink/brush strokes of Skills 1 and 2, you perform consecutive dashes, jumping into the air after succeeding 3 to 5 dashes. During the jump, you are untargetable and deal continuous magic damage, instantly assassinating squishy enemy heroes.
strategy.lateGame: In late-game teamfights, you must perform Skill 3 dashes carefully to avoid enemy CC and focus fire. Dashes can be paused to weave in skills or attacks, so time it right, ensure 5 successful dashes, and unleash overwhelming AoE damage from an invincible state into the enemy formation.
strategy.teamfight: Enter from the flank after allies initiate, prepare brush strokes with Skills 1 and 2, and then start dashing with Skill 3. Enter the invincible jump state to completely nullify enemy attacks while dealing massive damage with continuous strikes from the air. Since there is damage reduction against the same target, hitting multiple people is ideal.
strategy.commonMistakes: Failing to hit enemies or brush strokes with Skill 3's dashes, missing the aerial jump, and being isolated in the enemy ranks is the mistake to avoid the most. Although the CD is partially reduced if the dash is interrupted, you won't become invincible and will be instantly killed, so practicing dashing along the paths of Skills 1 and 2 is mandatory.
strategy.strengths: The overwhelming continuous magic damage from the 'untargetable' and 'all CC cleanse' state of Skill 3's jump is the greatest strength. Mobility is also extremely high, and the explosive damage and HP recovery from the passive combined with skills provide high-level capabilities in both single-target assassination and survivability.
strategy.weaknesses: The operational difficulty of Skill 3 is extremely high; without successful dashes, decent burst damage or survivability cannot be demonstrated. Additionally, after the jump ends, you are completely defenseless, so if you fail to finish off the enemy, or receive hard CC during the dashes before becoming invincible, you will be helplessly taken down.

passive.name: Passive: Howling Blade
passive.description: Attack twice quickly with the twin swords "Red Shadow" and a protective attack, dealing 75 (22 + 30% physical attack) magic damage and 75 (22 + 30% physical attack) physical damage. The first attack can track the target, and the third attack will simultaneously swing the twin swords, dealing 115 (36 + 45% physical attack) true damage, and the interval between subsequent attacks will be slightly longer. Normal attacks and skills that hit enemy heroes gain Fury, increasing physical and magical defense penetration by up to 40% and damage reduction by 20%. When anger reaches the upper limit, recovers 168 HP (80 + 50% physical attack) (the lower the HP, the greater the recovery amount. Up to 3 times. Cooldown is 8 seconds). Allain's physical life steal is 75% and also activates when dealing magic/true damage. Allain's normal attacks only have 60% special effects.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Meteor Kill
skill1.description:
Slashes forward, dealing 177 (110 + 38% physical attack) magic damage to the target and 177 (110 + 38% physical attack) physical damage twice, knocking up the target in front for 0.75 seconds, and performs an additional attack according to the number of stamps on itself.
1 stack: Adds 177 (110 + 38% physical attack) magic damage and 177 (110 + 38% physical attack) physical damage.
2 stacks: Increases knockup range and adds 177 (110 + 38% physical attack) magic damage and 177 (110 + 38% physical attack) physical damage. Can be activated while moving slowly.
3 stacks: Increases the knockup range, deals an additional 177 (110 + 38% physical attack) true damage and 312 (180 + 75% physical attack) true damage, and gains super armor effect at the same time. Can be activated while moving slowly.
Stacks of engravings are obtained when 3 normal attacks/skills hit. Meteor Slash has a 50% chance to activate the critical hit/lifesteal special effect when attacking normally.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Moonlight Flash
skill2.description: Charges in the specified direction, dealing 144 (100 + 25% physical attack) physical damage and 144 (100 + 25% physical attack) magic damage to enemies along the route, and reduces enemy movement speed by 15% for 1.5 seconds. Additionally, your movement speed is increased by 10% for 3 seconds, and the attack speed of your next 3 basic attacks is increased by 40%. "Moonlight Flash" can be stacked once every 10 seconds (varies depending on cooldown reduction), and can be stacked up to 2 times.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Death at Sunset
skill3.description: Select a target hero and fly into the air for 1.5 seconds, making them mobile and untargetable. It then hits the ground, dealing 254 (175+45% physical attack) physical damage and 254 (175+45% physical attack) magic damage to enemy heroes. The closer you are to the Blade of Memories, the more damage you receive. Up to 100%. Each time the Blade of Remembrance hits an enemy hero, it gains Imprint and Fury. If the target is an ally, give yourself and that ally a 627 (450+100% physical attack) shield when falling. This shield amount increases by 1/3 for each enemy hero you damage.

synergy.reason: Combined with Allain's ultimate, you can charge forward and take out the enemy's core in an instant.
Because the shield and guaranteed damage can strongly support Allain's survivability and firepower.

counters.reason: In order to completely contain Allain's combos and transition to invincibility with a guaranteed suppression skill.

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, actively brawl utilizing the passive dual-sword attacks and healing. Hit enemies with basic attacks and skills to build up Rage, gaining an advantage with physical/magic penetration and damage reduction effects. Aim for knock-ups with Skill 1's upward slash, stack marks, and push forward with additional true damage and super armor.
strategy.midGame: Utilize Skill 2's dash to close in on enemies, gaining movement speed and attack speed buffs for follow-up attacks. After acquiring Skill 3, target an enemy hero to approach in an untargetable state, applying immense pressure with physical/magic hybrid damage upon landing and mark acquisition. Flying to an ally to grant a shield and provide support is also effective.
strategy.lateGame: Up to 40% armor penetration, 20% damage reduction, and 75% lifesteal from Rage become powerfully effective. Dive into the enemy ranks with Skill 3, smashing the ground to deal massive damage while building Rage. Utilize the true damage and super armor from Skill 1 at 3 stacks to slice through the enemy frontline.
strategy.teamfight: Dive into the enemy's main force with Skill 3, or fly to a comrade in crisis to provide a shield while joining the fray. Due to passive Rage, you exhibit high durability and healing power even in the center of the enemy formation. Activate Skill 1 at max stacks to ignore enemy CC with super armor while delivering true damage.
strategy.commonMistakes: Since durability is low without Rage, charging into the enemy formation without building it up means the passive reduction and healing won't take effect, resulting in being quickly killed. Also, since Skill 1's super armor and true damage don't trigger without 3 stacks of marks, wasting the skill by neglecting stack management is dangerous.
strategy.strengths: Being able to wield physical, magic, and true damage makes this hero highly effective against enemy defensive items, which is their greatest strength. Along with penetration, damage reduction, and high lifesteal from Rage, Skill 3's untargetability and ally shielding boast unrivaled tenacity and burst damage in both 1v1s and teamfights.
strategy.weaknesses: Because you need to build Rage and marks, true potential cannot be demonstrated unless you get close and consistently land attacks. If continuously kited, stacks cannot be maintained, lowering both durability and burst damage. Additionally, since the special effects of basic attacks only trigger at 60%, reliance on equipment needs attention.

synergy.reason: With acceleration and recovery skills, it will strongly support the maneuvers necessary to maintain the soul chain.

counters.reason: Because of his rich movement skills, he can easily break the chain of his soul and keep his distance.

strategy.earlyGame: Dominate lanes and the jungle utilizing the continuous damage of the passive Soul Chain and the Path of Rebirth of Skill 1. On the path, you gain the ability to ignore terrain and increased movement speed, so use walls to accelerate the path and aim for ganks. Use the execution effect of the Divine Shaman mode upon reactivation to securely clear monsters and secure kills in the early game.
strategy.midGame: Create the Boundary of Life and Death with Skill 2, applying marks to enemies it touches. Sustain the front line by gaining healing effects and extra damage from enhanced basic attacks against marked enemies. The Soul Chain can link up to 3 targets, so positioning to continuously deal damage and heal while involving multiple enemies is powerful.
strategy.lateGame: Unleash the power of the Divine Shaman with Skill 3, diving from the sky to deal physical damage and knockback to enemies. The more enemy heroes hit, the greater the healing effect, and you can summon spirits to fight alongside you. For 8 seconds after landing, you enter Divine Shaman mode, where basic attacks instantly kill enemies below 10% HP, making you the crux of teamfights.
strategy.teamfight: Use the terrain ignorance of Skill 1 to enter from the optimal position, and dive into the enemy formation with Skill 3 to deal wide-area damage and knockback while recovering a massive amount of HP. Scatter marks with Skill 2, link with multiple targets via the Soul Chain to heal, and systematically eliminate weakened enemies with the execution effect of Divine Shaman mode.
strategy.commonMistakes: The Soul Chain deals continuous damage by linking to enemies, so moving too far away and breaking the link significantly drops your burst damage. Also, since the healing and spirit effects of Skill 3 are weakened if it doesn't hit enemy heroes, wasting it in an empty area severely impairs your survivability and decisive power.
strategy.strengths: Possesses excellent continuous combat capabilities: continuous healing from the passive Soul Chain and Skill 2, high mobility ignoring terrain with Skill 1, and HP percentage execution in Divine Shaman mode. The strength in chaotic fights is overwhelming, utilizing the numerical advantage of summoned spirits and simultaneously attacking/healing multiple enemies.
strategy.weaknesses: Burst damage is relatively low, relying heavily on the continuous damage of the Soul Chain, so if enemies escape quickly, full damage cannot be dealt. Additionally, you need to stay near the enemy formation to leverage healing and execution effects, meaning if you get locked down by a powerful CC chain, you will be taken down without doing anything.

passive.name: Passive: Soul Resonance
passive.description:
When using a normal attack, the resonance of the sword will link with the enemy's soul for 3 seconds. Soul Chain deals 88% (50% of physical attack) physical damage each time. While the soul chain is connected, the damage frequency will gradually increase, and when it ends, it will deal an additional 211 (70 + 80% of physical attack) magic damage.
100% special effect is applied to the damage at the beginning and end of Soul Chain, and 40% special effect is applied to all other damage.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Path of Passing
skill1.description:
Opens the path to death and deals 280 (280 + 95% of additional physical attack) magic damage to the target hit.
Path of Death accelerates its generation when it touches a wall or the wall of life and death, and deals an additional 150 (150 + 60% of additional physical attack) magic damage on subsequent hits. While on the road, you gain the ability to ignore terrain and increase your movement speed by 50%. When the path first touches a wall, it creates a bridge of death, greatly increasing movement speed when the Halt of Purges crosses it. At the same time, reduce the movement speed of nearby enemies by 15%.
Activating the skill again will call back the sword and enter shaman mode for 4 seconds. Kills a target with less than 10% HP during a normal attack (750-1500% for monsters, changes depending on hero level).
This skill deals the same contact damage to non-hero units, and is automatically recalled when the skill count ends.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Death's Precipice
skill2.description:
Swings around with a sword, dealing 200 (200 + 60% of additional physical attack) physical damage to the enemy in front, reducing movement speed by 25% for 1 second, and creating a wall of life and death. Gives a mark to enemies who touch the wall of life and death, and deals 140 (140 + 45% of additional physical attack) magic damage to enemies who touch dead branches and reduces movement speed by 25% for 1 second.
The next normal attack will be strengthened, dealing 50% additional damage to the target and recovering 65 (65 + 1.5% of additional maximum HP) HP. If there are targets with the engraving within the attack range, Soul Chain activates the same additional damage and healing effect on all targets with the engraving.
This skill knocks back non-hero units. Soul Chain can link up to 3 targets. If linked with a non-hero unit, the healing effect will be 50%.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Conductor of Souls
skill3.description:
Unleash the power of a shaman and descend from the sky, dealing 380 (380 + 150% of additional physical attack) physical damage to nearby enemies and knocking them back for 0.5 seconds. Unleash the power of a shaman and descend from the sky, dealing 380 (380 + 150% of additional physical attack) physical damage to nearby enemies and knocking them back for 0.5 seconds.
After landing, it becomes a shaman mode for 8 seconds and kills targets with less than 10% HP (750-1500 for monsters, depending on hero level) when using normal attacks.
Healing effect increases by 50% for each enemy hero hit. Up to 100%.The spiritual body's physical attack is 30% of that of the Halt of Purges, and it can only attack normally, but its movement speed and attack speed are faster.

passive.name: Passive: Hidden Dragon
passive.description:
Attributes corresponding to the last two skills used by Blue Sky Dragon are saved (updated each time a skill is used). The main attribute deals 165 (+100% of physical attack) physical damage. The sub-attribute deals 49 (+30% of physical attack) physical damage to other nearby enemies.
When the skill is activated, the Blue Sky Dragon acquires 1 stack of Dragon Soul Mark (up to 3 stacks). After acquiring the engraving, the next normal attack will consume all engravings and attack the target with the dragon soul. The number of attacks increases by 1 for each stack of engraving consumed. This enhanced normal attack has different effects depending on the current main attribute.
When performing additional attacks using the Dragon Soul Seal, the second and subsequent attacks will only deal 50% damage. When switching attributes, the same attributes are preferentially retained.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Flaming Palm
skill1.description:
Releases sword energy, dealing 300 (300 + 60% of additional physical attack) physical damage and 75 (75 + 15% of additional physical attack) true damage (+50% splash damage to the rear of the target) to up to 3 targets within range. Additionally, obtain 1 stack of Dragon Soul Seal.
When you activate "Twilight Palm Flame", you will gain 1 stack of fire attribute and add 15 physical damage to your normal attacks. The following effects will be exerted depending on the currently saved attributes.
Both main and sub are fire attributes: Damage increases by an additional 50%.
Main attribute is fire: The first attack of the enhanced normal attack deals 103 (54 + 30% of physical attack) true damage, and the next two attacks deal 103 (54 + 30% of physical attack) physical damage.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Downpour
skill2.description:
Releases sword energy, dealing 240 (240 + 35% of additional physical attack) physical damage (+50% splash damage to the rear of the target) to up to 3 targets within range. If hit, recovers 150 (150 + 2.5% of additional maximum HP) HP.
When you activate "Blue Sky Rain", you will gain 1 stack of water attributes and recover 18 HP and 0.5% of maximum MP with normal attacks.
Acquires the following effects depending on the currently saved attributes.
・Both main and sub are water attributes: Recovery effect increases by an additional 50%.
・Main attribute is water: Enhanced Normal attack deals 103 (54 + 30% of physical attack) physical damage 3 times, and when the attack hits, recovers 150 HP (150 + 2.5% of additional maximum HP).
If the skill hits a non-hero unit, the healing effect will be 20%. If a normal attack hits a non-hero unit, the healing effect is reduced to 50%.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Riding the Wind
skill3.description:
Releases sword energy, dealing 240 (240 + 35% of additional physical attack) physical damage (+50% splash damage to the back of the target) to up to 3 targets within range, and knocking them back. Also, own movement speed increases by 30% (decreases continuously).
When you activate ``Slash Slash'', you will gain 1 stack of wind attributes and increase your movement speed by 16.
Acquires the following effects depending on the currently saved attributes.
・Both main and sub attributes are wind attributes: Movement speed is additionally increased by 50%.
・Main attribute is wind attribute: Deals 103 (54 + 30% of physical attack) physical damage and knocks back 3 times when using a strengthened normal attack.

skill4.name: Skill 4: Infinite Vastness
skill4.description:
Soars into the sky as a dragon for up to 4.5 seconds. During this time, you will be unable to attack, ignore the terrain, and become unable to target (can be interrupted by tapping again). After that, it swoops forward (untargetable), deals 400 (400 + 80% of additional physical attack) physical damage to enemies along the route, reduces movement speed by 15%, deals the same damage again to enemies at the landing point, and applies the Scary Dragon effect for 1 second.
After that, attack speed increases by 20% and range increases by 50 for 10 seconds, and attacks with Shinryuken. During this time, additional damage will be dealt to up to 2 nearby targets using 2 attributes.
Targets given the Scary Dragon effect will automatically move away from the Blue Sky Dragon. During flight, you can freely change the attributes currently held.

synergy.reason: With a strong shield and CC on the first move, Loong can create an environment where he can safely deal damage.

counters.reason: Because it is easy to avoid Loong's burst damage due to its ability to negate projectiles and high mobility.
Because the skill that nullifies physical damage can completely block Loong's firepower.

strategy.earlyGame: Understand the passive that saves elements by casting skills twice, and manage your lane. Choose between Fire (Skill 1) for extra and true damage, or Water (Skill 2) for HP recovery, depending on the situation. Build Dragon Soul marks by hitting skills, and engage in favorable damage trades with enhanced basic attacks.
strategy.midGame: Since effects are amplified by combining main and sub elements, skill rotation tailored to the combat situation is vital. When pursuing, use Wind (Skill 3) to increase movement speed and apply knockbacks; when durability is needed, stack Water to increase healing by 50%, sustaining mid-game fights with powerful basic attacks consuming marks.
strategy.lateGame: In the late game, stack Fire to maximize true damage and extra damage, unleashing burst damage that crushes even enemy tanks. Elements update with every skill use, so advanced mechanics are required to constantly track which skills were used last two, consuming up to 3 stacks of Dragon Soul marks to deliver consecutive attacks.
strategy.teamfight: Flexibly switch elements based on the situation. If you want to lock down enemies, focus on Wind for consecutive knockbacks; to deal burst damage, charge in with Fire as the main element. Utilize self-healing from Skill 2 and AoE splash damage from skills to fight on the frontline, catching many enemies in the crossfire.
strategy.commonMistakes: The biggest mistake is ignoring the passive 'last two skill elements' and indiscriminately spamming skills, triggering unintended enhanced basic attacks. Getting Water or Wind effects when you need to output damage lets enemies escape, so you should make it a habit to constantly check the icons and status of your current main and sub elements.
strategy.strengths: The versatility to handle any situation by selectively using the three elements of Fire (burst/true damage), Water (healing/durability), and Wind (mobility/CC) is the greatest strength. Furthermore, with AoE damage from skills and consecutive attacks upon consuming marks, this hero boasts high combat power in both 1v1s and teamfights.
strategy.weaknesses: Because effects change drastically depending on the order of skill usage, the difficulty of operation and situational judgment is extremely high. Failing to manage the passive elements often results in being a jack-of-all-trades, master of none. Lacking defensive stat boosts, you expose your squishiness if you receive focus fire or CC that outpaces the healing of the Water element.

passive.name: Parting Moon
passive.description: Holds 3 moon rings. Casting "Swift Foot" or "Flowing Light" consumes 1 moon ring. When all moon rings are consumed, the next basic attack is enhanced, and "Swift Foot" and "Flowing Light" enter normal cooldowns. The enhanced basic attack generates 3 orbs, each dealing 50 (+10% Magical Attack) magical damage. When "Swift Foot" and "Flowing Light" hit, they apply 1 stack of mark to the target. Hitting them again triggers a different effect.

skill1.name: Swift Foot
skill1.description: Releases a beam of moonlight forward, dealing 350 (+75% Magical Attack) magical damage. If the target is marked, deals an additional 100 (+22% Magical Attack) true damage.

skill2.name: Flowing Light
skill2.description: Fires 3 glass orbs. The first deals 120 (+22% Magical Attack) magical damage, and the remaining two deal 60 (+11% Magical Attack) magical damage each. Additionally, gains a 250 (+40% Magical Attack) shield for 3s, and movement speed gradually increases by 30% over 2s. If the target is marked, stuns them for 0.5s (stun effect is reduced to 50% if triggered repeatedly).

skill3.name: Phantom Sea Moon
skill3.description: Links with an enemy hero for 1.5s. While linked, gains 50% damage reduction. If the distance becomes too far, the link breaks and the cooldown of "Phantom Sea Moon" is reduced by 75%. After 1.5s, drags the linked target into the Phantom Sea Domain. Inside the domain, gains a 300 (+70% Magical Attack) shield, and skill cooldowns are reduced by 50%. Casting "Phantom Sea Moon" again inside the domain pushes the target 700 units away.

synergy.reason: His strong crowd control can reliably pin down targets, ensuring the ultimate's link doesn't break.
Her high burst damage can easily finish off enemies right after they exit the domain.

counters.reason: Her multiple dashes allow her to instantly create distance and easily break the ultimate's link.
His guaranteed suppress can interrupt the ultimate during its cast time.

strategy.earlyGame: Be conscious of your passive Moon Ring, fighting while applying marks to enemies with the Moonlight of Skill 1 and the Orbs of Skill 2. Enhanced basic attacks after fully consuming the Moon Ring generate Orbs that deal extra magic damage, so skillfully interweave skills and basic attacks from the early game to take the initiative in the lane.
strategy.midGame: Utilize the additional effects against marked enemies. Landing Skill 1 for true damage and Skill 2 for a stun creates a powerful combo. In dangerous situations, use the link from Skill 3 to gain damage reduction, enter the Water Moon Illusion to gain a shield, reduce skill cooldowns, and turn the tables to counterattack.
strategy.lateGame: In the late game, the use of Skill 3 is the key. Link to the enemy carry to reduce incoming damage, protect yourself by entering the Illusion, and cast skills again within the Illusion to distance yourself from the enemy, bewildering them with unpredictable movements. It is also possible to initiate teamfights with true damage and stuns from marks.
strategy.teamfight: Deploy Orbs with Skill 2 to deal damage to multiple enemies and generate shields, increasing mobility to position yourself. Link Skill 3 to the core hero of the enemy team to halve incoming damage, dragging them into the Illusion to create a favorable situation. Manage mark stacks to accurately deliver AoE stuns and true damage.
strategy.commonMistakes: Neglecting Moon Ring stack management and failing to execute an enhanced basic attack at crucial moments is a heavy blow. Also, since the link from Skill 3 is canceled if you distance yourself too much, be extremely careful of the mistake where the enemy escapes and breaks the link before you enter the Illusion, denying you the shield and CD reduction benefits.
strategy.strengths: Powerful additional effects of true damage and stuns against marked enemies, combined with high self-defense from 50% damage reduction and shielding via Skill 3, are the core strengths. Through cooldown reduction and distance manipulation via the Illusion, you can continuously scatter burst damage and CC while confounding enemies.
strategy.weaknesses: To maximize burst damage and CC, the steps of 'applying marks' and 'consuming Moon Rings' are required, making instantaneous burst damage difficult to achieve. Also, the macro to maintain the link of Skill 3 is challenging, and if a highly mobile enemy breaks the link, you lose your defensive means and become squishy.

passive.name: Gift of the Stars
passive.description: When a skill hits an enemy unit, Yao recovers 70 (+2% extra Max HP) Health (healing is halved if it misses). Each skill cast grants 1 stack of Star Power. When Star Power reaches its maximum stacks, skills are enhanced for 3s. Casting an enhanced skill enhances his next basic attack, causing him to dash toward the enemy and deal an additional 100 (+30% Physical Attack) physical damage.

skill1.name: Starlight Slice
skill1.description: Yao swings his sword rapidly, dealing 380 (+120% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to nearby enemies.

forms.description: Yao swings his sword rapidly, dealing 380 (+120% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to nearby enemies.

Yao swings his sword rapidly, dealing 380 (+120% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to nearby enemies and enhancing his next basic attack. Star Power mimics this skill; if Yao and the Star Power hit a unit simultaneously, his Attack Speed increases by 40%.

skill2.name: Star Rush
skill2.description:
Yao dashes along a curved path, dealing 180 (+70% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies along the route and slowing them by 25% for 1-2s.
——A sword technique learned from wandering swordsmen. A flash of divine speed, like a swallow chasing the stars.

forms.description:
Yao dashes along a curved path, dealing 180 (+70% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies along the route and slowing them by 25% for 1-2s.
——A sword technique learned from wandering swordsmen. A flash of divine speed, like a swallow chasing the stars.

Yao moves quickly to the right, dealing 180 (+70% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies along the route, slowing them by 25% for 1-2s, and enhancing his next basic attack. Star Power mimics this skill to the left; if Yao and the Star Power hit a unit simultaneously, the target is knocked up for 0.75s.

skill3.name: Return to Dust
skill3.description:
Yao returns to his position from 2s ago (up to a maximum distance of 600), clears the Star Split effect, and deals 420 (+140% extra Physical Attack) magical damage to enemies along the path and at the destination.
Passive: 40% of damage taken is converted into Star Split, which is deducted from his HP over 2s.

forms.description:
Yao returns to his position from 2s ago (up to a maximum distance of 600), clears the Star Split effect, and deals 420 (+140% extra Physical Attack) magical damage to enemies along the path and at the destination.
Passive: 40% of damage taken is converted into Star Split, which is deducted from his HP over 2s.

Yao returns to his position from 2s ago (up to 600 distance), clears the Star Split effect, and deals 420 (+140% extra Physical Attack) magical damage to enemies along the path and at the destination, enhancing his next basic attack. Star Power mimics this skill; if Yao and the Star Power hit a unit simultaneously, the target is slowed by 25% for 2s.
Passive: 40% of damage taken is converted into Star Split and consumed over 2s.

synergy.reason: By gathering enemies in one spot, Yao can land all his powerful AoE skills at once.
His speed boost and healing further elevate Yao's core strengths of high mobility and survivability.

counters.reason: His guaranteed suppress lock down Yao's mobility, and completely bypasses his Star Split shield.
Similarly powerful suppress completely nullifies Yao's continuous combos and evasions, easily taking him down.

strategy.earlyGame: Utilize the passive that recovers HP upon hitting skills to actively engage in damage trades during the laning phase. Be mindful of Star Power stacks, build the gauge with Skills 1 and 2, and continuously pressure enemies with the dash (enhanced basic attack) from enhanced skills at max stacks.
strategy.midGame: Skill combos become diverse. Approach with the dash of Skill 2 to reduce movement speed, then continuously unleash enhanced skills. The passive Star Split, which converts 40% of incoming damage into damage over 2 seconds, provides more durability than meets the eye, allowing for aggressive plays even in small skirmishes.
strategy.lateGame: The ability of Skill 3 to 'return to the position of 2 seconds ago' shines. After diving into the enemy ranks and wreaking havoc with Skills 1 and 2, return to a safe zone with Skill 3 while clearing Star Split damage and dealing massive damage to enemies on the path. Confound enemies with an endless combo interwoven with enhanced skills.
strategy.teamfight: Always manage Star Power stacks and enter combat in an enhanced state. Cut into the enemy formation with Skill 2, chip away with the AoE damage of Skill 1, and just before enemy counterattacks or CC arrive, use Skill 3 to return to your original position. Play the role of disrupting the enemy formation with hit-and-away tactics, nullifying incoming damage while escaping.
strategy.commonMistakes: Exhausting your skills without keeping track of Star Power stacks, leaving you stranded in the enemy formation unable to perform enhanced skills or enhanced basic attack dashes, is fatal. Also, if the 'position 2 seconds ago' returned to by Skill 3 is right in the middle of the enemy, you'll dive to your own death, so spatial awareness of past positions is essential.
strategy.strengths: The overwhelming number of moves from spamming skills and the combo of enhanced skills/dashes via Star Power are the greatest strengths. With Star Split delaying incoming damage and Skill 3 rewinding positioning to cancel it out, you possess a tricky survivability that allows you to parry enemy attacks on the frontline despite being an assassin.
strategy.weaknesses: The base damage of the skills isn't particularly high, so failing to execute combos accurately results in insufficient burst damage. Furthermore, since Skill 3's position rewinding is fixed at 2 seconds ago, the player's spatial awareness is tested, making the operational difficulty extremely high. Taking hard CC prevents skill rotation, leading to death from Star Split damage.

passive.name: Maiden's Trick
passive.description:
Enhanced
The farther Shi is from the enemy, the higher her skill damage. Damage increases by 5% for every 100 units of distance, up to a maximum of 40%.

skill1.name: Silk Imprint
skill1.description:
Shi throws her artifact to a target location, dealing 120 (+24% Magical Attack) magical damage to enemies in the area and pulling them towards it. Furthermore, she wraps a "Silk Imprint" around one enemy hero. Tapping the skill again within 5s forces the enemy to move a certain distance in a designated direction. The artifact's CC can interrupt enemy skill casts.
The effect cannot be triggered if the enemy is too far from Shi. "Silk Imprint" deals double damage to non-hero units.

skill2.name: Silky Beauty
skill2.description:
Shi throws an energy orb that stops upon hitting a monster, dealing 150 (+50% Magical Attack) magical damage every 0.5s to nearby enemies until it explodes. If the orb continuously hits the same target (excluding the first hit), the damage is reduced to 25%. The first enemy hit is slowed by 15% for 1.5s.
After 2s, the orb explodes, dealing 150 (+33% Magical Attack) magical damage, with double damage to enemies at the blast center. The orb will automatically move towards enemies marked with "Silk Imprint".

skill3.name: Sole Focus
skill3.description: Shi enters an enhanced state for 8s, gaining expanded vision and instantly resetting the cooldown of "Silky Beauty". While enhanced, if "Silk Imprint" hits, she can force the enemy to move twice, and the cooldown of "Silky Beauty" is halved. Upon casting the ultimate, she gains 50% movement speed for 0.5s.
