synergy.reason: Can gather enemies in an area, perfectly setting up Shi's pull skill and AoE damage.
Shi's pull can forcibly drag enemies into Huang Zhong's devastating attack range.

counters.reason: His AoE debuff removal and CC immunity completely neutralize Shi's pulling mechanics.
His fast dashes and silence allow him to close the gap and burst Shi down before she can pull him.

strategy.earlyGame: Because the passive increases damage the further you are from the enemy, always maintain maximum range in combat. Place the energy spheres of Skill 2 on minion waves to safely clear the lane with continuous and explosive damage, while using the pull of Skill 1 to poke enemies and maintain a favorable distance.
strategy.midGame: The tactic of applying 'Silk Binding Mark' to an enemy with Skill 1 and casting it again to pull the enemy towards your allies becomes powerful. Skill 2's energy spheres automatically track marked enemies, dealing massive damage simultaneously with the pull, picking off enemies one by one to secure kills in mid-game skirmishes.
strategy.lateGame: Activate Skill 3 to empower yourself for 8 seconds and reset the CD of Skill 2. Moreover, the pull from the mark of Skill 1 can be used twice, allowing you to completely neutralize key enemy heroes. It is crucial to maximize the distance scaling passive to continuously provide CC and burst damage safely from the rear.
strategy.teamfight: Position yourself at the very back of the team, activate Skill 3 to expand vision, and spam Skill 2 to poke. Don't miss naive enemy positioning; land Skill 1 to drag them into the allied formation twice, forcing isolation. When targeted yourself, use Skill 1 to push enemies away as a means of self-defense.
strategy.commonMistakes: A common mistake is forgetting the mechanic that the pull effect won't trigger if you move too far from the enemy after applying the mark with Skill 1, missing CC opportunities by focusing solely on escaping. Exquisite positioning is required to maintain a safe distance (greater distance = more damage) while staying within the effective range of Skill 1.
strategy.strengths: The unique and powerful hard CC of Skill 1, 'forcing the enemy's position to move in a specified direction', is the biggest strength. This allows you to drag the enemy carry in front of your allies or push them under towers. The CD reset and double movement effect from Skill 3 dramatically enhance this disruption capability.
strategy.weaknesses: Self-defense relies entirely on the CC of Skill 1, and lacking any mobility skills makes you very weak when approached by assassins. Additionally, maintaining the distance necessary to leverage the passive is difficult, and missing the throw of Skill 1 significantly lowers your presence, making it hard to both deal burst damage and protect yourself.

passive.name: Fiery Temper
passive.description:
For every 5% Attack Speed gained, cooldown is reduced by 1%. Hitting targets with basic attacks and skills grants 5 Anger (critical hits grant 10). When Anger reaches its maximum, Meng Ya enters an Excited state.
Excited State: Enhances all skills and reduces the cooldowns of "Awesome Barrage" and "Heavy Shot" by 25%-50%. For every 1 point of excess Anger, he recovers (0-3 + 1% Physical Attack) HP.
If no Anger is gained for a period of time, it will begin to decay continuously after 3 seconds.

skill1.name: Awesome Barrage
skill1.description:
Meng Ya uses "Apocalyptor" to strafe for 5s, dealing 96 (40 + 34% Physical Attack) physical damage per shot. Movement speed is reduced by 50 while strafing, and the skill enters cooldown when the strafe ends.
Excited State: Bullets gain a piercing effect, dealing 50% damage to subsequent enemies. Firing rate increases by 1% for every 2% Attack Speed (up to 100%).
Can be held to sustain fire, and damage can critically hit. You can pause the strafe to cast other skills. Hitting enemies grants 5 Anger; critical hits grant 10.
For every 2% Attack Speed, the skill's cast speed increases by 1%.

skill2.name: Heavy Shot
skill2.description:
Meng Ya fires a heavy round in a target direction, dealing 200 (+50% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies hit and slowing them by 25% for 2s. Upon hitting an enemy, a wall, or reaching max range, it explodes into 5 splinters. Splinters bounce off walls, dealing 410 (+100% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to the first enemy hit.
Excited State: The number of splinters increases from 5 to 9.
Hitting enemies with the heavy round or splinters grants 5 Anger. If multiple splinters hit the same target within 1s, the damage is reduced to 25%.

skill3.name: Missile Mayhem
skill3.description:
Meng Ya scans the surrounding area. If allied heroes attack enemies within range, a mark is applied to the target's location (up to 5). After scanning, he fires missiles that deal 450 (+120% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies hit. Hits on the same target deal 75% reduced damage, and grant him 10 Anger. After scanning, he fires up to 1 missile per second for 8s (if canceled or interrupted, cooldown is reduced by 50%).
Excited State: The minimum firing interval for subsequent missiles is reduced from 1s to 0.5s.
Holding the skill expands the search range for up to 3s. While scanning, Meng Ya's movement speed increases by 50%.

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, prioritize using Skill 1's Strafe to pressure enemies while accumulating Fury. Once your Fury is maxed and you enter the Excited state, skill cooldowns are reduced, and Skill 1 can pierce through to damage enemies behind the target, allowing you to clear minions and harass simultaneously. However, since your Movement Speed decreases during Strafe, be careful of enemy approaches and ganks.
strategy.midGame: In the mid game, increasing your Attack Speed will enhance Skill 1's fire rate and reduce its cooldown, significantly boosting your damage output. Use Skill 2's Heavy Cannon to slow enemies, taking advantage of its wall reflections and splitting shotgun blasts to gain the upper hand in narrow spaces. When allies initiate a fight, it's crucial to hold your Ultimate to increase your Movement Speed and expand your search range, providing long-range missile support to secure kills.
strategy.lateGame: In the late game, the key is to keep your Fury high and quickly enter the Excited state during teamfights. In the Excited state, you recover HP whenever your Fury exceeds the cap, enhancing your sustained combat ability. Your Ultimate's wide-range missile support is extremely powerful in teamfights, but since your mobility is relatively low, make sure to secure a safe position behind your allies to keep up Skill 1's Strafe.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, activate your Ultimate in sync with your allies' attacks to rain down a massive amount of missiles and deal damage to the entire enemy team. In the Excited state, the missile firing interval is shortened and Skill 2's scatter shots increase, allowing you to unleash overwhelming AoE burst damage. Your role is to survive tenaciously by utilizing the HP recovery effect when Fury exceeds the limit, continuously dealing damage and wearing down enemies with Skill 1.
strategy.commonMistakes: If you start a fight without managing your Fury, your skills won't be enhanced, and you won't reach your full damage potential. Also, be aware that your Movement Speed drops by 50 during Skill 1's Strafe, making it harder to dodge powerful enemy skills and CC. Since your Ultimate's missiles only fire when allies attack, activating it while isolated or when allies cannot engage often wastes the skill.
strategy.strengths: Your strengths lie in the massive skill enhancements from the Excited state and the high sustained combat ability from HP recovery when Fury exceeds the limit. Increasing Attack Speed directly translates to reduced skill cooldowns and increased fire rates, allowing you to efficiently utilize basic attack-oriented stats despite being skill-dependent. Additionally, the ultra-long-range missile support from your Ultimate is excellent for providing powerful cover fire for allies even from a distance.
strategy.weaknesses: Your weakness is that your skills have low performance when Fury is not accumulated, making you highly dependent on the Excited state. While using Skill 1, your Movement Speed is reduced, making it hard to dodge enemy skills. Since you lack mobility skills to compensate, escaping close-quarters combat is extremely difficult. Also, because your Ultimate's missiles rely on allied attacks, it cannot show its true value in situations where you cannot coordinate with your team.

synergy.reason: Acts as a frontline shield, creating a safe environment for Meng Ya to dish out sustained long-range damage.
Provides excellent support and healing, perfectly setting up the conditions Meng Ya needs to maintain his DPS in team fights.

counters.reason: High mobility allows her to close the gap quickly and burst him down, exploiting his lack of escape tools.
Applies high pressure and mobility from the early game, easily getting close to deal lethal damage.

passive.name: Hunter
passive.description: When an enemy's HP falls below 30%, they are marked as "Prey". Basic attacks and skills against marked enemies deal an additional 7.5% - 15% true damage.

skill1.name: Wavebreaker
skill1.description:
Harnesses magic to enter a submerged state for 3s, increasing movement speed by 40. When it ends, Lam returns to normal state and the CD is reduced by 50%.
Casting "Wavebreaker" while submerged causes him to leap out of the water and attack. He dashes in a target direction, dealing 300 (+120% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies along the path and slowing them by 25% for 1.5s.

skill2.name: Space Split
skill2.description:
Slashes enemies twice, dealing 225 (+45% extra Physical Attack) physical damage per hit. Each cast grants 1 mark, and additional marks are gained for each enemy hit (cannot gain multiple marks from the same enemy. Max 3 marks). Hitting enemies heals him for 85 (+15% extra Physical Attack + 1% extra Max HP) HP (halved for non-hero units).
When marks are stacked, basic attacks consume 1 mark to become enhanced. Enhanced basic attacks cause him to dash to the enemy, dealing 177 (50 + 75% Physical Attack) physical damage and healing for 85 (+15% extra Physical Attack + 1% extra Max HP) HP. Each follow-up attack reduces the cooldown of Space Split by 10% - 20%.
Casting Space Split while submerged makes him dash in a target direction and reduces the CD of Wavebreaker by 25%.

skill3.name: Death From Below
skill3.description:
Throws a dagger in a target direction, drastically slowing enemies along the path for 0.5s. After a short delay, he dashes to the dagger's location, dealing 520 (+180% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies along the path and pulling them to the dagger's destination.
If submerged, he pulls enemies along the path to the end of the dash and reduces the CD of Wavebreaker by 50%.

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, focus on farming and sustaining with Skill 2. Hit multiple enemies or minions with Skill 2 to stack marks, and maintain your HP while dealing damage with the enhanced basic attack's continuous dashes. Utilize the Movement Speed boost from Skill 1's Dive mode for quick rotations between lanes and to effectively roam, ensuring you finish off enemies dropping below 30% HP (the target of your Passive's additional true damage).
strategy.midGame: In the mid game, use your high mobility to actively roam the map and target isolated or low-HP enemies. The combo of diving with Skill 1 and launching a surprise attack with Skill 2 or your Ultimate is highly effective. If you cast your Ultimate while in Dive mode, you can drag enemies in your path to the endpoint. Use this to pull enemies toward your allies or block their escape routes, racking up kills and taking control of the match.
strategy.lateGame: In late game teamfights, the standard approach is to wait for your allies to whittle down the enemies' HP before going in, maximizing your Passive's true damage. Approach from the enemy's blind spot in Dive mode, and use your Ultimate to drag multiple enemies, disrupting their formation. In the middle of the enemy formation, continuously chain Skill 2 and enhanced basic attacks to cycle heals and cooldown reductions, demonstrating incredible tenacity.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, initiating or splitting the enemy formation by using your Ultimate's multi-target drag from Skill 1's Dive mode is extremely powerful. Hit Skill 2 in dense enemy areas to quickly max out your mark stacks, and maneuver with the continuous pursuit and HP recovery of your enhanced basic attacks. Since true damage triggers when an enemy's HP drops below 30%, it is crucial to prioritize low-HP targets, securing kills to increase Skill 2's rotation rate.
strategy.commonMistakes: If you charge headfirst into the enemy team, your low durability puts you at risk of being instantly burst down upon receiving CC. Additionally, failing to manage Skill 2's mark stacks and not properly looping your enhanced basic attack dashes, healing, and cooldown reductions will prevent you from utilizing the hero's sustained combat strength. Focusing only on high-HP enemies where your Passive's true damage won't trigger is also inefficient and should be avoided.
strategy.strengths: Your greatest strengths are the extremely high mobility from Skill 1's Dive and the damage output and self-sustain from looping Skill 2 with enhanced basic attacks. Furthermore, your Passive deals true damage to enemies below 30% HP, making you an exceptionally effective Assassin at finishing off targets with a sliver of health left. The forced displacement from your Ultimate while in Dive mode also contributes greatly to disrupting enemy formations in teamfights.
strategy.weaknesses: Since your survivability relies on healing, you have a glaring weakness: if enemies hit you with strong CC (like stuns or suppression) and break your skill and basic attack loop, you can be killed without being able to do anything. Also, your healing is halved against non-hero units, reducing your tenacity when only minions or monsters are around. You also struggle to solo kill high-HP enemies since your Passive won't activate.

synergy.reason: His powerful CC pins down enemies, creating the perfect window for Lam to land his high-burst combos.
Disrupts the enemy formation and draws attention at the frontline, creating safe opportunities for Lam to dive the backline.

counters.reason: His silence prevents Lam from chaining the skills he relies on for mobility, leaving him stranded.
Frequent long-range stuns hinder Lam's approach, making it hard to utilize his high mobility.

passive.name: mirror image
passive.description:
When the main body and mirror image damage an enemy, they each give two types of marks. When an enemy acquires both marks, the marks are consumed, dealing slashing damage equal to 8% of the target's missing HP, and resetting the cooldowns of Skill 1 and Skill 2. The reset effect can only be activated once every 5 seconds on the same target.
When you use the skill, a mirror image will appear for 2 seconds. The mirror image imitates the main body's actions, dealing 30% damage and gaining 30% recovery.

skill1.name: Opening the door
skill1.description:
Charges forward and deals 90 (90 + 60% of physical attack) physical damage to enemies in front and reduces movement speed by 30% (for 1 second).
At the same time, the next normal attack is strengthened and deals an additional 90 (90 + 60% of physical attack) physical damage.

skill2.name: splitting sky
skill2.description:
Deals 90 (90 + 70% of physical attack) physical damage to surrounding enemies and reduces movement speed by 50% (duration: 1 second). Recovers 60 (60 + 24% of physical attack) HP with each hit and applies an effect that reduces movement speed.
If multiple targets are hit, the amount healed is reduced by up to 25% for each subsequent target.

skill3.name: Migata
skill3.description: Acquires 1 stack of ``Migata'' at regular intervals (up to 2 stacks), and when used, activates ``Kyoka Suigetsu'' and replaces its position with its mirror image.

skill4.name: Kyoka Suigetsu
skill4.description:
Charges forward, stuns enemies along the route for 0.5 seconds, and creates a mirror space when it reaches the end. When the space is expanded, enemies in the rush path are drawn to the center of the space. Deployment duration is 10 seconds.
After deploying in space, when a skill is activated or the main body and mirror image perform a normal attack, mirror shards will fly towards the enemy, dealing slashing damage equal to 2% of the target's lost HP. Enemies that touch the edge of the space will take 30 (30 + 30% of physical attack) physical damage and have their movement speed reduced by 50%. While in space, damage taken is reduced by 10% and HP is recovered by 10 (10 + 40% of physical attack) every second.

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, it's crucial to use Skill 1 and Skill 2 to unleash dual attacks from both your main body and your mirror image, consuming Passive marks to reset your cooldowns. Rack up damage with Skill 1's dash and enhanced basic attacks, while using Skill 2's AoE damage to heal yourself and clear waves or jungle camps. Rapidly cycling your skills is key to utilizing the execution damage based on the target's lost HP when triggering marks.
strategy.midGame: In the mid game, leverage your mobility to actively initiate ganks. Your strength lies in your burst damage: apply marks by hitting enemies with your main body and mirror image, and instantly finish them off with continuous attacks using cooldown resets. Once you learn your Ultimate's swap between your main body and mirror image, you can dodge enemy attacks while striking from unpredictable angles, so actively aim for ambushes from outside their vision and tower dives.
strategy.lateGame: In the late game, your primary role is to securely assassinate low-durability backline targets like Marksmen and Mages. Flank or approach from behind in teamfights, instantly consume marks with your main body and mirror image combo, and secure kills with damage based on their lost HP. Mastering your Ultimate's swap to spread the enemy's focus, ensure your own safety, and perform hit-and-run tactics is essential.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, avoid charging straight down the middle; instead, aim for the moment the enemy formation breaks or when their backline is isolated. Cast your skills to summon your mirror image, scattering AoE damage and marks, and utilize skill resets to unleash a continuous barrage of skills. When powerful enemy skills or CC come your way, use your Ultimate's 'Mirror Illusion' to swap positions with your mirror image to dodge them, fighting while thoroughly confusing the enemy.
strategy.commonMistakes: If you fail to hit enemies with both your main body and mirror image to fully consume marks, your cooldowns won't reset, causing your damage to plummet. Additionally, forgetting that Skill 2 slows your own Movement Speed upon cast and using it while fleeing can backfire and allow enemies to catch up. You must also be mindful of the 5-second cooldown on the reset effect for the same target.
strategy.strengths: Your greatest strengths are the overwhelming burst damage from your main body and mirror image, and the skill cooldown reset mechanic from consuming marks. Because you deal execution damage based on lost HP, you excel exceptionally at finishing off enemies. Moreover, Skill 2 provides sustain through HP recovery, and your Ultimate's position swap allows for a highly flexible playstyle where you can confuse enemies, deal damage, and disengage safely.
strategy.weaknesses: Since you rely on skill cooldown resets, any mechanical mistake or enemy dodge that prevents you from consuming a mark will instantly leave you defenseless. Furthermore, because Skill 2 reduces your own Movement Speed when used, poor positioning will leave you vulnerable. With low durability, if your Ultimate swaps are predicted and you are hit with CC, your fragility will be exposed, and you will be taken down in an instant.

synergy.reason: This is because it stops the enemy's movement with a wide range of CC and creates a perfect starting point for skill combos.
Both have high mobility and burst damage, allowing them to quickly annihilate the enemy's backline.

counters.reason: This is because they have extremely high durability and CC, and their skill combos and movements are blocked and they cannot get kills.
Due to the targeted stun and overwhelming burst damage, there is a high risk of being defeated before you can get close.

passive.name: A strange connection with the mountain
passive.description:
Due to a mysterious relationship with monsters, when you get close to a monster, recovery becomes a release (obtains a buff).
When a normal attack hits an enemy, it drops a seed bomb and attracts nearby seed bombs. Picking up a seed bomb yourself increases attack speed and movement speed by 3% for 2.5 seconds (up to 6 stacks).
When an enemy touches the seed bomb, it deals 30 (30 + 10% of additional physical attack) physical damage.

skill1.name: rapid growth
skill1.description:
Create a paradise for animals by growing plants. The attack speed and movement speed of Mountain Child and allied monsters will be increased by 15%, HP will be recovered by 40 (40 + 0.6% of additional maximum HP), and allied monsters will come to the paradise and fight together. The status of companion monsters increases with hero level, and the probability of summoning multiple monsters increases with skill level.
50% of the mountain child's status is reflected in the attack power, attack speed, and critical rate of allied monsters.

skill2.name: Roll! Ponpon!
skill2.description:
"Roll! Pon-Pon!": Move forward while rolling with the Pon-Pon (disable hard CC once). While spinning, gain 40 damage block, deal 240 (240 + 80% of additional physical attack) physical damage to hit enemies, and stun them for 1/2 second. If you hit a wall during the effect period, you will bounce (up to 3 times) and the rotation distance will be reset, the skill effect will increase by 25%, and your next normal attack will be strengthened. Enhanced normal attack releases 3 seed bombs in succession, dealing an additional 60 (60 + 20% of additional physical attack) physical damage.
"Roll! Agudo!": The mountain child rolls forward and strengthens his next normal attack. Enhanced normal attack releases 3 seed bombs in succession, dealing an additional 60 (60 + 20% of additional physical attack) physical damage.

skill3.name: gorge gathering
skill3.description:
Mounted: Pom Pom moves away from the mountain child and jumps to the specified location, dealing 460 (460 + 150% of additional physical attack) physical damage and stunning her for 2/3 seconds. After that, Pom Pom will act alone (50% of Mountain Child's status and 100% of normal attack effect will be reflected).
Separated state: Pom Pom enters recovery mode when defeated. Returns to battle when HP reaches maximum. While Pom Pom is alive, it can be recalled by reactivating it.

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, utilize your Passive to quickly farm by getting close to jungle monsters and setting them free to gain buffs. Pick up the seed bombs dropped from your basic attacks to increase your Movement Speed and Attack Speed, enhancing your jungle clear efficiency. When ganking a lane, use Skill 2's rolling dash with Qiuqiu to hit and stun enemies, then rack up damage with your enhanced basic attack seed bombs.
strategy.midGame: In the mid game, unleash the power of the monsters you've gathered. Use Skill 1 to grow flora and summon animal companions, pushing towers and gaining the upper hand in teamfights alongside your allies. Within Skill 1's AoE, your Attack Speed and Movement Speed increase, and your HP recovers, making territorial combat extremely powerful. By bouncing off walls with Skill 2, you can execute technical ambushes utilizing stuns and damage amplification.
strategy.lateGame: In the late game, stick with your allies and act as a Support/Tank, elevating your team's overall combat power with Skill 1's AoE buffs and monster summons. Engaging the enemy carries by casting your Ultimate to make Qiuqiu jump to a designated location, dealing massive damage and a stun to disrupt their formation, is crucial. While Qiuqiu fights independently, your main body is squishy, so provide cover fire with seed bombs from a safe distance.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, your primary role is to dominate the battlefield by granting powerful AoE buffs and healing to your allies and summoned monsters with Skill 1. Target dense enemy clusters with your Ultimate to send Qiuqiu charging in, providing an AoE stun to spark the engagement. Skill 2 can negate one hard CC, allowing you to slip past enemy disruptions and wreak havoc in their backline. Pay close attention to the positioning of your main body while separated from Qiuqiu.
strategy.commonMistakes: A common mistake is forgetting that your main body has extremely low durability after detaching Qiuqiu with your Ultimate; stepping too far forward will result in instant death. Additionally, be careful of misdirecting your Skill 2 roll by hitting a wall and bouncing in an unintended direction, causing you to miss your stun or end up stranded deep within enemy lines. Engaging without setting monsters free to stack your buffs will prevent you from unleashing your true power.
strategy.strengths: Your unique mechanic of setting monsters free without killing them allows for safe and unconventional jungle clearing. With Skill 1's powerful AoE buffs and healing for allies, along with summoning multiple monsters to fight alongside you, you excel exceptionally at pushing and controlling teamfights. Your Ultimate's long-range AoE stun with Qiuqiu is a potent initiation tool, and Skill 2 provides mobility and disruption while offering CC immunity.
strategy.weaknesses: After detaching Qiuqiu with your Ultimate, your main body has very low stats and is highly vulnerable to Assassins. If Qiuqiu is defeated, it takes time to return to combat, significantly reducing your fighting power during that window. Additionally, if you haven't gathered enough jungle monsters, the benefits of Skill 1 are weak; if the enemy controls the game's pace and invades your jungle, it becomes very difficult to recover.

synergy.reason: Continuous recovery greatly increases the ability to continue fighting both when mounted and when not mounted.
It provides a solid front line and shield, allowing the main body to continue delivering firepower from a safe distance.

counters.reason: Due to its extremely high mobility, it is easy for the jungle to be destroyed, and your farm in the early stages will be hindered.
This is because they are targeted when they are defenseless when they dismount, and it is difficult to escape due to their high pursuit ability.

passive.name: Guardian of Fireflies
passive.description:
When you deal damage with a normal attack, a firefly will be summoned, dealing 100 (100 + 20% of magic attack + 3% of additional maximum HP) magic damage to the enemy and inflicting stealth immunity for 3 seconds. At regular intervals, recovers 60 HP (60 + 10% of magic attack + 1.5% of additional maximum HP) of the nearby ally hero with the lowest HP.
If there are no enemies nearby, charging a normal attack will restore 60 HP (60 + 10% of magic attack + 1.5% of additional maximum HP), but HP recovery of allies will be interrupted.

skill1.name: The power of kuwagi
skill1.description:
Channels the "power of fireflies" into the specified area, dealing 500 (500 + 10% of magic attack) magic damage to enemies within the area and knocking them up for 1 second. Hitting an enemy consumes Firefly's power and deals 100 (100 + 20% of magic attack + 3% of additional maximum HP) magic damage for each stack of Firefly's power.
If there are friendly heroes nearby, consume Firefly's Power and heal the lowest HP of the friendly hero by 60 (60 + 10% of magic attack + 1.5% of additional maximum HP) for each stack of Firefly's Power.
"Power of Firefly": You can obtain 1 stack of Firefly's power every 1.5 seconds (up to 9 stacks). The skill range expands according to the number of stacks.

skill2.name: Wind riding
skill2.description:
Fly to the selected bush, obtain 1 stack of "Firefly Power", and summon 1 firefly every time you fly a certain distance. After 1 second, the fireflies fly towards the hero with the lowest HP, dealing 100 (100 + 20% of magic attack + 3% of additional maximum HP) magic damage to the enemy and inflicting stealth immunity for 3 seconds, or restoring 60 (60 + 10% of magic attack + 1.5% of additional maximum HP) HP of allied heroes.
This skill can be charged 3 times, and when tapped, it flies towards the selected bush.

skill3.name: faint fireflies
skill3.description:
Summons a "bush of light" to the specified location. If activated again within 6 seconds, it will fly to the summoned bush and receive 1 stack of Firefly Power.
“Thicket of Light”: 1 second after summoning the Thicket of Light, it releases fireflies every second towards 5 nearby heroes. Each firefly deals 100 (100 + 20% of magic attack + 3% of additional maximum HP) magic damage or restores 60 (60 + 10% of magic attack + 1.5% of additional maximum HP) HP to an ally hero. When an enemy unit enters a bush of light, it gains vision of that hero. Fireflies caused by bushes of light will no longer appear when they are about to disappear. After the light bush disappears, it becomes a normal bush for 30 seconds.

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, utilize your Passive's basic attack firefly summons to dominate the laning phase by dealing magic damage, harassing enemies by revealing their stealth, and healing your allies. When there are no enemies around, charge your basic attack to heal your own HP and maintain durability. Keep track of your 'Power of Fireflies' stacks, which accumulate every 1.5 seconds; casting Skill 1 with high stacks will deal massive damage and an AoE knockup.
strategy.midGame: In the mid game, use Skill 2 to fly between bushes, utilizing your high mobility to traverse the map, support allies, and scout for enemies. When moving through bushes with Skill 2, fireflies automatically attack and reveal enemies or heal allies, making it extremely powerful in skirmishes. To prepare for fights in areas without bushes, summon a 'Bush of Light' with your Ultimate, and control the area with continuous AoE damage and healing from the emitted fireflies.
strategy.lateGame: In the late game, a fully stacked wide-range Skill 1 knockup becomes a crucial CC that can decide the outcome of teamfights. Against enemy Assassins with stealth, use your Passive or skills to hit them with fireflies, applying a 3-second stealth reveal to protect your backline allies. Place your Ultimate's 'Bush of Light' along enemy advance routes or near objectives to secure vision and provide continuous support.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, the ideal approach is to use a fully stacked Skill 1 to knock up multiple enemies for an initiation or counter-engage, while simultaneously restoring a significant amount of your allies' HP. Place your Ultimate in the center of the battlefield to fire fireflies at up to 5 surrounding heroes every second, providing continuous support across the frontlines. Use Skill 2 to dart between natural bushes and your summoned Bush of Light, dodging enemy attacks while maintaining an advantageous position.
strategy.commonMistakes: Since Skill 1's range and additional effects (damage and healing) depend on your 'Power of Fireflies' stacks, a common mistake is rushing to cast it without stacks, resulting in a weak impact. Additionally, while charging your basic attack to heal yourself in areas without enemies, healing for your allies is interrupted; abandoning your allies to prioritize your own recovery during a fight can easily cause you to lose the teamfight.
strategy.strengths: Your strength is a highly versatile Support toolkit capable of healing allies, damaging enemies, applying knockups, revealing stealth, and securing vision. The fireflies from your Passive and skills automatically track targets, ensuring reliable support. Thanks to your bush-to-bush mobility with Skill 2 and bush-summoning with your Ultimate, you possess extremely high map mobility, survivability, and area control in local skirmishes by utilizing the terrain.
strategy.weaknesses: To maximize the effect of your core Skill 1, you must spend time accumulating 'Power of Fireflies' stacks, meaning you cannot chain powerful skills in quick succession. Since part of your mobility and support relies on 'bushes', you struggle in open areas when your Ultimate is on cooldown and no natural bushes are nearby. You are also vulnerable to sudden burst damage or continuous hard CC, and you have a high dependency on your allies.

synergy.reason: Continuous recovery and protection can greatly increase the survival rate against the low-mobility, high-firepower Marksman.
As Tanks continue to fight on the front lines, their high recovery power can raise their actual durability to the maximum.

counters.reason: This is because his movement is blocked by a powerful restraint skill, making it impossible to utilize his mobility to escape by flying over a wall.
Due to the high burst damage, there is a risk of being killed instantly before you can use recovery or escape skills.

passive.name: splash gradient
passive.description:
All skills are considered starting skills. When you hit an enemy with any skill, the next skill you fire will be strengthened as an additional move, and you will receive 1 stack of Engraving for 4 seconds (up to 5 stacks).
When stacked 3 times, the next normal attack will be strengthened to the additional technique "Splash Gradation". Rush towards the enemy and attack 7 times, dealing 121 (80 + 25% of physical attack) physical damage with each attack and reducing enemy movement speed by 10%. The final damage deals physical damage equal to 12% of the target's missing maximum HP. Gain a 21% to 35% damage reduction effect while using enhanced normal attacks.
The additional skill inflicts a disabling effect on the enemy hit, and each stack reduces the enemy's attack speed by 10% to 20% for 4 seconds (maximum 2 stacks). Each time the enemy performs a normal attack, they remove one stack of disfigurement effects.

skill1.name: try slash
skill1.description: Starting technique: Fires a try slash in the specified direction, and after a certain period of time, deals 366 (200 + 100% of physical attack) physical damage to enemies along the route. When Trislash hits an enemy, Rose Swordsman's movement speed increases by 80%. This effect reverts over 2 seconds.

forms.description: Starting technique: Fires a try slash in the specified direction, and after a certain period of time, deals 366 (200 + 100% of physical attack) physical damage to enemies along the route. When Trislash hits an enemy, Rose Swordsman's movement speed increases by 80%. This effect reverts over 2 seconds.

Additional technique: Rapidly fires a try slash in the specified direction, and after a shorter time lag than the starting technique, deals 475 (260 + 130% of physical attack) physical damage to enemies along the route. When Trislash hits an enemy, Rose Swordsman's movement speed increases by 80%. This effect reverts over 2 seconds.

skill2.name: splash fant
skill2.description: Starting technique: Rush in the specified direction and quickly stab 6 times. Deals 69 (45 + 15% of physical attack) physical damage to the enemy hit each time, and recovers own HP by 70 (70 + 1% of additional maximum HP).

forms.description: Starting technique: Rush in the specified direction and quickly stab 6 times. Deals 69 (45 + 15% of physical attack) physical damage to the enemy hit each time, and recovers own HP by 70 (70 + 1% of additional maximum HP).

Additional skill: Rush in the specified direction and quickly stab 6 times. Deals 69 (45 + 15% of physical attack) physical damage to the enemy hit each time, and restores own maximum HP by 70 (70 + 1% of maximum HP). CD is shorter than the starting move.

skill3.name: power gradation
skill3.description:
Starting technique: Draws 7 sword marks on the spot and attacks nearby enemies up to 3 times, dealing 320 (320 + 75% of additional physical attack) physical damage each time and reducing movement speed by 25% for 1 second. While the skill is active, you will gain super armor status and a passive effect that reduces damage taken.
If you damage an enemy multiple times, the damage from the second time onwards will be reduced to 30%.

forms.description:
Starting technique: Draws 7 sword marks on the spot and attacks nearby enemies up to 3 times, dealing 320 (320 + 75% of additional physical attack) physical damage each time and reducing movement speed by 25% for 1 second. While the skill is active, you will gain super armor status and a passive effect that reduces damage taken.
If you damage an enemy multiple times, the damage from the second time onwards will be reduced to 30%.

Additional technique: Quickly draws 7 sword marks on the spot (skill activation speed is faster than the starting technique), attacks nearby enemies up to 3 times, deals 320 (320 + 75% of physical attack) physical damage each time, and reduces movement speed by 25% for 1 second.
If you damage an enemy multiple times, the damage from the second time onwards will be reduced to 30%.
After completing the sword formation, activate "Power Gradation" and deal the same amount of damage again to the enemy in the center of the sword mark, knocking them up for 0.75 seconds. While the skill is active, you will gain super armor status and a passive effect that reduces damage taken.

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, poke enemies with Skill 1's ranged attack while utilizing the 80% Movement Speed boost on hit to maintain a safe distance. Focus on hitting enemies with your skills to unlock 'Additional Skills' and build up your Passive mark stacks. Use Skill 2's dash and heal to gain an advantage in lane trades, and make it your basic strategy to unleash massive damage all at once with your enhanced basic attack 'Splash Graduation' when you reach 3 stacks.
strategy.midGame: In the mid game, your true power shines as you acquire cooldown reduction items and increase your skill rotation rate. Continuously hit skills to maintain your 'Additional Skills', and apply the Wound effect's Attack Speed reduction to win brawls against physical attackers. While casting your enhanced basic attack, you gain damage reduction, which, when combined with your Ultimate's Super Armor, allows you to endure enemy CC and burst damage.
strategy.lateGame: In late-game teamfights, utilize your high durability and sustained damage to dive deep into the enemy backline and relentlessly hunt down squishy Marksmen and Mages. Seamlessly chain your skills and enhanced basic attacks, ensuring kills with additional damage based on the target's lost HP. Even when drawing enemy focus, you must display incredible tenacity using the damage reduction from your enhanced basic attacks and your Ultimate, along with Skill 2's healing, to hold the frontline.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, ideally, you should prepare by hitting minions or jungle monsters to build your mark stacks so you can enter the fray with your enhanced basic attack ready. Dash into the enemy formation with Skill 2, use your Ultimate's AoE damage and Movement Speed slow to pin them down, and unleash the 7-hit combo of your enhanced basic attack. It's crucial to apply the Attack Speed reduction debuff from your Additional Skills to the enemy carries, drastically cutting their damage output.
strategy.commonMistakes: If you fail to hit enemies with your skills and remain stuck on 'Starting Skills', you won't build Passive marks or trigger your enhanced basic attack, causing your damage to plummet. Another common mistake is charging defenselessly into the enemy team without the damage reduction or Super Armor from your enhanced basic attack or your Ultimate, resulting in instant death. Neglecting to manage Wound stacks and fighting head-on without reducing the enemy's Attack Speed is also dangerous.
strategy.strengths: Your greatest strengths are your flowing combo attacks initiated by skill hits, and the percentage damage and damage reduction provided by your enhanced basic attack 'Splash Graduation'. The Attack Speed reduction debuff from your Additional Skills gives you an overwhelming advantage against basic attack-reliant heroes. With Skill 2's high healing and your Ultimate's Super Armor, you boast extremely high sustained combat ability and dueling power, making you hard to kill even when diving the enemy team.
strategy.weaknesses: Because you must consistently hit skills to perform optimally, your mechanical difficulty is high, and you struggle against highly evasive or long-range enemies. Since you rely heavily on skill combos, being hit by a Silence will shut you down completely. Additionally, the Wound effect is removed when the enemy lands a basic attack, making the debuff less effective against heroes with slow, heavy-hitting attacks or Mages.

synergy.reason: 強力なCCで敵を拘束してくれるため、パッシブをスタックしやすくコンボを繋げやすい。
By gathering multiple enemies in one place, you can maximize area damage and recovery effects.

counters.reason: Silence forces skill combos to be interrupted and prevents stacking of passives.
Due to its high mobility and kite ability, it does not allow you to get close, and you can only deal damage from a distance.

passive.name: Resonance/Burning
passive.description:
Communicate with the divine spear "Kasuka" and gain 1 stack of resonance when the skill is activated. Consumes all stacks during a normal attack, activates the resonance skills ``Firmness - Battle Stop'', ``Eagerness - Breaking Castle'', and ``True Intention - Ryohara'' according to the number of stacks, and shortens the CD of ``Dangetsu'' and ``Oigumo'' by 1.5 seconds.
Resonance skills can be critical hits. Activation speed increases according to own attack speed (reflection rate 50%).

skill1.name: Dangetsu
skill1.description: Raise the spear forward and then swing it down. Deals 180 (180 + 60% of additional physical attack) physical damage (2 times in total) and knocks up for 0.5 seconds when thrusting up.

forms.description: Raise the spear forward and then swing it down. Deals 180 (180 + 60% of additional physical attack) physical damage (2 times in total) and knocks up for 0.5 seconds when thrusting up.

Performs continuous attacks with a spear. Increases movement speed by 25 for 1 second, deals 124 (55 + 40% of physical attack) physical damage to enemies (4 times total), and also gains 260 (260 + 4.5% of additional HP) shield (for 2 seconds).
Activates with normal attack when resonance stacks 1.

skill2.name: Oigumo
skill2.description: Raise your spear, charge and charge (maximum movement speed increased by 25%). After charging, charge forward and mow down the enemy, dealing 180 (180 + 60% of additional physical attack) to 360 (360 + 120% of additional physical attack) physical damage and reducing movement speed by 15% for 1 second.

forms.description: Raise your spear, charge and charge (maximum movement speed increased by 25%). After charging, charge forward and mow down the enemy, dealing 180 (180 + 60% of additional physical attack) to 360 (360 + 120% of additional physical attack) physical damage and reducing movement speed by 15% for 1 second.

Performs three consecutive attacks. The first two attacks each deal 129 (60 + 40% of physical attack) physical damage to the enemy and restore 90 HP (90 + 1.3% of additional HP). The final attack deals 258 (120 + 80% of physical attack) physical damage and restores 180 HP (180 + 2.5% of additional HP).
Activates with normal attack when resonance stacks 2. The lower the HP, the more the recovery amount will be (up to 2x).

skill3.name: extra star
skill3.description:
Fills the tip of the spear with flame and moves it back and forth towards the front area, dealing 180 (180 + 60% of additional physical attack) physical damage each time and knocking it up.
When the maximum distance is reached, it will pierce continuously, dealing 90 (90 + 30% of additional physical attack) physical damage each time (4 times in total).
While the skill is active, you will be in a super armor state, reducing damage taken by 30%, and can activate the second stage skill "Roar of Spears" which mowing down and burning the front.
Scrape deals 180 (180 + 60% of additional physical attack) physical damage, and Burn Area deals 50 (50 + 16% of additional physical attack) magic damage once every 0.2 seconds for 3 seconds.
The second stage can be activated while "Tracing Star" is activated. Mows down the surrounding area, cancels the subsequent charge, deals 120 (120 + 40% of additional physical attack) physical damage, and burns a circular area for 3 seconds.

forms.description:
Fills the tip of the spear with flame and moves it back and forth towards the front area, dealing 180 (180 + 60% of additional physical attack) physical damage each time and knocking it up.
When the maximum distance is reached, it will pierce continuously, dealing 90 (90 + 30% of additional physical attack) physical damage each time (4 times in total).
While the skill is active, you will be in a super armor state, reducing damage taken by 30%, and can activate the second stage skill "Roar of Spears" which mowing down and burning the front.
Scrape deals 180 (180 + 60% of additional physical attack) physical damage, and Burn Area deals 50 (50 + 16% of additional physical attack) magic damage once every 0.2 seconds for 3 seconds.
The second stage can be activated while "Tracing Star" is activated. Mows down the surrounding area, cancels the subsequent charge, deals 120 (120 + 40% of additional physical attack) physical damage, and burns a circular area for 3 seconds.

After jumping into the air and gathering strength, it unleashes a ferocious blow. Deals 333 (160 + 100% of physical attack) physical damage to enemies on the route (the lower the target's HP, the more damage). The damage is maximized when the target's HP falls below 50%, dealing 666 (320 + 200% of physical attack) physical damage.
If you attack normally with 3 resonance stacks, you can ignore the terrain while jumping up. Additionally, movement speed increases by 200% and damage taken is reduced by 10%.

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, utilize Skill 1's knockup and Skill 2's charged dash to trade damage while applying CC to your enemies. Since every skill use grants a Passive Resonance stack, it's crucial to adjust your stack count according to the situation and unleash the most optimal Resonance Skill (enhanced basic attack). Consume stacks to reduce the cooldowns of Skill 1 and Skill 2, dominating the lane and jungle by overwhelming your opponent with rapid strikes.
strategy.midGame: In the mid game, use your mobility and strong CC to actively initiate ganks. Charge Skill 2 to boost your Movement Speed, quickly close the gap, and slow enemies with a sweep. Follow up with Skill 1's knockup, then consume your Resonance stacks for massive damage. Once you learn your Ultimate, you can continuously knock up enemies in a wide area while gaining Super Armor and damage reduction, drastically improving your control in skirmishes.
strategy.lateGame: In the late game, coordinate with your allies and take on the role of precisely engaging the enemy carries. Disrupt the enemy formation with your Ultimate's back-and-forth knockups and continuous thrusts, relying on Super Armor and damage reduction to survive focused fire. Afterward, deploy the 2nd phase 'Roar of the Spear' to sweep and create a burning area, zoning enemies with continuous magic damage while endlessly spamming enhanced basic attacks to cycle your cooldowns.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, the timing and placement of your Ultimate will largely decide the outcome. Target dense enemy clusters with your Ultimate to knock up multiple targets and set up kills for your team. During your Ultimate, you are in a Super Armor state, allowing you to force your way through enemy CC. Depending on the situation, cancel Skill 2's dash to immediately trigger the 2nd phase burn, cutting off the enemy's escape while continuously dealing damage with stack-consuming basic attacks.
strategy.commonMistakes: Failing to manage your Passive Resonance stacks and haphazardly using the wrong enhanced basic attack for the situation will prevent you from maximizing your damage and utility. Also, beware of exposing yourself to enemy attacks while charging Skill 2, or misjudging your dash distance and ending up stranded deep within enemy lines. Overestimating your Ultimate's powerful damage reduction and Super Armor by diving too deep without allied follow-up should also be avoided.
strategy.strengths: Your strength lies in your highly adaptable playstyle, made possible by a variety of enhanced basic attacks dependent on your skill usage count. Since triggering enhanced basic attacks reduces skill cooldowns, you boast a very high attack rate and excellent sustained combat ability. The multiple knockups from Skill 1 and your Ultimate, combined with the Super Armor and damage reduction during your Ultimate, grant you extremely high initiation power and survivability in teamfights.
strategy.weaknesses: Because managing multiple skill combos and Resonance stacks is mandatory, the mechanical difficulty is very high, meaning player proficiency directly dictates your performance. Since you rely on a flurry of strikes, your instant burst damage is somewhat lower compared to traditional Assassins. Additionally, your teamfight presence drops while your powerful Ultimate is on cooldown, so if enemies dodge your skills or shut you down with hard CC before you cast, you will die without showing your tenacity.

synergy.reason: This is because you can control the enemy, create a reliable opening, and create a situation where you can start a combo from a dive.
It provides shielding and protection, greatly increasing your chances of survival when diving deep into enemy lines.

counters.reason: Because it has its own CC release ability and stun, it can easily counterattack divers.
Their high mobility keeps them at a distance, and their high firepower burst damage cuts them down before they can get close.

passive.name: Passive Skill: Gyokuxing/Star Eye
passive.description:
Can move on walls, and normal attacks become long-range attacks (can also be activated while jumping).
The following effects are applied on the wall.
Gyokuxing: Hiyoshi charge speed increased by 10%
Star Eye: Increases visibility by 15%
CC Penalty: Movement speed reduction/CC time +25% granted on walls

skill1.name: Skill 1: Magadama/Tengeki
skill1.description:
Moves using the ball as a stepping stone, dealing 360 (360 + 110% of additional physical attack) physical damage to enemies along the route and knocking them up for 0.75 seconds. Also increases own attack speed by 50% and strengthens the next 3 normal attacks.
Close range: Deals an additional 75 (75 + 20% of additional physical attack) physical damage and recovers own HP by 155 (155 + 1.5% of additional maximum HP) each time.
Long range: Deals an additional 50 (50 + 13% of additional physical attack) physical damage each time. After hitting, knock away the nearest unit within range.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Stepping Ball/Hibio
skill2.description:
Step Ball: Jump from the ground to the specified wall. Jump distance increases by 4% with each level up.
Flying: Jumps from wall to wall, toying with the opponent.
You can stack 1 stack of "Tread Ball/Higai" every 10 seconds (up to 4 stacks).

skill3.name: Skill 3: Seal of condensed ball
skill3.description:
Mows down the enemy with the jade blade, inflicting 270 (270 + 80% of additional physical attack) physical damage, inflicts a seal on all targets (on the wall, you can activate a coagulate mark with a movement effect), and becomes able to activate a "curd jade attack".
"Coagulant Attack": Explodes the engraving with the sword, dealing 160 (160 + 50% of additional physical attack) physical damage to each target. This attack negates the untargetable state. If the target's HP is 1/8 or less, you can always destroy the enemy by turning it into a ball.

strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, take advantage of your Passive wall-climbing and ranged basic attacks to safely clear the jungle and harass lanes. On top of walls, your vision expands, and the overwhelming mobility of jumping from wall to wall with Skill 2 allows you to ambush enemies from unpredictable routes. Drop down from a wall with Skill 1 to knock up the enemy, using the enhanced melee basic attack's bonus damage and HP recovery to gain the upper hand in early skirmishes.
strategy.midGame: In the mid game, use your fully stacked Skill 2 to quickly traverse the entire map, aggressively hunting down isolated or low-HP enemies. The combo of applying marks to enemies in a wide area from a wall with your Ultimate, then dropping down with Skill 1 to deliver CC and enhanced basic attacks, is highly effective. Don't miss the moment an enemy's HP drops; activate your Ultimate's 2nd phase to burst the mark, ignoring untargetability and securing a guaranteed kill with the execute effect.
strategy.lateGame: In late-game teamfights, to compensate for your low durability, you must avoid frontal engagements and instead scout the enemy formation from atop the walls. Once your allies engage and the enemies' HP is whittled down, launch a surprise attack from the wall. Apply marks to multiple enemies with your Ultimate's wide sweep, and fulfill your role as an Assassin by instantly executing enemies below 1/8 HP with 'Jade-ify'. After securing kills, retreat back to the walls to enforce hit-and-run tactics.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, secure vision from the safety of the walls and wait for an opportunity to strike the enemy backline. Drop down with Skill 1 to disrupt their formation with a knockup, and control the distance using the ranged knockback from your enhanced basic attacks. Most importantly, your use of your Ultimate's execute effect is key; after spreading marks to the entire enemy team, you are expected to act as a finisher, wiping out low-HP targets.
strategy.commonMistakes: While walls offer excellent mobility and vision, they come with a hefty penalty: the duration of any Movement Speed slows or CC you receive is increased by 25%. A common mistake is taking a fatal blow on a wall because you caught an enemy disruption skill. Also, neglecting Skill 2's stack management and getting stranded on the ground without a way back up leaves you extremely vulnerable. Over-fixating on your Ultimate's execute effect and casting it before the enemy's HP is low will result in lacking damage.
strategy.strengths: Your greatest strength is the overwhelming mobility and unparalleled ambush routes provided by your Passive wall-walking and Skill 2, things no other hero can do. Combined with the broad vision from atop walls, your Ultimate's ability to ignore untargetability and unconditionally execute enemies below 1/8 HP poses a massive threat. The versatility between Skill 1's knockup and your enhanced basic attacks allows you to flexibly adapt to both melee and ranged combat situations.
strategy.weaknesses: If you are hit by CC while on a wall, the penalty extends its duration, heavily restricting your options against enemies with hard CC. Because you specialize in mobility, your base durability is low, making you extremely weak in straightforward ground brawls. Furthermore, to fully utilize your Ultimate's powerful execute effect, you need your allies to poke down the enemies' HP first, as you lack the sustained firepower to burst them down from full health alone.

synergy.reason: In group battles, it absorbs the enemy's attention and CC skills, allowing you to safely intervene in battle.
It breaks up enemy formations on the front lines, making it easy to aim at isolated enemies or soft targets in the rear.

counters.reason: Targeted hard CC will surely stop the movement and completely neutralize high mobility such as wall movement.
A strong suppression effect can stop a surprise attack and cause the enemy to focus on you.
